Verdict is CONDITIONAL until they are covered or ruled out.`);\n2008:       }\n2009:       if (severity.P2 > 0 && lines.length === 0) {\n2010:         lines.push(`- ${severity.P2} active P2 finding(s) — advisory only; release is not blocked by P2 alone, but the debt is tracked here so it does not disappear.`);\n2011:       }\n2012:       if (lines.length === 0) {\n2013:         lines.push('- None active beyond normal review uncertainty.');\n2014:       }\n2015:       return lines;\n2016:     })(),\n2017:     '',\n2018:     '<!-- /ANCHOR:active-risks -->',\n2019:     '',\n2020:   ].join('\\n');\n2021: }\n2022: \n2023: /**\n2024:  * Reduce JSONL state, iteration files, and strategy into synchronized registry,\n2025:  * strategy, and dashboard outputs. Idempotent: repeated calls produce identical results.\n2026:  *\n2027:  * @param {string} specFolder - Path to the target spec folder for the review packet\n2028:  * @param {Object} [options] - Reducer options\n2029:  * @param {boolean} [options.write=true] - Write outputs to disk when true\n2030:  * @returns {Object} Paths and content for registry, strategy, and dashboard\n2031:  */\n2032: function reduceReviewState(specFolder, options = {}) {\n2033:   const write = options.write !== false;\n2034:   const lenient = Boolean(options.lenient);\n2035:   const createMissingAnchors = Boolean(options.createMissingAnchors);\n2036:   const emitResourceMapOutput = Boolean(options.emitResourceMap);\n2037:   const resolvedSpecFolder = path.resolve(specFolder);\n2038:   const { artifactDir: reviewDir } = resolveArtifactRoot(resolvedSpecFolder, 'review');\n2039:   const configPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-config.json');\n2040:   const stateLogPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-state.jsonl');\n2041:   const strategyPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-strategy.md');\n2042:   const registryPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-findings-registry.json');\n2043:   const dashboardPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-dashboard.md');\n2044:   const resourceMapPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'resource-map.md');\n2045:   const iterationDir = path.join(reviewDir, 'iterations');\n2046:   const deltaDir = path.join(reviewDir, 'deltas');\n2047: \n2048:   const config = readJson(configPath);\n2049:   const { records: allRecords, corruptionWarnings } = parseJsonlDetailed(readUtf8(stateLogPath));\n2050:   // Allowlist: only iteration + event types bear completion semantics. Progress\n2051:   // records (type:'progress') are additive liveness signals — they reset the\n2052:   // no-progress watchdog but MUST NOT count as iterations or completion events.\n2053:   const records = filterCompletionBearingRecords(allRecords);\n2054:   const terminalStop = buildTerminalStopState(records);\n2055:   const lineage = buildLineageState(config, records, terminalStop);\n2056:   config.sessionId = lineage.sessionId || config.sessionId || '';\n2057:   config.parentSessionId = lineage.parentSessionId;\n2058:   config.lineageMode = lineage.lineageMode;\n2059:   config.generation = lineage.generation;\n2060:   config.continuedFromRun = lineage.continuedFromRun;\n2061:   if (terminalStop?.stopReason) {\n2062:     config.stopReason = terminalStop.stopReason;\n2063:   }\n2064:   const strategyContent = fs.existsSync(strategyPath) ? readUtf8(strategyPath) : '';\n2065:   const strategyDimensions = parseStrategyDimensions(strategyContent);\n2066:   const iterationFiles = fs.existsSync(iterationDir)\n2067:     ? fs.readdirSync(iterationDir)\n2068:         .filter((fileName) => /^iteration-\\d+\\.md$/.test(fileName))\n2069:         .sort()\n2070:         .map((fileName) => parseIterationFile(path.join(iterationDir, fileName)))\n2071:     : [];\n2072: \n2073:   // Load delta payloads up-front so the finding registry can use structured\n2074:   // rows first while the resource-map emit path reuses the same parsed data.\n2075:   const deltaPayloads = fs.existsSync(deltaDir) ? loadDeltaPayloads(deltaDir) : [];\n2076:   const flattenedDeltaRecords = deltaPayloads\n2077:     .flat()\n2078:     .filter((record) => record !== null && record !== undefined);\n2079: \n2080:   const pivotEvents = loadPivotEventRecords(reviewDir);\n2081:   const registry = buildRegistry(\n2082:     strategyDimensions,\n2083:     iterationFiles,\n2084:     records,\n2085:     config,\n2086:     corruptionWarnings,\n2087:     flattenedDeltaRecords,\n2088:     records.concat(pivotEvents),\n2089:   );\n2090:   const strategy = updateStrategyContent(strategyContent, registry, iterationFiles, { createMissingAnchors }, records);\n2091:   const dashboard = renderDashboard(config, registry, records, iterationFiles);\n2092:   let resourceMap = null;\n2093:   let resourceMapSkipped = true;\n2094:   let resourceMapSkipReason = null;\n2095: \n2096:   if (corruptionWarnings.length > 0 && !lenient) {\n2097:     throw createCorruptionError(stateLogPath, corruptionWarnings);\n2098:   }\n2099: \n2100:   if (emitResourceMapOutput) {\n2101:     if (getResourceMapEmitSetting(config) === false) {\n2102:       resourceMapSkipReason = 'config.resource_map.emit=false';\n2103:     } else {\n2104:       // Reuse the deltaPayloads already loaded above to avoid re-reading.\n2105:       if (!deltaPayloads.length) {\n2106:         resourceMapSkipReason = 'no delta files found';\n2107:       } else {\n2108:         resourceMap = emitResourceMap({\n2109:           shape: 'review',\n2110:           deltas: deltaPayloads,\n2111:           packet: {\n2112:             title: config.reviewTarget || path.basename(resolvedSpecFolder),\n2113:             specFolder: resolvedSpecFolder,\n2114:           },\n2115:           scope: `review convergence output for ${path.basename(resolvedSpecFolder)}`,\n2116:           createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n2117:         });\n2118:         resourceMapSkipped = false;\n2119:       }\n2120:     }\n2121:   }\n2122: \n2123:   if (write) {\n2124:     writeUtf8(registryPath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\\n`);\n2125:     if (strategyContent) {\n2126:       writeUtf8(strategyPath, strategy.endsWith('\\n') ? strategy : `${strategy}\\n`);\n2127:     }\n2128:     writeUtf8(dashboardPath, dashboard);\n2129:     if (emitResourceMapOutput && resourceMap) {\n2130:       writeUtf8(resourceMapPath, resourceMap);\n2131:     }\n2132:   }\n2133: \n2134:   return {\n2135:     configPath,\n2136:     stateLogPath,\n2137:     strategyPath,\n2138:     registryPath,\n2139:     dashboardPath,\n2140:     resourceMapPath,\n2141:     registry,\n2142:     strategy,\n2143:     dashboard,\n2144:     resourceMap,\n2145:     resourceMapSkipped,\n2146:     resourceMapSkipReason,\n2147:     corruptionWarnings,\n2148:     hasCorruption: corruptionWarnings.length > 0,\n2149:   };\n2150: }\n2151: \n2152: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n2153: // 6. CLI ENTRY POINT\n2154: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n2155: \n2156: if (require.main === module) {\n2157:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n2158:   const lenient = args.includes('--lenient');\n2159:   const createMissingAnchors = args.includes('--create-missing-anchors');\n2160:   const emitResourceMapOutput = args.includes('--emit-resource-map');\n2161:   const positional = args.filter((arg) => !arg.startsWith('--'));\n2162:   const specFolder = positional[0];\n2163: \n2164:   if (!specFolder) {\n2165:     process.stderr.write(\n2166:       'Usage: node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder> [--lenient] [--create-missing-anchors] [--emit-resource-map]\\n',\n2167:     );\n2168:     process.exit(1);\n2169:   }\n2170: \n2171:   try {\n2172:     const result = reduceReviewState(specFolder, {\n2173:       write: true,\n2174:       lenient,\n2175:       createMissingAnchors,\n2176:       emitResourceMap: emitResourceMapOutput,\n2177:     });\n2178:     process.stdout.write(\n2179:       `${JSON.stringify(\n2180:         {\n2181:           registryPath: result.registryPath,\n2182:           dashboardPath: result.dashboardPath,\n2183:           strategyPath: result.strategyPath,\n2184:           openFindingsCount: result.registry.openFindingsCount,\n2185:           resolvedFindingsCount: result.registry.resolvedFindingsCount,\n2186:           convergenceScore: result.registry.convergenceScore,\n2187:           graphConvergenceScore: result.registry.graphConvergenceScore,\n2188:           searchDebtCount: Array.isArray(result.registry.searchDebt) ? result.registry.searchDebt.length : 0,\n2189:           corruptionCount: result.corruptionWarnings.length,\n2190:           resourceMapPath: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapPath : null,\n2191:           resourceMapSkipped: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipped : null,\n2192:           resourceMapSkipReason: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipReason : null,\n2193:         },\n2194:         null,\n2195:         2,\n2196:       )}\\n`,\n2197:     );\n2198:   } catch (error) {\n2199:     if (error && error.code === 'STATE_CORRUPTION') {\n2200:       process.stderr.write(`${error.message}\\n`);\n2201:       process.exit(2);\n2202:     }\n2203:     process.stderr.write(`[deep-review] reducer failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\\n`);\n2204:     process.exit(3);\n2205:   }\n2206: }\n2207: \n2208: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n2209: // 7. EXPORTS\n2210: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n2211: \n2212: module.exports = {\n2213:   REQUIRED_DIMENSIONS,\n2214:   SEVERITY_KEYS,\n2215:   SEVERITY_WEIGHTS,\n2216:   buildGraphConvergenceRollup,\n2217:   parseIterationFile,\n2218:   parseJsonl,\n2219:   parseJsonlDetailed,\n2220:   parseFindingLine,\n2221:   replaceAnchorSection,\n2222:   reduceReviewState,\n2223:   deltaRecordToFinding,\n2224:   deriveDashboardStatus,\n2225:   collapseFindingsByDedupKey,\n2226:   findingDedupKey,\n2227:   buildTraceabilityRollup,\n2228:   validateReviewRecordFields,\n2229: };\n\n(End of file - total 2229 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"        lines.push(`- Claim-adjudication gate last failed at run ${latestClaimAdjudication.run ?? '?'}${missing}. STOP is vetoed until every active P0/P1 has a typed claim-adjudication packet.`);\n      }\n      const latestBlockedStop = registry.blockedStopHistory?.at(-1);\n      if (latestBlockedStop && Array.isArray(latestBlockedStop.blockedBy) && latestBlockedStop.blockedBy.length > 0) {\n        lines.push(`- Latest blocked_stop at run ${latestBlockedStop.run ?? '?'}: ${formatBlockedByList(latestBlockedStop.blockedBy)}. Recovery: ${latestBlockedStop.recoveryStrategy || 'see dashboard §BLOCKED STOPS'}.`);\n      }\n      if (hasSearchDebt) {\n        lines.push(`- ${registry.searchDebt.length} search-debt obligation(s) remain deferred or blocked. Verdict is CONDITIONAL until they are covered or ruled out.`);\n      }\n      if (severity.P2 > 0 && lines.length === 0) {\n        lines.push(`- ${severity.P2} active P2 finding(s) — advisory only; release is not blocked by P2 alone, but the debt is tracked here so it does not disappear.`);\n      }\n      if (lines.length === 0) {\n        lines.push('- None active beyond normal review uncertainty.');\n      }\n      return lines;\n    })(),\n    '',\n    '<!-- /ANCHOR:active-risks -->',\n    '',","truncated":false,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs","text":"        lines.push(`- Claim-adjudication gate last failed at run ${latestClaimAdjudication.run ?? '?'}${missing}. STOP is vetoed until every active P0/P1 has a typed claim-adjudication packet.`);\n      }\n      const latestBlockedStop = registry.blockedStopHistory?.at(-1);\n      if (latestBlockedStop && Array.isArray(latestBlockedStop.blockedBy) && latestBlockedStop.blockedBy.length > 0) {\n        lines.push(`- Latest blocked_stop at run ${latestBlockedStop.run ?? '?'}: ${formatBlockedByList(latestBlockedStop.blockedBy)}. Recovery: ${latestBlockedStop.recoveryStrategy || 'see dashboard §BLOCKED STOPS'}.`);\n      }\n      if (hasSearchDebt) {\n        lines.push(`- ${registry.searchDebt.length} search-debt obligation(s) remain deferred or blocked. Verdict is CONDITIONAL until they are covered or ruled out.`);\n      }\n      if (severity.P2 > 0 && lines.length === 0) {\n        lines.push(`- ${severity.P2} active P2 finding(s) — advisory only; release is not blocked by P2 alone, but the debt is tracked here so it does not disappear.`);\n      }\n      if (lines.length === 0) {\n        lines.push('- None active beyond normal review uncertainty.');\n      }\n      return lines;\n    })(),\n    '',\n    '<!-- /ANCHOR:active-risks -->',\n    '',\n  ].join('\\n');\n}\n\n/**\n * Reduce JSONL state, iteration files, and strategy into synchronized registry,\n * strategy, and dashboard outputs. Idempotent: repeated calls produce identical results.\n *\n * @param {string} specFolder - Path to the target spec folder for the review packet\n * @param {Object} [options] - Reducer options\n * @param {boolean} [options.write=true] - Write outputs to disk when true\n * @returns {Object} Paths and content for registry, strategy, and dashboard\n */\nfunction reduceReviewState(specFolder, options = {}) {\n  const write = options.write !== false;\n  const lenient = Boolean(options.lenient);\n  const createMissingAnchors = Boolean(options.createMissingAnchors);\n  const emitResourceMapOutput = Boolean(options.emitResourceMap);\n  const resolvedSpecFolder = path.resolve(specFolder);\n  const { artifactDir: reviewDir } = resolveArtifactRoot(resolvedSpecFolder, 'review');\n  const configPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-config.json');\n  const stateLogPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-state.jsonl');\n  const strategyPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-strategy.md');\n  const registryPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-findings-registry.json');\n  const dashboardPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-dashboard.md');\n  const resourceMapPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'resource-map.md');\n  const iterationDir = path.join(reviewDir, 'iterations');\n  const deltaDir = path.join(reviewDir, 'deltas');\n\n  const config = readJson(configPath);\n  const { records: allRecords, corruptionWarnings } = parseJsonlDetailed(readUtf8(stateLogPath));\n  // Allowlist: only iteration + event types bear completion semantics. Progress\n  // records (type:'progress') are additive liveness signals — they reset the\n  // no-progress watchdog but MUST NOT count as iterations or completion events.\n  const records = filterCompletionBearingRecords(allRecords);\n  const terminalStop = buildTerminalStopState(records);\n  const lineage = buildLineageState(config, records, terminalStop);\n  config.sessionId = lineage.sessionId || config.sessionId || '';\n  config.parentSessionId = lineage.parentSessionId;\n  config.lineageMode = lineage.lineageMode;\n  config.generation = lineage.generation;\n  config.continuedFromRun = lineage.continuedFromRun;\n  if (terminalStop?.stopReason) {\n    config.stopReason = terminalStop.stopReason;\n  }\n  const strategyContent = fs.existsSync(strategyPath) ? readUtf8(strategyPath) : '';\n  const strategyDimensions = parseStrategyDimensions(strategyContent);\n  const iterationFiles = fs.existsSync(iterationDir)\n    ? fs.readdirSync(iterationDir)\n        .filter((fileName) => /^iteration-\\d+\\.md$/.test(fileName))\n        .sort()\n        .map((fileName) => parseIterationFile(path.join(iterationDir, fileName)))\n    : [];\n\n  // Load delta payloads up-front so the finding registry can use structured\n  // rows first while the resource-map emit path reuses the same parsed data.\n  const deltaPayloads = fs.existsSync(deltaDir) ? loadDeltaPayloads(deltaDir) : [];\n  const flattenedDeltaRecords = deltaPayloads\n    .flat()\n    .filter((record) => record !== null && record !== undefined);\n\n  const pivotEvents = loadPivotEventRecords(reviewDir);\n  const registry = buildRegistry(\n    strategyDimensions,\n    iterationFiles,\n    records,\n    config,\n    corruptionWarnings,\n    flattenedDeltaRecords,\n    records.concat(pivotEvents),\n  );\n  const strategy = updateStrategyContent(strategyContent, registry, iterationFiles, { createMissingAnchors }, records);\n  const dashboard = renderDashboard(config, registry, records, iterationFiles);\n  let resourceMap = null;\n  let resourceMapSkipped = true;\n  let resourceMapSkipReason = null;\n\n  if (corruptionWarnings.length > 0 && !lenient) {\n    throw createCorruptionError(stateLogPath, corruptionWarnings);\n  }\n\n  if (emitResourceMapOutput) {\n    if (getResourceMapEmitSetting(config) === false) {\n      resourceMapSkipReason = 'config.resource_map.emit=false';\n    } else {\n      // Reuse the deltaPayloads already loaded above to avoid re-reading.\n      if (!deltaPayloads.length) {\n        resourceMapSkipReason = 'no delta files found';\n      } else {\n        resourceMap = emitResourceMap({\n          shape: 'review',\n          deltas: deltaPayloads,\n          packet: {\n            title: config.reviewTarget || path.basename(resolvedSpecFolder),\n            specFolder: resolvedSpecFolder,\n          },\n          scope: `review convergence output for ${path.basename(resolvedSpecFolder)}`,\n          createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n        });\n        resourceMapSkipped = false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (write) {\n    writeUtf8(registryPath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\\n`);\n    if (strategyContent) {\n      writeUtf8(strategyPath, strategy.endsWith('\\n') ? strategy : `${strategy}\\n`);\n    }\n    writeUtf8(dashboardPath, dashboard);\n    if (emitResourceMapOutput && resourceMap) {\n      writeUtf8(resourceMapPath, resourceMap);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return {\n    configPath,\n    stateLogPath,\n    strategyPath,\n    registryPath,\n    dashboardPath,\n    resourceMapPath,\n    registry,\n    strategy,\n    dashboard,\n    resourceMap,\n    resourceMapSkipped,\n    resourceMapSkipReason,\n    corruptionWarnings,\n    hasCorruption: corruptionWarnings.length > 0,\n  };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// 6. CLI ENTRY POINT\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n  const lenient = args.includes('--lenient');\n  const createMissingAnchors = args.includes('--create-missing-anchors');\n  const emitResourceMapOutput = args.includes('--emit-resource-map');\n  const positional = args.filter((arg) => !arg.startsWith('--'));\n  const specFolder = positional[0];\n\n  if (!specFolder) {\n    process.stderr.write(\n      'Usage: node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder> [--lenient] [--create-missing-anchors] [--emit-resource-map]\\n',\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const result = reduceReviewState(specFolder, {\n      write: true,\n      lenient,\n      createMissingAnchors,\n      emitResourceMap: emitResourceMapOutput,\n    });\n    process.stdout.write(\n      `${JSON.stringify(\n        {\n          registryPath: result.registryPath,\n          dashboardPath: result.dashboardPath,\n          strategyPath: result.strategyPath,\n          openFindingsCount: result.registry.openFindingsCount,\n          resolvedFindingsCount: result.registry.resolvedFindingsCount,\n          convergenceScore: result.registry.convergenceScore,\n          graphConvergenceScore: result.registry.graphConvergenceScore,\n          searchDebtCount: Array.isArray(result.registry.searchDebt) ? result.registry.searchDebt.length : 0,\n          corruptionCount: result.corruptionWarnings.length,\n          resourceMapPath: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapPath : null,\n          resourceMapSkipped: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipped : null,\n          resourceMapSkipReason: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipReason : null,\n        },\n        null,\n        2,\n      )}\\n`,\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (error && error.code === 'STATE_CORRUPTION') {\n      process.stderr.write(`${error.message}\\n`);\n      process.exit(2);\n    }\n    process.stderr.write(`[deep-review] reducer failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\\n`);\n    process.exit(3);\n  }\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// 7. EXPORTS\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nmodule.exports = {\n  REQUIRED_DIMENSIONS,\n  SEVERITY_KEYS,\n  SEVERITY_WEIGHTS,\n  buildGraphConvergenceRollup,\n  parseIterationFile,\n  parseJsonl,\n  parseJsonlDetailed,\n  parseFindingLine,\n  replaceAnchorSection,\n  reduceReviewState,\n  deltaRecordToFinding,\n  deriveDashboardStatus,\n  collapseFindingsByDedupKey,\n  findingDedupKey,\n  buildTraceabilityRollup,\n  validateReviewRecordFields,\n};","lineStart":2000,"lineEnd":2229,"totalLines":2229,"truncated":false}},"title":".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs","time":{"start":1783915678338,"end":1783915678343}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_0c493fdd5a26901e016a54649dc4148191b887d2cc9832a453"}},"id":"prt_f59a9083d0014y8heUghKYTJw4","sessionID":"ses_0a65ac472ffeDn3G1fX5LbU2k8","messageID":"msg_f59a8fadd001gho3ovUTIkDS3H"}}
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/spec.md:186:| 15 | 015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/ | Recalibrate the two miscalibrated shared validators (SECTION_COUNTS, ANCHORS_VALID), reconcile phases 010-014 status/metadata, and correct the packet's rename narrative, fingerprint overclaims and dangling cross-references found by a four-reviewer audit | Complete |
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/spec.md:15:    recent_action: "Authored spec from four-reviewer audit dossier PKT/DOC/VAL findings"
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/spec.md:66:This is **Phase 15** of packet 032, the first of eight remediation phases (015-021 plus 009) opened from the 2026-07-03 four-reviewer audit (`../scratch/2026-07-03-four-reviewer-audit-findings.md`, sections C/D). This phase closes the audit's documentation findings (DOC-1, DOC-2), spec-packet bookkeeping findings (PKT-1 through PKT-15), and the validator findings (VAL-1, VAL-2 — VAL-2 confirmed 2026-07-03 while authoring phase 016 docs). It touches ZERO plugin behavior — `.opencode/plugins/mk-goal.js` is out of scope here and belongs to phase 016 onward.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/spec.md:97:A four-reviewer audit (plugin code, documentation, spec packet, speckit integration) found that packet 032's own bookkeeping has drifted from reality across phases 007-014: three-way status disagreements, stale "N of M" phase-count labels contradicted by later phases, a false rename history repeated in four documents, an overstated "packet-wide" fingerprint fix, and dangling cross-references left behind when review/research folders were archived and their paths reused by later audits. Separately, two shared validator rules are miscalibrated: `SECTION_COUNTS` warns on the literal `**Given**` acceptance-scenario marker count, which the lean templates no longer include by default, so every clean Level-1 phase doc in this packet (and in unrelated packets, reproduced on `system-speckit/028`) fails `validate.sh --strict` for a reason unrelated to real documentation quality; and `ANCHORS_VALID` (via the compiled `orchestrator.ts`'s hardcoded `OPTIONAL_TEMPLATE_ANCHORS` set) false-errors on every real Level-2 packet because it never learned the Level-2 optional anchors that the newer `template-structure.js` helper already treats as optional, confirmed against both an unrelated packet (`anobel.com/002-link-card-button-and-mobile-animation`) and this packet's own in-flight phase 016 Level-2 docs.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/spec.md:213:- None. The four-reviewer audit dossier already resolved every design question this phase needs (recalibrate the validator against real template counts, not pad docs; correct narrative using git-verified lineage; recompute fingerprints with the same mechanism phase 013 used).
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/plan.md:63:- [x] Problem statement clear and scope documented (spec.md, sourced from the four-reviewer audit dossier's sections C/D and Validator finding)
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/description.json:4:  "description": "A four-reviewer audit (plugin code, documentation, spec packet, speckit integration) found that packet 032's own bookkeeping has drifted from reality",
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/015-packet-hygiene-and-narrative-integrity/graph-metadata.json:206:    "causal_summary": "A four-reviewer audit (plugin code, documentation, spec packet, speckit integration) found that packet 032's own bookkeeping has drifted from reality across phases 007-014: three-way status disagreements, stale \"N of M\" phase-count labels contradicted by later phases, a false rename history repeated in four documents, an overstated \"packet-wide\" fingerprint fix, and dangling cross-references left behind when review/research folders were archived and their paths reused by later audits",
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/020-capability-additions/spec.md:3:description: "The goal plugin lacks small operator-facing capabilities from the 2026-07-03 four-reviewer audit backlog (e-3): archived goals are invisible, no health introspection, pause is one-way, tokenBudget is not routed from the command surface, autonomy caps are hardcoded, usage_limited is permanent until manual intervention, and provider-limit detection is brittle."
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/020-capability-additions/spec.md:15:    recent_action: "Authored spec from the four-reviewer audit dossier (e-3 backlog)"
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/020-capability-additions/spec.md:67:This is **Phase 20** of the goal-plugin review remediation program, covering the additions backlog (§e-3) of the four-reviewer audit dossier (`../scratch/2026-07-03-four-reviewer-audit-findings.md`): e-3.2, e-3.4, e-3.5, e-3.6, e-3.7, e-3.8, e-3.9, plus a documented deferral of e-3.10. Item e-3.3 (log rotation + timestamps) is NOT here — it is folded into F1's fix in phase 016; e-3.1 (completion verifier) is design-gated in phase 021.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/109-skill-readme-standardization/021-system-code-graph-readme/context/seats/iter-001/mimo.jsonl:6:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780844555944,"sessionID":"ses_15d64cbc8ffep7tNH8gvu3pwjL","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_a391871ead324b81bc13e366","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/INSTALL_GUIDE.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/INSTALL_GUIDE.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: title: \"System Code Graph Native Bootstrap\"\n3: description: \"Installation, configuration, verification, and operator notes for the standalone mk-code-index MCP server providing structural AST indexing, blast-radius analysis, neighborhood context, and change detection.\"\n4: trigger_phrases:\n5:   - \"install mk-code-index\"\n6:   - \"install system-code-graph\"\n7:   - \"mk-code-index install\"\n8:   - \"code graph install\"\n9:   - \"system code graph bootstrap\"\n10:   - \"mk_code_index install\"\n11: ---\n12: \n13: # System Code Graph Native Bootstrap\n14: \n15: <!-- sk-doc-template: skill_reference_install_guide -->\n16: \n17: This is the canonical bootstrap guide for the standalone System Code Graph MCP server. The server runs as `mk_code_index` (filesystem key `mk-code-index`), separate from `mk-spec-memory` and `mk_skill_advisor`, while exposing the 8 public tool ids `code_graph_scan`, `code_graph_query`, `code_graph_classify_query_intent`, `code_graph_status`, `code_graph_context`, `code_graph_verify`, `code_graph_apply`, and `detect_changes`.\n18: \n19: ---\n20: \n21: ## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n22: \n23: Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n24: \n25: ```\n26: I want to install the System Code Graph MCP server (mk_code_index) from .opencode/skills/system-code-graph\n27: \n28: Please help me:\n29: 1. Verify I have Node.js >=20.11.0 and npm installed\n30: 2. Install dependencies and build the standalone TypeScript MCP server\n31: 3. Confirm the compiled entrypoint exists at mcp_server/dist/index.js\n32: 4. Add the mk_code_index server entry to my runtime config (I'm using: [OpenCode / Claude Code / Codex / Gemini])\n33: 5. Verify code_graph_status, code_graph_scan, and code_graph_verify respond\n34: \n35: Guide me through each step with the exact commands I need to run.\n36: ```\n37: \n38: Your AI assistant will:\n39: - Verify Node.js >=20.11.0 and npm are available\n40: - Install and build the standalone code-graph MCP server\n41: - Confirm the launcher and compiled entrypoint are present\n42: - Configure `mk_code_index` for your runtime (the five INDEX_* flags ship `false` for a quiet, low-disk index)\n43: - Confirm the 8 structural tools register and respond\n44: \n45: **Expected setup time:** 3-5 minutes\n46: \n47: ---\n48: \n49: ## 1. OVERVIEW\n50: \n51: System Code Graph is a TypeScript MCP server under `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/` that registers the `mk-code-index` server identity. The runtime package is published privately as `@spec-kit/system-code-graph` and ships a Node launcher at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`. The launcher boots the compiled entrypoint at `mcp_server/dist/index.js` after loading `.env.local` overrides, applying the optional maintainer-mode flag, and guarding the database path against external locations.\n52: \n53: The server is runtime-standalone: it does not depend on `mk-spec-memory` being installed or running first. Its database lives at `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite`. This skill-local path is the canonical location, shared across runtimes via the `.opencode/skills` symlink. A former shared location (`.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/`) is auto-migrated back to this skill-local location on first launch.\n54: \n55: Public MCP namespace: `mcp__mk_code_index__*`. Hyphens in the server name become underscores in the namespace prefix per MCP convention.\n56: \n57: | Field | Value |\n58: |---|---|\n59: | Skill version | `1.0.3.2` |\n60: | Runtime package | `@spec-kit/system-code-graph` |\n61: | Server name | `mk-code-index` |\n62: | Config key | `mk_code_index` |\n63: | Launcher | `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` |\n64: | Entry point | `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` |\n65: | Database (default) | `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite` |\n66: | MCP tools | 8 (see [README.md](./README.md) §3.2) |\n67: \n68: ---\n69: \n70: ## 2. PREREQUISITES\n71: \n72: - Node.js >= 20.11.0 (matches `mcp-doctor.sh` minimum threshold; check with `node --version`).\n73: - npm available on PATH.\n74: - Repository root as the current working directory.\n75: - Runtime MCP configuration with an entry for `mk_code_index` in the active config (one of: `opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.devin/config.json`, `.vscode/mcp.json`).\n76: \n77: `mk-spec-memory` is NOT a prerequisite. Code Graph does not need the memory MCP server to be running first. The TypeScript build emits only this package's runtime files under `mcp_server/dist/`.\n78: \n79: ---\n80: \n81: ## 3. INSTALLATION\n82: \n83: Install dependencies and build the standalone TypeScript MCP server:\n84: \n85: ```bash\n86: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph install\n87: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build\n88: ```\n89: \n90: Verify the compiled entrypoint exists:\n91: \n92: ```bash\n93: test -f .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js && echo \"Installed\"\n94: ```\n95: \n96: The launcher is already committed at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` and does not need a separate install step. Start or refresh the `mk_code_index` MCP server in the active runtime after build.\n97: \n98: ---\n99: \n100: ## 4. CONFIGURATION\n101: \n102: Each runtime expects an MCP server entry with the same launcher invocation. The canonical block (from `opencode.json`) is:\n103: \n104: ```json\n105: {\n106:   \"mcp\": {\n107:     \"mk_code_index\": {\n108:       \"type\": \"local\",\n109:       \"command\": [\"node\", \".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs\"],\n110:       \"environment\": {\n111:         \"_NOTE_1_DB\": \"Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides.\",\n112:         \"_NOTE_2_TOOLS\": \"Registers 8 tools: code_graph_scan/query/classify_query_intent/context/status/verify/apply, detect_changes. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_code_index__*\",\n113:         \"_NOTE_3_INDEX_DEFAULTS\": \"INDEX_* committed defaults are false (end-user safe). Maintainer mode: set SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true in .env.local (gitignored); the launcher will force all 5 INDEX_* to true at startup.\",\n114:         \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS\": \"false\",\n115:         \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS\": \"false\",\n116:         \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS\": \"false\",\n117:         \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS\": \"false\",\n118:         \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS\": \"false\"\n119:       }\n120:     }\n121:   }\n122: }\n123: ```\n124: \n125: Equivalent TOML for `.codex/config.toml`:\n126: \n127: ```toml\n128: [mcp_servers.mk_code_index]\n129: command = \"node\"\n130: args = [\".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs\"]\n131: \n132: [mcp_servers.mk_code_index.env]\n133: _NOTE_1_DB = \"Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides.\"\n134: _NOTE_2_TOOLS = \"Registers 8 tools: code_graph_scan/query/classify_query_intent/context/status/verify/apply, detect_changes. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_code_index__*\"\n135: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS = \"false\"\n136: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS = \"false\"\n137: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS = \"false\"\n138: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS = \"false\"\n139: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS = \"false\"\n140: ```\n141: \n142: Runtime-config locations:\n143: \n144: | Runtime | Path |\n145: |---|---|\n146: | OpenCode | `opencode.json` |\n147: | Claude Code | `.claude/mcp.json` |\n148: | Codex CLI | `.codex/config.toml` |\n149: | Devin CLI | `.devin/config.json` |\n150: | VSCode | `.vscode/mcp.json` |\n151: \n152: The five `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` flags ship as `false` so end users get a quiet, low-disk index. Maintainers who need full structural coverage enable them via maintainer mode (see [§7](#7-database-and-maintainer-mode)).\n153: \n154: ---\n155: \n156: ## 5. VERIFICATION\n157: \n158: Verify native tool registration through `mk_code_index`:\n159: \n160: ```text\n161: mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_status({})\n162: mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_scan({ \"incremental\": true })\n163: mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_verify({})\n164: ```\n165: \n166: Expected:\n167: \n168: - `code_graph_status` returns `readiness`, `canonicalReadiness`, `trustState`, `lastScanAt`, `schemaVersion`, and graph-quality metadata.\n169: - `code_graph_scan` returns scan metadata with updated file, node, and edge counts. Use `incremental: false` when the stored scope fingerprint differs from the requested scope.\n170: - `code_graph_verify` returns gold-query battery results once a fresh scan has populated the graph.\n171: \n172: Also verify the active runtime lists `mk_code_index` alongside `mk-spec-memory` and `mk_skill_advisor` (the three core native MCP servers shipped by this repository).\n173: \n174: ---\n175: \n176: ## 6. NATIVE PACKAGE CHECKS\n177: \n178: Run before declaring bootstrap complete:\n179: \n180: ```bash\n181: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit -p .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/tsconfig.json\n182: test ! -e .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/dist\n183: node -e \"import('./.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/tool-schemas.js')\"\n184: .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/node_modules/.bin/vitest --config .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/vitest.config.ts --run code-graph\n185: ```\n186: \n187: Current code-graph baseline:\n188: \n189: | Metric | Expected |\n190: | --- | --- |\n191: | Active MCP tools | 8 |\n192: | TypeScript build | Exit 0 from `tsc --build` |\n193: | Vitest focused run | Pass on the `code-graph` suite (runtime-detection + structural-contract + handler suites) |\n194: | Standalone-storage guard | Launcher rejects DB paths outside the workspace |\n195: | Dist boundary | Root-level `dist/` is absent. Build output lives under `mcp_server/dist/`. |\n196: | Production sibling imports | 0 relative imports from `system-spec-kit` or `system-skill-advisor` production source. Test fixtures may still model integration boundaries. |\n197: \n198: ---\n199: \n200: ## 7. DATABASE AND MAINTAINER MODE\n201: \n202: ### Database location\n203: \n204: Default: `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite`.\n205: \n206: Override with `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` (env var or `.env.local`). The launcher enforces a standalone-storage guard: the override must resolve inside the workspace root. External absolute paths are rejected at startup.\n207: \n208: ### Migration\n209: \n210: Installs with a database at the former shared location (`.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/`) are auto-migrated back to the skill-local location (`mcp_server/database/`) on next launch. The former database file is preserved as a backup; the launcher copies (does not move) the SQLite triplet, readiness marker, and launcher state file.\n211: \n212: ### Indexing scope flags\n213: \n214: | Flag | Default | Effect |\n215: |---|---|---|\n216: | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/skills/**`. Also accepts comma-separated `sk-*` allowlist (e.g. `sk-doc,sk-git`). |\n217: | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/agents/**`. |\n218: | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/commands/**`. |\n219: | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS` | `false` | Include `<active-spec-folder>/**`. |\n220: | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/plugins/**`. |\n221: \n222: End-user defaults are all `false` so the indexer is quiet and the SQLite file stays small. Per-call `code_graph_scan` arguments (`includeSkills`, `includeAgents`, `includeCommands`, `includeSpecs`, `includePlugins`) override the env defaults for one-shot scans.\n223: \n224: ### Maintainer mode\n225: \n226: Maintainers who index the full repo set:\n227: \n228: ```bash\n229: echo 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true' >> .env.local\n230: ```\n231: \n232: `.env.local` is gitignored. The launcher reads it on startup, and when the flag is `true` it forces all 5 `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` flags to `true` before spawning the server, overriding any committed `false` values from the runtime config. Per-call scan args still override.\n233: \n234: ---\n235: \n236: ## 8. TROUBLESHOOTING\n237: \n238: | What You See | Cause | Fix |\n239: |---|---|---|\n240: | Launcher exits with \"standalone-storage guard violation\" | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` resolves outside the workspace. | Use a path inside the repo, or unset the override. |\n241: | `requiredAction: \"code_graph_scan\"` in tool responses | Graph is stale, missing, or scope-mismatched. | Run `code_graph_scan` with the intended scope. Use `incremental: false` for scope changes. |\n242: | Skill files do not appear in scan results | `.opencode/skills/**` is excluded by default. | Set `includeSkills: true` per-call, or set `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true` (or enable maintainer mode). |\n243: | `parserHealth: \"quarantined\"` | Parser failures were added to the skip-list. | Inspect `parserSkipList.sample` from `code_graph_status`, then repair or accept the quarantine. |\n244: | Unknown MCP tool error mentions `mk-code-index` | Tool name not registered in `mcp_server/tools/code-graph-tools.ts`. | Add the schema, handler export, and dispatch case in one change. |\n245: | Config still uses `system_code_graph` namespace | Pre-packet-010 config not updated. | Rename the entry to `mk_code_index`, point at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, and use `mcp__mk_code_index__*` for all tool references. |\n246: | Root-level `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/dist/` exists | Stale pre-cleanup build output is still present. | Run `npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run clean && npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build`. |\n247: | `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index` fails `root_dist_absent` | Doctor detected stale root-level build output. | Run `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index --fix`, or run the clean and build commands above manually. |\n248: | Cross-runtime config audit needed | Want to verify checked-in runtimes are consistent. | `for f in opencode.json .claude/mcp.json .codex/config.toml .devin/config.json .vscode/mcp.json; do printf '%-30s ' \"$f\"; grep -c 'mk_code_index\\|mk-code-index' \"$f\"; done` should show >= 1 hit per file. |\n249: | `/doctor:mcp install --server mk_code_index` fails with \"Unknown server\" | `doctor_mcp_install.yaml` was not updated to include `mk_code_index` in `valid_values`. | Run `/doctor:update` to refresh subsystem coverage, or patch `valid_values` directly. |\n250: \n251: Runtime diagnostics are available via the `/doctor code-graph` slash-command surface (read-only Phase A by default). See `.opencode/install_guides/SET-UP - Code Graph.md` for the full diagnostic walkthrough.\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 9. RELATED RESOURCES\n256: \n257: | Document | Purpose |\n258: | --- | --- |\n259: | [README.md](./README.md) | Skill overview, quick start, tool reference, FAQ. |\n260: | [SKILL.md](./SKILL.md) | Runtime routing instructions and invariants. |\n261: | [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md) | System architecture, dependency direction, subsystem boundaries. |\n262: | [feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md](./feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md) | Current feature inventory. |\n263: | [manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md](./manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md) | Operator validation scenarios. |\n264: | [`SET-UP - Code Graph.md`](../../install_guides/SET-UP%20-%20Code%20Graph.md) | Runtime diagnostics via `/doctor code-graph` (Phase A read-only). |\n265: | [`.opencode/install_guides/README.md`](../../install_guides/README.md) | Master install guide, Phase 3 §10.4 mk-code-index subsection. |\n\n(End of file - total 265 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\ntitle: \"System Code Graph Native Bootstrap\"\ndescription: \"Installation, configuration, verification, and operator notes for the standalone mk-code-index MCP server providing structural AST indexing, blast-radius analysis, neighborhood context, and change detection.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"install mk-code-index\"\n  - \"install system-code-graph\"\n  - \"mk-code-index install\"\n  - \"code graph install\"\n  - \"system code graph bootstrap\"\n  - \"mk_code_index install\"\n---\n\n# System Code Graph Native Bootstrap\n\n<!-- sk-doc-template: skill_reference_install_guide -->\n\nThis is the canonical bootstrap guide for the standalone System Code Graph MCP server. The server runs as `mk_code_index` (filesystem key `mk-code-index`), separate from `mk-spec-memory` and `mk_skill_advisor`, while exposing the 8 public tool ids `code_graph_scan`, `code_graph_query`, `code_graph_classify_query_intent`, `code_graph_status`, `code_graph_context`, `code_graph_verify`, `code_graph_apply`, and `detect_changes`.\n\n---\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/INSTALL_GUIDE.md","text":"---\ntitle: \"System Code Graph Native Bootstrap\"\ndescription: \"Installation, configuration, verification, and operator notes for the standalone mk-code-index MCP server providing structural AST indexing, blast-radius analysis, neighborhood context, and change detection.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"install mk-code-index\"\n  - \"install system-code-graph\"\n  - \"mk-code-index install\"\n  - \"code graph install\"\n  - \"system code graph bootstrap\"\n  - \"mk_code_index install\"\n---\n\n# System Code Graph Native Bootstrap\n\n<!-- sk-doc-template: skill_reference_install_guide -->\n\nThis is the canonical bootstrap guide for the standalone System Code Graph MCP server. The server runs as `mk_code_index` (filesystem key `mk-code-index`), separate from `mk-spec-memory` and `mk_skill_advisor`, while exposing the 8 public tool ids `code_graph_scan`, `code_graph_query`, `code_graph_classify_query_intent`, `code_graph_status`, `code_graph_context`, `code_graph_verify`, `code_graph_apply`, and `detect_changes`.\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\nCopy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n\n```\nI want to install the System Code Graph MCP server (mk_code_index) from .opencode/skills/system-code-graph\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Verify I have Node.js >=20.11.0 and npm installed\n2. Install dependencies and build the standalone TypeScript MCP server\n3. Confirm the compiled entrypoint exists at mcp_server/dist/index.js\n4. Add the mk_code_index server entry to my runtime config (I'm using: [OpenCode / Claude Code / Codex / Gemini])\n5. Verify code_graph_status, code_graph_scan, and code_graph_verify respond\n\nGuide me through each step with the exact commands I need to run.\n```\n\nYour AI assistant will:\n- Verify Node.js >=20.11.0 and npm are available\n- Install and build the standalone code-graph MCP server\n- Confirm the launcher and compiled entrypoint are present\n- Configure `mk_code_index` for your runtime (the five INDEX_* flags ship `false` for a quiet, low-disk index)\n- Confirm the 8 structural tools register and respond\n\n**Expected setup time:** 3-5 minutes\n\n---\n\n## 1. OVERVIEW\n\nSystem Code Graph is a TypeScript MCP server under `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/` that registers the `mk-code-index` server identity. The runtime package is published privately as `@spec-kit/system-code-graph` and ships a Node launcher at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`. The launcher boots the compiled entrypoint at `mcp_server/dist/index.js` after loading `.env.local` overrides, applying the optional maintainer-mode flag, and guarding the database path against external locations.\n\nThe server is runtime-standalone: it does not depend on `mk-spec-memory` being installed or running first. Its database lives at `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite`. This skill-local path is the canonical location, shared across runtimes via the `.opencode/skills` symlink. A former shared location (`.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/`) is auto-migrated back to this skill-local location on first launch.\n\nPublic MCP namespace: `mcp__mk_code_index__*`. Hyphens in the server name become underscores in the namespace prefix per MCP convention.\n\n| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n| Skill version | `1.0.3.2` |\n| Runtime package | `@spec-kit/system-code-graph` |\n| Server name | `mk-code-index` |\n| Config key | `mk_code_index` |\n| Launcher | `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` |\n| Entry point | `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js` |\n| Database (default) | `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite` |\n| MCP tools | 8 (see [README.md](./README.md) §3.2) |\n\n---\n\n## 2. PREREQUISITES\n\n- Node.js >= 20.11.0 (matches `mcp-doctor.sh` minimum threshold; check with `node --version`).\n- npm available on PATH.\n- Repository root as the current working directory.\n- Runtime MCP configuration with an entry for `mk_code_index` in the active config (one of: `opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.devin/config.json`, `.vscode/mcp.json`).\n\n`mk-spec-memory` is NOT a prerequisite. Code Graph does not need the memory MCP server to be running first. The TypeScript build emits only this package's runtime files under `mcp_server/dist/`.\n\n---\n\n## 3. INSTALLATION\n\nInstall dependencies and build the standalone TypeScript MCP server:\n\n```bash\nnpm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph install\nnpm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build\n```\n\nVerify the compiled entrypoint exists:\n\n```bash\ntest -f .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js && echo \"Installed\"\n```\n\nThe launcher is already committed at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs` and does not need a separate install step. Start or refresh the `mk_code_index` MCP server in the active runtime after build.\n\n---\n\n## 4. CONFIGURATION\n\nEach runtime expects an MCP server entry with the same launcher invocation. The canonical block (from `opencode.json`) is:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcp\": {\n    \"mk_code_index\": {\n      \"type\": \"local\",\n      \"command\": [\"node\", \".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs\"],\n      \"environment\": {\n        \"_NOTE_1_DB\": \"Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides.\",\n        \"_NOTE_2_TOOLS\": \"Registers 8 tools: code_graph_scan/query/classify_query_intent/context/status/verify/apply, detect_changes. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_code_index__*\",\n        \"_NOTE_3_INDEX_DEFAULTS\": \"INDEX_* committed defaults are false (end-user safe). Maintainer mode: set SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true in .env.local (gitignored); the launcher will force all 5 INDEX_* to true at startup.\",\n        \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS\": \"false\",\n        \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS\": \"false\",\n        \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS\": \"false\",\n        \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS\": \"false\",\n        \"SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS\": \"false\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nEquivalent TOML for `.codex/config.toml`:\n\n```toml\n[mcp_servers.mk_code_index]\ncommand = \"node\"\nargs = [\".opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs\"]\n\n[mcp_servers.mk_code_index.env]\n_NOTE_1_DB = \"Database lives at .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite by default; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR overrides.\"\n_NOTE_2_TOOLS = \"Registers 8 tools: code_graph_scan/query/classify_query_intent/context/status/verify/apply, detect_changes. MCP namespace: mcp__mk_code_index__*\"\nSPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS = \"false\"\nSPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS = \"false\"\nSPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS = \"false\"\nSPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS = \"false\"\nSPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS = \"false\"\n```\n\nRuntime-config locations:\n\n| Runtime | Path |\n|---|---|\n| OpenCode | `opencode.json` |\n| Claude Code | `.claude/mcp.json` |\n| Codex CLI | `.codex/config.toml` |\n| Devin CLI | `.devin/config.json` |\n| VSCode | `.vscode/mcp.json` |\n\nThe five `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` flags ship as `false` so end users get a quiet, low-disk index. Maintainers who need full structural coverage enable them via maintainer mode (see [§7](#7-database-and-maintainer-mode)).\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFICATION\n\nVerify native tool registration through `mk_code_index`:\n\n```text\nmcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_status({})\nmcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_scan({ \"incremental\": true })\nmcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_verify({})\n```\n\nExpected:\n\n- `code_graph_status` returns `readiness`, `canonicalReadiness`, `trustState`, `lastScanAt`, `schemaVersion`, and graph-quality metadata.\n- `code_graph_scan` returns scan metadata with updated file, node, and edge counts. Use `incremental: false` when the stored scope fingerprint differs from the requested scope.\n- `code_graph_verify` returns gold-query battery results once a fresh scan has populated the graph.\n\nAlso verify the active runtime lists `mk_code_index` alongside `mk-spec-memory` and `mk_skill_advisor` (the three core native MCP servers shipped by this repository).\n\n---\n\n## 6. NATIVE PACKAGE CHECKS\n\nRun before declaring bootstrap complete:\n\n```bash\n.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit -p .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/tsconfig.json\ntest ! -e .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/dist\nnode -e \"import('./.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/tool-schemas.js')\"\n.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/node_modules/.bin/vitest --config .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/vitest.config.ts --run code-graph\n```\n\nCurrent code-graph baseline:\n\n| Metric | Expected |\n| --- | --- |\n| Active MCP tools | 8 |\n| TypeScript build | Exit 0 from `tsc --build` |\n| Vitest focused run | Pass on the `code-graph` suite (runtime-detection + structural-contract + handler suites) |\n| Standalone-storage guard | Launcher rejects DB paths outside the workspace |\n| Dist boundary | Root-level `dist/` is absent. Build output lives under `mcp_server/dist/`. |\n| Production sibling imports | 0 relative imports from `system-spec-kit` or `system-skill-advisor` production source. Test fixtures may still model integration boundaries. |\n\n---\n\n## 7. DATABASE AND MAINTAINER MODE\n\n### Database location\n\nDefault: `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database/code-graph.sqlite`.\n\nOverride with `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` (env var or `.env.local`). The launcher enforces a standalone-storage guard: the override must resolve inside the workspace root. External absolute paths are rejected at startup.\n\n### Migration\n\nInstalls with a database at the former shared location (`.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/`) are auto-migrated back to the skill-local location (`mcp_server/database/`) on next launch. The former database file is preserved as a backup; the launcher copies (does not move) the SQLite triplet, readiness marker, and launcher state file.\n\n### Indexing scope flags\n\n| Flag | Default | Effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/skills/**`. Also accepts comma-separated `sk-*` allowlist (e.g. `sk-doc,sk-git`). |\n| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/agents/**`. |\n| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/commands/**`. |\n| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS` | `false` | Include `<active-spec-folder>/**`. |\n| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/plugins/**`. |\n\nEnd-user defaults are all `false` so the indexer is quiet and the SQLite file stays small. Per-call `code_graph_scan` arguments (`includeSkills`, `includeAgents`, `includeCommands`, `includeSpecs`, `includePlugins`) override the env defaults for one-shot scans.\n\n### Maintainer mode\n\nMaintainers who index the full repo set:\n\n```bash\necho 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true' >> .env.local\n```\n\n`.env.local` is gitignored. The launcher reads it on startup, and when the flag is `true` it forces all 5 `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` flags to `true` before spawning the server, overriding any committed `false` values from the runtime config. Per-call scan args still override.\n\n---\n\n## 8. TROUBLESHOOTING\n\n| What You See | Cause | Fix |\n|---|---|---|\n| Launcher exits with \"standalone-storage guard violation\" | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` resolves outside the workspace. | Use a path inside the repo, or unset the override. |\n| `requiredAction: \"code_graph_scan\"` in tool responses | Graph is stale, missing, or scope-mismatched. | Run `code_graph_scan` with the intended scope. Use `incremental: false` for scope changes. |\n| Skill files do not appear in scan results | `.opencode/skills/**` is excluded by default. | Set `includeSkills: true` per-call, or set `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true` (or enable maintainer mode). |\n| `parserHealth: \"quarantined\"` | Parser failures were added to the skip-list. | Inspect `parserSkipList.sample` from `code_graph_status`, then repair or accept the quarantine. |\n| Unknown MCP tool error mentions `mk-code-index` | Tool name not registered in `mcp_server/tools/code-graph-tools.ts`. | Add the schema, handler export, and dispatch case in one change. |\n| Config still uses `system_code_graph` namespace | Pre-packet-010 config not updated. | Rename the entry to `mk_code_index`, point at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, and use `mcp__mk_code_index__*` for all tool references. |\n| Root-level `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/dist/` exists | Stale pre-cleanup build output is still present. | Run `npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run clean && npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build`. |\n| `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index` fails `root_dist_absent` | Doctor detected stale root-level build output. | Run `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index --fix`, or run the clean and build commands above manually. |\n| Cross-runtime config audit needed | Want to verify checked-in runtimes are consistent. | `for f in opencode.json .claude/mcp.json .codex/config.toml .devin/config.json .vscode/mcp.json; do printf '%-30s ' \"$f\"; grep -c 'mk_code_index\\|mk-code-index' \"$f\"; done` should show >= 1 hit per file. |\n| `/doctor:mcp install --server mk_code_index` fails with \"Unknown server\" | `doctor_mcp_install.yaml` was not updated to include `mk_code_index` in `valid_values`. | Run `/doctor:update` to refresh subsystem coverage, or patch `valid_values` directly. |\n\nRuntime diagnostics are available via the `/doctor code-graph` slash-command surface (read-only Phase A by default). See `.opencode/install_guides/SET-UP - Code Graph.md` for the full diagnostic walkthrough.\n\n---\n\n## 9. RELATED RESOURCES\n\n| Document | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| [README.md](./README.md) | Skill overview, quick start, tool reference, FAQ. |\n| [SKILL.md](./SKILL.md) | Runtime routing instructions and invariants. |\n| [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md) | System architecture, dependency direction, subsystem boundaries. |\n| [feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md](./feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md) | Current feature inventory. |\n| [manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md](./manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md) | Operator validation scenarios. |\n| [`SET-UP - Code Graph.md`](../../install_guides/SET-UP%20-%20Code%20Graph.md) | Runtime diagnostics via `/doctor code-graph` (Phase A read-only). |\n| [`.opencode/install_guides/README.md`](../../install_guides/README.md) | Master install guide, Phase 3 §10.4 mk-code-index subsection. |","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":265,"totalLines":265,"truncated":false}},"title":".opencode/skills/system-code-graph/INSTALL_GUIDE.md","time":{"start":1780844555930,"end":1780844555941}},"id":"prt_ea29b6e96001Q7N0056BqWO38X","sessionID":"ses_15d64cbc8ffep7tNH8gvu3pwjL","messageID":"msg_ea29b36fc001M0dFXmLcc758FC"}}

Phase 016's REQ-004 still wants the disabled-flag fix; if current code already passes the test, REQ-004 should be downgraded or re-scoped.","finalSeverity":"P1","confidence":0.85,"downgradeTrigger":"If phase 016's REQ-004 adds additional defense-in-depth beyond the current `if (!eventOptions.enabled) return;` (e.g. log-and-warn, return explicit error, additional env-var defense), the finding stays P1; if REQ-004 is a no-op for F4, downgrade to P2."}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/109-skill-readme-standardization/014-mcp-code-mode-readme/context/seats/iter-001/mimo.jsonl:5:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780836935634,"sessionID":"ses_15dd90497ffesWatPtiSJXIuCW","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_789d4036c03d488eb7d24fa5","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # MCP Code Mode Installation Guide\n2: \n3: Complete installation and configuration guide for the Code Mode MCP server. This enables TypeScript-based orchestration of external MCP tools, giving you unified access to MyService, Figma, ClickUp, GitHub, Chrome DevTools and other MCP servers through a single `call_tool_chain()` interface. It delivers 98.7% context reduction and 60% faster execution compared to individual tool calls, with type-safe invocation and automatic tool discovery.\n4: \n5: > **Version:** 2.0.0\n6: > **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n7: > **Package**: `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` | **Dependencies**: Node.js 18+, .utcp_config.json\n8: \n9: ---\n10: \n11: ## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n12: \n13: **Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:**\n14: \n15: ```\n16: I want to install the MCP Code Mode server for TypeScript tool orchestration.\n17: \n18: Please help me:\n19: 1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n20: 2. Verify I have npx available for running MCP servers\n21: 3. Create the required configuration files (.utcp_config.json and .env)\n22: 4. Configure Code Mode for my AI environment (I'm using: [Claude Code / OpenCode / VS Code Copilot])\n23: 5. Add my first MCP server (e.g., MyService, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub)\n24: 6. Verify the installation is working with a test search\n25: 7. Test a basic tool call using the correct naming pattern\n26: \n27: My preferred MCP servers are: [MyService / ClickUp / Figma / GitHub / Chrome DevTools / other]\n28: \n29: Guide me through each step with the exact commands and configuration needed.\n30: ```\n31: \n32: **What the AI will do:**\n33: - Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system\n34: - Create `.utcp_config.json` configuration file\n35: - Create `.env` file for API keys and secrets (with proper security)\n36: - Configure Code Mode for your specific AI platform\n37: - Add MCP server definitions for your preferred tools\n38: - Test the four available tools: `call_tool_chain`, `search_tools`, `list_tools`, `tool_info`\n39: - Show you the critical naming convention: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}`\n40: - Demonstrate progressive tool discovery\n41: \n42: **Expected setup time:** 10-15 minutes\n43: \n44: ---\n45: \n46: ## 1. OVERVIEW\n47: \n48: Code Mode MCP is a TypeScript execution environment that provides unified access to 159 MCP tools across 6 manuals through progressive disclosure. Instead of exposing all tools to your AI context (causing token exhaustion), Code Mode provides a single execution environment where tools are accessed programmatically and loaded on-demand.\n49: \n50: ### Core Principle\n51: \n52: > **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint. Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.\n53: \n54: ### Source Repository\n55: \n56: | Property             | Value                                                                                                     |\n57: | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n58: | **GitHub**           | [universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode](https://github.com/universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode) |\n59: | **npm (MCP Server)** | `@utcp/code-mode-mcp`                                                                                     |\n60: | **npm (Library)**    | `@utcp/code-mode`                                                                                         |\n61: | **Stars**            | 1.2k+                                                                                                     |\n62: | **License**          | MPL-2.0                                                                                                   |\n63: \n64: > **Note**: This is the official UTCP (Universal Tool Calling Protocol) implementation, not to be confused with other \"code mode\" projects like `replicate/replicate-mcp-code-mode` (Replicate-specific) or `jx-codes/codemode-mcp` (abandoned).\n65: \n66: ### Current Configuration\n67: \n68: | Manual                | Tools   | Package                                 |\n69: | --------------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------- |\n70: | **github**            | 26      | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`   |\n71: | **figma**             | 18      | `figma-developer-mcp`                   |\n72: | **chrome_devtools_1** | 26      | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`            |\n73: | **chrome_devtools_2** | 26      | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` (parallel) |\n74: | **clickup**           | 21      | `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server`        |\n75: | **myservice**           | 42      | `mcp-remote` (SSE)                      |\n76: | **Total**             | **159** | **6 manuals**                           |\n77: \n78: ### The \"2-3 MCP Server Wall\" Problem\n79: \n80: Traditional tool exposure breaks at scale. Each additional server costs context tokens:\n81: \n82: **Traditional Approach:**\n83: ```\n84: Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200\n85: Tokens:    30k     60k     90k    141k    300k    600k\n86: Usable?    yes     yes     no      no      no      no\n87: ```\n88: \n89: **Code Mode Solution:**\n90: ```\n91: Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200+\n92: Tokens:    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k\n93: Usable?    yes     yes     yes     yes     yes     yes\n94: ```\n95: \n96: Code Mode keeps your context flat at ~1.6k tokens regardless of how many servers you add.\n97: \n98: ### Architecture Overview\n99: \n100: ```\n101: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n102: │  AI Agent (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code Copilot)          │\n103: │                                                             │\n104: │  Sees: Only 4 tools in context (~1.6k tokens)               │\n105: │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │\n106: │  │ • call_tool_chain   (Execute TypeScript)              │  │\n107: │  │ • search_tools      (Progressive discovery)           │  │\n108: │  │ • list_tools        (List all available)              │  │\n109: │  │ • tool_info         (Get tool interface)              │  │\n110: │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │\n111: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘\n112:                            │ TypeScript Code\n113:                            ▼\n114: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n115: │  Code Mode MCP Server (V8 Isolate Sandbox)                  │\n116: │  • Executes TypeScript with tool access                     │\n117: │  • Routes calls to appropriate MCP servers                  │\n118: │  • Returns results + logs                                   │\n119: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘\n120:                            │ Tool Calls\n121:                            ▼\n122: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n123: │  MCP Servers: MyService, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub, Chrome, etc. │\n124: │  (159 tools accessible via Code Mode)                       │\n125: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n126: ```\n127: \n128: ### How It Compares\n129: \n130: | Feature          | Traditional MCP             | Code Mode MCP           |\n131: | ---------------- | --------------------------- | ----------------------- |\n132: | **Context Cost** | ~3k tokens per tool         | ~1.6k total (all tools) |\n133: | **Max Tools**    | 2-3 servers (context limit) | Unlimited servers       |\n134: | **Discovery**    | All tools upfront           | Progressive (on-demand) |\n135: | **Multi-Tool**   | Multiple API calls          | Single execution        |\n136: | **State**        | Manual context management   | Automatic persistence   |\n137: | **Execution**    | ~500ms per tool             | ~300ms for 4 tools      |\n138: \n139: ---\n140: \n141: ## 2. PREREQUISITES\n142: \n143: Before installing Code Mode MCP, ensure you have the following.\n144: \n145: ### Required\n146: \n147: - **Node.js 18 or higher**\n148:   ```bash\n149:   node --version\n150:   # Should show v18.x or higher\n151:   ```\n152: \n153: - **npm/npx** (comes with Node.js)\n154:   ```bash\n155:   npm --version\n156:   npx --version\n157:   ```\n158: \n159: - **MCP-Compatible Client** (one of the following):\n160:   - Claude Code CLI\n161:   - OpenCode CLI\n162:   - VS Code with GitHub Copilot\n163:   - Windsurf\n164: \n165: ### Optional but Recommended\n166: \n167: - **API Keys** for the MCP servers you want to use:\n168:   - ClickUp: API key + Team ID (Settings > Apps)\n169:   - Figma: Personal Access Token (Settings > Access Tokens)\n170:   - GitHub: Personal Access Token (Settings > Developer settings)\n171:   - MyService: OAuth (configured in MyService dashboard)\n172: \n173: - **Git** for version control\n174: \n175: ### Validation: `phase_1_complete`\n176: \n177: **Checklist:**\n178: - [ ] Node.js 18+ installed\n179: - [ ] .env file exists with API keys\n180: - [ ] .utcp_config.json exists\n181: \n182: **Quick Verification:**\n183: ```bash\n184: node --version && [ -f .env ] && [ -f .utcp_config.json ] && echo \"PASS\" || echo \"FAIL\"\n185: ```\n186: \n187: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n188: \n189: ---\n190: \n191: ## 3. INSTALLATION\n192: \n193: ### Step 1: Choose Installation Location\n194: \n195: Decide where to place your Code Mode configuration:\n196: \n197: ```bash\n198: # Option A: Project-specific (recommended for isolated projects)\n199: cd /path/to/your/project\n200: \n201: # Option B: Global location (for shared configuration)\n202: cd ~/CloudStorage/MCP\\ Servers\n203: ```\n204: \n205: ### Step 2: Create Configuration Directory Structure\n206: \n207: ```bash\n208: # Create directory structure (optional, for local config files)\n209: mkdir -p .opencode/mcp-code-mode\n210: ```\n211: \n212: ### Step 3: Create .utcp_config.json\n213: \n214: Create the main configuration file in your project root:\n215: \n216: ```bash\n217: cat > .utcp_config.json << 'EOF'\n218: {\n219:   \"load_variables_from\": [\n220:     {\n221:       \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n222:       \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n223:     }\n224:   ],\n225:   \"tool_repository\": {\n226:     \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\"\n227:   },\n228:   \"tool_search_strategy\": {\n229:     \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\"\n230:   },\n231:   \"manual_call_templates\": []\n232: }\n233: EOF\n234: ```\n235: \n236: ### Step 4: Create .env File\n237: \n238: Create an environment file for secrets:\n239: \n240: ```bash\n241: cat > .env << 'EOF'\n242: # =============================================================================\n243: # API Keys - Standard Variables\n244: # =============================================================================\n245: \n246: # ClickUp Configuration\n247: # CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here\n248: # CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here\n249: \n250: # Figma Configuration\n251: # FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here\n252: \n253: # GitHub Configuration\n254: # GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here\n255: \n256: # Voyage AI (for Narsil neural search)\n257: # VOYAGE_API_KEY=pa-your_voyage_key_here\n258: \n259: # =============================================================================\n260: # Code Mode Prefixed Variables (REQUIRED for Code Mode)\n261: # =============================================================================\n262: # Code Mode requires variables prefixed with the manual name.\n263: # Copy your API keys to these prefixed versions.\n264: \n265: # clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here\n266: # clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here\n267: # figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here\n268: # github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here\n269: EOF\n270: ```\n271: \n272: **Add `.env` to `.gitignore`:**\n273: \n274: ```bash\n275: echo \".env\" >> .gitignore\n276: ```\n277: \n278: ### Step 5: Verify Files Created\n279: \n280: ```bash\n281: ls -la .utcp_config.json .env\n282: \n283: # Expected output:\n284: # -rw-r--r--  .utcp_config.json\n285: # -rw-r--r--  .env\n286: ```\n287: \n288: ### Validation: `phase_2_complete`\n289: \n290: **Checklist:**\n291: - [ ] code_mode entry in opencode.json\n292: - [ ] UTCP_CONFIG_FILE env var set\n293: - [ ] code-mode-mcp accessible\n294: \n295: **Quick Verification:**\n296: ```bash\n297: grep -q '\"code_mode\"' opencode.json && echo \"PASS\" || echo \"FAIL\"\n298: ```\n299: \n300: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n301: \n302: ---\n303: \n304: ## 4. CONFIGURATION\n305: \n306: ### Option A: Configure for Claude Code CLI\n307: \n308: Add to `.mcp.json` in your project root:\n309: \n310: ```json\n311: {\n312:   \"mcpServers\": {\n313:     \"code-mode\": {\n314:       \"command\": \"npx\",\n315:       \"args\": [\"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"],\n316:       \"env\": {\n317:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/your/.utcp_config.json\"\n318:       }\n319:     }\n320:   }\n321: }\n322: ```\n323: \n324: ### Option B: Configure for OpenCode\n325: \n326: Add to `opencode.json` in your project root.\n327: \n328: **Option B1: NPM Package (Recommended for most users)**\n329: ```json\n330: {\n331:   \"mcp\": {\n332:     \"code_mode\": {\n333:       \"type\": \"local\",\n334:       \"command\": [\n335:         \"npx\",\n336:         \"-y\",\n337:         \"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"\n338:       ],\n339:       \"environment\": {\n340:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \".utcp_config.json\"\n341:       }\n342:     }\n343:   }\n344: }\n345: ```\n346: \n347: **Option B2: Embedded Source (For bundled projects)**\n348: \n349: Use this if the Code Mode source is embedded in your project at `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/`:\n350: \n351: ```json\n352: {\n353:   \"mcp\": {\n354:     \"code_mode\": {\n355:       \"type\": \"local\",\n356:       \"command\": [\n357:         \"node\",\n358:         \".opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js\"\n359:       ],\n360:       \"environment\": {\n361:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \".utcp_config.json\"\n362:       }\n363:     }\n364:   }\n365: }\n366: ```\n367: \n368: > **Note:** The embedded approach requires running `npm install` in the `mcp_server/` directory first. This is useful for projects that want to bundle the MCP server source for version control and portability.\n369: \n370: ### Option C: Configure for VS Code Copilot\n371: \n372: Add to `.vscode/mcp.json`:\n373: \n374: ```json\n375: {\n376:   \"mcpServers\": {\n377:     \"code-mode\": {\n378:       \"command\": \"npx\",\n379:       \"args\": [\"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"],\n380:       \"env\": {\n381:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/your/.utcp_config.json\"\n382:       }\n383:     }\n384:   }\n385: }\n386: ```\n387: \n388: ### Package Name Note\n389: \n390: The package `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` is the official UTCP Code Mode MCP server. Some older documentation may reference `utcp-mcp`. Both should work, but `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` is the current recommended package.\n391: \n392: ---\n393: \n394: ### SECURITY WARNING\n395: \n396: Never hardcode API keys directly in `.utcp_config.json`. Always use environment variable references.\n397: \n398: **Wrong (Security Risk):**\n399: ```json\n400: {\n401:   \"env\": {\n402:     \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"pk_224591351_ACTUAL_KEY_HERE\"\n403:   }\n404: }\n405: ```\n406: \n407: **Correct (Secure):**\n408: ```json\n409: {\n410:   \"env\": {\n411:     \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\"\n412:   }\n413: }\n414: ```\n415: \n416: Define the actual value in `.env` with the manual name prefix:\n417: ```bash\n418: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}\n419: # If your manual name is \"clickup\", use:\n420: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_224591351_your_actual_key\n421: ```\n422: \n423: See the \"CRITICAL: Prefixed Environment Variables\" section below for full details.\n424: \n425: **Security Checklist:**\n426: ```\n427: [ ] .env file is in .gitignore\n428: [ ] No hardcoded API keys in .utcp_config.json\n429: [ ] All secrets use ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax\n430: [ ] .env file permissions are restricted (chmod 600 .env)\n431: ```\n432: \n433: ---\n434: \n435: ### CRITICAL: Prefixed Environment Variables\n436: \n437: Code Mode requires PREFIXED environment variables. This is the number one cause of \"Variable not found\" errors.\n438: \n439: When Code Mode loads a manual configuration, it looks for environment variables with the manual name as a prefix. For example:\n440: \n441: | Manual Name | Config Variable | Required .env Variable |\n442: |-------------|-----------------|------------------------|\n443: | `figma` | `${FIGMA_API_KEY}` | `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` |\n444: | `github` | `${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}` | `github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` |\n445: | `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` |\n446: | `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` |\n447: \n448: **Error you'll see without prefixed variables:**\n449: ```\n450: Error during batch registration for manual 'figma': Variable 'figma_FIGMA_API_KEY'\n451: referenced in call template configuration not found.\n452: ```\n453: \n454: **Solution:** Add both unprefixed and prefixed versions to your `.env`:\n455: \n456: ```bash\n457: # Standard variables (for other tools)\n458: FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\n459: GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\n460: CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your-clickup-key\n461: CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your-team-id\n462: \n463: # Code Mode prefixed versions (REQUIRED for Code Mode)\n464: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\n465: github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\n466: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your-clickup-key\n467: clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your-team-id\n468: ```\n469: \n470: **Why both?** The unprefixed versions are used by other tools and scripts. The prefixed versions are specifically for Code Mode's variable substitution system.\n471: \n472: ---\n473: \n474: ### Adding MCP Servers\n475: \n476: Add servers to `.utcp_config.json` in the `manual_call_templates` array:\n477: \n478: #### GitHub\n479: \n480: ```json\n481: {\n482:   \"name\": \"github\",\n483:   \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n484:   \"config\": {\n485:     \"mcpServers\": {\n486:       \"github\": {\n487:         \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n488:         \"command\": \"npx\",\n489:         \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@modelcontextprotocol/server-github\"],\n490:
