**ClickUp**         | `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server`         | 21    | API Key + Team ID     |\n| **Figma**           | `figma-developer-mcp`                    | 18    | Personal Access Token |\n| **Chrome DevTools** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`             | 26    | None                  |\n\n### Configuration Paths\n\n| Client              | Configuration File |\n| ------------------- | ------------------ |\n| **Claude Code**     | `.mcp.json`        |\n| **OpenCode**        | `opencode.json`    |\n| **VS Code Copilot** | `.vscode/mcp.json` |\n| **Windsurf**        | `.mcp.toml`        |\n\n### Helper Commands\n\n```bash\n# Validate JSON configuration\npython3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n\n# Check environment variables\ncat .env | grep -v \"^#\" | grep -v \"^$\"\n\n# Test npx command manually\nnpx -y @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\n\n# List manual names from config\ncat .utcp_config.json | grep '\"name\"'\n```\n\n### Performance Metrics\n\n| Metric              | Traditional MCP   | Code Mode        | Improvement     |\n| ------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | --------------- |\n| **Context tokens**  | 141k (47 tools)   | 1.6k (159 tools) | 98.7% reduction |\n| **Execution time**  | ~2000ms (4 tools) | ~300ms (4 tools) | 85% faster      |\n| **API round trips** | 15+               | 1                | 93% reduction   |\n\n### Project Structure\n\n```\nyour-project/\n├── .utcp_config.json     # MCP server definitions\n├── .env                   # API keys (gitignored)\n├── .env.example           # Template for team\n├── .mcp.json              # Claude Code config (optional)\n├── opencode.json          # OpenCode config (optional)\n└── .vscode/\n    └── mcp.json           # VS Code config (optional)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference Card\n\n### Essential Commands\n\n```bash\n# Create configuration\ncat > .utcp_config.json << 'EOF'\n{\n  \"load_variables_from\": [\n    { \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\", \"env_file_path\": \".env\" }\n  ],\n  \"tool_repository\": { \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\" },\n  \"tool_search_strategy\": { \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\" },\n  \"manual_call_templates\": []\n}\nEOF\n\n# Validate JSON\npython3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n\n# Check environment variables\nenv | grep -E \"(CLICKUP|FIGMA|GITHUB)\"\n```\n\n### NAMING PATTERN\n\n```typescript\n// Pattern: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n//          ───┬───  ─────┬─────  ────┬────\n//             │          │           └── Tool from MCP server\n//             │          └── Server name + underscore + tool\n//             └── Manual name (from \"name\" field)\n\n// Examples:\nmyservice.myservice_sites_list({});\ngithub.github_get_issue({ owner: \"foo\", repo: \"bar\", issue_number: 123 });\nclickup.clickup_create_task({ name: \"My Task\", listName: \"Sprint\" });\nfigma.figma_get_file({ fileId: \"abc123\" });\nchrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_new_page({});\n```\n\n### Common Workflows\n\n**Tool Discovery:**\n```typescript\nconst tools = await search_tools({ task_description: \"myservice cms\", limit: 10 });\nconsole.log(tools.map(t => t.name));\n```\n\n**Single Tool Call:**\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})`\n});\n```\n\n**Multi-Tool with Error Handling:**\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    try {\n      const a = await github.github_get_repo({ owner: \"x\", repo: \"y\" });\n      const b = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: a.name });\n      return { success: true, a, b };\n    } catch (error) {\n      return { success: false, error: error.message };\n    }\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000\n});\n```\n\n### Validation Checklists\n\n**Installation:**\n```\n[ ] Node.js 18+ installed\n[ ] .utcp_config.json created and valid\n[ ] .env created with required keys\n[ ] .env in .gitignore\n[ ] AI client configured\n[ ] Code Mode tools visible\n```\n\n**Before Each Session:**\n```\n[ ] Correct naming pattern: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n[ ] Timeout calculated for complexity\n[ ] Error handling in place\n[ ] API keys not expired\n```\n\n---\n\n## Version History\n\n| Version | Date       | Changes                                                                 |\n| ------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| v2.0.0  | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, standardized phase_N_complete validation naming, TOC moved after AI-First Install Guide, Quick Reference Card added to Resources |\n| v1.0.0  | 2025-01-01 | Initial release .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/109-skill-readme-standardization/014-mcp-code-mode-readme/context/seats/iter-001/mimo.jsonl:32:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780836968057,"sessionID":"ses_15dd90497ffesWatPtiSJXIuCW","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_1f1cdd6d64f3436d8c354e28","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env bash\n2: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n3: # COMPONENT: MCP CODE MODE DOCTOR\n4: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n5: # Read-only health check for the mcp-code-mode MCP server.\n6: # Catches missing dependencies that would prevent the server\n7: # starting at first MCP request.\n8: #\n9: # Usage: bash .opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh [--strict]\n10: #\n11: # Exit Codes:\n12: #   0  - Health checks passed (or advisory mode complete)\n13: #   1  - Invalid arguments\n14: #   20 - mcp_server dist missing\n15: #   26 - Runtime Node imports missing (utcp/code-mode-mcp, MCP SDK)\n16: \n17: set -euo pipefail\n18: \n19: SCRIPT_DIR=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")\" && pwd)\"\n20: SKILL_DIR=\"$(dirname \"$SCRIPT_DIR\")\"\n21: MCP_DIR=\"$SKILL_DIR/mcp_server\"\n22: \n23: STRICT_MODE=false\n24: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n25:     case \"$1\" in\n26:         --strict) STRICT_MODE=true; shift ;;\n27:         -h|--help) echo \"Usage: $0 [--strict]\"; exit 0 ;;\n28:         *) echo \"Unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 1 ;;\n29:     esac\n30: done\n31: \n32: log_pass() { printf '  PASS: %s\\n' \"$1\"; }\n33: log_warn() { printf '  WARN: %s\\n' \"$1\"; }\n34: \n35: echo \"=== mcp-code-mode Doctor ===\"\n36: echo \"MCP server: $MCP_DIR\"\n37: echo \"\"\n38: \n39: if [[ ! -d \"$MCP_DIR/node_modules\" ]]; then\n40:     log_warn \"MCP server node_modules missing — run \\`npm install\\` in $MCP_DIR.\"\n41:     exit 20\n42: fi\n43: log_pass \"MCP server node_modules present\"\n44: \n45: NODE_BIN=\"$(command -v node || true)\"\n46: if [[ -z \"$NODE_BIN\" ]]; then\n47:     log_warn \"node not on PATH\"\n48:     [[ \"$STRICT_MODE\" == true ]] && exit 26\n49:     exit 0\n50: fi\n51: log_pass \"Node interpreter: $NODE_BIN\"\n52: \n53: # Critical runtime imports for code-mode MCP. The skill IS the @utcp/code-mode-mcp\n54: # package itself (mcp_server/package.json `name`); test the SDK + the @utcp helper\n55: # packages it bundles, plus verify dist/index.js loads without ImportError.\n56: DEP_CHECK_MODULES=\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js @utcp/code-mode @utcp/cli\"\n57: DEP_CHECK_MISSING=()\n58: for mod in $DEP_CHECK_MODULES; do\n59:     ( cd \"$MCP_DIR\" && \"$NODE_BIN\" -e \"require('$mod')\" 2>/dev/null ) || DEP_CHECK_MISSING+=(\"$mod\")\n60: done\n61: \n62: # Also smoke-test the dist entry point if present.\n63: ENTRY_BROKEN=false\n64: if [[ -f \"$MCP_DIR/dist/index.js\" ]]; then\n65:     if ! ( cd \"$MCP_DIR\" && \"$NODE_BIN\" --check dist/index.js 2>/dev/null ); then\n66:         ENTRY_BROKEN=true\n67:         DEP_CHECK_MISSING+=(\"dist/index.js (syntax/load error)\")\n68:     fi\n69: fi\n70: \n71: if [[ ${#DEP_CHECK_MISSING[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then\n72:     log_pass \"Runtime imports OK (MCP SDK + @utcp/code-mode + @utcp/cli + dist/index.js)\"\n73: else\n74:     log_warn \"Runtime imports FAILED — missing or broken: ${DEP_CHECK_MISSING[*]}\"\n75:     echo \"  Fix via: ( cd $MCP_DIR && npm install )\"\n76:     if [[ \"$ENTRY_BROKEN\" == true ]]; then\n77:         echo \"  dist/index.js failed --check — re-run \\`npm run build\\` in $MCP_DIR.\"\n78:     fi\n79:     if [[ \"$STRICT_MODE\" == true ]]; then\n80:         exit 26\n81:     fi\n82: fi\n83: \n84: echo \"\"\n85: echo \"Doctor done.\"\n\n(End of file - total 85 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# COMPONENT: MCP CODE MODE DOCTOR\n# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Read-only health check for the mcp-code-mode MCP server.\n# Catches missing dependencies that would prevent the server\n# starting at first MCP request.\n#\n# Usage: bash .opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh [--strict]\n#\n# Exit Codes:\n#   0  - Health checks passed (or advisory mode complete)\n#   1  - Invalid arguments\n#   20 - mcp_server dist missing\n#   26 - Runtime Node imports missing (utcp/code-mode-mcp, MCP SDK)\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nSCRIPT_DIR=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")\" && pwd)\"\nSKILL_DIR=\"$(dirname \"$SCRIPT_DIR\")\"","truncated":false,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh","text":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# COMPONENT: MCP CODE MODE DOCTOR\n# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Read-only health check for the mcp-code-mode MCP server.\n# Catches missing dependencies that would prevent the server\n# starting at first MCP request.\n#\n# Usage: bash .opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh [--strict]\n#\n# Exit Codes:\n#   0  - Health checks passed (or advisory mode complete)\n#   1  - Invalid arguments\n#   20 - mcp_server dist missing\n#   26 - Runtime Node imports missing (utcp/code-mode-mcp, MCP SDK)\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nSCRIPT_DIR=\"$(cd \"$(dirname \"${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")\" && pwd)\"\nSKILL_DIR=\"$(dirname \"$SCRIPT_DIR\")\"\nMCP_DIR=\"$SKILL_DIR/mcp_server\"\n\nSTRICT_MODE=false\nwhile [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case \"$1\" in\n        --strict) STRICT_MODE=true; shift ;;\n        -h|--help) echo \"Usage: $0 [--strict]\"; exit 0 ;;\n        *) echo \"Unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 1 ;;\n    esac\ndone\n\nlog_pass() { printf '  PASS: %s\\n' \"$1\"; }\nlog_warn() { printf '  WARN: %s\\n' \"$1\"; }\n\necho \"=== mcp-code-mode Doctor ===\"\necho \"MCP server: $MCP_DIR\"\necho \"\"\n\nif [[ ! -d \"$MCP_DIR/node_modules\" ]]; then\n    log_warn \"MCP server node_modules missing — run \\`npm install\\` in $MCP_DIR.\"\n    exit 20\nfi\nlog_pass \"MCP server node_modules present\"\n\nNODE_BIN=\"$(command -v node || true)\"\nif [[ -z \"$NODE_BIN\" ]]; then\n    log_warn \"node not on PATH\"\n    [[ \"$STRICT_MODE\" == true ]] && exit 26\n    exit 0\nfi\nlog_pass \"Node interpreter: $NODE_BIN\"\n\n# Critical runtime imports for code-mode MCP. The skill IS the @utcp/code-mode-mcp\n# package itself (mcp_server/package.json `name`); test the SDK + the @utcp helper\n# packages it bundles, plus verify dist/index.js loads without ImportError.\nDEP_CHECK_MODULES=\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js @utcp/code-mode @utcp/cli\"\nDEP_CHECK_MISSING=()\nfor mod in $DEP_CHECK_MODULES; do\n    ( cd \"$MCP_DIR\" && \"$NODE_BIN\" -e \"require('$mod')\" 2>/dev/null ) || DEP_CHECK_MISSING+=(\"$mod\")\ndone\n\n# Also smoke-test the dist entry point if present.\nENTRY_BROKEN=false\nif [[ -f \"$MCP_DIR/dist/index.js\" ]]; then\n    if ! ( cd \"$MCP_DIR\" && \"$NODE_BIN\" --check dist/index.js 2>/dev/null ); then\n        ENTRY_BROKEN=true\n        DEP_CHECK_MISSING+=(\"dist/index.js (syntax/load error)\")\n    fi\nfi\n\nif [[ ${#DEP_CHECK_MISSING[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then\n    log_pass \"Runtime imports OK (MCP SDK + @utcp/code-mode + @utcp/cli + dist/index.js)\"\nelse\n    log_warn \"Runtime imports FAILED — missing or broken: ${DEP_CHECK_MISSING[*]}\"\n    echo \"  Fix via: ( cd $MCP_DIR && npm install )\"\n    if [[ \"$ENTRY_BROKEN\" == true ]]; then\n        echo \"  dist/index.js failed --check — re-run \\`npm run build\\` in $MCP_DIR.\"\n    fi\n    if [[ \"$STRICT_MODE\" == true ]]; then\n        exit 26\n    fi\nfi\n\necho \"\"\necho \"Doctor done.\"","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":85,"totalLines":85,"truncated":false}},"title":".opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/scripts/doctor.sh","time":{"start":1780836968048,"end":1780836968056}},"id":"prt_ea227a666001wTaqlOvLTNJoRv","sessionID":"ses_15dd90497ffesWatPtiSJXIuCW","messageID":"msg_ea22793f00016aY2epyquddGbZ"}}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/109-skill-readme-standardization/014-mcp-code-mode-readme/context/seats/iter-001/deepseek.jsonl:5:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780836934643,"sessionID":"ses_15dd90497ffeHiobTGh0JRvEW2","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_03_9nlPasT524NqDrhf98334693","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # MCP Code Mode Installation Guide\n2: \n3: Complete installation and configuration guide for the Code Mode MCP server. This enables TypeScript-based orchestration of external MCP tools, giving you unified access to MyService, Figma, ClickUp, GitHub, Chrome DevTools and other MCP servers through a single `call_tool_chain()` interface. It delivers 98.7% context reduction and 60% faster execution compared to individual tool calls, with type-safe invocation and automatic tool discovery.\n4: \n5: > **Version:** 2.0.0\n6: > **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n7: > **Package**: `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` | **Dependencies**: Node.js 18+, .utcp_config.json\n8: \n9: ---\n10: \n11: ## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n12: \n13: **Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:**\n14: \n15: ```\n16: I want to install the MCP Code Mode server for TypeScript tool orchestration.\n17: \n18: Please help me:\n19: 1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n20: 2. Verify I have npx available for running MCP servers\n21: 3. Create the required configuration files (.utcp_config.json and .env)\n22: 4. Configure Code Mode for my AI environment (I'm using: [Claude Code / OpenCode / VS Code Copilot])\n23: 5. Add my first MCP server (e.g., MyService, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub)\n24: 6. Verify the installation is working with a test search\n25: 7. Test a basic tool call using the correct naming pattern\n26: \n27: My preferred MCP servers are: [MyService / ClickUp / Figma / GitHub / Chrome DevTools / other]\n28: \n29: Guide me through each step with the exact commands and configuration needed.\n30: ```\n31: \n32: **What the AI will do:**\n33: - Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system\n34: - Create `.utcp_config.json` configuration file\n35: - Create `.env` file for API keys and secrets (with proper security)\n36: - Configure Code Mode for your specific AI platform\n37: - Add MCP server definitions for your preferred tools\n38: - Test the four available tools: `call_tool_chain`, `search_tools`, `list_tools`, `tool_info`\n39: - Show you the critical naming convention: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}`\n40: - Demonstrate progressive tool discovery\n41: \n42: **Expected setup time:** 10-15 minutes\n43: \n44: ---\n45: \n46: ## 1. OVERVIEW\n47: \n48: Code Mode MCP is a TypeScript execution environment that provides unified access to 159 MCP tools across 6 manuals through progressive disclosure. Instead of exposing all tools to your AI context (causing token exhaustion), Code Mode provides a single execution environment where tools are accessed programmatically and loaded on-demand.\n49: \n50: ### Core Principle\n51: \n52: > **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint. Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.\n53: \n54: ### Source Repository\n55: \n56: | Property             | Value                                                                                                     |\n57: | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n58: | **GitHub**           | [universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode](https://github.com/universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode) |\n59: | **npm (MCP Server)** | `@utcp/code-mode-mcp`                                                                                     |\n60: | **npm (Library)**    | `@utcp/code-mode`                                                                                         |\n61: | **Stars**            | 1.2k+                                                                                                     |\n62: | **License**          | MPL-2.0                                                                                                   |\n63: \n64: > **Note**: This is the official UTCP (Universal Tool Calling Protocol) implementation, not to be confused with other \"code mode\" projects like `replicate/replicate-mcp-code-mode` (Replicate-specific) or `jx-codes/codemode-mcp` (abandoned).\n65: \n66: ### Current Configuration\n67: \n68: | Manual                | Tools   | Package                                 |\n69: | --------------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------- |\n70: | **github**            | 26      | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`   |\n71: | **figma**             | 18      | `figma-developer-mcp`                   |\n72: | **chrome_devtools_1** | 26      | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`            |\n73: | **chrome_devtools_2** | 26      | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` (parallel) |\n74: | **clickup**           | 21      | `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server`        |\n75: | **myservice**           | 42      | `mcp-remote` (SSE)                      |\n76: | **Total**             | **159** | **6 manuals**                           |\n77: \n78: ### The \"2-3 MCP Server Wall\" Problem\n79: \n80: Traditional tool exposure breaks at scale. Each additional server costs context tokens:\n81: \n82: **Traditional Approach:**\n83: ```\n84: Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200\n85: Tokens:    30k     60k     90k    141k    300k    600k\n86: Usable?    yes     yes     no      no      no      no\n87: ```\n88: \n89: **Code Mode Solution:**\n90: ```\n91: Tools:     10      20      30      47      100     200+\n92: Tokens:    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k\n93: Usable?    yes     yes     yes     yes     yes     yes\n94: ```\n95: \n96: Code Mode keeps your context flat at ~1.6k tokens regardless of how many servers you add.\n97: \n98: ### Architecture Overview\n99: \n100: ```\n101: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n102: │  AI Agent (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code Copilot)          │\n103: │                                                             │\n104: │  Sees: Only 4 tools in context (~1.6k tokens)               │\n105: │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │\n106: │  │ • call_tool_chain   (Execute TypeScript)              │  │\n107: │  │ • search_tools      (Progressive discovery)           │  │\n108: │  │ • list_tools        (List all available)              │  │\n109: │  │ • tool_info         (Get tool interface)              │  │\n110: │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │\n111: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘\n112:                            │ TypeScript Code\n113:                            ▼\n114: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n115: │  Code Mode MCP Server (V8 Isolate Sandbox)                  │\n116: │  • Executes TypeScript with tool access                     │\n117: │  • Routes calls to appropriate MCP servers                  │\n118: │  • Returns results + logs                                   │\n119: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘\n120:                            │ Tool Calls\n121:                            ▼\n122: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n123: │  MCP Servers: MyService, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub, Chrome, etc. │\n124: │  (159 tools accessible via Code Mode)                       │\n125: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n126: ```\n127: \n128: ### How It Compares\n129: \n130: | Feature          | Traditional MCP             | Code Mode MCP           |\n131: | ---------------- | --------------------------- | ----------------------- |\n132: | **Context Cost** | ~3k tokens per tool         | ~1.6k total (all tools) |\n133: | **Max Tools**    | 2-3 servers (context limit) | Unlimited servers       |\n134: | **Discovery**    | All tools upfront           | Progressive (on-demand) |\n135: | **Multi-Tool**   | Multiple API calls          | Single execution        |\n136: | **State**        | Manual context management   | Automatic persistence   |\n137: | **Execution**    | ~500ms per tool             | ~300ms for 4 tools      |\n138: \n139: ---\n140: \n141: ## 2. PREREQUISITES\n142: \n143: Before installing Code Mode MCP, ensure you have the following.\n144: \n145: ### Required\n146: \n147: - **Node.js 18 or higher**\n148:   ```bash\n149:   node --version\n150:   # Should show v18.x or higher\n151:   ```\n152: \n153: - **npm/npx** (comes with Node.js)\n154:   ```bash\n155:   npm --version\n156:   npx --version\n157:   ```\n158: \n159: - **MCP-Compatible Client** (one of the following):\n160:   - Claude Code CLI\n161:   - OpenCode CLI\n162:   - VS Code with GitHub Copilot\n163:   - Windsurf\n164: \n165: ### Optional but Recommended\n166: \n167: - **API Keys** for the MCP servers you want to use:\n168:   - ClickUp: API key + Team ID (Settings > Apps)\n169:   - Figma: Personal Access Token (Settings > Access Tokens)\n170:   - GitHub: Personal Access Token (Settings > Developer settings)\n171:   - MyService: OAuth (configured in MyService dashboard)\n172: \n173: - **Git** for version control\n174: \n175: ### Validation: `phase_1_complete`\n176: \n177: **Checklist:**\n178: - [ ] Node.js 18+ installed\n179: - [ ] .env file exists with API keys\n180: - [ ] .utcp_config.json exists\n181: \n182: **Quick Verification:**\n183: ```bash\n184: node --version && [ -f .env ] && [ -f .utcp_config.json ] && echo \"PASS\" || echo \"FAIL\"\n185: ```\n186: \n187: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n188: \n189: ---\n190: \n191: ## 3. INSTALLATION\n192: \n193: ### Step 1: Choose Installation Location\n194: \n195: Decide where to place your Code Mode configuration:\n196: \n197: ```bash\n198: # Option A: Project-specific (recommended for isolated projects)\n199: cd /path/to/your/project\n200: \n201: # Option B: Global location (for shared configuration)\n202: cd ~/CloudStorage/MCP\\ Servers\n203: ```\n204: \n205: ### Step 2: Create Configuration Directory Structure\n206: \n207: ```bash\n208: # Create directory structure (optional, for local config files)\n209: mkdir -p .opencode/mcp-code-mode\n210: ```\n211: \n212: ### Step 3: Create .utcp_config.json\n213: \n214: Create the main configuration file in your project root:\n215: \n216: ```bash\n217: cat > .utcp_config.json << 'EOF'\n218: {\n219:   \"load_variables_from\": [\n220:     {\n221:       \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n222:       \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n223:     }\n224:   ],\n225:   \"tool_repository\": {\n226:     \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\"\n227:   },\n228:   \"tool_search_strategy\": {\n229:     \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\"\n230:   },\n231:   \"manual_call_templates\": []\n232: }\n233: EOF\n234: ```\n235: \n236: ### Step 4: Create .env File\n237: \n238: Create an environment file for secrets:\n239: \n240: ```bash\n241: cat > .env << 'EOF'\n242: # =============================================================================\n243: # API Keys - Standard Variables\n244: # =============================================================================\n245: \n246: # ClickUp Configuration\n247: # CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here\n248: # CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here\n249: \n250: # Figma Configuration\n251: # FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here\n252: \n253: # GitHub Configuration\n254: # GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here\n255: \n256: # Voyage AI (for Narsil neural search)\n257: # VOYAGE_API_KEY=pa-your_voyage_key_here\n258: \n259: # =============================================================================\n260: # Code Mode Prefixed Variables (REQUIRED for Code Mode)\n261: # =============================================================================\n262: # Code Mode requires variables prefixed with the manual name.\n263: # Copy your API keys to these prefixed versions.\n264: \n265: # clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here\n266: # clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here\n267: # figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here\n268: # github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here\n269: EOF\n270: ```\n271: \n272: **Add `.env` to `.gitignore`:**\n273: \n274: ```bash\n275: echo \".env\" >> .gitignore\n276: ```\n277: \n278: ### Step 5: Verify Files Created\n279: \n280: ```bash\n281: ls -la .utcp_config.json .env\n282: \n283: # Expected output:\n284: # -rw-r--r--  .utcp_config.json\n285: # -rw-r--r--  .env\n286: ```\n287: \n288: ### Validation: `phase_2_complete`\n289: \n290: **Checklist:**\n291: - [ ] code_mode entry in opencode.json\n292: - [ ] UTCP_CONFIG_FILE env var set\n293: - [ ] code-mode-mcp accessible\n294: \n295: **Quick Verification:**\n296: ```bash\n297: grep -q '\"code_mode\"' opencode.json && echo \"PASS\" || echo \"FAIL\"\n298: ```\n299: \n300: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n301: \n302: ---\n303: \n304: ## 4. CONFIGURATION\n305: \n306: ### Option A: Configure for Claude Code CLI\n307: \n308: Add to `.mcp.json` in your project root:\n309: \n310: ```json\n311: {\n312:   \"mcpServers\": {\n313:     \"code-mode\": {\n314:       \"command\": \"npx\",\n315:       \"args\": [\"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"],\n316:       \"env\": {\n317:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/your/.utcp_config.json\"\n318:       }\n319:     }\n320:   }\n321: }\n322: ```\n323: \n324: ### Option B: Configure for OpenCode\n325: \n326: Add to `opencode.json` in your project root.\n327: \n328: **Option B1: NPM Package (Recommended for most users)**\n329: ```json\n330: {\n331:   \"mcp\": {\n332:     \"code_mode\": {\n333:       \"type\": \"local\",\n334:       \"command\": [\n335:         \"npx\",\n336:         \"-y\",\n337:         \"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"\n338:       ],\n339:       \"environment\": {\n340:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \".utcp_config.json\"\n341:       }\n342:     }\n343:   }\n344: }\n345: ```\n346: \n347: **Option B2: Embedded Source (For bundled projects)**\n348: \n349: Use this if the Code Mode source is embedded in your project at `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/`:\n350: \n351: ```json\n352: {\n353:   \"mcp\": {\n354:     \"code_mode\": {\n355:       \"type\": \"local\",\n356:       \"command\": [\n357:         \"node\",\n358:         \".opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js\"\n359:       ],\n360:       \"environment\": {\n361:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \".utcp_config.json\"\n362:       }\n363:     }\n364:   }\n365: }\n366: ```\n367: \n368: > **Note:** The embedded approach requires running `npm install` in the `mcp_server/` directory first. This is useful for projects that want to bundle the MCP server source for version control and portability.\n369: \n370: ### Option C: Configure for VS Code Copilot\n371: \n372: Add to `.vscode/mcp.json`:\n373: \n374: ```json\n375: {\n376:   \"mcpServers\": {\n377:     \"code-mode\": {\n378:       \"command\": \"npx\",\n379:       \"args\": [\"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"],\n380:       \"env\": {\n381:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/your/.utcp_config.json\"\n382:       }\n383:     }\n384:   }\n385: }\n386: ```\n387: \n388: ### Package Name Note\n389: \n390: The package `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` is the official UTCP Code Mode MCP server. Some older documentation may reference `utcp-mcp`. Both should work, but `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` is the current recommended package.\n391: \n392: ---\n393: \n394: ### SECURITY WARNING\n395: \n396: Never hardcode API keys directly in `.utcp_config.json`. Always use environment variable references.\n397: \n398: **Wrong (Security Risk):**\n399: ```json\n400: {\n401:   \"env\": {\n402:     \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"pk_224591351_ACTUAL_KEY_HERE\"\n403:   }\n404: }\n405: ```\n406: \n407: **Correct (Secure):**\n408: ```json\n409: {\n410:   \"env\": {\n411:     \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\"\n412:   }\n413: }\n414: ```\n415: \n416: Define the actual value in `.env` with the manual name prefix:\n417: ```bash\n418: # Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}\n419: # If your manual name is \"clickup\", use:\n420: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_224591351_your_actual_key\n421: ```\n422: \n423: See the \"CRITICAL: Prefixed Environment Variables\" section below for full details.\n424: \n425: **Security Checklist:**\n426: ```\n427: [ ] .env file is in .gitignore\n428: [ ] No hardcoded API keys in .utcp_config.json\n429: [ ] All secrets use ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax\n430: [ ] .env file permissions are restricted (chmod 600 .env)\n431: ```\n432: \n433: ---\n434: \n435: ### CRITICAL: Prefixed Environment Variables\n436: \n437: Code Mode requires PREFIXED environment variables. This is the number one cause of \"Variable not found\" errors.\n438: \n439: When Code Mode loads a manual configuration, it looks for environment variables with the manual name as a prefix. For example:\n440: \n441: | Manual Name | Config Variable | Required .env Variable |\n442: |-------------|-----------------|------------------------|\n443: | `figma` | `${FIGMA_API_KEY}` | `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` |\n444: | `github` | `${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}` | `github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` |\n445: | `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` |\n446: | `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` |\n447: \n448: **Error you'll see without prefixed variables:**\n449: ```\n450: Error during batch registration for manual 'figma': Variable 'figma_FIGMA_API_KEY'\n451: referenced in call template configuration not found.\n452: ```\n453: \n454: **Solution:** Add both unprefixed and prefixed versions to your `.env`:\n455: \n456: ```bash\n457: # Standard variables (for other tools)\n458: FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\n459: GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\n460: CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your-clickup-key\n461: CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your-team-id\n462: \n463: # Code Mode prefixed versions (REQUIRED for Code Mode)\n464: figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\n465: github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\n466: clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your-clickup-key\n467: clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your-team-id\n468: ```\n469: \n470: **Why both?** The unprefixed versions are used by other tools and scripts. The prefixed versions are specifically for Code Mode's variable substitution system.\n471: \n472: ---\n473: \n474: ### Adding MCP Servers\n475: \n476: Add servers to `.utcp_config.json` in the `manual_call_templates` array:\n477: \n478: #### GitHub\n479: \n480: ```json\n481: {\n482:   \"name\": \"github\",\n483:   \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n484:   \"config\": {\n485:     \"mcpServers\": {\n486:       \"github\": {\n487:         \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n488:         \"command\": \"npx\",\n489:         \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@modelcontextprotocol/server-github\"],\n490:         \"env\": {\n491:           \"GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN\": \"${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}\"\n492:         }\n493:       }\n494:     }\n495:   }\n496: }\n497: ```\n498: \n499: #### MyService (Remote SSE)\n500: \n501: ```json\n502: {\n503:   \"name\": \"myservice\",\n504:   \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n505:   \"config\": {\n506:     \"mcpServers\": {\n507:       \"myservice\": {\n508:         \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n509:         \"command\": \"npx\",\n510:         \"args\": [\"mcp-remote\", \"https://mcp.myservice.com/sse\"],\n511:         \"env\": {}\n512:       }\n513:     }\n514:   }\n515: }\n516: ```\n517: \n518: #### ClickUp (Local with Auth)\n519: \n520: ```json\n521: {\n522:   \"name\": \"clickup\",\n523:   \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n524:   \"config\": {\n525:     \"mcpServers\": {\n526:       \"clickup\": {\n527:         \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n528:         \"command\": \"npx\",\n529:         \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\"],\n530:         \"env\": {\n531:           \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\",\n532:           \"CLICKUP_TEAM_ID\": \"${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}\"\n533:         }\n534:       }\n535:     }\n536:   }\n537: }\n538: ```\n539: \n540: #### Figma\n541: \n542: ```json\n543: {\n544:   \"name\": \"figma\",\n545:   \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n546:   \"config\": {\n547:     \"mcpServers\": {\n548:       \"figma\": {\n549:         \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n550:         \"command\": \"npx\",\n551:         \"args\": [\"-y\", \"figma-developer-mcp\", \"--stdio\"],\n552:         \"env\": {\n553:           \"FIGMA_API_KEY\": \"${FIGMA_API_KEY}\"\n554:         }\n555:       }\n556:     }\n557:   }\n558: }\n559: ```\n560: \n561: #### Chrome DevTools (Multiple Instances)\n562: \n563: ```json\n564: {\n565:   \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\",\n566:   \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n567:   \"config\": {\n568:     \"mcpServers\": {\n569:       \"chrome_devtools_1\": {\n570:         \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n571:         \"command\": \"npx\",\n572:         \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n573:         \"env\": {}\n574:       }\n575:     }\n576:   }\n577: }\n578: ```\n579: \n580: ### Complete .utcp_config.json Example\n581: \n582: ```json\n583: {\n584:   \"load_variables_from\": [\n585:     {\n586:       \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n587:       \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n588:     }\n589:   ],\n590:   \"tool_repository\": {\n591:     \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\"\n592:   },\n593:   \"tool_search_strategy\": {\n594:     \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\"\n595:   },\n596:   \"manual_call_templates\": [\n597:     {\n598:       \"name\": \"github\",\n599:       \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n600:       \"config\": {\n601:         \"mcpServers\": {\n602:           \"github\": {\n603:             \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n604:             \"command\": \"npx\",\n605:             \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@modelcontextprotocol/server-github\"],\n606:             \"env\": {\n607:               \"GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN\": \"${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}\"\n608:             }\n609:           }\n610:         }\n611:       }\n612:     },\n613:     {\n614:       \"name\": \"myservice\",\n615:       \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n616:       \"config\": {\n617:         \"mcpServers\": {\n618:           \"myservice\": {\n619:             \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n620:             \"command\": \"npx\",\n621:             \"args\": [\"mcp-remote\", \"https://mcp.myservice.com/sse\"],\n622:             \"env\": {}\n623:           }\n624:         }\n625:       }\n626:     },\n627:     {\n628:       \"name\": \"clickup\",\n629:       \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n630:       \"config\": {\n631:         \"mcpServers\": {\n632:           \"clickup\": {\n633:             \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n634:             \"command\": \"npx\",\n635:             \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\"],\n636:             \"env\": {\n637:               \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\",\n638:               \"CLICKUP_TEAM_ID\": \"${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}\"\n639:             }\n640:           }\n641:         }\n642:       }\n643:     },\n644:     {\n645:       \"name\": \"figma\",\n646:       \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n647:       \"config\": {\n648:         \"mcpServers\": {\n649:           \"figma\": {\n650:             \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n651:             \"command\": \"npx\",\n652:             \"args\": [\"-y\", \"figma-developer-mcp\", \"--stdio\"],\n653:             \"env\": {\n654:               \"FIGMA_API_KEY\": \"${FIGMA_API_KEY}\"\n655:             }\n656:           }\n657:         }\n658:       }\n659:     }\n660:   ]\n661: }\n662: ```\n663: \n664: ### Validation: `phase_3_complete`\n665: \n666: **Checklist:**\n667: - [ ] Platform config file updated (`.mcp.json`, `opencode.json`, or `.vscode/mcp.json`)\n668: - [ ] .utcp_config.json populated with MCP servers\n669: - [ ] Prefixed env vars added for each manual\n670: - [ ] .env added to .gitignore\n671: \n672: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n673: \n674: ---\n675: \n676: ## 5. VERIFICATION\n677: \n678: ### Check 1: Verify Configuration Files\n679: \n680: ```bash\n681: # Check .utcp_config.json is valid JSON\n682: python3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n683: \n684: # Check environment variables are defined\n685: cat .env | grep -v \"^#\" | grep -v \"^$\"\n686: ```\n687: \n688: ### Check 2: Verify in Your AI Client\n689: \n690: **In Claude Code:**\n691: ```bash\n692: # Start Claude Code session\n693: claude\n694: \n695: # Ask about available tools\n696: > What MCP tools are available?\n697: \n698: # Expected: code-mode tools should appear (call_tool_chain, search_tools, etc.)\n699: ```\n700: \n701: **In OpenCode:**\n702: ```bash\n703: opencode\n704: \n705: > List available MCP tools\n706: \n707: # Expected: Code Mode tools should appear\n708: ```\n709: \n710: ### Check 3: Test Tool Discovery\n711: \n712: ```\n713: # In your AI chat:\n714: Use search_tools to find tools related to \"myservice sites\"\n715: ```\n716: \n717: The response should show available MyService tools.\n718: \n719: ### Check 4: Test a Basic Call\n720: \n721: ```typescript\n722: // In your AI chat:\n723: // Use call_tool_chain to list all available tools:\n724: \n725: call_tool_chain({\n726:   code: `\n727:     const allTools = await list_tools();\n728:     console.log('Total tools:', allTools.tools.length);\n729:     console.log('Sample tools:', allTools.tools.slice(0, 10));\n730:     return allTools;\n731:   `\n732: });\n733: ```\n734: \n735: ### Validation: `phase_4_complete`\n736: \n737: **Checklist:**\n738: - [ ] list_tools() returns tools\n739: - [ ] Tool naming pattern understood\n740: - [ ] At least one manual accessible\n741: \n742: **Quick Verification:**\n743: ```bash\n744: # Test in OpenCode: call list_tools() and verify output\n745: echo \"Run: list_tools() in OpenCode to verify\"\n746: ```\n747: \n748: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n749: \n750: ---\n751: \n752: ## 6. USAGE\n753: \n754: ### CRITICAL: Naming Pattern\n755: \n756: The number one most common error is using the wrong function names. Wrong names produce \"Tool not found\" errors.\n757: \n758: **Pattern:**\n759: ```\n760: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n761: ```\n762: \n763: All tool calls must follow this exact pattern with a dot after the manual name and an underscore before the tool name.\n764: \n765: **Examples:**\n766: \n767: | Manual              | Pattern                                      | Example Call                                               |\n768: | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |\n769: | `myservice`           | `myservice.myservice_{tool}`                     | `myservice.myservice_sites_list({})`                           |\n770: | `github`            | `github.github_{tool}`                       | `github.github_get_issue({...})`                           |\n771: | `clickup`           | `clickup.clickup_{tool}`                     | `clickup.clickup_create_task({...})`                       |\n772: | `figma`             | `figma.figma_{tool}`                         | `figma.figma_get_file({...})`                              |\n773: | `chrome_devtools_1` | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool}` | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({...})` |\n774: \n775: ### Common Mistakes\n776: \n777: | Error                         | Wrong                          | Correct                        |\n778: | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |\n779: | **Missing second part**       | `myservice.sites_list()`         | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n780: | **Dot instead of underscore** | `myservice.myservice.sites_list()` | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n781: | **camelCase**                 | `myservice.myservice_sitesList()`  | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n782: | **Wrong manual name**         | `wf.myservice_sites_list()`      | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n783: \n784: ### Why This Pattern?\n785: \n786: The naming follows the `.utcp_config.json` structure:\n787: ```json\n788: {\n789:   \"name\": \"myservice\",           // First part (manual name)\n790:   \"config\": {\n791:     \"mcpServers\": {\n792:       \"myservice\": { ... }       // Second part (server name, joined with underscore to tool)\n793:     }\n794:   }\n795: }\n796: // Tool name comes from the MCP server, combined with underscore\n797: // Result: myservice.myservice_sites_list\n798: ```\n799: \n800: ### Basic Workflow\n801: \n802: **Step 1: Discover Tools**\n803: \n804: ```typescript\n805: // Search for relevant tools\n806: search_tools({\n807:   task_description: \"myservice site management\",\n808:   limit: 10\n809: });\n810: \n811: // Returns: Tool names and descriptions (minimal tokens)\n812: ```\n813: \n814: **Step 2: Get Tool Details (Optional)**\n815: \n816: ```typescript\n817: // Get full interface for specific tool\n818: tool_info({\n819:   tool_name: \"myservice.myservice_sites_list\"\n820: });\n821: \n822: // Returns: Full TypeScript interface definition\n823: ```\n824: \n825: **Step 3: Execute Tool**\n826: \n827: ```typescript\n828: // Execute TypeScript with direct tool access\n829: call_tool_chain({\n830:   code: `\n831:     const sites = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n832:     console.log('Found sites:', sites.sites.length);\n833:     return sites;\n834:   `\n835: });\n836: ```\n837: \n838: ### Multi-Tool Orchestration\n839: \n840: ```typescript\n841: // State persists across all operations in a single execution\n842: call_tool_chain({\n843:   code: `\n844:     // Step 1: Get Figma design\n845:     const design = await figma.figma_get_file({ fileId: \"abc123\" });\n846: \n847:     // Step 2: Create ClickUp task (design data available)\n848:     const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({\n849:       name: \\`Implement: \\${design.name}\\`,\n850:       listName: \"Development Sprint\",\n851:       description: \\`Design has \\${design.document.children.length} components\\`\n852:     });\n853: \n854:     // Step 3: Create GitHub issue (both design and task data available)\n855:     const issue = await github.github_create_issue({\n856:       owner: \"myorg\",\n857:       repo: \"myrepo\",\n858:       title: \\`Design: \\${design.name}\\`,\n859:       body: \\`ClickUp task: \\${task.url}\\`\n860:     });\n861: \n862:     return { design, task, issue };\n863:   `,\n864:   timeout: 60000  // Extended timeout for complex workflow\n865: });\n866: ```\n867: \n868: ### Timeout Calculation\n869: \n870: Use this formula to calculate the right timeout for your workflow:\n871: \n872: ```\n873: timeout = base_overhead + (num_tools × tool_avg) + safety_margin\n874: ```\n875: \n876: **Default Values:**\n877: \n878: | Component       | Value    | Description                     |\n879: | --------------- | -------- | ------------------------------- |\n880: | `base_overhead` | 5,000ms  | Initial connection and setup    |\n881: | `tool_avg`      | 3,000ms  | Average per-tool execution time |\n882: | `safety_margin` | 10,000ms | Buffer for network variability  |\n883: \n884: **Calculation Examples:**\n885: \n886: | Tools         | Calculation                | Result   | Recommended   |\n887: | ------------- | -------------------------- | -------- | ------------- |\n888: | **1 tool**    | 5000 + (1 x 3000) + 10000  | 18,000ms | **30,000ms**  |\n889: | **2 tools**   | 5000 + (2 x 3000) + 10000  | 21,000ms | **30,000ms**  |\n890: | **4 tools**   | 5000 + (4 x 3000) + 10000  | 27,000ms | **60,000ms**  |\n891: | **6 tools**   | 5000 + (6 x 3000) + 10000  | 33,000ms | **60,000ms**  |\n892: | **10 tools**  | 5000 + (10 x 3000) + 10000 | 45,000ms | **120,000ms** |\n893: | **15+ tools** | 5000 + (15 x 3000) + 10000 | 60,000ms | **120,000ms** |\n894: \n895: **Quick Reference:**\n896: \n897: | Complexity             | Timeout  | Use Case                    |\n898: | ---------------------- | -------- | --------------------------- |\n899: | **Simple** (1-2 tools) | `30000`  | List sites, get single item |\n900: | **Medium** (3-5 tools) | `60000`  | Create task + update CMS    |\n901: | **Complex** (6+ tools) | `120000` | Full design-to-dev pipeline |\n902: \n903: **Usage:**\n904: \n905: ```typescript\n906: call_tool_chain({\n907:   code: `\n908:     // Complex multi-tool workflow\n909:     const sites = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n910:     const collections = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: sites.sites[0].id });\n911:     const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: \"Review collections\" });\n912:     return { sites, collections, task };\n913:   `,\n914:   timeout: 60000  // 3 tools, use 60000ms\n915: });\n916: ```\n917: \n918: ### Validation: `phase_5_complete`\n919: \n920: **Checklist:**\n921: - [ ] Naming pattern applied correctly\n922: - [ ] Timeout calculated for workflow complexity\n923: - [ ] Tool discovery working via search_tools\n924: - [ ] At least one call_tool_chain execution succeeded\n925: \n926: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n927: \n928: ---\n929: \n930: ## 7. FEATURES\n931: \n932: ### 7.1 call_tool_chain\n933: \n934: **Purpose**: Execute TypeScript code with direct access to all configured MCP tools.\n935: \n936: **Parameters**:\n937: - `code` (string, required): TypeScript code to execute\n938: - `timeout` (number, optional): Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000)\n939: - `max_output_size` (number, optional): Max output characters (default: 200000)\n940: \n941: **Example**:\n942: ```typescript\n943: call_tool_chain({\n944:   code: `\n945:     const sites = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n946:     return sites;\n947:   `,\n948:   timeout: 60000\n949: });\n950: ```\n951: \n952: **Returns**: `{ result: any, logs: string[] }`\n953: \n954: ### 7.2 search_tools\n955: \n956: **Purpose**: Progressive discovery. Search for tools by task description.\n957: \n958: **Parameters**:\n959: - `task_description` (string, required): Natural language description of task\n960: - `limit` (number, optional): Maximum results to return (default: 10)\n961: \n962: **Example**:\n963: ```typescript\n964: search_tools({\n965:   task_description: \"create tasks in ClickUp\",\n966:   limit: 5\n967: });\n968: ```\n969: \n970: **Returns**: Array of tool names with descriptions (minimal tokens)\n971: \n972: ### 7.3 list_tools\n973: \n974: **Purpose**: List all available tools from all configured MCP servers.\n975: \n976: **Parameters**: None\n977: \n978: **Example**:\n979: ```typescript\n980: list_tools();\n981: ```\n982: \n983: **Returns**: `{ tools: string[] }` containing all available tool names\n984: \n985: ### 7.4 tool_info\n986: \n987: **Purpose**: Get complete TypeScript interface for a specific tool.\n988: \n989: **Parameters**:\n990: - `tool_name` (string, required): Full tool name (e.g., \"myservice.myservice_sites_list\")\n991: \n992: **Example**:\n993: ```typescript\n994: tool_info({\n995:   tool_name: \"clickup.clickup_create_task\"\n996: });\n997: ```\n998: \n999: **Returns**: Full TypeScript interface definition with parameter types\n1000: \n1001: ---\n1002: \n1003: ## 8. EXAMPLES\n1004: \n1005: ### Example 1: MyService Site Management\n1006: \n1007: **Scenario**: List all sites and their collections\n1008: \n1009: ```typescript\n1010: call_tool_chain({\n1011:   code: `\n1012:     // Get all sites\n1013:     const sitesResult = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n1014:     const sites = sitesResult.sites;\n1015: \n1016:     console.log(\\`Found \\${sites.length} sites\\`);\n1017: \n1018:     // Get collections for first site\n1019:     if (sites.length > 0) {\n1020:       const collections = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({\n1021:         site_id: sites[0].id\n1022:       });\n1023: \n1024:       console.log(\\`Site \"\\${sites[0].displayName}\" has \\${collections.collections.length} collections\\`);\n1025:     }\n1026: \n1027:     return { sites, collections };\n1028:   `,\n1029:   timeout: 30000\n1030: });\n1031: ```\n1032: \n1033: ### Example 2: GitHub Issue Creation\n1034: \n1035: **Scenario**: Create an issue with labels\n1036: \n1037: ```typescript\n1038: call_tool_chain({\n1039:   code: `\n1040:     // Create GitHub issue\n1041:     const issue = await github.github_create_issue({\n1042:       owner: \"myorg\",\n1043:       repo: \"myrepo\",\n1044:       title: \"Implement User Authentication\",\n1045:       body: \"## Description\\\\n\\\\nImplement OAuth2 login flow\",\n1046:       labels: [\"feature\", \"auth\"]\n1047:     });\n1048: \n1049:     console.log(\\`Created issue: #\\${issue.number}\\`);\n1050:     console.log(\\`URL: \\${issue.html_url}\\`);\n1051: \n1052:     return issue;\n1053:   `,\n1054:   timeout: 30000\n1055: });\n1056: ```\n1057: \n1058: ### Example 3: ClickUp Task Creation\n1059: \n1060: **Scenario**: Create a task with tags\n1061: \n1062: ```typescript\n1063: call_tool_chain({\n1064:   code: `\n1065:     // Create main task\n1066:     const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({\n1067:       name: \"Implement User Authentication\",\n1068:       listName: \"Development Sprint\",\n1069:       description: \"Implement OAuth2 login flow\",\n1070:       tags: [\"feature\", \"auth\"],\n1071:       priority: 2\n1072:     });\n1073: \n1074:     console.log(\\`Created task: \\${task.name} (ID: \\${task.id})\\`);\n1075:     console.log(\\`URL: \\${task.url}\\`);\n1076: \n1077:     return task;\n1078:   `,\n1079:   timeout: 30000\n1080: });\n1081: ```\n1082: \n1083: ### Example 4: Multi-Tool Workflow\n1084: \n1085: **Scenario**: Figma to GitHub to MyService pipeline\n1086: \n1087: ```typescript\n1088: call_tool_chain({\n1089:   code: `\n1090:     try {\n1091:       // Step 1: Get Figma design\n1092:       console.log('Fetching Figma design...');\n1093:       const design = await figma.figma_get_file({\n1094:         fileId: \"YOUR_FIGMA_FILE_ID\"\n1095:       });\n1096:       console.log(\\`Design: \\${design.name}\\`);\n1097: \n1098:       // Step 2: Create GitHub issue\n1099:       console.log('Creating GitHub issue...');\n1100:       const issue = await github.github_create_issue({\n1101:         owner: \"myorg\",\n1102:         repo: \"myrepo\",\n1103:         title: \\`Implement: \\${design.name}\\`,\n1104:         body: \\`Components: \\${design.document.children.length}\\`\n1105:       });\n1106:       console.log(\\`Issue created: \\${issue.html_url}\\`);\n1107: \n1108:       // Step 3: Add to external CMS queue\n1109:       console.log('Adding to external CMS...');\n1110:       const cmsItem = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create_item_live({\n1111:         collection_id: \"YOUR_COLLECTION_ID\",\n1112:         request: {\n1113:           items: [{\n1114:             fieldData: {\n1115:               name: design.name,\n1116:               \"github-url\": issue.html_url,\n1117:               status: \"Queued\",\n1118:               \"created-at\": new Date().toISOString()\n1119:             }\n1120:           }]\n1121:         }\n1122:       });\n1123: \n1124:       console.log('Pipeline complete!');\n1125:       return {\n1126:         success: true,\n1127:         design: design.name,\n1128:         issueUrl: issue.html_url,\n1129:         cmsItemId: cmsItem.id\n1130:       };\n1131: \n1132:     } catch (error) {\n1133:       console.error('Pipeline failed:', error.message);\n1134:       return { success: false, error: error.message };\n1135:     }\n1136:   `,\n1137:   timeout: 120000\n1138: });\n1139: ```\n1140: \n1141: ### Example 5: Chrome DevTools Automation\n1142: \n1143: **Scenario**: Take a screenshot and check console errors\n1144: \n1145: ```typescript\n1146: call_tool_chain({\n1147:   code: `\n1148:     // Create new browser page\n1149:     const page = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_new_page({});\n1150:     console.log(\\`Page created: \\${page.pageId}\\`);\n1151: \n1152:     // Navigate to URL\n1153:     await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n1154:       pageId: page.pageId,\n1155:       url: \"https://example.com\"\n1156:     });\n1157:     console.log('Navigation complete');\n1158: \n1159:     // Wait for page load\n1160:     await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 3000));\n1161: \n1162:     // Take screenshot\n1163:     const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({\n1164:       pageId: page.pageId\n1165:     });\n1166:     console.log('Screenshot captured');\n1167: \n1168:     // Get console messages\n1169:     const consoleMessages = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_get_console_message({\n1170:       pageId: page.pageId\n1171:     });\n1172: \n1173:     // Close page\n1174:     await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_close_page({ pageId: page.pageId });\n1175: \n1176:     return {\n1177:       screenshot: screenshot,\n1178:       consoleErrors: consoleMessages.filter(m => m.level === 'error')\n1179:     };\n1180:   `,\n1181:   timeout: 60000\n1182: });\n1183: ```\n1184: \n1185: ### Example 6: Error Handling Pattern\n1186: \n1187: **Scenario**: Resilient workflow with fallbacks\n1188: \n1189: ```typescript\n1190: call_tool_chain({\n1191:   code: `\n1192:     const results = {\n1193:       successes: [],\n1194:       failures: []\n1195:     };\n1196: \n1197:     // Helper for safe execution\n1198:     async function tryExecute(name, fn) {\n1199:       try {\n1200:         const result = await fn();\n1201:         results.successes.push({ name, result });\n1202:         return result;\n1203:       } catch (error) {\n1204:         results.failures.push({ name, error: error.message });\n1205:         console.error(\\`\\${name} failed: \\${error.message}\\`);\n1206:         return null;\n1207:       }\n1208:     }\n1209: \n1210:     // Execute operations with fallbacks\n1211:     const sites = await tryExecute('list-sites',\n1212:       () => myservice.myservice_sites_list({})\n1213:     );\n1214: \n1215:     if (sites) {\n1216:       await tryExecute('get-collections',\n1217:         () => myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: sites.sites[0].id })\n1218:       );\n1219:     }\n1220: \n1221:     console.log(\\`Complete: \\${results.successes.length} succeeded, \\${results.failures.length} failed\\`);\n1222:     return results;\n1223:   `,\n1224:   timeout: 60000\n1225: });\n1226: ```\n1227: \n1228: ---\n1229: \n1230: ## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING\n1231: \n1232: ### Error Message Quick Reference\n1233: \n1234: | Error Message                                | Cause                        | Solution                                  |\n1235: | -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |\n1236: | `Tool not found: myservice.sites_list`         | Missing second manual part   | Use `myservice.myservice_sites_list`          |\n1237: | `Tool not found: myservice.myservice.sites_list` | Dot instead of underscore    | Use `myservice.myservice_sites_list`          |\n1238: | `Execution timeout exceeded`                 | Complex operation            | Increase `timeout` parameter              |\n1239: | `UTCP_CONFIG_FILE not set`                   | Missing environment variable | Set path to `.utcp_config.json`           |\n1240: | `Environment variable X not found`           | Missing in .env              | Add variable to `.env` file               |\n1241: | `Variable 'manual_VAR' not found`            | Missing prefixed variable    | Add `{manual}_{VAR}` to `.env`            |\n1242: | `TypeError: X is not a function`             | Wrong naming pattern         | Check exact tool name with `search_tools` |\n1243: | `Failed to start MCP server`                 | Package or auth issue        | Test command manually in terminal         |\n1244: | `Invalid JSON`                               | Config syntax error          | Validate with `python3 -m json.tool`      |\n1245: \n1246: ### Prefixed Variable Not Found\n1247: \n1248: **Problem**: `Error during batch registration for manual 'X': Variable 'X_VAR' not found`\n1249: \n1250: **Cause**: Code Mode requires environment variables prefixed with the manual name.\n1251: \n1252: **Solution**:\n1253: 1. Identify the manual name from the error (e.g., `figma`, `clickup`, `github`)\n1254: 2. Add the prefixed variable to `.env`:\n1255:    ```bash\n1256:    # For error: Variable 'figma_FIGMA_API_KEY' not found\n1257:    figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\n1258: \n1259:    # For error: Variable 'github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN' not found\n1260:    github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\n1261:    ```\n1262: 3. Restart OpenCode/Code Mode after updating `.env`\n1263: \n1264: See the [Prefixed Environment Variables](#critical-prefixed-environment-variables) section for the complete list.\n1265: \n1266: ### Tool Not Found Error\n1267: \n1268: **Problem**: `Error: Tool not found: myservice.sites_list`\n1269: \n1270: **Cause**: Missing the second manual/server part in the tool name\n1271: \n1272: **Solution**:\n1273: 1. Use correct naming pattern: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}`\n1274:    ```typescript\n1275:    // Wrong: missing second part\n1276:    await myservice.sites_list({});\n1277: \n1278:    // Wrong: dot instead of underscore\n1279:    await myservice.myservice.sites_list({});\n1280: \n1281:    // Correct\n1282:    await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n1283:    ```\n1284: \n1285: 2. Use tool discovery to find exact names:\n1286:    ```typescript\n1287:    const tools = await search_tools({\n1288:      task_description: \"myservice sites\",\n1289:      limit: 10\n1290:    });\n1291:    console.log(tools.map(t => t.name));\n1292:    ```\n1293: \n1294: ### Environment Variables Not Loading\n1295: \n1296: **Problem**: `Error: Environment variable CLICKUP_API_KEY not found`\n1297: \n1298: **Solutions**:\n1299: 1. Check `.env` file exists in same directory as `.utcp_config.json`\n1300: \n1301: 2. Verify the variable is defined (not commented out):\n1302:    ```bash\n1303:    cat .env | grep CLICKUP_API_KEY\n1304:    # Should show: CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_...\n1305:    ```\n1306: \n1307: 3. Check `load_variables_from` config is correct:\n1308:    ```json\n1309:    \"load_variables_from\": [\n1310:      {\n1311:        \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n1312:        \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n1313:      }\n1314:    ]\n1315:    ```\n1316: \n1317: 4. Restart your AI client after changing `.env`\n1318: \n1319: ### MCP Server Fails to Start\n1320: \n1321: **Problem**: `Error: Failed to start MCP server: myservice`\n1322: \n1323: **Solutions**:\n1324: 1. Test the command manually:\n1325:    ```bash\n1326:    npx mcp-remote https://mcp.myservice.com/sse\n1327:    ```\n1328: \n1329: 2. Check npm/npx is in PATH:\n1330:    ```bash\n1331:    which npx\n1332:    ```\n1333: \n1334: 3. Install the package directly if the cached version fails:\n1335:    ```bash\n1336:    npm install -g @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\n1337:    ```\n1338: \n1339: 4. Check for missing dependencies or auth issues\n1340: \n1341: ### Tools Not Appearing\n1342: \n1343: **Problem**: `search_tools` returns empty results\n1344: \n1345: **Solutions**:\n1346: 1. Verify MCP servers are configured in `.utcp_config.json`\n1347: \n1348: 2. Check manual names do not have invalid characters:\n1349:    ```json\n1350:    // Good\n1351:    \"name\": \"myservice\"\n1352:    \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\"\n1353: \n1354:    // Bad (hyphens not allowed)\n1355:    \"name\": \"myservice-api\"\n1356:    \"name\": \"my server\"\n1357:    ```\n1358: \n1359: 3. Restart AI client after configuration changes\n1360: \n1361: 4. Check for JSON syntax errors:\n1362:    ```bash\n1363:    python3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n1364:    ```\n1365: \n1366: ### Timeout Errors\n1367: \n1368: **Problem**: `Error: Execution timeout exceeded`\n1369: \n1370: **Solutions**:\n1371: 1. Increase timeout using the calculation formula:\n1372:    ```typescript\n1373:    call_tool_chain({\n1374:      code: `...`,\n1375:      timeout: 120000  // 2 minutes for complex workflows\n1376:    });\n1377:    ```\n1378: \n1379: 2. Break large operations into smaller chunks\n1380: \n1381: 3. Use parallel execution where possible:\n1382:    ```typescript\n1383:    const [result1, result2] = await Promise.all([\n1384:      tool1.tool1_action(),\n1385:      tool2.tool2_action()\n1386:    ]);\n1387:    ```\n1388: \n1389: ### TypeError: Not a Function\n1390: \n1391: **Problem**: `TypeError: myservice.myservice is not a function`\n1392: \n1393: **Cause**: Trying to call the server object as a function\n1394: \n1395: **Solution**: Add the tool name after the second part:\n1396: ```typescript\n1397: // Wrong: missing tool name\n1398: await myservice.myservice();\n1399: \n1400: // Correct: include tool name\n1401: await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n1402: ```\n1403: \n1404: ---\n1405: \n1406: ## 10. RESOURCES\n1407: \n1408: ### Documentation\n1409: \n1410: - **MCP Protocol**: https://modelcontextprotocol.io\n1411: - **MCP Servers List**: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers\n1412: \n1413: ### MCP Server Packages\n1414: \n1415: | Server              | Package                                  | Tools | Authentication        |\n1416: | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----- | --------------------- |\n1417: | **GitHub**          | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`    | 26    | Personal Access Token |\n1418: | **MyService**         | `mcp-remote https://mcp.myservice.com/sse` | 42    | OAuth (dashboard)     |\n1419: | **ClickUp**         | `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server`         | 21    | API Key + Team ID     |\n1420: | **Figma**           | `figma-developer-mcp`                    | 18    | Personal Access Token |\n1421: | **Chrome DevTools** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`             | 26    | None                  |\n1422: \n1423: ### Configuration Paths\n1424: \n1425: | Client              | Configuration File |\n1426: | ------------------- | ------------------ |\n1427: | **Claude Code**     | `.mcp.json`        |\n1428: | **OpenCode**        | `opencode.json`    |\n1429: | **VS Code Copilot** | `.vscode/mcp.json` |\n1430: | **Windsurf**        | `.mcp.toml`        |\n1431: \n1432: ### Helper Commands\n1433: \n1434: ```bash\n1435: # Validate JSON configuration\n1436: python3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n1437: \n1438: # Check environment variables\n1439: cat .env | grep -v \"^#\" | grep -v \"^$\"\n1440: \n1441: # Test npx command manually\n1442: npx -y @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\n1443: \n1444: # List manual names from config\n1445: cat .utcp_config.json | grep '\"name\"'\n1446: ```\n1447: \n1448: ### Performance Metrics\n1449: \n1450: | Metric              | Traditional MCP   | Code Mode        | Improvement     |\n1451: | ------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | --------------- |\n1452: | **Context tokens**  | 141k (47 tools)   | 1.6k (159 tools) | 98.7% reduction |\n1453: | **Execution time**  | ~2000ms (4 tools) | ~300ms (4 tools) | 85% faster      |\n1454: | **API round trips** | 15+               | 1                | 93% reduction   |\n1455: \n1456: ### Project Structure\n1457: \n1458: ```\n1459: your-project/\n1460: ├── .utcp_config.json     # MCP server definitions\n1461: ├── .env                   # API keys (gitignored)\n1462: ├── .env.example           # Template for team\n1463: ├── .mcp.json              # Claude Code config (optional)\n1464: ├── opencode.json          # OpenCode config (optional)\n1465: └── .vscode/\n1466:     └── mcp.json           # VS Code config (optional)\n1467: ```\n1468: \n1469: ---\n1470: \n1471: ## Quick Reference Card\n1472: \n1473: ### Essential Commands\n1474: \n1475: ```bash\n1476: # Create configuration\n1477: cat > .utcp_config.json << 'EOF'\n1478: {\n1479:   \"load_variables_from\": [\n1480:     { \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\", \"env_file_path\": \".env\" }\n1481:   ],\n1482:   \"tool_repository\": { \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\" },\n1483:   \"tool_search_strategy\": { \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\" },\n1484:   \"manual_call_templates\": []\n1485: }\n1486: EOF\n1487: \n1488: # Validate JSON\n1489: python3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n1490: \n1491: # Check environment variables\n1492: env | grep -E \"(CLICKUP|FIGMA|GITHUB)\"\n1493: ```\n1494: \n1495: ### NAMING PATTERN\n1496: \n1497: ```typescript\n1498: // Pattern: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n1499: //          ───┬───  ─────┬─────  ────┬────\n1500: //             │          │           └── Tool from MCP server\n1501: //             │          └── Server name + underscore + tool\n1502: //             └── Manual name (from \"name\" field)\n1503: \n1504: // Examples:\n1505: myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n1506: github.github_get_issue({ owner: \"foo\", repo: \"bar\", issue_number: 123 });\n1507: clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: \"My Task\", listName: \"Sprint\" });\n1508: figma.figma_get_file({ fileId: \"abc123\" });\n1509: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_new_page({});\n1510: ```\n1511: \n1512: ### Common Workflows\n1513: \n1514: **Tool Discovery:**\n1515: ```typescript\n1516: const tools = await search_tools({ task_description: \"myservice cms\", limit: 10 });\n1517: console.log(tools.map(t => t.name));\n1518: ```\n1519: \n1520: **Single Tool Call:**\n1521: ```typescript\n1522: call_tool_chain({\n1523:   code: `await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})`\n1524: });\n1525: ```\n1526: \n1527: **Multi-Tool with Error Handling:**\n1528: ```typescript\n1529: call_tool_chain({\n1530:   code: `\n1531:     try {\n1532:       const a = await github.github_get_repo({ owner: \"x\", repo: \"y\" });\n1533:       const b = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: a.name });\n1534:       return { success: true, a, b };\n1535:     } catch (error) {\n1536:       return { success: false, error: error.message };\n1537:     }\n1538:   `,\n1539:   timeout: 60000\n1540: });\n1541: ```\n1542: \n1543: ### Validation Checklists\n1544: \n1545: **Installation:**\n1546: ```\n1547: [ ] Node.js 18+ installed\n1548: [ ] .utcp_config.json created and valid\n1549: [ ] .env created with required keys\n1550: [ ] .env in .gitignore\n1551: [ ] AI client configured\n1552: [ ] Code Mode tools visible\n1553: ```\n1554: \n1555: **Before Each Session:**\n1556: ```\n1557: [ ] Correct naming pattern: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n1558: [ ] Timeout calculated for complexity\n1559: [ ] Error handling in place\n1560: [ ] API keys not expired\n1561: ```\n1562: \n1563: ---\n1564: \n1565: ## Version History\n1566: \n1567: | Version | Date       | Changes                                                                 |\n1568: | ------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n1569: | v2.0.0  | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, standardized phase_N_complete validation naming, TOC moved after AI-First Install Guide, Quick Reference Card added to Resources |\n1570: | v1.0.0  | 2025-01-01 | Initial release                                                         |\n\n(End of file - total 1570 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# MCP Code Mode Installation Guide\n\nComplete installation and configuration guide for the Code Mode MCP server. This enables TypeScript-based orchestration of external MCP tools, giving you unified access to MyService, Figma, ClickUp, GitHub, Chrome DevTools and other MCP servers through a single `call_tool_chain()` interface. It delivers 98.7% context reduction and 60% faster execution compared to individual tool calls, with type-safe invocation and automatic tool discovery.\n\n> **Version:** 2.0.0\n> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n> **Package**: `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` | **Dependencies**: Node.js 18+, .utcp_config.json\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\n**Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:**\n\n```\nI want to install the MCP Code Mode server for TypeScript tool orchestration.\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n2. Verify I have npx available for running MCP servers","truncated":false,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md","text":"# MCP Code Mode Installation Guide\n\nComplete installation and configuration guide for the Code Mode MCP server. This enables TypeScript-based orchestration of external MCP tools, giving you unified access to MyService, Figma, ClickUp, GitHub, Chrome DevTools and other MCP servers through a single `call_tool_chain()` interface. It delivers 98.7% context reduction and 60% faster execution compared to individual tool calls, with type-safe invocation and automatic tool discovery.\n\n> **Version:** 2.0.0\n> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n> **Package**: `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` | **Dependencies**: Node.js 18+, .utcp_config.json\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\n**Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:**\n\n```\nI want to install the MCP Code Mode server for TypeScript tool orchestration.\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n2. Verify I have npx available for running MCP servers\n3. Create the required configuration files (.utcp_config.json and .env)\n4. Configure Code Mode for my AI environment (I'm using: [Claude Code / OpenCode / VS Code Copilot])\n5. Add my first MCP server (e.g., MyService, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub)\n6. Verify the installation is working with a test search\n7. Test a basic tool call using the correct naming pattern\n\nMy preferred MCP servers are: [MyService / ClickUp / Figma / GitHub / Chrome DevTools / other]\n\nGuide me through each step with the exact commands and configuration needed.\n```\n\n**What the AI will do:**\n- Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system\n- Create `.utcp_config.json` configuration file\n- Create `.env` file for API keys and secrets (with proper security)\n- Configure Code Mode for your specific AI platform\n- Add MCP server definitions for your preferred tools\n- Test the four available tools: `call_tool_chain`, `search_tools`, `list_tools`, `tool_info`\n- Show you the critical naming convention: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}`\n- Demonstrate progressive tool discovery\n\n**Expected setup time:** 10-15 minutes\n\n---\n\n## 1. OVERVIEW\n\nCode Mode MCP is a TypeScript execution environment that provides unified access to 159 MCP tools across 6 manuals through progressive disclosure. Instead of exposing all tools to your AI context (causing token exhaustion), Code Mode provides a single execution environment where tools are accessed programmatically and loaded on-demand.\n\n### Core Principle\n\n> **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint. Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.\n\n### Source Repository\n\n| Property             | Value                                                                                                     |\n| -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| **GitHub**           | [universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode](https://github.com/universal-tool-calling-protocol/code-mode) |\n| **npm (MCP Server)** | `@utcp/code-mode-mcp`                                                                                     |\n| **npm (Library)**    | `@utcp/code-mode`                                                                                         |\n| **Stars**            | 1.2k+                                                                                                     |\n| **License**          | MPL-2.0                                                                                                   |\n\n> **Note**: This is the official UTCP (Universal Tool Calling Protocol) implementation, not to be confused with other \"code mode\" projects like `replicate/replicate-mcp-code-mode` (Replicate-specific) or `jx-codes/codemode-mcp` (abandoned).\n\n### Current Configuration\n\n| Manual                | Tools   | Package                                 |\n| --------------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| **github**            | 26      | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`   |\n| **figma**             | 18      | `figma-developer-mcp`                   |\n| **chrome_devtools_1** | 26      | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`            |\n| **chrome_devtools_2** | 26      | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` (parallel) |\n| **clickup**           | 21      | `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server`        |\n| **myservice**           | 42      | `mcp-remote` (SSE)                      |\n| **Total**             | **159** | **6 manuals**                           |\n\n### The \"2-3 MCP Server Wall\" Problem\n\nTraditional tool exposure breaks at scale. Each additional server costs context tokens:\n\n**Traditional Approach:**\n```\nTools:     10      20      30      47      100     200\nTokens:    30k     60k     90k    141k    300k    600k\nUsable?    yes     yes     no      no      no      no\n```\n\n**Code Mode Solution:**\n```\nTools:     10      20      30      47      100     200+\nTokens:    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k    1.6k\nUsable?    yes     yes     yes     yes     yes     yes\n```\n\nCode Mode keeps your context flat at ~1.6k tokens regardless of how many servers you add.\n\n### Architecture Overview\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  AI Agent (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code Copilot)          │\n│                                                             │\n│  Sees: Only 4 tools in context (~1.6k tokens)               │\n│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │\n│  │ • call_tool_chain   (Execute TypeScript)              │  │\n│  │ • search_tools      (Progressive discovery)           │  │\n│  │ • list_tools        (List all available)              │  │\n│  │ • tool_info         (Get tool interface)              │  │\n│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │\n└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │ TypeScript Code\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Code Mode MCP Server (V8 Isolate Sandbox)                  │\n│  • Executes TypeScript with tool access                     │\n│  • Routes calls to appropriate MCP servers                  │\n│  • Returns results + logs                                   │\n└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │ Tool Calls\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  MCP Servers: MyService, ClickUp, Figma, GitHub, Chrome, etc. │\n│  (159 tools accessible via Code Mode)                       │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### How It Compares\n\n| Feature          | Traditional MCP             | Code Mode MCP           |\n| ---------------- | --------------------------- | ----------------------- |\n| **Context Cost** | ~3k tokens per tool         | ~1.6k total (all tools) |\n| **Max Tools**    | 2-3 servers (context limit) | Unlimited servers       |\n| **Discovery**    | All tools upfront           | Progressive (on-demand) |\n| **Multi-Tool**   | Multiple API calls          | Single execution        |\n| **State**        | Manual context management   | Automatic persistence   |\n| **Execution**    | ~500ms per tool             | ~300ms for 4 tools      |\n\n---\n\n## 2. PREREQUISITES\n\nBefore installing Code Mode MCP, ensure you have the following.\n\n### Required\n\n- **Node.js 18 or higher**\n  ```bash\n  node --version\n  # Should show v18.x or higher\n  ```\n\n- **npm/npx** (comes with Node.js)\n  ```bash\n  npm --version\n  npx --version\n  ```\n\n- **MCP-Compatible Client** (one of the following):\n  - Claude Code CLI\n  - OpenCode CLI\n  - VS Code with GitHub Copilot\n  - Windsurf\n\n### Optional but Recommended\n\n- **API Keys** for the MCP servers you want to use:\n  - ClickUp: API key + Team ID (Settings > Apps)\n  - Figma: Personal Access Token (Settings > Access Tokens)\n  - GitHub: Personal Access Token (Settings > Developer settings)\n  - MyService: OAuth (configured in MyService dashboard)\n\n- **Git** for version control\n\n### Validation: `phase_1_complete`\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Node.js 18+ installed\n- [ ] .env file exists with API keys\n- [ ] .utcp_config.json exists\n\n**Quick Verification:**\n```bash\nnode --version && [ -f .env ] && [ -f .utcp_config.json ] && echo \"PASS\" || echo \"FAIL\"\n```\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 3. INSTALLATION\n\n### Step 1: Choose Installation Location\n\nDecide where to place your Code Mode configuration:\n\n```bash\n# Option A: Project-specific (recommended for isolated projects)\ncd /path/to/your/project\n\n# Option B: Global location (for shared configuration)\ncd ~/CloudStorage/MCP\\ Servers\n```\n\n### Step 2: Create Configuration Directory Structure\n\n```bash\n# Create directory structure (optional, for local config files)\nmkdir -p .opencode/mcp-code-mode\n```\n\n### Step 3: Create .utcp_config.json\n\nCreate the main configuration file in your project root:\n\n```bash\ncat > .utcp_config.json << 'EOF'\n{\n  \"load_variables_from\": [\n    {\n      \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n      \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"tool_repository\": {\n    \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\"\n  },\n  \"tool_search_strategy\": {\n    \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\"\n  },\n  \"manual_call_templates\": []\n}\nEOF\n```\n\n### Step 4: Create .env File\n\nCreate an environment file for secrets:\n\n```bash\ncat > .env << 'EOF'\n# =============================================================================\n# API Keys -
