Standard Variables\n# =============================================================================\n\n# ClickUp Configuration\n# CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here\n# CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here\n\n# Figma Configuration\n# FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here\n\n# GitHub Configuration\n# GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here\n\n# Voyage AI (for Narsil neural search)\n# VOYAGE_API_KEY=pa-your_voyage_key_here\n\n# =============================================================================\n# Code Mode Prefixed Variables (REQUIRED for Code Mode)\n# =============================================================================\n# Code Mode requires variables prefixed with the manual name.\n# Copy your API keys to these prefixed versions.\n\n# clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your_api_key_here\n# clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your_team_id_here\n# figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your_token_here\n# github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your_token_here\nEOF\n```\n\n**Add `.env` to `.gitignore`:**\n\n```bash\necho \".env\" >> .gitignore\n```\n\n### Step 5: Verify Files Created\n\n```bash\nls -la .utcp_config.json .env\n\n# Expected output:\n# -rw-r--r--  .utcp_config.json\n# -rw-r--r--  .env\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_2_complete`\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] code_mode entry in opencode.json\n- [ ] UTCP_CONFIG_FILE env var set\n- [ ] code-mode-mcp accessible\n\n**Quick Verification:**\n```bash\ngrep -q '\"code_mode\"' opencode.json && echo \"PASS\" || echo \"FAIL\"\n```\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 4. CONFIGURATION\n\n### Option A: Configure for Claude Code CLI\n\nAdd to `.mcp.json` in your project root:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"code-mode\": {\n      \"command\": \"npx\",\n      \"args\": [\"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"],\n      \"env\": {\n        \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/your/.utcp_config.json\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Option B: Configure for OpenCode\n\nAdd to `opencode.json` in your project root.\n\n**Option B1: NPM Package (Recommended for most users)**\n```json\n{\n  \"mcp\": {\n    \"code_mode\": {\n      \"type\": \"local\",\n      \"command\": [\n        \"npx\",\n        \"-y\",\n        \"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"\n      ],\n      \"environment\": {\n        \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \".utcp_config.json\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Option B2: Embedded Source (For bundled projects)**\n\nUse this if the Code Mode source is embedded in your project at `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/`:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcp\": {\n    \"code_mode\": {\n      \"type\": \"local\",\n      \"command\": [\n        \"node\",\n        \".opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js\"\n      ],\n      \"environment\": {\n        \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \".utcp_config.json\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n> **Note:** The embedded approach requires running `npm install` in the `mcp_server/` directory first. This is useful for projects that want to bundle the MCP server source for version control and portability.\n\n### Option C: Configure for VS Code Copilot\n\nAdd to `.vscode/mcp.json`:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"code-mode\": {\n      \"command\": \"npx\",\n      \"args\": [\"@utcp/code-mode-mcp\"],\n      \"env\": {\n        \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/your/.utcp_config.json\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Package Name Note\n\nThe package `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` is the official UTCP Code Mode MCP server. Some older documentation may reference `utcp-mcp`. Both should work, but `@utcp/code-mode-mcp` is the current recommended package.\n\n---\n\n### SECURITY WARNING\n\nNever hardcode API keys directly in `.utcp_config.json`. Always use environment variable references.\n\n**Wrong (Security Risk):**\n```json\n{\n  \"env\": {\n    \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"pk_224591351_ACTUAL_KEY_HERE\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Correct (Secure):**\n```json\n{\n  \"env\": {\n    \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\nDefine the actual value in `.env` with the manual name prefix:\n```bash\n# Code Mode requires prefixed variable names: {manual_name}_{VAR}\n# If your manual name is \"clickup\", use:\nclickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_224591351_your_actual_key\n```\n\nSee the \"CRITICAL: Prefixed Environment Variables\" section below for full details.\n\n**Security Checklist:**\n```\n[ ] .env file is in .gitignore\n[ ] No hardcoded API keys in .utcp_config.json\n[ ] All secrets use ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax\n[ ] .env file permissions are restricted (chmod 600 .env)\n```\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL: Prefixed Environment Variables\n\nCode Mode requires PREFIXED environment variables. This is the number one cause of \"Variable not found\" errors.\n\nWhen Code Mode loads a manual configuration, it looks for environment variables with the manual name as a prefix. For example:\n\n| Manual Name | Config Variable | Required .env Variable |\n|-------------|-----------------|------------------------|\n| `figma` | `${FIGMA_API_KEY}` | `figma_FIGMA_API_KEY` |\n| `github` | `${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}` | `github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` |\n| `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_API_KEY}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY` |\n| `clickup` | `${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}` | `clickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID` |\n\n**Error you'll see without prefixed variables:**\n```\nError during batch registration for manual 'figma': Variable 'figma_FIGMA_API_KEY'\nreferenced in call template configuration not found.\n```\n\n**Solution:** Add both unprefixed and prefixed versions to your `.env`:\n\n```bash\n# Standard variables (for other tools)\nFIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\nGITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\nCLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your-clickup-key\nCLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your-team-id\n\n# Code Mode prefixed versions (REQUIRED for Code Mode)\nfigma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\ngithub_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\nclickup_CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_your-clickup-key\nclickup_CLICKUP_TEAM_ID=your-team-id\n```\n\n**Why both?** The unprefixed versions are used by other tools and scripts. The prefixed versions are specifically for Code Mode's variable substitution system.\n\n---\n\n### Adding MCP Servers\n\nAdd servers to `.utcp_config.json` in the `manual_call_templates` array:\n\n#### GitHub\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"github\",\n  \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n  \"config\": {\n    \"mcpServers\": {\n      \"github\": {\n        \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n        \"command\": \"npx\",\n        \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@modelcontextprotocol/server-github\"],\n        \"env\": {\n          \"GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN\": \"${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### MyService (Remote SSE)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"myservice\",\n  \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n  \"config\": {\n    \"mcpServers\": {\n      \"myservice\": {\n        \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n        \"command\": \"npx\",\n        \"args\": [\"mcp-remote\", \"https://mcp.myservice.com/sse\"],\n        \"env\": {}\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### ClickUp (Local with Auth)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"clickup\",\n  \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n  \"config\": {\n    \"mcpServers\": {\n      \"clickup\": {\n        \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n        \"command\": \"npx\",\n        \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\"],\n        \"env\": {\n          \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\",\n          \"CLICKUP_TEAM_ID\": \"${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Figma\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"figma\",\n  \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n  \"config\": {\n    \"mcpServers\": {\n      \"figma\": {\n        \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n        \"command\": \"npx\",\n        \"args\": [\"-y\", \"figma-developer-mcp\", \"--stdio\"],\n        \"env\": {\n          \"FIGMA_API_KEY\": \"${FIGMA_API_KEY}\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Chrome DevTools (Multiple Instances)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\",\n  \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n  \"config\": {\n    \"mcpServers\": {\n      \"chrome_devtools_1\": {\n        \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n        \"command\": \"npx\",\n        \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n        \"env\": {}\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Complete .utcp_config.json Example\n\n```json\n{\n  \"load_variables_from\": [\n    {\n      \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n      \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"tool_repository\": {\n    \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\"\n  },\n  \"tool_search_strategy\": {\n    \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\"\n  },\n  \"manual_call_templates\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"github\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"github\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@modelcontextprotocol/server-github\"],\n            \"env\": {\n              \"GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN\": \"${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}\"\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"myservice\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"myservice\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"mcp-remote\", \"https://mcp.myservice.com/sse\"],\n            \"env\": {}\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"clickup\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"clickup\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"-y\", \"@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\"],\n            \"env\": {\n              \"CLICKUP_API_KEY\": \"${CLICKUP_API_KEY}\",\n              \"CLICKUP_TEAM_ID\": \"${CLICKUP_TEAM_ID}\"\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"figma\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"figma\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"-y\", \"figma-developer-mcp\", \"--stdio\"],\n            \"env\": {\n              \"FIGMA_API_KEY\": \"${FIGMA_API_KEY}\"\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_3_complete`\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Platform config file updated (`.mcp.json`, `opencode.json`, or `.vscode/mcp.json`)\n- [ ] .utcp_config.json populated with MCP servers\n- [ ] Prefixed env vars added for each manual\n- [ ] .env added to .gitignore\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFICATION\n\n### Check 1: Verify Configuration Files\n\n```bash\n# Check .utcp_config.json is valid JSON\npython3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n\n# Check environment variables are defined\ncat .env | grep -v \"^#\" | grep -v \"^$\"\n```\n\n### Check 2: Verify in Your AI Client\n\n**In Claude Code:**\n```bash\n# Start Claude Code session\nclaude\n\n# Ask about available tools\n> What MCP tools are available?\n\n# Expected: code-mode tools should appear (call_tool_chain, search_tools, etc.)\n```\n\n**In OpenCode:**\n```bash\nopencode\n\n> List available MCP tools\n\n# Expected: Code Mode tools should appear\n```\n\n### Check 3: Test Tool Discovery\n\n```\n# In your AI chat:\nUse search_tools to find tools related to \"myservice sites\"\n```\n\nThe response should show available MyService tools.\n\n### Check 4: Test a Basic Call\n\n```typescript\n// In your AI chat:\n// Use call_tool_chain to list all available tools:\n\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    const allTools = await list_tools();\n    console.log('Total tools:', allTools.tools.length);\n    console.log('Sample tools:', allTools.tools.slice(0, 10));\n    return allTools;\n  `\n});\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_4_complete`\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] list_tools() returns tools\n- [ ] Tool naming pattern understood\n- [ ] At least one manual accessible\n\n**Quick Verification:**\n```bash\n# Test in OpenCode: call list_tools() and verify output\necho \"Run: list_tools() in OpenCode to verify\"\n```\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 6. USAGE\n\n### CRITICAL: Naming Pattern\n\nThe number one most common error is using the wrong function names. Wrong names produce \"Tool not found\" errors.\n\n**Pattern:**\n```\n{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n```\n\nAll tool calls must follow this exact pattern with a dot after the manual name and an underscore before the tool name.\n\n**Examples:**\n\n| Manual              | Pattern                                      | Example Call                                               |\n| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `myservice`           | `myservice.myservice_{tool}`                     | `myservice.myservice_sites_list({})`                           |\n| `github`            | `github.github_{tool}`                       | `github.github_get_issue({...})`                           |\n| `clickup`           | `clickup.clickup_{tool}`                     | `clickup.clickup_create_task({...})`                       |\n| `figma`             | `figma.figma_{tool}`                         | `figma.figma_get_file({...})`                              |\n| `chrome_devtools_1` | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool}` | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({...})` |\n\n### Common Mistakes\n\n| Error                         | Wrong                          | Correct                        |\n| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |\n| **Missing second part**       | `myservice.sites_list()`         | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n| **Dot instead of underscore** | `myservice.myservice.sites_list()` | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n| **camelCase**                 | `myservice.myservice_sitesList()`  | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n| **Wrong manual name**         | `wf.myservice_sites_list()`      | `myservice.myservice_sites_list()` |\n\n### Why This Pattern?\n\nThe naming follows the `.utcp_config.json` structure:\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"myservice\",           // First part (manual name)\n  \"config\": {\n    \"mcpServers\": {\n      \"myservice\": { ... }       // Second part (server name, joined with underscore to tool)\n    }\n  }\n}\n// Tool name comes from the MCP server, combined with underscore\n// Result: myservice.myservice_sites_list\n```\n\n### Basic Workflow\n\n**Step 1: Discover Tools**\n\n```typescript\n// Search for relevant tools\nsearch_tools({\n  task_description: \"myservice site management\",\n  limit: 10\n});\n\n// Returns: Tool names and descriptions (minimal tokens)\n```\n\n**Step 2: Get Tool Details (Optional)**\n\n```typescript\n// Get full interface for specific tool\ntool_info({\n  tool_name: \"myservice.myservice_sites_list\"\n});\n\n// Returns: Full TypeScript interface definition\n```\n\n**Step 3: Execute Tool**\n\n```typescript\n// Execute TypeScript with direct tool access\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    const sites = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n    console.log('Found sites:', sites.sites.length);\n    return sites;\n  `\n});\n```\n\n### Multi-Tool Orchestration\n\n```typescript\n// State persists across all operations in a single execution\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    // Step 1: Get Figma design\n    const design = await figma.figma_get_file({ fileId: \"abc123\" });\n\n    // Step 2: Create ClickUp task (design data available)\n    const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({\n      name: \\`Implement: \\${design.name}\\`,\n      listName: \"Development Sprint\",\n      description: \\`Design has \\${design.document.children.length} components\\`\n    });\n\n    // Step 3: Create GitHub issue (both design and task data available)\n    const issue = await github.github_create_issue({\n      owner: \"myorg\",\n      repo: \"myrepo\",\n      title: \\`Design: \\${design.name}\\`,\n      body: \\`ClickUp task: \\${task.url}\\`\n    });\n\n    return { design, task, issue };\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000  // Extended timeout for complex workflow\n});\n```\n\n### Timeout Calculation\n\nUse this formula to calculate the right timeout for your workflow:\n\n```\ntimeout = base_overhead + (num_tools × tool_avg) + safety_margin\n```\n\n**Default Values:**\n\n| Component       | Value    | Description                     |\n| --------------- | -------- | ------------------------------- |\n| `base_overhead` | 5,000ms  | Initial connection and setup    |\n| `tool_avg`      | 3,000ms  | Average per-tool execution time |\n| `safety_margin` | 10,000ms | Buffer for network variability  |\n\n**Calculation Examples:**\n\n| Tools         | Calculation                | Result   | Recommended   |\n| ------------- | -------------------------- | -------- | ------------- |\n| **1 tool**    | 5000 + (1 x 3000) + 10000  | 18,000ms | **30,000ms**  |\n| **2 tools**   | 5000 + (2 x 3000) + 10000  | 21,000ms | **30,000ms**  |\n| **4 tools**   | 5000 + (4 x 3000) + 10000  | 27,000ms | **60,000ms**  |\n| **6 tools**   | 5000 + (6 x 3000) + 10000  | 33,000ms | **60,000ms**  |\n| **10 tools**  | 5000 + (10 x 3000) + 10000 | 45,000ms | **120,000ms** |\n| **15+ tools** | 5000 + (15 x 3000) + 10000 | 60,000ms | **120,000ms** |\n\n**Quick Reference:**\n\n| Complexity             | Timeout  | Use Case                    |\n| ---------------------- | -------- | --------------------------- |\n| **Simple** (1-2 tools) | `30000`  | List sites, get single item |\n| **Medium** (3-5 tools) | `60000`  | Create task + update CMS    |\n| **Complex** (6+ tools) | `120000` | Full design-to-dev pipeline |\n\n**Usage:**\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    // Complex multi-tool workflow\n    const sites = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n    const collections = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: sites.sites[0].id });\n    const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: \"Review collections\" });\n    return { sites, collections, task };\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000  // 3 tools, use 60000ms\n});\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_5_complete`\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Naming pattern applied correctly\n- [ ] Timeout calculated for workflow complexity\n- [ ] Tool discovery working via search_tools\n- [ ] At least one call_tool_chain execution succeeded\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 7. FEATURES\n\n### 7.1 call_tool_chain\n\n**Purpose**: Execute TypeScript code with direct access to all configured MCP tools.\n\n**Parameters**:\n- `code` (string, required): TypeScript code to execute\n- `timeout` (number, optional): Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000)\n- `max_output_size` (number, optional): Max output characters (default: 200000)\n\n**Example**:\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    const sites = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n    return sites;\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000\n});\n```\n\n**Returns**: `{ result: any, logs: string[] }`\n\n### 7.2 search_tools\n\n**Purpose**: Progressive discovery. Search for tools by task description.\n\n**Parameters**:\n- `task_description` (string, required): Natural language description of task\n- `limit` (number, optional): Maximum results to return (default: 10)\n\n**Example**:\n```typescript\nsearch_tools({\n  task_description: \"create tasks in ClickUp\",\n  limit: 5\n});\n```\n\n**Returns**: Array of tool names with descriptions (minimal tokens)\n\n### 7.3 list_tools\n\n**Purpose**: List all available tools from all configured MCP servers.\n\n**Parameters**: None\n\n**Example**:\n```typescript\nlist_tools();\n```\n\n**Returns**: `{ tools: string[] }` containing all available tool names\n\n### 7.4 tool_info\n\n**Purpose**: Get complete TypeScript interface for a specific tool.\n\n**Parameters**:\n- `tool_name` (string, required): Full tool name (e.g., \"myservice.myservice_sites_list\")\n\n**Example**:\n```typescript\ntool_info({\n  tool_name: \"clickup.clickup_create_task\"\n});\n```\n\n**Returns**: Full TypeScript interface definition with parameter types\n\n---\n\n## 8. EXAMPLES\n\n### Example 1: MyService Site Management\n\n**Scenario**: List all sites and their collections\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    // Get all sites\n    const sitesResult = await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n    const sites = sitesResult.sites;\n\n    console.log(\\`Found \\${sites.length} sites\\`);\n\n    // Get collections for first site\n    if (sites.length > 0) {\n      const collections = await myservice.myservice_collections_list({\n        site_id: sites[0].id\n      });\n\n      console.log(\\`Site \"\\${sites[0].displayName}\" has \\${collections.collections.length} collections\\`);\n    }\n\n    return { sites, collections };\n  `,\n  timeout: 30000\n});\n```\n\n### Example 2: GitHub Issue Creation\n\n**Scenario**: Create an issue with labels\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    // Create GitHub issue\n    const issue = await github.github_create_issue({\n      owner: \"myorg\",\n      repo: \"myrepo\",\n      title: \"Implement User Authentication\",\n      body: \"## Description\\\\n\\\\nImplement OAuth2 login flow\",\n      labels: [\"feature\", \"auth\"]\n    });\n\n    console.log(\\`Created issue: #\\${issue.number}\\`);\n    console.log(\\`URL: \\${issue.html_url}\\`);\n\n    return issue;\n  `,\n  timeout: 30000\n});\n```\n\n### Example 3: ClickUp Task Creation\n\n**Scenario**: Create a task with tags\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    // Create main task\n    const task = await clickup.clickup_create_task({\n      name: \"Implement User Authentication\",\n      listName: \"Development Sprint\",\n      description: \"Implement OAuth2 login flow\",\n      tags: [\"feature\", \"auth\"],\n      priority: 2\n    });\n\n    console.log(\\`Created task: \\${task.name} (ID: \\${task.id})\\`);\n    console.log(\\`URL: \\${task.url}\\`);\n\n    return task;\n  `,\n  timeout: 30000\n});\n```\n\n### Example 4: Multi-Tool Workflow\n\n**Scenario**: Figma to GitHub to MyService pipeline\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    try {\n      // Step 1: Get Figma design\n      console.log('Fetching Figma design...');\n      const design = await figma.figma_get_file({\n        fileId: \"YOUR_FIGMA_FILE_ID\"\n      });\n      console.log(\\`Design: \\${design.name}\\`);\n\n      // Step 2: Create GitHub issue\n      console.log('Creating GitHub issue...');\n      const issue = await github.github_create_issue({\n        owner: \"myorg\",\n        repo: \"myrepo\",\n        title: \\`Implement: \\${design.name}\\`,\n        body: \\`Components: \\${design.document.children.length}\\`\n      });\n      console.log(\\`Issue created: \\${issue.html_url}\\`);\n\n      // Step 3: Add to external CMS queue\n      console.log('Adding to external CMS...');\n      const cmsItem = await myservice.myservice_collections_items_create_item_live({\n        collection_id: \"YOUR_COLLECTION_ID\",\n        request: {\n          items: [{\n            fieldData: {\n              name: design.name,\n              \"github-url\": issue.html_url,\n              status: \"Queued\",\n              \"created-at\": new Date().toISOString()\n            }\n          }]\n        }\n      });\n\n      console.log('Pipeline complete!');\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        design: design.name,\n        issueUrl: issue.html_url,\n        cmsItemId: cmsItem.id\n      };\n\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error('Pipeline failed:', error.message);\n      return { success: false, error: error.message };\n    }\n  `,\n  timeout: 120000\n});\n```\n\n### Example 5: Chrome DevTools Automation\n\n**Scenario**: Take a screenshot and check console errors\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    // Create new browser page\n    const page = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_new_page({});\n    console.log(\\`Page created: \\${page.pageId}\\`);\n\n    // Navigate to URL\n    await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n      pageId: page.pageId,\n      url: \"https://example.com\"\n    });\n    console.log('Navigation complete');\n\n    // Wait for page load\n    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 3000));\n\n    // Take screenshot\n    const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({\n      pageId: page.pageId\n    });\n    console.log('Screenshot captured');\n\n    // Get console messages\n    const consoleMessages = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_get_console_message({\n      pageId: page.pageId\n    });\n\n    // Close page\n    await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_close_page({ pageId: page.pageId });\n\n    return {\n      screenshot: screenshot,\n      consoleErrors: consoleMessages.filter(m => m.level === 'error')\n    };\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000\n});\n```\n\n### Example 6: Error Handling Pattern\n\n**Scenario**: Resilient workflow with fallbacks\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    const results = {\n      successes: [],\n      failures: []\n    };\n\n    // Helper for safe execution\n    async function tryExecute(name, fn) {\n      try {\n        const result = await fn();\n        results.successes.push({ name, result });\n        return result;\n      } catch (error) {\n        results.failures.push({ name, error: error.message });\n        console.error(\\`\\${name} failed: \\${error.message}\\`);\n        return null;\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Execute operations with fallbacks\n    const sites = await tryExecute('list-sites',\n      () => myservice.myservice_sites_list({})\n    );\n\n    if (sites) {\n      await tryExecute('get-collections',\n        () => myservice.myservice_collections_list({ site_id: sites.sites[0].id })\n      );\n    }\n\n    console.log(\\`Complete: \\${results.successes.length} succeeded, \\${results.failures.length} failed\\`);\n    return results;\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000\n});\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING\n\n### Error Message Quick Reference\n\n| Error Message                                | Cause                        | Solution                                  |\n| -------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |\n| `Tool not found: myservice.sites_list`         | Missing second manual part   | Use `myservice.myservice_sites_list`          |\n| `Tool not found: myservice.myservice.sites_list` | Dot instead of underscore    | Use `myservice.myservice_sites_list`          |\n| `Execution timeout exceeded`                 | Complex operation            | Increase `timeout` parameter              |\n| `UTCP_CONFIG_FILE not set`                   | Missing environment variable | Set path to `.utcp_config.json`           |\n| `Environment variable X not found`           | Missing in .env              | Add variable to `.env` file               |\n| `Variable 'manual_VAR' not found`            | Missing prefixed variable    | Add `{manual}_{VAR}` to `.env`            |\n| `TypeError: X is not a function`             | Wrong naming pattern         | Check exact tool name with `search_tools` |\n| `Failed to start MCP server`                 | Package or auth issue        | Test command manually in terminal         |\n| `Invalid JSON`                               | Config syntax error          | Validate with `python3 -m json.tool`      |\n\n### Prefixed Variable Not Found\n\n**Problem**: `Error during batch registration for manual 'X': Variable 'X_VAR' not found`\n\n**Cause**: Code Mode requires environment variables prefixed with the manual name.\n\n**Solution**:\n1. Identify the manual name from the error (e.g., `figma`, `clickup`, `github`)\n2. Add the prefixed variable to `.env`:\n   ```bash\n   # For error: Variable 'figma_FIGMA_API_KEY' not found\n   figma_FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_your-figma-key\n\n   # For error: Variable 'github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN' not found\n   github_GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_your-github-token\n   ```\n3. Restart OpenCode/Code Mode after updating `.env`\n\nSee the [Prefixed Environment Variables](#critical-prefixed-environment-variables) section for the complete list.\n\n### Tool Not Found Error\n\n**Problem**: `Error: Tool not found: myservice.sites_list`\n\n**Cause**: Missing the second manual/server part in the tool name\n\n**Solution**:\n1. Use correct naming pattern: `{manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}`\n   ```typescript\n   // Wrong: missing second part\n   await myservice.sites_list({});\n\n   // Wrong: dot instead of underscore\n   await myservice.myservice.sites_list({});\n\n   // Correct\n   await myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n   ```\n\n2. Use tool discovery to find exact names:\n   ```typescript\n   const tools = await search_tools({\n     task_description: \"myservice sites\",\n     limit: 10\n   });\n   console.log(tools.map(t => t.name));\n   ```\n\n### Environment Variables Not Loading\n\n**Problem**: `Error: Environment variable CLICKUP_API_KEY not found`\n\n**Solutions**:\n1. Check `.env` file exists in same directory as `.utcp_config.json`\n\n2. Verify the variable is defined (not commented out):\n   ```bash\n   cat .env | grep CLICKUP_API_KEY\n   # Should show: CLICKUP_API_KEY=pk_...\n   ```\n\n3. Check `load_variables_from` config is correct:\n   ```json\n   \"load_variables_from\": [\n     {\n       \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\",\n       \"env_file_path\": \".env\"\n     }\n   ]\n   ```\n\n4. Restart your AI client after changing `.env`\n\n### MCP Server Fails to Start\n\n**Problem**: `Error: Failed to start MCP server: myservice`\n\n**Solutions**:\n1. Test the command manually:\n   ```bash\n   npx mcp-remote https://mcp.myservice.com/sse\n   ```\n\n2. Check npm/npx is in PATH:\n   ```bash\n   which npx\n   ```\n\n3. Install the package directly if the cached version fails:\n   ```bash\n   npm install -g @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\n   ```\n\n4. Check for missing dependencies or auth issues\n\n### Tools Not Appearing\n\n**Problem**: `search_tools` returns empty results\n\n**Solutions**:\n1. Verify MCP servers are configured in `.utcp_config.json`\n\n2. Check manual names do not have invalid characters:\n   ```json\n   // Good\n   \"name\": \"myservice\"\n   \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\"\n\n   // Bad (hyphens not allowed)\n   \"name\": \"myservice-api\"\n   \"name\": \"my server\"\n   ```\n\n3. Restart AI client after configuration changes\n\n4. Check for JSON syntax errors:\n   ```bash\n   python3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n   ```\n\n### Timeout Errors\n\n**Problem**: `Error: Execution timeout exceeded`\n\n**Solutions**:\n1. Increase timeout using the calculation formula:\n   ```typescript\n   call_tool_chain({\n     code: `...`,\n     timeout: 120000  // 2 minutes for complex workflows\n   });\n   ```\n\n2. Break large operations into smaller chunks\n\n3. Use parallel execution where possible:\n   ```typescript\n   const [result1, result2] = await Promise.all([\n     tool1.tool1_action(),\n     tool2.tool2_action()\n   ]);\n   ```\n\n### TypeError: Not a Function\n\n**Problem**: `TypeError: myservice.myservice is not a function`\n\n**Cause**: Trying to call the server object as a function\n\n**Solution**: Add the tool name after the second part:\n```typescript\n// Wrong: missing tool name\nawait myservice.myservice();\n\n// Correct: include tool name\nawait myservice.myservice_sites_list({});\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. RESOURCES\n\n### Documentation\n\n- **MCP Protocol**: https://modelcontextprotocol.io\n- **MCP Servers List**: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers\n\n### MCP Server Packages\n\n| Server              | Package                                  | Tools | Authentication        |\n| ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----- | --------------------- |\n| **GitHub**          | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`    | 26    | Personal Access Token |\n| **MyService**         | `mcp-remote https://mcp.myservice.com/sse` | 42    | OAuth (dashboard)     |\n| **ClickUp**         | `@taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server`         | 21    | API Key + Team ID     |\n| **Figma**           | `figma-developer-mcp`                    | 18    | Personal Access Token |\n| **Chrome DevTools** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`             | 26    | None                  |\n\n### Configuration Paths\n\n| Client              | Configuration File |\n| ------------------- | ------------------ |\n| **Claude Code**     | `.mcp.json`        |\n| **OpenCode**        | `opencode.json`    |\n| **VS Code Copilot** | `.vscode/mcp.json` |\n| **Windsurf**        | `.mcp.toml`        |\n\n### Helper Commands\n\n```bash\n# Validate JSON configuration\npython3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n\n# Check environment variables\ncat .env | grep -v \"^#\" | grep -v \"^$\"\n\n# Test npx command manually\nnpx -y @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server\n\n# List manual names from config\ncat .utcp_config.json | grep '\"name\"'\n```\n\n### Performance Metrics\n\n| Metric              | Traditional MCP   | Code Mode        | Improvement     |\n| ------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | --------------- |\n| **Context tokens**  | 141k (47 tools)   | 1.6k (159 tools) | 98.7% reduction |\n| **Execution time**  | ~2000ms (4 tools) | ~300ms (4 tools) | 85% faster      |\n| **API round trips** | 15+               | 1                | 93% reduction   |\n\n### Project Structure\n\n```\nyour-project/\n├── .utcp_config.json     # MCP server definitions\n├── .env                   # API keys (gitignored)\n├── .env.example           # Template for team\n├── .mcp.json              # Claude Code config (optional)\n├── opencode.json          # OpenCode config (optional)\n└── .vscode/\n    └── mcp.json           # VS Code config (optional)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference Card\n\n### Essential Commands\n\n```bash\n# Create configuration\ncat > .utcp_config.json << 'EOF'\n{\n  \"load_variables_from\": [\n    { \"variable_loader_type\": \"dotenv\", \"env_file_path\": \".env\" }\n  ],\n  \"tool_repository\": { \"tool_repository_type\": \"in_memory\" },\n  \"tool_search_strategy\": { \"tool_search_strategy_type\": \"tag_and_description_word_match\" },\n  \"manual_call_templates\": []\n}\nEOF\n\n# Validate JSON\npython3 -m json.tool < .utcp_config.json\n\n# Check environment variables\nenv | grep -E \"(CLICKUP|FIGMA|GITHUB)\"\n```\n\n### NAMING PATTERN\n\n```typescript\n// Pattern: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n//          ───┬───  ─────┬─────  ────┬────\n//             │          │           └── Tool from MCP server\n//             │          └── Server name + underscore + tool\n//             └── Manual name (from \"name\" field)\n\n// Examples:\nmyservice.myservice_sites_list({});\ngithub.github_get_issue({ owner: \"foo\", repo: \"bar\", issue_number: 123 });\nclickup.clickup_create_task({ name: \"My Task\", listName: \"Sprint\" });\nfigma.figma_get_file({ fileId: \"abc123\" });\nchrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_new_page({});\n```\n\n### Common Workflows\n\n**Tool Discovery:**\n```typescript\nconst tools = await search_tools({ task_description: \"myservice cms\", limit: 10 });\nconsole.log(tools.map(t => t.name));\n```\n\n**Single Tool Call:**\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `await myservice.myservice_sites_list({})`\n});\n```\n\n**Multi-Tool with Error Handling:**\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain({\n  code: `\n    try {\n      const a = await github.github_get_repo({ owner: \"x\", repo: \"y\" });\n      const b = await clickup.clickup_create_task({ name: a.name });\n      return { success: true, a, b };\n    } catch (error) {\n      return { success: false, error: error.message };\n    }\n  `,\n  timeout: 60000\n});\n```\n\n### Validation Checklists\n\n**Installation:**\n```\n[ ] Node.js 18+ installed\n[ ] .utcp_config.json created and valid\n[ ] .env created with required keys\n[ ] .env in .gitignore\n[ ] AI client configured\n[ ] Code Mode tools visible\n```\n\n**Before Each Session:**\n```\n[ ] Correct naming pattern: {manual_name}.{manual_name}_{tool_name}\n[ ] Timeout calculated for complexity\n[ ] Error handling in place\n[ ] API keys not expired\n```\n\n---\n\n## Version History\n\n| Version | Date       | Changes                                                                 |\n| ------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| v2.0.0  | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, standardized phase_N_complete validation naming, TOC moved after AI-First Install Guide, Quick Reference Card added to Resources |\n| v1.0.0  | 2025-01-01 | Initial release COMBINED STOP RULE:\n644:                a) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_ALLOWED\": decision = \"STOP\"\n645:                b) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_BLOCKED\":\n646:                     decision = \"BLOCKED\"\n647:                     stop_blocked = true\n648:                     stopReason = \"blockedStop\"\n649:                     # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the\n650:                     # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`\n651:                     # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full\n652:                     # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator inspection.\n653:                     blocked_by_json = JSON.stringify(graph_blockers_json.map(b => typeof b === \"string\" ? b : (b && b.type) || \"graph_blocker\"))\n654:                     blocked_by_csv = graph_blockers_csv\n655:                     graph_blocker_detail_json = graph_blockers_json\n656:                     recovery_strategy = \"Resolve review coverage-graph blockers before stopping.\"\n657:                c) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"CONTINUE\":\n658:                     decision = \"CONTINUE\"\n659:                     stop_blocked = false\n660:                     reason = \"graph_convergence_continue\"\n661:                d) else keep the existing STUCK_RECOVERY or CONTINUE outcome\n662:         outputs:\n663:           - decision: \"STOP | BLOCKED | STUCK_RECOVERY | CONTINUE\"\n664:           - reason: \"Human-readable stop reason\"\n665:           - stop_blocked: \"Boolean true when STOP is vetoed by inline legal-stop gates or graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED\"\n666:           - blocked_by_json: \"JSON array of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED\"\n667:           - blocked_by_csv: \"Comma-separated list of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED\"\n668:           - convergence_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for convergenceGate\"\n669:           - convergence_gate_score: \"Numeric score recorded for convergenceGate\"\n670:           - dimension_coverage_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for dimensionCoverageGate\"\n671:           - dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json: \"JSON array of covered review dimensions\"\n672:           - dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json: \"JSON array of missing review dimensions\"\n673:           - p0_resolution_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for p0ResolutionGate\"\n674:           - active_p0_count: \"Active P0 finding count at stop-check time\"\n675:           - evidence_density_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for evidenceDensityGate\"\n676:           - avg_evidence_per_finding: \"Numeric evidence density for active findings\"\n677:           - hotspot_saturation_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for hotspotSaturationGate\"\n678:           - claim_adjudication_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for claimAdjudicationGate (false vetoes STOP)\"\n679:           - claim_adjudication_active_count: \"Count of active P0/P1 findings evaluated by claimAdjudicationGate\"\n680:           - fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for fixCompletenessReplayGate (false vetoes STOP on security-sensitive fix reruns)\"\n681:           - security_sensitive_fix_scope: \"Boolean true when the review is a security-sensitive fix rerun\"\n682:           - fix_completeness_replay_required_rows: \"Count of closed-gate replay rows required before STOP\"\n683:           - fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows: \"Count of required closed-gate replay rows that passed with evidence\"\n684:           - candidate_coverage_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for candidateCoverageGate (false vetoes STOP for unresolved reducer-owned search debt or uncovered required bug classes)\"\n685:           - candidate_coverage_search_debt_json: \"JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations used as blocked-stop evidence\"\n686:           - candidate_coverage_missing_json: \"JSON array of required bug classes missing from candidateCoverage.covered\"\n687:           - graphless_fallback_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for graphlessFallbackGate (false vetoes STOP when graphless fallback evidence is absent or unavailable_blocked)\"\n688:           - graphless_fallback_mode: \"Latest reducer-owned searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode value\"\n689:           - graphless_fallback_missing_json: \"JSON array of required bug classes missing cited fallback ledger rows\"\n690:           - graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason: \"Explicit unavailability reason when graphCoverageMode == unavailable_blocked\"\n691:           - graph_decision: \"CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED from step_graph_convergence\"\n692:           - graph_signals_json: \"JSON object returned by graph convergence\"\n693:           - graph_blockers_json: \"JSON array of graph blockers\"\n694:           - graph_blockers_csv: \"Comma-separated graph blocker summary\"\n695:           - recovery_strategy: \"One-line hint describing the next review action when decision == BLOCKED or STUCK_RECOVERY\"\n696: \n697:       step_emit_blocked_stop:\n698:         action: \"Persist blocked-stop evidence whenever the review legal-stop decision tree vetoes STOP\"\n699:         if_blocked:\n700:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"blocked_stop\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"run\":{current_iteration},\"blockedBy\":{blocked_by_json},\"gateResults\":{\"convergenceGate\":{\"pass\":{convergence_gate_pass},\"score\":{convergence_gate_score}},\"dimensionCoverageGate\":{\"pass\":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},\"covered\":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},\"missing\":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},\"p0ResolutionGate\":{\"pass\":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},\"activeP0\":{active_p0_count}},\"evidenceDensityGate\":{\"pass\":{evidence_density_gate_pass},\"avgEvidencePerFinding\":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},\"hotspotSaturationGate\":{\"pass\":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},\"claimAdjudicationGate\":{\"pass\":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},\"activeP0P1\":{claim_adjudication_active_count}},\"fixCompletenessReplayGate\":{\"pass\":{fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass},\"securitySensitive\":{security_sensitive_fix_scope},\"requiredRows\":{fix_completeness_replay_required_rows},\"passingRows\":{fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows}},\"candidateCoverageGate\":{\"pass\":{candidate_coverage_gate_pass},\"searchDebt\":{candidate_coverage_search_debt_json},\"missing\":{candidate_coverage_missing_json}},\"graphlessFallbackGate\":{\"pass\":{graphless_fallback_gate_pass},\"mode\":\"{graphless_fallback_mode}\",\"missing\":{graphless_fallback_missing_json},\"unavailabilityReason\":\"{graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason}\"}},\"graphBlockerDetail\":{graph_blockers_json},\"recoveryStrategy\":\"{recovery_strategy}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n701:           log: \"Blocked STOP persisted for review loop. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}\"\n702:         if_not_blocked:\n703:           proceed: true\n704: \n705:       step_handle_convergence:\n706:         action: \"Act on convergence decision\"\n707:         if_stop:\n708:           branch_on: \"convergence_mode == 'divergent' AND reason == 'all_dimensions_clean'\"\n709:           if_divergent_eligible:\n710:             eligibility_contract:\n711:               required_decision: \"STOP\"\n712:               eligible_reasons: [all_dimensions_clean]\n713:               excluded_reasons: [maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused]\n714:               fail_closed: \"Any missing or ambiguous reason uses if_existing_terminal_path without preparing a pivot. The nine legal-stop gates have already run and remain unchanged.\"\n715:             steps:\n716:               step_prepare_divergent_pivot:\n717:                 action: \"Generate state-grounded read-only review candidates and prepare or resume the pivot transaction\"\n718:                 command: |\n719:                   node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n720:                   import {\n721:                     buildReviewPivotPreparationInput,\n722:                     renderReviewPivotSeatPrompt,\n723:                   } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/scripts/divergent-review-pivot.ts';\n724:                   import {\n725:                     preparePivotTransaction,\n726:                   } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n727: \n728:                   const input = buildReviewPivotPreparationInput({\n729:                     artifactRoot: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n730:                     configPath: '{state_paths.config}',\n731:                     stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n732:                     strategyPath: '{state_paths.strategy}',\n733:                     registryPath: '{state_paths.findings_registry}',\n734:                     currentIteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n735:                     maxIterations: Number('{max_iterations}'),\n736:                     triggerReason: '{reason}',\n737:                   });\n738:                   const prepared = await preparePivotTransaction(input);\n739:                   const seatPrompts = prepared.status === 'pending'\n740:                     ? Object.fromEntries(prepared.missingSeats.map((seat) => [\n741:                         seat.id,\n742:                         renderReviewPivotSeatPrompt(seat, prepared.dispatchContext),\n743:                       ]))\n744:                     : {};\n745:                   process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify({\n746:                     ...prepared,\n747:                     seatPrompts,\n748:                     boundaryRejections: input.boundaryRejections,\n749:                   })}\\n`);\n750:                   EOF\n751:                 working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n752:                 outputs:\n753:                   - pivot_prepare_json: \"The complete parsed JSON result\"\n754:                   - pivot_prepare_status: \"status from the prepare result\"\n755:                   - pivot_id: \"pivotId from the prepare result\"\n756:                   - pivot_paths_json: \"paths from the prepare result as JSON\"\n757:                   - pivot_resumed: \"resumed from the prepare result\"\n758:                   - pivot_missing_seat_ids: \"missingSeats ids from a pending result\"\n759:                   - pivot_seat_001_json: \"The seat-001 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n760:                   - pivot_seat_002_json: \"The seat-002 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n761:                   - pivot_seat_003_json: \"The seat-003 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n762:                   - pivot_seat_001_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-001']\"\n763:                   - pivot_seat_002_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-002']\"\n764:                   - pivot_seat_003_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-003']\"\n765:                   - pivot_dispatch_context_json: \"dispatchContext from a pending result as JSON\"\n766:                 short_circuit: \"When status is completed or failed, skip all dispatch and record steps and bind this result as pivot_result.\"\n767: \n768:               step_dispatch_pivot_seat_001:\n769:                 action: \"Natively dispatch the analytical read-only review direction-selection seat\"\n770:                 skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-001'\"\n771:                 capture_timing: true\n772:                 if_native:\n773:                   dispatch:\n774:                     agent: deep-review\n775:                     model: opus\n776:                     context_source: \"pivot_seat_001_prompt\"\n777:                   wait_for_completion: true\n778:                 failure_contract: \"A native dispatch failure binds status=rejected, a {name,message} error, and the measured timestamps/duration, then proceeds to step_record_pivot_seat_001.\"\n779:                 outputs:\n780:                   - pivot_seat_001_dispatch_status: \"fulfilled or rejected\"\n781:                   - pivot_seat_001_output_json: \"Raw native return serialized as JSON when fulfilled\"\n782:                   - pivot_seat_001_error_json: \"{name,message} when rejected\"\n783:                   - pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso: \"Dispatch start timestamp\"\n784:                   - pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso: \"Dispatch completion timestamp\"\n785:                   - pivot_seat_001_duration_ms: \"Dispatch duration in milliseconds\"\n786: \n787:               step_record_pivot_seat_001:\n788:                 action: \"Durably record the seat-001 native dispatch outcome\"\n789:                 skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-001'\"\n790:                 command: |\n791:                   node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n792:                   import { recordPivotSeatResult } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n793:                   const status = '{pivot_seat_001_dispatch_status}';\n794:                   const outcome = status === 'fulfilled'\n795:                     ? { status, output: {pivot_seat_001_output_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_001_duration_ms}') }\n796:                     : { status: 'rejected', error: {pivot_seat_001_error_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_001_duration_ms}') };\n797:                   process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(recordPivotSeatResult({ paths: {pivot_paths_json}, pivotId: '{pivot_id}', seat: {pivot_seat_001_json}, outcome }))}\\n`);\n798:                   EOF\n799:                 working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n800: \n801:               step_dispatch_pivot_seat_002:\n802:                 action: \"Natively dispatch the critical read-only review <<'EOF'\n872:                   import { finalizePivotTransaction } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n873:  working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n876:                 outputs:\n877:                   - pivot_finalize_json: \"The complete parsed final result\"\n878: \n879:               step_resolve_divergent_pivot_result:\n880:                 action: \"Use prepare's terminal result on resume/short-circuit, otherwise use finalize's result\"\n881:                 branch_on: \"pivot_prepare_status\"\n882:                 if_pending: { bind: { pivot_result: \"{pivot_finalize_json}\" } }\n883:                 if_completed_or_failed: { bind: { pivot_result: \"{pivot_prepare_json}\" } }\n884: \n885:               step_apply_divergent_pivot_result:\n886:                 action: \"Adopt a successful read-only review direction or fail closed to the existing synthesis path\"\n887:                 branch_on: \"pivot_result.status\"\n888:                 if_completed:\n889:                   set:\n890:                     next_dimension: \"{pivot_result.restoredFocus}\"\n891:                     pivot_lineage: \"Pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} selected {pivot_result.selectedCandidate.id} from source iteration {current_iteration}.\"\n892:                     saturated_directions: \"{pivot_result.completedEvent.saturatedDirections}\"\n893:                   command: \"node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}\"\n894:                   log: \"Divergent review pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} restored focus to {pivot_result.restoredFocus}; continuing iteration {current_iteration}.\"\n895:                   proceed_to: step_normalize_pause_events\n896:                 if_failed:\n897:                   log: \"Divergent review pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} failed closed ({pivot_result.failureReason}); proceeding to synthesis.\"\n898:                   skip_to: phase_synthesis\n899:             on_error:\n900:               log: \"Divergent review pivot integration error; failing closed to the existing synthesis path.\"\n901:               skip_to: phase_synthesis\n902:           if_existing_terminal_path:\n903:             log: \"Loop stopping. Reason: {reason}\"\n904:             skip_to: phase_synthesis\n905:         if_blocked:\n906:           log: \"Legal-stop gates blocked STOP. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Continuing review.\"\n907:         if_stuck_recovery:\n908:           log: \"Stuck detected ({stuck_count} consecutive no-progress). Entering recovery.\"\n909:           recovery_strategies:\n910:             - \"Change granularity: file-level → function-level → line-level review\"\n911:             - \"Traceability protocol replay: re-run unresolved core or overlay checks\"\n912:             - \"Escalate severity review: re-examine P2 findings for potential P1 upgrades\"\n913:           pivot_target: \"Pivot to the least-covered dimension (the review dimension with the fewest iteration passes and lowest coverage ratio).\"\n914:           set:\n915:             next_dimension: \"RECOVERY: {recovery_strategy}. Least-covered dimension: {least_explored}\"\n916:             pending_stop_event: \"stuck_recovery\"\n917:             pending_stop_reason: \"stuckRecovery\"\n918:             pending_stop_detail: \"Recovery path activated after repeated no-progress iterations\"\n919:           reset: { stuck_count: 0 }\n920:         if_continue:\n921:           log: \"Iteration {current_iteration}: Continuing. Dimension: {next_dimension}\"\n922: \n923:       step_normalize_pause_events:\n924:         action: \"Rewrite raw pause and recovery events to the frozen STOP_REASONS enum at emission time: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused\"\n925:         if_pending_pause_event:\n926:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"userPaused\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"stopReason\":\"userPaused\",\"reason\":\"{pending_stop_detail}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n927:           message: \"Review paused. Delete review/.deep-review-pause to resume.\"\n928:           reset:\n929:             pending_stop_event: null\n930:             pending_stop_reason: null\n931:             pending_stop_detail: null\n932:           halt: true\n933:         if_pending_stuck_recovery:\n934:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"stuckRecovery\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"stopReason\":\"stuckRecovery\",\"fromIteration\":{current_iteration},\"strategy\":\"{recovery_strategy}\",\"targetDimension\":\"{least_explored}\",\"outcome\":\"pending\",\"reason\":\"{pending_stop_detail}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n935:           reset:\n936:             pending_stop_event: null\n937:             pending_stop_reason: null\n938:             pending_stop_detail: null\n939:         if_no_pending_event:\n940:           proceed: true\n941: \n942:       step_generate_state_summary:\n943:         action: \"Generate compact state summary for dispatch context (~200 tokens)\"\n944:         generate: |\n945:           STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):\n946:           Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}\n947:           Dimension: {next_dimension}\n948:           Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}\n949:           Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})\n950:           Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}\n951:           {resource_map_prompt_line}\n952:           Coverage Age: {coverage_age}\n953:           Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}\n954:           Stuck count: {stuck_count}\n955:           Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\n956: \n957:       step_marker_scan:\n958:         action: \"H-2 loop-prevention guard: scan previous iteration's rendered prompt for nested review marker\"\n959:         algorithm: |\n960:           if current_iteration > 1:\n961:             PREV_PROMPT_PATH = \"{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{previous_iteration}.md\"\n962:             if file_exists(PREV_PROMPT_PATH):\n963:               PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE = head -1 of PREV_PROMPT_PATH\n964:               if PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE matches ^(DEEP-REVIEW|DEEP-RESEARCH|CODE-REVIEW)$:\n965:                 log \"ERROR: nested review-of-review loop detected (marker found in previous iteration prompt). Aborting.\"\n966:                 halt with exit code 2\n967:         note: |\n968:           False-positive prevention: only matches when the first line is exactly one of the\n969:           three canonical markers. Markers appearing in comments or body text are ignored.\n970:           True-positive triggers only when a previous iteration's rendered prompt literally\n971:           starts with a deep-loop marker, indicating a nested review-of-review dispatch.\n972: \n973:       step_dispatch_iteration:\n974:         action: \"Dispatch @deep-review agent for one review iteration\"\n975:         resolve_executor:\n976:           source: \"config.executor\"\n977:           loader: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig\"\n978:         pre_dispatch:\n979:           generate_summary: |\n980:             STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):\n981:             Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}\n982:             Dimension: {next_dimension}\n983:             Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}\n984:             Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})\n985:             Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}\n986:             {resource_map_prompt_line}\n987:             Coverage Age: {coverage_age}\n988:             Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}\n989:             Stuck count: {stuck_count}\n990:             Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\n991:           render_prompt_pack:\n992:             template_path: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl\"\n993:             renderer: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts#renderPromptPack\"\n994:             output_path: \"{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md\"\n995:             variables:\n996:               state_summary: \"{state_summary}\"\n997:               current_iteration: \"{current_iteration}\"\n998:               max_iterations: \"{max_iterations}\"\n999:               next_dimension: \"{next_dimension}\"\n1000:               review_target: \"{review_target}\"\n1001:               review_scope_files: \"{review_scope_files}\"\n1002:               p0_count: \"{p0_count}\"\n1003:               p1_count: \"{p1_count}\"\n1004:               p2_count: \"{p2_count}\"\n1005:               pivot_lineage: \"{pivot_lineage}\"\n1006:               saturated_directions: \"{saturated_directions}\"\n1007:               state_paths_config: \"{state_paths.config}\"\n1008:               state_paths_state_log: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1009:               state_paths_findings_registry: \"{state_paths.findings_registry}\"\n1010:               state_paths_strategy: \"{state_paths.strategy}\"\n1011:               state_paths_iteration_pattern: \"{state_paths.iteration_pattern}\"\n1012:               state_paths_delta_pattern: \"{state_paths.delta_pattern}\"\n1013:         pre_dispatch_audit:\n1014:           skip_when: \"config.executor.kind == 'native'\"\n1015:           call: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts#writeFirstRecordExecutor\"\n1016:           args:\n1017:             stateLogPath: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1018:             executor: \"{config.executor}\"\n1019:             iteration: \"{current_iteration}\"\n1020:         branch_on: \"config.executor.kind\"\n1021:         if_native:\n1022:           dispatch:\n1023:             agent: deep-review\n1024:             model: opus\n1025:             context_source: \"rendered_prompt_pack\"\n1026:           wait_for_completion: true\n1027:         if_cli_copilot:\n1028:           command: |\n1029:             node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n1030:             import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n1031:             import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';\n1032: \n1033:             import { runAuditedExecutorCommand } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts';\n1034:             import { buildCopilotPromptArg } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts';\n1035: \n1036:             const promptPath = '{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md';\n1037:             const prompt = readFileSync(promptPath, 'utf8');\n1038: \n1039:             // Workflow-resolved spec folder is the only legal write authority.\n1040:             // The helper (`validateSpecFolder` inside `buildCopilotPromptArg`)\n1041:             // is the single source of truth for rejecting empty / unresolved\n1042:             // template / whitespace-only / string-coerced empty values. The\n1043:             // YAML hands the raw `{spec_folder}` directly; on rejection the\n1044:             // helper safe-fails to `type:\"missing\", writeIntent:true` (Gate-3\n1045:             // enforcement, --allow-all-tools stripped).\n1046:             const specFolderRaw = '{spec_folder}';\n1047:             const targetAuthority = { type: 'approved', specFolder: specFolderRaw };\n1048: \n1049:             const baseArgs = [\n1050:               '-p',\n1051:               prompt,\n1052:               '--model',\n1053:               '{config.executor.model}',\n1054:               '--allow-all-tools',\n1055:               '--no-ask-user',\n1056:             ];\n1057: \n1058:             const built = buildCopilotPromptArg({\n1059:               promptPath,\n1060:               prompt,\n1061:               targetAuthority,\n1062:               baseArgv: baseArgs,\n1063:             });\n1064: \n1065:             // Large-prompt @PROMPT_PATH wrapper: helper emits a `promptFileBody`\n1066:             // containing `<TARGET AUTHORITY preamble>\\n\\n---\\n\\n<original>`.\n1067:             // Write it to disk so the file Copilot reads via @path opens with\n1068:             // the authority block. Without this, recovered/bootstrap folder\n1069:             // mentions inside the file could still anchor the model.\n1070:             if (built.promptFileBody !== undefined) {\n1071:               writeFileSync(promptPath, built.promptFileBody, 'utf8');\n1072:             }\n1073: \n1074:             // Copilot is routed through the audited executor wrapper so it produces\n1075:             // INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts alongside claude-code/opencode\n1076:             // There is no cli-copilot kind in the executor schema, so it\n1077:             // dispatches as kind:'native' (full env passthrough, no recursion-guard\n1078:             // layer configured for copilot); the receipt still records the real\n1079:             // 'copilot' command + built.argv as facts.\n1080:             const dispatchFacts = {\n1081:               command: 'deep/review',\n1082:               mode: 'review',\n1083:               specFolder: '{spec_folder}',\n1084:               artifactDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n1085:               iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n1086:               executorKind: '{config.executor.kind}',\n1087:               branchId: '{config.executor.kind}',\n1088:               sessionId: '{session_id}',\n1089:               generation: Number('{generation}'),\n1090:             };\n1091:             const dispatchId = 'review-i{current_iteration}-g{generation}';\n1092:             process.exit(\n1093:               runAuditedExecutorCommand({\n1094:                 command: 'copilot',\n1095:                 args: built.argv,\n1096:                 cwd: '{repo_root}',\n1097:                 timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n1098:                 stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n1099:                 executor: {\n1100:                   kind: 'native',\n1101:                   model: '{config.executor.model}',\n1102:                   configDir: null,\n1103:                   reasoningEffort: null,\n1104:                   serviceTier: null,\n1105:                   sandboxMode: null,\n1106:                   timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n1107:                   governor: null,\n1108:                 },\n1109:                 iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n1110:                 receiptDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}/dispatch-receipts',\n1111:                 dispatchId,\n1112:               }),\n1113:             );\n1114:             EOF\n1115:           working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n1116:           timeout_seconds: \"{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}\"\n1117:           wait_for_completion: true\n1118:           notes:\n1119:             - \"Routed through runAuditedExecutorCommand so copilot produces INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts; built.argv (@PROMPT_PATH or positional) is forwarded verbatim as the receipt facts\"\n1120:             - \"copilot has no stdin support; prompt is positional\"\n1121:             - \"Size check uses 16KB threshold; above that uses @PROMPT_PATH wrapper prompt\"\n1122:             - \"Large-prompt path relies on copilot honoring @path references within its prompt string\"\n1123:             - \"buildCopilotPromptArg attaches a TARGET AUTHORITY preamble bound to the workflow-approved spec folder; recovered context cannot override it (authority guard source: .opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml)\"\n1124:             - \"If spec_folder is missing/malformed/unresolved on a write-intent dispatch, the helper enforces plan-only by replacing the prompt with a Gate-3 question and stripping --allow-all-tools\"\n1125:             - \"Large-prompt wrapper mode rewrites the prompt file at promptPath to include the TARGET AUTHORITY preamble (prefix + divider + original body); the argv becomes a bare @promptPath reference\"\n1126:         if_cli_claude_code:\n1127:           command: |\n1128:             node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n1129:             import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n1130:             import { createHash } from const executor = {\n1137:               kind: 'cli-claude-code',\n1138:               model: '{config.executor.model}',\n1139:               configDir: configDir || null,\n1140: admits it.\n1162:             const parentEnv = { ...process.env };\n1163:             if (configDir) {\n1164:               parentEnv.CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR = configDir;\n1165:             }\n1166: dispatchFacts = {\n1246:               command: 'deep/review',\n1247:               mode: 'review',\n1248:               specFolder: '{spec_folder}',\n1249:               artifactDir: executor,\n1265:                 iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n1266:                 // opencode v1.14.39 reads stdin at startup before session creation;\n1267:                 // an empty input gives EOF stdin, equivalent to </dev/null.\n1268:                 input: '',\n1269:                 receiptDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}/dispatch-receipts',\n1270: v1.14.39 reads stdin at startup and hangs at 0% CPU after +60s snapshot prune without it (the `cli-external/cli-opencode` mode's SKILL.md (`.opencode/skills/cli-external/cli-opencode/SKILL.md`) §4 ALWAYS rule)\"\n1280:             - \"opencode run takes a positional message; prompt is the route-proof preamble + iteration body\"\n1281:             - \"--variant maps reasoningEffort to opencode's provider-specific reasoning lever (e.g., high, medium, minimal)\"\n1282:             - \"--dangerously-skip-permissions enables hands-off automation; sandboxMode='read-only' is not currently honored (no opencode equivalent)\"\n1283:             - \"cli-opencode self-invocation guard: this single-executor branch must only run from a non-OpenCode dispatch surface (Claude Code/raw shell). Explicit parallel-detached cli-opencode fan-out is handled by step_fanout_spawn_cli/fanout-run.cjs, not this branch. the `cli-external/cli-opencode` mode's SKILL.md (`.opencode/skills/cli-external/cli-opencode/SKILL.md`) §SELF-INVOCATION PROHIBITED contract is the authoritative gate\"\n1284:         post_dispatch_validate:\n1285:           validator: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/post-dispatch-validate.ts#validateIterationOutputs\"\n1286:           command: \"node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/verify-iteration.cjs --loop-type review --artifact-dir {artifact_dir} --iteration {current_iteration}\"\n1287:           command_note: \"Mechanical leaf-reliability gate: exit 0 = complete; non-zero prints one machine reason (iteration_file_missing|iteration_verdict_missing|state_record_missing|route_proof_missing|route_proof_mismatch|delta_file_missing). The script enforces the artifact + route-proof contract instead of trusting the leaf to self-report, and a non-zero exit triggers on_failure.redispatch_once.\"\n1288:           assert_exists: \"{state_paths.iteration_pattern}\"\n1289:           assert_appended: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1290:           assert_jsonl_fields: [type, iteration, mode, run, status, focus, dimensions, filesReviewed, findingsCount, findingsSummary, findingsNew, findingDetails, newFindingsRatio, sessionId, generation, lineageMode, timestamp, durationMs]\n1291:           route_proof:\n1292:             mode: review\n1293:             target_agent: deep-review\n1294:             agent_definition_loaded: true\n1295:             resolved_route: \"Resolved route: mode=review target_agent=deep-review\"\n1296:           dispatchReceipt:\n1297:             receiptDir: \"{state_paths.packet_dir}/dispatch-receipts\"\n1298:             dispatchId: \"review-i{current_iteration}-g{generation}\"\n1299:           assert_canonical_type: \"iteration\"\n1300:           assert_delta_file_exists: \"{state_paths.delta_pattern}\"\n1301:           assert_delta_file_has_iteration_record: true\n1302:           failure_reasons:\n1303:             - iteration_file_missing\n1304:             - iteration_file_empty\n1305:             - jsonl_not_appended\n1306:             - jsonl_missing_fields\n1307:             - jsonl_parse_error\n1308:             - jsonl_wrong_type\n1309:             - delta_file_missing\n1310:             - delta_file_empty\n1311:             - delta_file_missing_iteration_record\n1312:             - executor_missing\n1313:             - dispatch_failure_logged\n1314:             - v2_missing_ledger\n1315:             - v2_uncited_ledger_row\n1316:             - v2_broken_linked_finding\n1317:             - v2_shallow_finding_details\n1318:             - delta_iteration_id_mismatch\n1319:             - state_delta_iteration_mismatch\n1320:             - route_proof_missing\n1321:             - route_proof_mismatch\n1322:           warning_surface:\n1323:             source: \"result.warnings\"\n1324:             action: \"emit schema_advisory canonical conflict event\"\n1325:             severity: \"advisory\"\n1326:             event_shape: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"schema_advisory\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"iteration\":{current_iteration},\"warnings\":{result.warnings},\"severity\":\"advisory\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\"}'\n1327:             note: \"Warnings do not fail dispatch. Hard failures still emit schema_mismatch through on_failure.\"\n1328:           on_failure:\n1329:             action: \"emit schema_mismatch canonical conflict event\"\n1330:             redispatch_once: \"On the FIRST failure of the mechanical command check for this iteration, re-run step_dispatch_iteration exactly once for the same iteration number, then re-run the check. If it still fails, record the iteration as error (per step_evaluate_results.on_missing_outputs) and continue. Never re-dispatch a given iteration more than once.\"\n1331:             escalation: \"After 3 consecutive failures across iterations, trigger stuck_recovery event with diagnostic context\"\n1332:         record_executor_audit:\n1333:           skip_when: \"config.executor.kind == 'native'\"\n1334:           target: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1335:           field: \"executor\"\n1336:           value:\n1337:             kind: \"{config.executor.kind}\"\n1338:             model: \"{config.executor.model}\"\n1339:             reasoningEffort: \"{config.executor.reasoningEffort}\"\n1340:             serviceTier: \"{config.executor.serviceTier}\"\n1341: \n1342:       step_evaluate_results:\n1343:         action: \"Verify iteration outputs and extract metrics\"\n1344:         verify:\n1345:           - file_exists: \"{state_paths.iteration_pattern}\"\n1346:           - jsonl_appended: \"New iteration record in state.jsonl\"\n1347:           - strategy_updated: \"Strategy.md has been modified\"\n1348:         extract:\n1349:           - newFindingsRatio: \"From latest JSONL iteration record\"\n1350:           - findings_count: \"From latest JSONL iteration record\"\n1351:           - severity_counts: \"From latest JSONL iteration record (P0, P1, P2)\"\n1352:           - dimensions: \"From latest JSONL iteration record\"\n1353:           - files_reviewed: \"From latest JSONL iteration record\"\n1354:           - status: \"From latest JSONL iteration record\"\n1355:           - reviewed_artifacts: \"From latest JSONL iteration record\"\n1356:           - traceability_checks: \"From latest JSONL iteration record when present\"\n1357:         on_missing_outputs:\n1358:           action: \"Mark iteration as 'error', continue to next\"\n1359:           # Canonical iteration record: include sessionId, generation, durationMs so the reducer can persist\n1360:           # the failure without dropping the iteration. Schema source: state_format.md §Iteration Records.\n1361:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"iteration\",\"iteration\":{current_iteration},\"run\":{current_iteration},\"mode\":\"review\",\"status\":\"error\",\"focus\":\"{next_dimension}\",\"dimensions\":[\"{next_dimension}\"],\"filesReviewed\":[],\"findingsCount\":0,\"findingsSummary\":{\"P0\":0,\"P1\":0,\"P2\":0},\"findingsNew\":{\"P0\":0,\"P1\":0,\"P2\":0},\"findingDetails\":[],\"traceabilityChecks\":{\"summary\":{\"required\":0,\"executed\":0,\"pass\":0,\"partial\":0,\"fail\":0,\"blocked\":0,\"notApplicable\":0,\"gatingFailures\":0},\"results\":[]},\"newFindingsRatio\":0.0,\"durationMs\":0,\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{config.lineage.sessionId}\",\"generation\":{config.lineage.generation},\"lineageMode\":\"{config.lineage.lineageMode}\"}'\n1362: \n1363:       step_post_iteration_claim_adjudication:\n1364:         action: \"Validate typed claim-adjudication packets for any new P0/P1 findings and persist the result to state.jsonl so the next iteration's claimAdjudicationGate can read it\"\n1365:         verify:\n1366:           - typed_packets_present: \"Every new P0/P1 finding in review/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md contains a claim-adjudication JSON block\"\n1367:           - packet_fields: \"Each packet includes claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, downgradeTrigger\"\n1368:           - missing_packet_ids: \"List of new P0/P1 finding IDs that are missing a typed packet or field; empty array when all packets validate\"\n1369:         on_pass:\n1370:           set:\n1371:             claim_adjudication_passed: true\n1372:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"claim_adjudication\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"run\":{current_iteration},\"passed\":true,\"activeP0P1\":{active_p0_p1_count},\"missingPackets\":[],\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n1373:           log: \"Claim adjudication passed for iteration {current_iteration}. All new P0/P1 findings have typed packets.\"\n1374:         on_fail:\n1375:           action: \"Mark the iteration as invalid for convergence. The next iteration's claimAdjudicationGate (step_check_convergence step 0) reads the persisted `passed: false` event and vetoes STOP until a follow-up iteration rewrites the packets.\"\n1376:           set:\n1377:             claim_adjudication_passed: false\n1378:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"claim_adjudication\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"run\":{current_iteration},\"passed\":false,\"activeP0P1\":{active_p0_p1_count},\"missingPackets\":{missing_packet_ids},\"reason\":\"Missing typed packet fields for new P0/P1 findings\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n1379:           log: \"Claim adjudication incomplete for new P0/P1 findings. Event persisted. The next iteration's step_check_convergence claim-adjudication pre-check will veto STOP while `passed: false` remains the latest event and active P0/P1 > 0.\"\n1380: \n1381:       step_reduce_review_state:\n1382:         action: \"Run reducer to refresh the canonical findings registry before dashboard generation\"\n1383:         command: \"node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}\"\n1384:         read:\n1385:           - \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1386:           - \"{state_paths.iteration_pattern}\"\n1387:           - \"{state_paths.findings_registry}\"\n1388:           - \"{state_paths.strategy}\"\n1389:         write:\n1390:           - \"{state_paths.findings_registry}\"\n1391:           - \"{state_paths.dashboard}\"\n1392:           - \"{state_paths.strategy}\"\n1393:         failure_modes:\n1394:           malformed_delta: \"skip + warn\"\n1395:           missing_iteration_file: \"no-op + error\"\n1396:           schema_mismatch: \"reject + conflict event\"\n1397:         guarantee: \"Idempotent for identical inputs\"\n1398:         note: \"Reducer outputs findingsRegistry, dashboardMetrics, and strategyUpdates. Re-running the same inputs must be idempotent.\"\n1399: \n1400:       step_graph_upsert:\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 634-1400.
