Use offset=1401 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"               fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows = numeric count of required closed-gate replay rows marked PASS with evidence\n               candidate_coverage_gate_pass = boolean\n               candidate_coverage_search_debt_json = JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations blocking STOP, else []\n               candidate_coverage_missing_json = JSON array of required bug classes not present in candidateCoverage.covered, else []\n               graphless_fallback_gate_pass = boolean\n               graphless_fallback_mode = latest reducer-owned searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode value\n               graphless_fallback_missing_json = JSON array of required bug classes without cited fallback ledger rows, else []\n               graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason = explicit reason when graphCoverageMode == \"unavailable_blocked\", else empty string\n               recovery_strategy = one-line hint describing the next review action needed to satisfy the failed gates; combine candidateCoverageGate and graphlessFallbackGate recovery guidance when both fail (when claimAdjudicationGate is the failing gate, prefer the step-0 recovery_strategy already set)\n          6. COMBINED STOP RULE:\n               a) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_ALLOWED\": decision = \"STOP\"\n               b) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_BLOCKED\":\n                    decision = \"BLOCKED\"\n                    stop_blocked = true\n                    stopReason = \"blockedStop\"\n                    # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the\n                    # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`\n                    # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full\n                    # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator inspection.\n                    blocked_by_json = JSON.stringify(graph_blockers_json.map(b => typeof b === \"string\" ? b : (b && b.type) || \"graph_blocker\"))","truncated":true,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_auto.yaml","text":"               fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows = numeric count of required closed-gate replay rows marked PASS with evidence\n               candidate_coverage_gate_pass = boolean\n               candidate_coverage_search_debt_json = JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations blocking STOP, else []\n               candidate_coverage_missing_json = JSON array of required bug classes not present in candidateCoverage.covered, else []\n               graphless_fallback_gate_pass = boolean\n               graphless_fallback_mode = latest reducer-owned searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode value\n               graphless_fallback_missing_json = JSON array of required bug classes without cited fallback ledger rows, else []\n               graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason = explicit reason when graphCoverageMode == \"unavailable_blocked\", else empty string\n               recovery_strategy = one-line hint describing the next review action needed to satisfy the failed gates; combine candidateCoverageGate and graphlessFallbackGate recovery guidance when both fail (when claimAdjudicationGate is the failing gate, prefer the step-0 recovery_strategy already set)\n          6. COMBINED STOP RULE:\n               a) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_ALLOWED\": decision = \"STOP\"\n               b) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_BLOCKED\":\n                    decision = \"BLOCKED\"\n                    stop_blocked = true\n                    stopReason = \"blockedStop\"\n                    # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the\n                    # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`\n                    # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full\n                    # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator inspection.\n                    blocked_by_json = JSON.stringify(graph_blockers_json.map(b => typeof b === \"string\" ? b : (b && b.type) || \"graph_blocker\"))\n                    blocked_by_csv = graph_blockers_csv\n                    graph_blocker_detail_json = graph_blockers_json\n                    recovery_strategy = \"Resolve review coverage-graph blockers before stopping.\"\n               c) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"CONTINUE\":\n                    decision = \"CONTINUE\"\n                    stop_blocked = false\n                    reason = \"graph_convergence_continue\"\n               d) else keep the existing STUCK_RECOVERY or CONTINUE outcome\n        outputs:\n          - decision: \"STOP | BLOCKED | STUCK_RECOVERY | CONTINUE\"\n          - reason: \"Human-readable stop reason\"\n          - stop_blocked: \"Boolean true when STOP is vetoed by inline legal-stop gates or graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED\"\n          - blocked_by_json: \"JSON array of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED\"\n          - blocked_by_csv: \"Comma-separated list of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED\"\n          - convergence_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for convergenceGate\"\n          - convergence_gate_score: \"Numeric score recorded for convergenceGate\"\n          - dimension_coverage_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for dimensionCoverageGate\"\n          - dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json: \"JSON array of covered review dimensions\"\n          - dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json: \"JSON array of missing review dimensions\"\n          - p0_resolution_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for p0ResolutionGate\"\n          - active_p0_count: \"Active P0 finding count at stop-check time\"\n          - evidence_density_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for evidenceDensityGate\"\n          - avg_evidence_per_finding: \"Numeric evidence density for active findings\"\n          - hotspot_saturation_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for hotspotSaturationGate\"\n          - claim_adjudication_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for claimAdjudicationGate (false vetoes STOP)\"\n          - claim_adjudication_active_count: \"Count of active P0/P1 findings evaluated by claimAdjudicationGate\"\n          - fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for fixCompletenessReplayGate (false vetoes STOP on security-sensitive fix reruns)\"\n          - security_sensitive_fix_scope: \"Boolean true when the review is a security-sensitive fix rerun\"\n          - fix_completeness_replay_required_rows: \"Count of closed-gate replay rows required before STOP\"\n          - fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows: \"Count of required closed-gate replay rows that passed with evidence\"\n          - candidate_coverage_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for candidateCoverageGate (false vetoes STOP for unresolved reducer-owned search debt or uncovered required bug classes)\"\n          - candidate_coverage_search_debt_json: \"JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations used as blocked-stop evidence\"\n          - candidate_coverage_missing_json: \"JSON array of required bug classes missing from candidateCoverage.covered\"\n          - graphless_fallback_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for graphlessFallbackGate (false vetoes STOP when graphless fallback evidence is absent or unavailable_blocked)\"\n          - graphless_fallback_mode: \"Latest reducer-owned searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode value\"\n          - graphless_fallback_missing_json: \"JSON array of required bug classes missing cited fallback ledger rows\"\n          - graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason: \"Explicit unavailability reason when graphCoverageMode == unavailable_blocked\"\n          - graph_decision: \"CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED from step_graph_convergence\"\n          - graph_signals_json: \"JSON object returned by graph convergence\"\n          - graph_blockers_json: \"JSON array of graph blockers\"\n          - graph_blockers_csv: \"Comma-separated graph blocker summary\"\n          - recovery_strategy: \"One-line hint describing the next review action when decision == BLOCKED or STUCK_RECOVERY\"\n\n      step_emit_blocked_stop:\n        action: \"Persist blocked-stop evidence whenever the review legal-stop decision tree vetoes STOP\"\n        if_blocked:\n          append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"blocked_stop\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"run\":{current_iteration},\"blockedBy\":{blocked_by_json},\"gateResults\":{\"convergenceGate\":{\"pass\":{convergence_gate_pass},\"score\":{convergence_gate_score}},\"dimensionCoverageGate\":{\"pass\":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},\"covered\":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},\"missing\":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},\"p0ResolutionGate\":{\"pass\":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},\"activeP0\":{active_p0_count}},\"evidenceDensityGate\":{\"pass\":{evidence_density_gate_pass},\"avgEvidencePerFinding\":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},\"hotspotSaturationGate\":{\"pass\":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},\"claimAdjudicationGate\":{\"pass\":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},\"activeP0P1\":{claim_adjudication_active_count}},\"fixCompletenessReplayGate\":{\"pass\":{fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass},\"securitySensitive\":{security_sensitive_fix_scope},\"requiredRows\":{fix_completeness_replay_required_rows},\"passingRows\":{fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows}},\"candidateCoverageGate\":{\"pass\":{candidate_coverage_gate_pass},\"searchDebt\":{candidate_coverage_search_debt_json},\"missing\":{candidate_coverage_missing_json}},\"graphlessFallbackGate\":{\"pass\":{graphless_fallback_gate_pass},\"mode\":\"{graphless_fallback_mode}\",\"missing\":{graphless_fallback_missing_json},\"unavailabilityReason\":\"{graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason}\"}},\"graphBlockerDetail\":{graph_blockers_json},\"recoveryStrategy\":\"{recovery_strategy}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n          log: \"Blocked STOP persisted for review loop. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}\"\n        if_not_blocked:\n          proceed: true\n\n      step_handle_convergence:\n        action: \"Act on convergence decision\"\n        if_stop:\n          branch_on: \"convergence_mode == 'divergent' AND reason == 'all_dimensions_clean'\"\n          if_divergent_eligible:\n            eligibility_contract:\n              required_decision: \"STOP\"\n              eligible_reasons: [all_dimensions_clean]\n              excluded_reasons: [maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused]\n              fail_closed: \"Any missing or ambiguous reason uses if_existing_terminal_path without preparing a pivot. The nine legal-stop gates have already run and remain unchanged.\"\n            steps:\n              step_prepare_divergent_pivot:\n                action: \"Generate state-grounded read-only review candidates and prepare or resume the pivot transaction\"\n                command: |\n                  node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n                  import {\n                    buildReviewPivotPreparationInput,\n                    renderReviewPivotSeatPrompt,\n                  } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/scripts/divergent-review-pivot.ts';\n                  import {\n                    preparePivotTransaction,\n                  } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n\n                  const input = buildReviewPivotPreparationInput({\n                    artifactRoot: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n                    configPath: '{state_paths.config}',\n                    stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n                    strategyPath: '{state_paths.strategy}',\n                    registryPath: '{state_paths.findings_registry}',\n                    currentIteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n                    maxIterations: Number('{max_iterations}'),\n                    triggerReason: '{reason}',\n                  });\n                  const prepared = await preparePivotTransaction(input);\n                  const seatPrompts = prepared.status === 'pending'\n                    ? Object.fromEntries(prepared.missingSeats.map((seat) => [\n                        seat.id,\n                        renderReviewPivotSeatPrompt(seat, prepared.dispatchContext),\n                      ]))\n                    : {};\n                  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify({\n                    ...prepared,\n                    seatPrompts,\n                    boundaryRejections: input.boundaryRejections,\n                  })}\\n`);\n                  EOF\n                working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n                outputs:\n                  - pivot_prepare_json: \"The complete parsed JSON result\"\n                  - pivot_prepare_status: \"status from the prepare result\"\n                  - pivot_id: \"pivotId from the prepare result\"\n                  - pivot_paths_json: \"paths from the prepare result as JSON\"\n                  - pivot_resumed: \"resumed from the prepare result\"\n                  - pivot_missing_seat_ids: \"missingSeats ids from a pending result\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_json: \"The seat-001 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_json: \"The seat-002 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_json: \"The seat-003 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-001']\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-002']\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-003']\"\n                  - pivot_dispatch_context_json: \"dispatchContext from a pending result as JSON\"\n                short_circuit: \"When status is completed or failed, skip all dispatch and record steps and bind this result as pivot_result.\"\n\n              step_dispatch_pivot_seat_001:\n                action: \"Natively dispatch the analytical read-only review direction-selection seat\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-001'\"\n                capture_timing: true\n                if_native:\n                  dispatch:\n                    agent: deep-review\n                    model: opus\n                    context_source: \"pivot_seat_001_prompt\"\n                  wait_for_completion: true\n                failure_contract: \"A native dispatch failure binds status=rejected, a {name,message} error, and the measured timestamps/duration, then proceeds to step_record_pivot_seat_001.\"\n                outputs:\n                  - pivot_seat_001_dispatch_status: \"fulfilled or rejected\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_output_json: \"Raw native return serialized as JSON when fulfilled\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_error_json: \"{name,message} when rejected\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso: \"Dispatch start timestamp\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso: \"Dispatch completion timestamp\"\n                  - pivot_seat_001_duration_ms: \"Dispatch duration in milliseconds\"\n\n              step_record_pivot_seat_001:\n                action: \"Durably record the seat-001 native dispatch outcome\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-001'\"\n                command: |\n                  node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n                  import { recordPivotSeatResult } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n                  const status = '{pivot_seat_001_dispatch_status}';\n                  const outcome = status === 'fulfilled'\n                    ? { status, output: {pivot_seat_001_output_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_001_duration_ms}') }\n                    : { status: 'rejected', error: {pivot_seat_001_error_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_001_duration_ms}') };\n                  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(recordPivotSeatResult({ paths: {pivot_paths_json}, pivotId: '{pivot_id}', seat: {pivot_seat_001_json}, outcome }))}\\n`);\n                  EOF\n                working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n\n              step_dispatch_pivot_seat_002:\n                action: \"Natively dispatch the critical read-only review direction-selection seat\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-002'\"\n                capture_timing: true\n                if_native:\n                  dispatch:\n                    agent: deep-review\n                    model: opus\n                    context_source: \"pivot_seat_002_prompt\"\n                  wait_for_completion: true\n                failure_contract: \"A native dispatch failure binds status=rejected, a {name,message} error, and the measured timestamps/duration, then proceeds to step_record_pivot_seat_002.\"\n                outputs:\n                  - pivot_seat_002_dispatch_status: \"fulfilled or rejected\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_output_json: \"Raw native return serialized as JSON when fulfilled\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_error_json: \"{name,message} when rejected\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_started_at_iso: \"Dispatch start timestamp\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_completed_at_iso: \"Dispatch completion timestamp\"\n                  - pivot_seat_002_duration_ms: \"Dispatch duration in milliseconds\"\n\n              step_record_pivot_seat_002:\n                action: \"Durably record the seat-002 native dispatch outcome\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-002'\"\n                command: |\n                  node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n                  import { recordPivotSeatResult } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n                  const status = '{pivot_seat_002_dispatch_status}';\n                  const outcome = status === 'fulfilled'\n                    ? { status, output: {pivot_seat_002_output_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_002_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_002_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_002_duration_ms}') }\n                    : { status: 'rejected', error: {pivot_seat_002_error_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_002_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_002_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_002_duration_ms}') };\n                  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(recordPivotSeatResult({ paths: {pivot_paths_json}, pivotId: '{pivot_id}', seat: {pivot_seat_002_json}, outcome }))}\\n`);\n                  EOF\n                working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n\n              step_dispatch_pivot_seat_003:\n                action: \"Natively dispatch the pragmatic read-only review direction-selection seat\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-003'\"\n                capture_timing: true\n                if_native:\n                  dispatch:\n                    agent: deep-review\n                    model: opus\n                    context_source: \"pivot_seat_003_prompt\"\n                  wait_for_completion: true\n                failure_contract: \"A native dispatch failure binds status=rejected, a {name,message} error, and the measured timestamps/duration, then proceeds to step_record_pivot_seat_003.\"\n                outputs:\n                  - pivot_seat_003_dispatch_status: \"fulfilled or rejected\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_output_json: \"Raw native return serialized as JSON when fulfilled\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_error_json: \"{name,message} when rejected\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_started_at_iso: \"Dispatch start timestamp\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_completed_at_iso: \"Dispatch completion timestamp\"\n                  - pivot_seat_003_duration_ms: \"Dispatch duration in milliseconds\"\n\n              step_record_pivot_seat_003:\n                action: \"Durably record the seat-003 native dispatch outcome\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-003'\"\n                command: |\n                  node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n                  import { recordPivotSeatResult } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n                  const status = '{pivot_seat_003_dispatch_status}';\n                  const outcome = status === 'fulfilled'\n                    ? { status, output: {pivot_seat_003_output_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_003_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_003_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_003_duration_ms}') }\n                    : { status: 'rejected', error: {pivot_seat_003_error_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_003_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_003_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_003_duration_ms}') };\n                  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(recordPivotSeatResult({ paths: {pivot_paths_json}, pivotId: '{pivot_id}', seat: {pivot_seat_003_json}, outcome }))}\\n`);\n                  EOF\n                working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n\n              step_finalize_divergent_pivot:\n                action: \"Finalize the prepared pivot after all missing native seats are durable\"\n                skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending'\"\n                command: |\n                  node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n                  import { finalizePivotTransaction } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n                  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(finalizePivotTransaction({ paths: {pivot_paths_json}, pivotId: '{pivot_id}', resumed: {pivot_resumed} }))}\\n`);\n                  EOF\n                working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n                outputs:\n                  - pivot_finalize_json: \"The complete parsed final result\"\n\n              step_resolve_divergent_pivot_result:\n                action: \"Use prepare's terminal result on resume/short-circuit, otherwise use finalize's result\"\n                branch_on: \"pivot_prepare_status\"\n                if_pending: { bind: { pivot_result: \"{pivot_finalize_json}\" } }\n                if_completed_or_failed: { bind: { pivot_result: \"{pivot_prepare_json}\" } }\n\n              step_apply_divergent_pivot_result:\n                action: \"Adopt a successful read-only review direction or fail closed to the existing synthesis path\"\n                branch_on: \"pivot_result.status\"\n                if_completed:\n                  set:\n                    next_dimension: \"{pivot_result.restoredFocus}\"\n                    pivot_lineage: \"Pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} selected {pivot_result.selectedCandidate.id} from source iteration {current_iteration}.\"\n                    saturated_directions: \"{pivot_result.completedEvent.saturatedDirections}\"\n                  command: \"node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}\"\n                  log: \"Divergent review pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} restored focus to {pivot_result.restoredFocus}; continuing iteration {current_iteration}.\"\n                  proceed_to: step_normalize_pause_events\n                if_failed:\n                  log: \"Divergent review pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} failed closed ({pivot_result.failureReason}); proceeding to synthesis.\"\n                  skip_to: phase_synthesis\n            on_error:\n              log: \"Divergent review pivot integration error; failing closed to the existing synthesis path.\"\n              skip_to: phase_synthesis\n          if_existing_terminal_path:\n            log: \"Loop stopping. Reason: {reason}\"\n            skip_to: phase_synthesis\n        if_blocked:\n          log: \"Legal-stop gates blocked STOP. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Continuing review.\"\n        if_stuck_recovery:\n          log: \"Stuck detected ({stuck_count} consecutive no-progress). Entering recovery.\"\n          recovery_strategies:\n            - \"Change granularity: file-level → function-level → line-level review\"\n            - \"Traceability protocol replay: re-run unresolved core or overlay checks\"\n            - \"Escalate severity review: re-examine P2 findings for potential P1 upgrades\"\n          pivot_target: \"Pivot to the least-covered dimension (the review dimension with the fewest iteration passes and lowest coverage ratio).\"\n          set:\n            next_dimension: \"RECOVERY: {recovery_strategy}. Least-covered dimension: {least_explored}\"\n            pending_stop_event: \"stuck_recovery\"\n            pending_stop_reason: \"stuckRecovery\"\n            pending_stop_detail: \"Recovery path activated after repeated no-progress iterations\"\n          reset: { stuck_count: 0 }\n        if_continue:\n          log: \"Iteration {current_iteration}: Continuing. Dimension: {next_dimension}\"\n\n      step_normalize_pause_events:\n        action: \"Rewrite raw pause and recovery events to the frozen STOP_REASONS enum at emission time: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused\"\n        if_pending_pause_event:\n          append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"userPaused\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"stopReason\":\"userPaused\",\"reason\":\"{pending_stop_detail}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n          message: \"Review paused. Delete review/.deep-review-pause to resume.\"\n          reset:\n            pending_stop_event: null\n            pending_stop_reason: null\n            pending_stop_detail: null\n          halt: true\n        if_pending_stuck_recovery:\n          append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"stuckRecovery\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"stopReason\":\"stuckRecovery\",\"fromIteration\":{current_iteration},\"strategy\":\"{recovery_strategy}\",\"targetDimension\":\"{least_explored}\",\"outcome\":\"pending\",\"reason\":\"{pending_stop_detail}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n          reset:\n            pending_stop_event: null\n            pending_stop_reason: null\n            pending_stop_detail: null\n        if_no_pending_event:\n          proceed: true\n\n      step_generate_state_summary:\n        action: \"Generate compact state summary for dispatch context (~200 tokens)\"\n        generate: |\n          STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):\n          Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}\n          Dimension: {next_dimension}\n          Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}\n          Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})\n          Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}\n          {resource_map_prompt_line}\n          Coverage Age: {coverage_age}\n          Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}\n          Stuck count: {stuck_count}\n          Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\n\n      step_marker_scan:\n        action: \"H-2 loop-prevention guard: scan previous iteration's rendered prompt for nested review marker\"\n        algorithm: |\n          if current_iteration > 1:\n            PREV_PROMPT_PATH = \"{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{previous_iteration}.md\"\n            if file_exists(PREV_PROMPT_PATH):\n              PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE = head -1 of PREV_PROMPT_PATH\n              if PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE matches ^(DEEP-REVIEW|DEEP-RESEARCH|CODE-REVIEW)$:\n                log \"ERROR: nested review-of-review loop detected (marker found in previous iteration prompt). Aborting.\"\n                halt with exit code 2\n        note: |\n          False-positive prevention: only matches when the first line is exactly one of the\n          three canonical markers. Markers appearing in comments or body text are ignored.\n          True-positive triggers only when a previous iteration's rendered prompt literally\n          starts with a deep-loop marker, indicating a nested review-of-review dispatch.\n\n      step_dispatch_iteration:\n        action: \"Dispatch @deep-review agent for one review iteration\"\n        resolve_executor:\n          source: \"config.executor\"\n          loader: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig\"\n        pre_dispatch:\n          generate_summary: |\n            STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):\n            Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}\n            Dimension: {next_dimension}\n            Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}\n            Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})\n            Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}\n            {resource_map_prompt_line}\n            Coverage Age: {coverage_age}\n            Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}\n            Stuck count: {stuck_count}\n            Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\n          render_prompt_pack:\n            template_path: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl\"\n            renderer: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts#renderPromptPack\"\n            output_path: \"{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md\"\n            variables:\n              state_summary: \"{state_summary}\"\n              current_iteration: \"{current_iteration}\"\n              max_iterations: \"{max_iterations}\"\n              next_dimension: \"{next_dimension}\"\n              review_target: \"{review_target}\"\n              review_scope_files: \"{review_scope_files}\"\n              p0_count: \"{p0_count}\"\n              p1_count: \"{p1_count}\"\n              p2_count: \"{p2_count}\"\n              pivot_lineage: \"{pivot_lineage}\"\n              saturated_directions: \"{saturated_directions}\"\n              state_paths_config: \"{state_paths.config}\"\n              state_paths_state_log: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n              state_paths_findings_registry: \"{state_paths.findings_registry}\"\n              state_paths_strategy: \"{state_paths.strategy}\"\n              state_paths_iteration_pattern: \"{state_paths.iteration_pattern}\"\n              state_paths_delta_pattern: \"{state_paths.delta_pattern}\"\n        pre_dispatch_audit:\n          skip_when: \"config.executor.kind == 'native'\"\n          call: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts#writeFirstRecordExecutor\"\n          args:\n            stateLogPath: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n            executor: \"{config.executor}\"\n            iteration: \"{current_iteration}\"\n        branch_on: \"config.executor.kind\"\n        if_native:\n          dispatch:\n            agent: deep-review\n            model: opus\n            context_source: \"rendered_prompt_pack\"\n          wait_for_completion: true\n        if_cli_copilot:\n          command: |\n            node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n            import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n            import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';\n\n            import { runAuditedExecutorCommand } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts';\n            import { buildCopilotPromptArg } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts';\n\n            const promptPath = '{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md';\n            const prompt = readFileSync(promptPath, 'utf8');\n\n            // Workflow-resolved spec folder is the only legal write authority.\n            // The helper (`validateSpecFolder` inside `buildCopilotPromptArg`)\n            // is the single source of truth for rejecting empty / unresolved\n            // template / whitespace-only / string-coerced empty values. The\n            // YAML hands the raw `{spec_folder}` directly; on rejection the\n            // helper safe-fails to `type:\"missing\", writeIntent:true` (Gate-3\n            // enforcement, --allow-all-tools stripped).\n            const specFolderRaw = '{spec_folder}';\n            const targetAuthority = { type: 'approved', specFolder: specFolderRaw };\n\n            const baseArgs = [\n              '-p',\n              prompt,\n              '--model',\n              '{config.executor.model}',\n              '--allow-all-tools',\n              '--no-ask-user',\n            ];\n\n            const built = buildCopilotPromptArg({\n              promptPath,\n              prompt,\n              targetAuthority,\n              baseArgv: baseArgs,\n            });\n\n            // Large-prompt @PROMPT_PATH wrapper: helper emits a `promptFileBody`\n            // containing `<TARGET AUTHORITY preamble>\\n\\n---\\n\\n<original>`.\n            // Write it to disk so the file Copilot reads via @path opens with\n            // the authority block. Without this, recovered/bootstrap folder\n            // mentions inside the file could still anchor the model.\n            if (built.promptFileBody !== undefined) {\n              writeFileSync(promptPath, built.promptFileBody, 'utf8');\n            }\n\n            // Copilot is routed through the audited executor wrapper so it produces\n            // INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts alongside claude-code/opencode\n            // There is no cli-copilot kind in the executor schema, so it\n            // dispatches as kind:'native' (full env passthrough, no recursion-guard\n            // layer configured for copilot); the receipt still records the real\n            // 'copilot' command + built.argv as facts.\n            const dispatchFacts = {\n              command: 'deep/review',\n              mode: 'review',\n              specFolder: '{spec_folder}',\n              artifactDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n              iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n              executorKind: '{config.executor.kind}',\n              branchId: '{config.executor.kind}',\n              sessionId: '{session_id}',\n              generation: Number('{generation}'),\n            };\n            const dispatchId = 'review-i{current_iteration}-g{generation}';\n            process.exit(\n              runAuditedExecutorCommand({\n                command: 'copilot',\n                args: built.argv,\n                cwd: '{repo_root}',\n                timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n                stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n                executor: {\n                  kind: 'native',\n                  model: '{config.executor.model}',\n                  configDir: null,\n                  reasoningEffort: null,\n                  serviceTier: null,\n                  sandboxMode: null,\n                  timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n                  governor: null,\n                },\n                iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n                receiptDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}/dispatch-receipts',\n                dispatchId,\n              }),\n            );\n            EOF\n          working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n          timeout_seconds: \"{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}\"\n          wait_for_completion: true\n          notes:\n            - \"Routed through runAuditedExecutorCommand so copilot produces INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts; built.argv (@PROMPT_PATH or positional) is forwarded verbatim as the receipt facts\"\n            - \"copilot has no stdin support; prompt is positional\"\n            - \"Size check uses 16KB threshold; above that uses @PROMPT_PATH wrapper prompt\"\n            - \"Large-prompt path relies on copilot honoring @path references within its prompt string\"\n            - \"buildCopilotPromptArg attaches a TARGET AUTHORITY preamble bound to the workflow-approved spec folder; recovered context cannot override it (authority guard source: .opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml)\"\n            - \"If spec_folder is missing/malformed/unresolved on a write-intent dispatch, the helper enforces plan-only by replacing the prompt with a Gate-3 question and stripping --allow-all-tools\"\n            - \"Large-prompt wrapper mode rewrites the prompt file at promptPath to include the TARGET AUTHORITY preamble (prefix + divider + original body); the argv becomes a bare @promptPath reference\"\n        if_cli_claude_code:\n          command: |\n            node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n            import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n            import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';\n\n            import { runAuditedExecutorCommand } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts';\n\n            const configDir = '{optional_claude_config_dir}';\n            const reasoningEffort = '{config.executor.reasoningEffort}' === 'null' ? null : '{config.executor.reasoningEffort}';\n            const executor = {\n              kind: 'cli-claude-code',\n              model: '{config.executor.model}',\n              configDir: configDir || null,\n              reasoningEffort,\n              serviceTier: null,\n              sandboxMode: null,\n              timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n              governor: null,\n            };\n            const args = [\n              '-p',\n              readFileSync('{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md', 'utf8'),\n              '--model',\n              executor.model,\n              '--permission-mode',\n              'acceptEdits',\n              '--output-format',\n              'text',\n            ];\n            if (executor.reasoningEffort) {\n              args.push('--effort', executor.reasoningEffort);\n            }\n\n            // CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR rides through guardContext.env; the CLAUDE_ prefix is\n            // on the cli-claude-code env allowlist so buildExecutorDispatchEnv admits it.\n            const parentEnv = { ...process.env };\n            if (configDir) {\n              parentEnv.CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR = configDir;\n            }\n\n            const dispatchFacts = {\n              command: 'deep/review',\n              mode: 'review',\n              specFolder: '{spec_folder}',\n              artifactDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n              iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n              executorKind: '{config.executor.kind}',\n              branchId: '{config.executor.kind}',\n              sessionId: '{session_id}',\n              generation: Number('{generation}'),\n            };\n            const dispatchId = 'review-i{current_iteration}-g{generation}';\n            process.exit(\n              runAuditedExecutorCommand({\n                command: 'claude',\n                args,\n                cwd: '{repo_root}',\n                timeoutSeconds: executor.timeoutSeconds,\n                stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n                executor,\n                iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n                guardContext: { env: parentEnv },\n                receiptDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}/dispatch-receipts',\n                dispatchId,\n              }),\n            );\n            EOF\n          working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n          timeout_seconds: \"{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}\"\n          wait_for_completion: true\n          render_hint:\n            optional_claude_config_dir: |\n              If config.executor.configDir is set and non-null, expand a leading ~ to HOME and bind that path.\n              Otherwise expand to empty string.\n          notes:\n            - \"Routed through runAuditedExecutorCommand so claude-code produces INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts; recursion guard + env allowlist enforced via buildExecutorDispatchEnv\"\n            - \"CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is forwarded through guardContext.env (CLAUDE_ prefix is allowlisted) so account-specific Claude Code routing is preserved\"\n            - \"permission-mode stays acceptEdits (review's existing iteration-write behavior); claude-code has no serviceTier flag\"\n            - \"claude-code supports --effort for reasoning depth\"\n        if_cli_opencode:\n          command: |\n            node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n            import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n            import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';\n\n            import { runAuditedExecutorCommand } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts';\n\n            const reasoningEffort = '{config.executor.reasoningEffort}' === 'null' ? null : '{config.executor.reasoningEffort}';\n            const executor = {\n              kind: 'cli-opencode',\n              model: '{config.executor.model}',\n              configDir: null,\n              reasoningEffort,\n              serviceTier: null,\n              sandboxMode: null,\n              timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n              governor: null,\n            };\n            const promptBody = readFileSync('{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md', 'utf8');\n            const message = 'Resolved route: mode=review; target_agent=@deep-review; execution=single_review_iteration; state_source=externalized_files; do_not_switch_mode=true'\n              + '\\n\\n'\n              + promptBody;\n            const args = [\n              'run',\n              '--model',\n              executor.model,\n              '--format',\n              'json',\n              '--dangerously-skip-permissions',\n              '--pure',\n              '--dir',\n              '{repo_root}',\n            ];\n            if (executor.reasoningEffort) {\n              args.push('--variant', executor.reasoningEffort);\n            }\n            args.push(message);\n\n            const dispatchFacts = {\n              command: 'deep/review',\n              mode: 'review',\n              specFolder: '{spec_folder}',\n              artifactDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n              iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n              executorKind: '{config.executor.kind}',\n              branchId: '{config.executor.kind}',\n              sessionId: '{session_id}',\n              generation: Number('{generation}'),\n            };\n            const dispatchId = 'review-i{current_iteration}-g{generation}';\n            process.exit(\n              runAuditedExecutorCommand({\n                command: 'opencode',\n                args,\n   \n563:   \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n564:   \"secure\": true\n565: }' 2>&1\n566: \n567: bdg stop 2>&1\n568: ```\n569: \n570: ### Pattern 5: Single Instance (MCP Fallback)\n571: \n572: When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\n573: \n574: ```typescript\n575: call_tool_chain(`\n576:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n577:   const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n578:   const logs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n579:   return { screenshot, logs };\n580: `)\n581: ```\n582: \n583: ### Pattern 6: Parallel Instances (MCP)\n584: \n585: Compare multiple sites at the same time:\n586: \n587: ```typescript\n588: call_tool_chain(`\n589:   // Instance 1: Production\n590:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n591:     url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n592:   });\n593: \n594:   // Instance 2: Staging (isolated browser)\n595:   await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n596:     url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n597:   });\n598: \n599:   // Capture both screenshots\n600:   const prod = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n601:   const staging = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n602: \n603:   return { production: prod, staging: staging };\n604: `)\n605: ```\n606: \n607: ### Tool Selection Guide\n608: \n609: | Scenario                 | Tool | Why                         |\n610: | ------------------------ | ---- | --------------------------- |\n611: | Quick screenshot         | CLI  | Fastest, lowest tokens      |\n612: | Console log check        | CLI  | Pipe to jq for filtering    |\n613: | Network trace            | CLI  | HAR export built in         |\n614: | Cookie manipulation      | CLI  | Full CDP access             |\n615: | Multi-tool workflow      | MCP  | Chain with other tools      |\n616: | Parallel browser testing | MCP  | Multiple isolated instances |\n617: | CDP method discovery     | CLI  | `--list`, `--describe`      |\n618: \n619: ### bdg Command Reference\n620: \n621: **Session Commands**\n622: \n623: | Command      | Description                     | Example                        |\n624: | ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n625: | `bdg <url>`  | Open URL, start browser session | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1` |\n626: | `bdg status` | Check session status            | `bdg status 2>&1`              |\n627: | `bdg stop`   | Stop browser, clean up session  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                |\n628: \n629: **Helper Commands**\n630: \n631: | Command                      | Description             | Example                             |\n632: | ---------------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------- |\n633: | `bdg screenshot <path>`      | Capture page screenshot | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` |\n634: | `bdg console logs`           | Get console messages    | `bdg console logs 2>&1 \\| jq '.'`   |\n635: | `bdg network cookies`        | Get all cookies         | `bdg network cookies 2>&1`          |\n636: | `bdg dom query \"<selector>\"` | Query DOM elements      | `bdg dom query \".my-class\" 2>&1`    |\n637: | `bdg js \"<expression>\"`      | Execute JavaScript      | `bdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1`      |\n638: | `bdg har export <path>`      | Export network as HAR   | `bdg har export trace.har 2>&1`     |\n639: \n640: **Discovery Commands**\n641: \n642: | Command                   | Description               | Example                                 |\n643: | ------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n644: | `bdg --version`           | Show CLI version          | `bdg --version`                         |\n645: | `bdg --list`              | List all CDP domains (53) | `bdg --list`                            |\n646: | `bdg --describe <domain>` | Show domain methods       | `bdg --describe Page`                   |\n647: | `bdg --describe <method>` | Show method signature     | `bdg --describe Page.captureScreenshot` |\n648: | `bdg --search <term>`     | Search CDP methods        | `bdg --search screenshot`               |\n649: \n650: **Raw CDP Commands**\n651: \n652: | Command                     | Description         | Example                                                     |\n653: | --------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |\n654: | `bdg cdp <method>`          | Execute CDP method  | `bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1`                                  |\n655: | `bdg cdp <method> '<json>'` | CDP with parameters | `bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\"name\":\"x\",\"value\":\"y\"}' 2>&1` |\n656: \n657: **Error Handling Pattern**\n658: \n659: Always use `2>&1` to capture stderr for error handling:\n660: \n661: ```bash\n662: #!/bin/bash\n663: trap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\n664: command -v bdg || { echo \"Install bdg first\"; exit 1; }\n665: bdg \"$URL\" 2>&1 || exit 1\n666: # ... operations ...\n667: # Cleanup runs automatically on exit\n668: ```\n669: \n670: ---\n671: \n672: ## 7. FEATURES\n673: \n674: | Feature            | CLI (bdg)                                   | MCP (Code Mode)                         |\n675: | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n676: | **Installation**   | `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Code Mode + UTCP config                 |\n677: | **Token Cost**     | Lowest                                      | Medium                                  |\n678: | **CDP Access**     | All 644 methods                             | MCP-exposed subset (26 tools)           |\n679: | **Self-Discovery** | `--list`, `--describe`, `--search`          | `search_tools()`                        |\n680: | **Best For**       | Quick debugging, inspection                 | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers |\n681: \n682: **Core capabilities available through both approaches:**\n683: - Screenshot capture\n684: - DOM inspection and querying\n685: - Console log access\n686: - Network monitoring and HAR export\n687: - CSS debugging\n688: - JavaScript execution\n689: - Cookie management\n690: - Full Chrome DevTools Protocol access\n691: \n692: ---\n693: \n694: ## 8. EXAMPLES\n695: \n696: ### Example 1: Full Debug Session\n697: \n698: ```bash\n699: #!/bin/bash\n700: # Complete debug session with error handling\n701: trap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\n702: \n703: # Verify CLI is available\n704: command -v bdg || { echo \"Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\"; exit 1; }\n705: \n706: # Start session\n707: bdg https://example.com 2>&1 || exit 1\n708: \n709: # Capture initial state\n710: bdg screenshot before.png 2>&1\n711: bdg console logs 2>&1 > console-before.json\n712: \n713: # Enable network monitoring\n714: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n715: sleep 3\n716: \n717: # Export network trace\n718: bdg har export session.har 2>&1\n719: bdg screenshot after.png 2>&1\n720: \n721: echo \"Debug session complete. Files: before.png, after.png, session.har, console-before.json\"\n722: ```\n723: \n724: ### Example 2: JavaScript Execution\n725: \n726: ```bash\n727: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n728: \n729: # Get page title\n730: bdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1\n731: \n732: # Count links on page\n733: bdg js \"document.querySelectorAll('a').length\" 2>&1\n734: \n735: # Get all link hrefs\n736: bdg js \"Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)\" 2>&1\n737: \n738: bdg stop 2>&1\n739: ```\n740: \n741: ### Example 3: DOM Query and Interaction\n742: \n743: ```bash\n744: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n745: \n746: # Query elements by class\n747: bdg dom query \".nav-link\" 2>&1\n748: \n749: # Click a button via JavaScript\n750: bdg js \"document.querySelector('#submit-btn').click()\" 2>&1\n751: \n752: # Check result after click\n753: bdg screenshot post-click.png 2>&1\n754: bdg console logs 2>&1\n755: \n756: bdg stop 2>&1\n757: ```\n758: \n759: ### Example 4: Network Analysis\n760: \n761: ```bash\n762: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n763: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n764: \n765: # Wait for full page load activity\n766: sleep 5\n767: bdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\n768: bdg stop 2>&1\n769: \n770: # Find slow requests (over 1 second)\n771: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n772: \n773: # Find failed requests\n774: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.response.status >= 400) | {url: .request.url, status: .response.status}' network-trace.har\n775: ```\n776: \n777: ### Example 5: Parallel Comparison (MCP)\n778: \n779: ```typescript\n780: // Compare production and staging side by side\n781: call_tool_chain(`\n782:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n783:     url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n784:   });\n785:   await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n786:     url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n787:   });\n788: \n789:   const prodScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n790:   const stagingScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n791:   const prodLogs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n792:   const stagingLogs = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_list_console_messages({});\n793: \n794:   return {\n795:     production: { screenshot: prodScreenshot, logs: prodLogs },\n796:     staging: { screenshot: stagingScreenshot, logs: stagingLogs }\n797:   };\n798: `)\n799: ```\n800: \n801: ### Example 6: Cookie Session Management\n802: \n803: ```bash\n804: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n805: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n806: \n807: # Export current cookies\n808: bdg network cookies 2>&1 > cookies.json\n809: \n810: # Inject an auth token\n811: bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n812:   \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n813:   \"value\": \"test-session-token\",\n814:   \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n815:   \"path\": \"/\",\n816:   \"secure\": true,\n817:   \"httpOnly\": true\n818: }' 2>&1\n819: \n820: # Reload to test authenticated state\n821: bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1\n822: bdg screenshot authenticated.png 2>&1\n823: \n824: bdg stop 2>&1\n825: ```\n826: \n827: ---\n828: \n829: ## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING\n830: \n831: ### Error/Cause/Fix Reference\n832: \n833: | Error | Cause | Fix |\n834: | ----- | ----- | --- |\n835: | `command not found: bdg` | CLI not installed or not in PATH | Run `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha`. Check PATH with `npm config get prefix` |\n836: | `Error: Could not find Chrome` | Chrome path not set or binary missing | Set `CHROME_PATH` env var. See path examples below |\n837: | `Error: No active session` | Session not started or crashed | Run `bdg stop 2>&1` then retry `bdg <url> 2>&1` |\n838: | `Failed to move to new namespace` | Linux sandbox restriction | Enable user namespaces or set `CHROME_FLAGS`. See Linux sandbox section |\n839: | `bdg fails on Windows` | Native Windows not supported | Install WSL with `wsl --install`, then install bdg inside WSL |\n840: | Chrome DevTools tools missing in Code Mode | UTCP config missing or disabled | Check `.utcp_config.json`, confirm `disabled: false`, restart your session |\n841: | MCP server fails to start | Port conflict or config error | Run `lsof -i :9222` and kill conflicting processes |\n842: | `EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::9222` | Port 9222 already taken | Run `kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)` to free the port |\n843: \n844: ### CLI: Command Not Found\n845: \n846: ```bash\n847: # Install bdg\n848: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n849: \n850: # Or fix PATH\n851: npm config get prefix\n852: export PATH=\"$(npm config get prefix)/bin:$PATH\"\n853: \n854: # Verify\n855: command -v bdg\n856: ```\n857: \n858: ### CLI: Chrome Not Found\n859: \n860: ```bash\n861: # macOS\n862: export CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n863: \n864: # Linux\n865: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n866: \n867: # Confirm Chrome exists at the path\n868: ls -la \"$CHROME_PATH\"\n869: \n870: # Add to your shell profile\n871: echo 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\n872: ```\n873: \n874: ### CLI: Session Won't Start\n875: \n876: ```bash\n877: # Force stop any existing sessions\n878: bdg stop 2>&1\n879: \n880: # Find zombie Chrome processes\n881: ps aux | grep -i chrome | grep -i debug\n882: \n883: # Kill zombie processes\n884: pkill -f \"chrome.*remote-debugging\"\n885: \n886: # Retry\n887: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n888: ```\n889: \n890: ### CLI: Sandbox Errors (Linux)\n891: \n892: Fix in order of preference:\n893: \n894: ```bash\n895: # Option 1: Enable user namespaces (preferred)\n896: sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n897: \n898: # Option 2: Disable setuid sandbox only\n899: export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n900: \n901: # Option 3: No sandbox (least secure, last resort)\n902: export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n903: ```\n904: \n905: ### CLI: Windows Not Supported\n906: \n907: ```bash\n908: # Install WSL\n909: wsl --install\n910: \n911: # Inside WSL, install bdg\n912: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n913: ```\n914: \n915: ### MCP: Tool Not Found\n916: \n917: 1. Open `.utcp_config.json` and confirm the chrome_devtools entry exists\n918: 2. Check that `disabled` is not set to `true`\n919: 3. Restart your Claude Code or OpenCode session\n920: 4. Run `list_tools()` and look for chrome_devtools_* entries\n921: \n922: ### MCP: Connection Failed\n923: \n924: ```bash\n925: # Test the MCP server directly\n926: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --version\n927: \n928: # Check for port conflicts\n929: lsof -i :9222\n930: \n931: # Kill any conflicting processes\n932: kill $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n933: ```\n934: \n935: ### MCP: Port Already in Use\n936: \n937: ```bash\n938: # Find the process using port 9222\n939: lsof -i :9222\n940: \n941: # Kill it\n942: kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n943: \n944: # Confirm the port is free\n945: lsof -i :9222\n946: # Should return nothing\n947: ```\n948: \n949: ---\n950: \n951: ## 10. RESOURCES\n952: \n953: ### Related Documentation\n954: \n955: | Document        | Location                                                                  | Purpose              |\n956: | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |\n957: | SKILL.md        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/SKILL.md`                      | Complete workflows   |\n958: | CDP Patterns    | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/cdp_patterns.md`    | Domain patterns      |\n959: | Troubleshooting | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/troubleshooting.md` | Detailed fixes       |\n960: | Examples        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/examples/README.md`            | Production templates |\n961: | Code Mode Guide | `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md`                          | Code Mode setup      |\n962: \n963: ### Configuration Paths\n964: \n965: | Client  | Configuration                 | Purpose                      |\n966: | ------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- |\n967: | **CLI** | `CHROME_PATH` env var         | Browser location             |\n968: | **MCP** | `.mcp.json` / `opencode.json` | Code Mode server             |\n969: | **MCP** | `.utcp_config.json`           | Chrome DevTools registration |\n970: \n971: ### MCP Tools Reference (26 tools)\n972: \n973: | Tool                    | Purpose            | CLI Equivalent                                   |\n974: | ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |\n975: | `navigate_page`         | Navigate to URL    | `bdg <url>`                                      |\n976: | `take_screenshot`       | Capture screenshot | `bdg screenshot`                                 |\n977: | `list_console_messages` | Get console logs   | `bdg console logs`                               |\n978: | `resize_page`           | Set viewport size  | `bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`     |\n979: | `click`                 | Click element      | `bdg js \"document.querySelector('...').click()\"` |\n980: | `type_text`             | Type into element  | `bdg js \"...\"`                                   |\n981: | `get_page_content`      | Get page HTML      | `bdg dom query \"html\"`                           |\n982: \n983: **Tool naming convention:** `{instance}.{instance}_{tool_name}`\n984: \n985: ```typescript\n986: // Example invocations\n987: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })\n988: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({})\n989: chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })  // parallel instance\n990: ```\n991: \n992: ---\n993: \n994: ## Quick Reference Card\n995: \n996: ### Installation\n997: \n998: ```bash\n999: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n1000: ```\n1001: \n1002: ### Discovery\n1003: \n1004: ```bash\n1005: bdg --list                    # List all CDP domains\n1006: bdg --describe Page           # Show domain methods\n1007: bdg --search screenshot       # Find methods by keyword\n1008: ```\n1009: \n1010: ### Session Control\n1011: \n1012: ```bash\n1013: bdg <url>                     # Start session\n1014: bdg status                    # Check session state\n1015: bdg stop                      # Stop session\n1016: ```\n1017: \n1018: ### Common Operations\n1019: \n1020: ```bash\n1021: bdg screenshot <path>         # Capture screenshot\n1022: bdg console logs              # Get console messages\n1023: bdg network cookies           # Get all cookies\n1024: bdg dom query \"<selector>\"    # Query DOM\n1025: bdg js \"<expression>\"         # Execute JavaScript\n1026: bdg har export <path>         # Export HAR file\n1027: ```\n1028: \n1029: ### MCP Quick Start\n1030: \n1031: ```typescript\n1032: // When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\n1033: call_tool_chain(`\n1034:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n1035:   const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n1036:   return screenshot;\n1037: `)\n1038: ```\n1039: \n1040: ### Environment Setup\n1041: \n1042: ```bash\n1043: # Add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc\n1044: export CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n1045: # Linux: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n1046: ```\n1047: \n1048: ### Validation Checkpoints Summary\n1049: \n1050: | Checkpoint          | Meaning                              |\n1051: | ------------------- | ------------------------------------ |\n1052: | `phase_1_complete`  | Prerequisites validated              |\n1053: | `phase_2_complete`  | CLI installation complete            |\n1054: | `phase_3_complete`  | Configuration complete               |\n1055: | `phase_4_complete`  | Verification complete                |\n1056: | `phase_5_complete`  | System operational                   |\n1057: \n1058: ---\n1059: \n1060: ## Version History\n1061: \n1062: | Version | Date       | Changes                                                   |\n1063: | ------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |\n1064: | 2.1.0   | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, formal checkpoints, STOP blocks   |\n1065: | 2.0.0   | 2025-10-01 | Two-approach architecture, MCP fallback, security section |\n1066: | 1.0.0   | 2025-06-01 | Initial CLI guide                                         |\n1067: \n1068: ---\n1069: \n1070: **Need help?** See [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) or load the `mcp-chrome-devtools` skill for detailed workflows.\n\n(End of file - total 1070 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Chrome DevTools Installation Guide\n\nComplete installation and configuration guide for Chrome DevTools browser debugging, providing AI assistants with browser control through two approaches. The CLI tool (bdg) offers direct, fast, token-efficient browser debugging. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode for multi-tool orchestration and parallel browser testing. Covers screenshot capture, DOM inspection, console evaluation, network monitoring and performance profiling.\n\n> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n> **Package:** `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | **Dependencies:** Node.js 18+, Chrome/Chromium\n\n**Version:** 2.1.0 | **Updated:** 2026-02-20 | **Protocol:** Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP)\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\nCopy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n\n```\nI want to install the Chrome DevTools CLI (bdg) for browser debugging.\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed","truncated":false,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/INSTALL_GUIDE.md","text":"# Chrome DevTools Installation Guide\n\nComplete installation and configuration guide for Chrome DevTools browser debugging, providing AI assistants with browser control through two approaches. The CLI tool (bdg) offers direct, fast, token-efficient browser debugging. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode for multi-tool orchestration and parallel browser testing. Covers screenshot capture, DOM inspection, console evaluation, network monitoring and performance profiling.\n\n> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n> **Package:** `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | **Dependencies:** Node.js 18+, Chrome/Chromium\n\n**Version:** 2.1.0 | **Updated:** 2026-02-20 | **Protocol:** Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP)\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\nCopy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n\n```\nI want to install the Chrome DevTools CLI (bdg) for browser debugging.\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n2. Install browser-debugger-cli globally\n3. Verify Chrome/Chromium is available\n4. Test with a basic screenshot capture\n5. (Optional) Configure MCP fallback if needed\n\nMy platform is: [macOS / Linux / Windows with WSL]\n\nGuide me through each step with the exact commands needed.\n```\n\nThe AI will:\n- Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system\n- Install `browser-debugger-cli` globally\n- Test Chrome/Chromium availability\n- Run a basic screenshot test to confirm installation\n- Configure MCP fallback if you need multi-tool workflows\n\n**Expected setup time:** 3 to 5 minutes\n\n### Quick Success Check (30 seconds)\n\nAfter installation, run these two tests immediately:\n\n1. Open your terminal and run: `bdg --version`\n2. Version output appears: CLI is working\n3. In Claude Code, ask: \"Take a screenshot of example.com\"\n4. Screenshot captured: full system is working\n\nNot working? Go to [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).\n\n---\n\n## 1. OVERVIEW\n\nChrome DevTools gives AI assistants browser debugging capabilities through two approaches. The CLI (`bdg`) is the primary method: direct, fast and token-efficient. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode and suits multi-tool orchestration or parallel browser testing.\n\n### Core Principle\n\n> **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint. Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.\n\n### Source Repository\n\n| Property         | Value                                                                                       |\n| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| **GitHub (CLI)** | [szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli](https://github.com/szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli)           |\n| **GitHub (MCP)** | [ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp](https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp) |\n| **npm (CLI)**    | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha`                                                                |\n| **npm (MCP)**    | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`                                                                |\n| **License**      | MIT / Apache-2.0                                                                            |\n\n### Two Approaches\n\n| Approach            | Package                      | When to Use                             | Token Cost |\n| ------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ---------- |\n| **CLI (bdg)**       | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Single browser, quick tasks, debugging  | Lowest     |\n| **MCP (Code Mode)** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers | Higher     |\n\nDefault to CLI. Use MCP only when CLI is not sufficient.\n\n### Decision Flowchart\n\n```\nTask received → Is bdg CLI available? (command -v bdg)\n                      │\n              ┌───────┴───────┐\n             YES              NO\n              │               │\n              ▼               ▼\n        Use CLI          Is Code Mode configured?\n        (fastest)               │\n                        ┌───────┴───────┐\n                       YES              NO\n                        │               │\n                        ▼               ▼\n                   Use MCP         Install CLI:\n                   (fallback)      npm i -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### Architecture Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    AI Client (Claude/OpenCode)               │\n└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘\n                              │\n              ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐\n              │                               │\n              ▼                               ▼\n┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n│   CLI Approach (Primary)│     │   MCP Approach (Fallback)   │\n│   browser-debugger-cli  │     │   Chrome DevTools MCP       │\n│                         │     │   via Code Mode             │\n│   - Direct terminal     │     │   - call_tool_chain()       │\n│   - Self-documenting    │     │   - Type-safe invocation    │\n│   - Unix composable     │     │   - Multi-tool integration  │\n│   - All 644 CDP methods │     │   - 26 exposed tools        │\n└────────────┬────────────┘     └──────────────┬──────────────┘\n             │                                  │\n             └──────────────┬───────────────────┘\n                            │\n                            ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n              │    Chrome DevTools Protocol │\n              │    (CDP via WebSocket)      │\n              │    Port 9222 (localhost)    │\n              └─────────────┬───────────────┘\n                            │\n                            ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n              │    Chrome/Chromium/Edge     │\n              └─────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### MCP Architecture Detail\n\nWhen using MCP, Chrome DevTools routes through Code Mode, not directly to your AI client:\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Your AI Client (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code)                │\n│  - CLI: bdg command (direct, standalone, no Code Mode)          │\n│  - MCP: 4 Code Mode tools → chrome_devtools_1.*                 │\n└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │ (MCP only)\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Code Mode MCP (configured in opencode.json)                    │\n│  - Reads .utcp_config.json for Chrome DevTools provider         │\n└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Chrome DevTools Provider (configured in .utcp_config.json)     │\n│  - 26 tools via chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool}      │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nChrome DevTools' 26 MCP tools would consume roughly 78k tokens if exposed natively. Code Mode provides on-demand access with 98% context reduction.\n\n### Performance Targets\n\n| Operation             | Target | Typical |\n| --------------------- | ------ | ------- |\n| Session startup       | <5s    | ~3s     |\n| Screenshot capture    | <2s    | ~1s     |\n| Console log retrieval | <1s    | ~500ms  |\n| DOM query             | <500ms | ~200ms  |\n| HAR export            | <3s    | ~2s     |\n\n---\n\n## 2. PREREQUISITES\n\n### Required Tools\n\n- **Node.js 18 or higher**\n  ```bash\n  node --version\n  # Should show v18.x or higher\n  ```\n\n- **npm** (comes with Node.js)\n  ```bash\n  npm --version\n  ```\n\n- **Chrome/Chromium Browser**, one of:\n  - Google Chrome (recommended)\n  - Chromium\n  - Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based)\n\n### Platform Support\n\n| Platform    | Status         | Notes                             |\n| ----------- | -------------- | --------------------------------- |\n| **macOS**   | Native support | Recommended                       |\n| **Linux**   | Native support | May need sandbox configuration    |\n| **Windows** | WSL only       | PowerShell and Git Bash not supported |\n\n### Common Setup Issues\n\n**Chrome path not found:**\n```bash\n# macOS\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n\n# Linux\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n# or\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/chromium-browser\"\n```\n\n**Windows users:** WSL is required. Install it first with: `wsl --install`\n\n---\n\n## 3. INSTALLATION\n\n### Step 1: Install browser-debugger-cli\n\n```bash\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### Step 2: Verify the binary\n\n```bash\n# Confirm installation\ncommand -v bdg || echo \"Installation failed\"\n\n# Check version\nbdg --version\n\n# List available CDP domains\nbdg --list\n```\n\n### Step 3: Test basic operation\n\n```bash\n# Start a session\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n\n# Take a screenshot\nbdg screenshot test.png 2>&1\n\n# Check console logs\nbdg console logs 2>&1\n\n# Stop the session\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Confirm screenshot was created\nls -la test.png\n```\n\n### Step 4: Configure Chrome path if needed\n\n```bash\n# macOS\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n\n# Linux\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n\n# Add to your shell profile for persistence\necho 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\nsource ~/.zshrc\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_1_complete`\n\nRun these checks to confirm prerequisites are met:\n\n| Check         | Command                  | Expected                     |\n| ------------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------- |\n| CLI installed | `command -v bdg`         | Path to bdg binary           |\n| Version       | `bdg --version`          | `browser-debugger-cli@x.x.x` |\n| CDP domains   | `bdg --list \\| head -5`  | `Page, DOM, Network...`      |\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix the issue before continuing.\n\n### Validation: `phase_2_complete`\n\nConfirm CLI installation is complete:\n\n| Check          | Command                             | Expected          |\n| -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------------- |\n| Session starts | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1`      | No errors         |\n| Screenshot     | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` | File created      |\n| Session stops  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                     | Clean exit        |\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 4. CONFIGURATION\n\nMCP is optional. Configure it only if you need multi-tool workflows or when CLI is not available.\n\n### MCP Approach: Code Mode Provider\n\nWhen using MCP, Chrome DevTools is a Code Mode provider, not a standalone MCP server.\n\n| Aspect             | What This Means                                                                |\n| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| **Configuration**  | Chrome DevTools goes in `.utcp_config.json`, not `opencode.json`               |\n| **Access Method**  | Tools are accessed via Code Mode's `call_tool_chain()`                         |\n| **Prerequisite**   | Code Mode MCP must be installed first. See [Code Mode INSTALL_GUIDE.md](../mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md) section 4 |\n| **Context Cost**   | Your AI sees only 4 Code Mode tools (~1.6k tokens), not 26 Chrome DevTools tools |\n| **Naming Pattern** | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool_name}`                              |\n\n### When to Use MCP\n\nUse MCP when you:\n- Already use Code Mode for other tools such as external services or Figma\n- Need to chain browser operations with other MCP tools\n- Require parallel browser testing to compare multiple sites at once\n- Need complex multi-step automation in TypeScript\n- Require type-safe tool invocation\n\n### Step 1: Verify Code Mode\n\nConfirm Code Mode is configured in your MCP settings. Use `.mcp.json` for Claude Code or `opencode.json` for OpenCode:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"code_mode\": {\n      \"command\": \"node\",\n      \"args\": [\"/path/to/code-mode-mcp/dist/index.js\"],\n      \"env\": {\n        \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/project/.utcp_config.json\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add Chrome DevTools to UTCP Config\n\nAdd the following to `.utcp_config.json`. Use `--isolated=true` to give each instance its own independent browser:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"manual_call_templates\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"chrome_devtools_1\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n            \"env\": {}\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_2\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"chrome_devtools_2\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n            \"env\": {}\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nRegister multiple instances for parallel testing. Each needs `--isolated=true`.\n\n### Step 3: Verify MCP tools\n\n```typescript\n// In Code Mode context\nconst tools = await list_tools();\nconsole.log(tools.filter(t => t.includes('chrome_devtools')));\n// Expected: chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page, chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot, etc.\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_3_complete`\n\n| Check            | Method                  | Expected                |\n| ---------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------- |\n| Config exists    | `cat .utcp_config.json` | chrome_devtools entry   |\n| Tools registered | `list_tools()`          | chrome_devtools_* tools |\n| Server starts    | Navigate to a URL       | No connection errors    |\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review your UTCP config and restart your AI client session.\n\n### Security Considerations\n\nPort 9222 runs the Chrome DevTools Protocol for remote debugging.
