It grants full browser control.\n\n**Risks:**\n- Full browser control to anyone with port access\n- Cookie and session theft\n- JavaScript injection\n- Local file system access via browser\n\n**Mitigations:**\n\n| Mitigation             | Implementation                             |\n| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |\n| Bind to localhost only | `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1`     |\n| Firewall rules         | Block port 9222 from external access       |\n| Development only       | Never expose on production networks        |\n| Network isolation      | Use in CI/CD with isolated networking      |\n\n```bash\n# Verify the port is localhost-only\nlsof -i :9222\n# Should show 127.0.0.1:9222, not *:9222\n```\n\n**Linux sandbox security:**\n\nChrome's sandbox provides process isolation. Avoid disabling it unless necessary.\n\n| Option                     | Security Level | When to Use               |\n| -------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------- |\n| Default sandbox            | Best           | Standard installations    |\n| `--disable-setuid-sandbox` | Medium         | When user namespaces work |\n| `--no-sandbox`             | Poor           | Last resort only          |\n\n**Preferred approach, from most to least secure:**\n\n1. Run in a container with proper permissions:\n   ```bash\n   docker run --cap-add=SYS_ADMIN chromium\n   ```\n\n2. Enable user namespaces:\n   ```bash\n   sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n   ```\n\n3. Disable setuid sandbox only:\n   ```bash\n   export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n   ```\n\n4. No-sandbox as a last resort:\n   ```bash\n   export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n   ```\n\n**CI/CD security flags:**\n```bash\nexport CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-gpu --disable-dev-shm-usage --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1\"\n```\n\n| Flag                                   | Purpose                        |\n| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n| `--disable-gpu`                        | Prevent GPU issues in headless |\n| `--disable-dev-shm-usage`              | Avoid shared memory issues     |\n| `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1` | Localhost binding only         |\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFICATION\n\n### One-Command Health Check\n\n```bash\n# Complete CLI verification in one command\nbdg --version 2>&1 && \\\n  bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \\\n  bdg screenshot /tmp/verify.png 2>&1 && \\\n  bdg stop 2>&1 && \\\n  ls -la /tmp/verify.png && \\\n  echo \"SUCCESS: All checks passed\" || \\\n  echo \"FAILED: Check error output above\"\n```\n\n### Full Verification Checklist\n\n| #   | Check          | Command                             | Expected Result                 |\n| --- | -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n| 1   | CLI installed  | `command -v bdg`                    | `/usr/local/bin/bdg` or similar |\n| 2   | Version        | `bdg --version`                     | `browser-debugger-cli@x.x.x`   |\n| 3   | CDP domains    | `bdg --list \\| wc -l`               | 53+ domains                     |\n| 4   | Session start  | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1`      | No errors                       |\n| 5   | Session status | `bdg status 2>&1`                   | `{\"state\": \"active\"}`           |\n| 6   | Screenshot     | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` | File created                    |\n| 7   | Session stop   | `bdg stop 2>&1`                     | Clean exit                      |\n\n### Verify in Your AI Client\n\nIn Claude Code or OpenCode, run:\n```\n> Take a screenshot of https://example.com\n```\n\nExpected result: bdg commands execute and a screenshot is captured and displayed.\n\n### Validation: `phase_4_complete`\n\nAll 7 checklist items above pass with no errors.\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** The system is not ready for use until all checks pass.\n\n### Validation: `phase_5_complete`\n\nYour AI client successfully captures a screenshot on request. The system is operational.\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails** - Check MCP configuration, restart your AI client, and review [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).\n\n---\n\n## 6. USAGE\n\n### Pattern 1: Quick Screenshot (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg screenshot output.png 2>&1\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Console Log Analysis (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg console logs 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level == \"error\")'\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Network Monitoring (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\nsleep 5\nbdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Analyze slow requests\njq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Cookie Manipulation (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n\n# Get cookies\nbdg network cookies 2>&1\n\n# Set a cookie\nbdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n  \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n  \"value\": \"secret-123\",\n  \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n  \"secure\": true\n}' 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pattern 5: Single Instance (MCP Fallback)\n\nWhen CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n  const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  const logs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n  return { screenshot, logs };\n`)\n```\n\n### Pattern 6: Parallel Instances (MCP)\n\nCompare multiple sites at the same time:\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  // Instance 1: Production\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n  });\n\n  // Instance 2: Staging (isolated browser)\n  await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n  });\n\n  // Capture both screenshots\n  const prod = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  const staging = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n\n  return { production: prod, staging: staging };\n`)\n```\n\n### Tool Selection Guide\n\n| Scenario                 | Tool | Why                         |\n| ------------------------ | ---- | --------------------------- |\n| Quick screenshot         | CLI  | Fastest, lowest tokens      |\n| Console log check        | CLI  | Pipe to jq for filtering    |\n| Network trace            | CLI  | HAR export built in         |\n| Cookie manipulation      | CLI  | Full CDP access             |\n| Multi-tool workflow      | MCP  | Chain with other tools      |\n| Parallel browser testing | MCP  | Multiple isolated instances |\n| CDP method discovery     | CLI  | `--list`, `--describe`      |\n\n### bdg Command Reference\n\n**Session Commands**\n\n| Command      | Description                     | Example                        |\n| ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n| `bdg <url>`  | Open URL, start browser session | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1` |\n| `bdg status` | Check session status            | `bdg status 2>&1`              |\n| `bdg stop`   | Stop browser, clean up session  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                |\n\n**Helper Commands**\n\n| Command                      | Description             | Example                             |\n| ---------------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------- |\n| `bdg screenshot <path>`      | Capture page screenshot | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` |\n| `bdg console logs`           | Get console messages    | `bdg console logs 2>&1 \\| jq '.'`   |\n| `bdg network cookies`        | Get all cookies         | `bdg network cookies 2>&1`          |\n| `bdg dom query \"<selector>\"` | Query DOM elements      | `bdg dom query \".my-class\" 2>&1`    |\n| `bdg js \"<expression>\"`      | Execute JavaScript      | `bdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1`      |\n| `bdg har export <path>`      | Export network as HAR   | `bdg har export trace.har 2>&1`     |\n\n**Discovery Commands**\n\n| Command                   | Description               | Example                                 |\n| ------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| `bdg --version`           | Show CLI version          | `bdg --version`                         |\n| `bdg --list`              | List all CDP domains (53) | `bdg --list`                            |\n| `bdg --describe <domain>` | Show domain methods       | `bdg --describe Page`                   |\n| `bdg --describe <method>` | Show method signature     | `bdg --describe Page.captureScreenshot` |\n| `bdg --search <term>`     | Search CDP methods        | `bdg --search screenshot`               |\n\n**Raw CDP Commands**\n\n| Command                     | Description         | Example                                                     |\n| --------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `bdg cdp <method>`          | Execute CDP method  | `bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1`                                  |\n| `bdg cdp <method> '<json>'` | CDP with parameters | `bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\"name\":\"x\",\"value\":\"y\"}' 2>&1` |\n\n**Error Handling Pattern**\n\nAlways use `2>&1` to capture stderr for error handling:\n\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\ntrap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\ncommand -v bdg || { echo \"Install bdg first\"; exit 1; }\nbdg \"$URL\" 2>&1 || exit 1\n# ... operations ...\n# Cleanup runs automatically on exit\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. FEATURES\n\n| Feature            | CLI (bdg)                                   | MCP (Code Mode)                         |\n| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| **Installation**   | `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Code Mode + UTCP config                 |\n| **Token Cost**     | Lowest                                      | Medium                                  |\n| **CDP Access**     | All 644 methods                             | MCP-exposed subset (26 tools)           |\n| **Self-Discovery** | `--list`, `--describe`, `--search`          | `search_tools()`                        |\n| **Best For**       | Quick debugging, inspection                 | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers |\n\n**Core capabilities available through both approaches:**\n- Screenshot capture\n- DOM inspection and querying\n- Console log access\n- Network monitoring and HAR export\n- CSS debugging\n- JavaScript execution\n- Cookie management\n- Full Chrome DevTools Protocol access\n\n---\n\n## 8. EXAMPLES\n\n### Example 1: Full Debug Session\n\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\n# Complete debug session with error handling\ntrap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\n\n# Verify CLI is available\ncommand -v bdg || { echo \"Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\"; exit 1; }\n\n# Start session\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1 || exit 1\n\n# Capture initial state\nbdg screenshot before.png 2>&1\nbdg console logs 2>&1 > console-before.json\n\n# Enable network monitoring\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\nsleep 3\n\n# Export network trace\nbdg har export session.har 2>&1\nbdg screenshot after.png 2>&1\n\necho \"Debug session complete. Files: before.png, after.png, session.har, console-before.json\"\n```\n\n### Example 2: JavaScript Execution\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n\n# Get page title\nbdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1\n\n# Count links on page\nbdg js \"document.querySelectorAll('a').length\" 2>&1\n\n# Get all link hrefs\nbdg js \"Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)\" 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Example 3: DOM Query and Interaction\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n\n# Query elements by class\nbdg dom query \".nav-link\" 2>&1\n\n# Click a button via JavaScript\nbdg js \"document.querySelector('#submit-btn').click()\" 2>&1\n\n# Check result after click\nbdg screenshot post-click.png 2>&1\nbdg console logs 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Example 4: Network Analysis\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n\n# Wait for full page load activity\nsleep 5\nbdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Find slow requests (over 1 second)\njq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n\n# Find failed requests\njq '.log.entries[] | select(.response.status >= 400) | {url: .request.url, status: .response.status}' network-trace.har\n```\n\n### Example 5: Parallel Comparison (MCP)\n\n```typescript\n// Compare production and staging side by side\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n  });\n  await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n  });\n\n  const prodScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  const stagingScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n  const prodLogs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n  const stagingLogs = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_list_console_messages({});\n\n  return {\n    production: { screenshot: prodScreenshot, logs: prodLogs },\n    staging: { screenshot: stagingScreenshot, logs: stagingLogs }\n  };\n`)\n```\n\n### Example 6: Cookie Session Management\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n\n# Export current cookies\nbdg network cookies 2>&1 > cookies.json\n\n# Inject an auth token\nbdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n  \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n  \"value\": \"test-session-token\",\n  \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n  \"path\": \"/\",\n  \"secure\": true,\n  \"httpOnly\": true\n}' 2>&1\n\n# Reload to test authenticated state\nbdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1\nbdg screenshot authenticated.png 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING\n\n### Error/Cause/Fix Reference\n\n| Error | Cause | Fix |\n| ----- | ----- | --- |\n| `command not found: bdg` | CLI not installed or not in PATH | Run `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha`. Check PATH with `npm config get prefix` |\n| `Error: Could not find Chrome` | Chrome path not set or binary missing | Set `CHROME_PATH` env var. See path examples below |\n| `Error: No active session` | Session not started or crashed | Run `bdg stop 2>&1` then retry `bdg <url> 2>&1` |\n| `Failed to move to new namespace` | Linux sandbox restriction | Enable user namespaces or set `CHROME_FLAGS`. See Linux sandbox section |\n| `bdg fails on Windows` | Native Windows not supported | Install WSL with `wsl --install`, then install bdg inside WSL |\n| Chrome DevTools tools missing in Code Mode | UTCP config missing or disabled | Check `.utcp_config.json`, confirm `disabled: false`, restart your session |\n| MCP server fails to start | Port conflict or config error | Run `lsof -i :9222` and kill conflicting processes |\n| `EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::9222` | Port 9222 already taken | Run `kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)` to free the port |\n\n### CLI: Command Not Found\n\n```bash\n# Install bdg\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n\n# Or fix PATH\nnpm config get prefix\nexport PATH=\"$(npm config get prefix)/bin:$PATH\"\n\n# Verify\ncommand -v bdg\n```\n\n### CLI: Chrome Not Found\n\n```bash\n# macOS\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n\n# Linux\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n\n# Confirm Chrome exists at the path\nls -la \"$CHROME_PATH\"\n\n# Add to your shell profile\necho 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\n```\n\n### CLI: Session Won't Start\n\n```bash\n# Force stop any existing sessions\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Find zombie Chrome processes\nps aux | grep -i chrome | grep -i debug\n\n# Kill zombie processes\npkill -f \"chrome.*remote-debugging\"\n\n# Retry\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n```\n\n### CLI: Sandbox Errors (Linux)\n\nFix in order of preference:\n\n```bash\n# Option 1: Enable user namespaces (preferred)\nsudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n\n# Option 2: Disable setuid sandbox only\nexport CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n\n# Option 3: No sandbox (least secure, last resort)\nexport CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n```\n\n### CLI: Windows Not Supported\n\n```bash\n# Install WSL\nwsl --install\n\n# Inside WSL, install bdg\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### MCP: Tool Not Found\n\n1. Open `.utcp_config.json` and confirm the chrome_devtools entry exists\n2. Check that `disabled` is not set to `true`\n3. Restart your Claude Code or OpenCode session\n4. Run `list_tools()` and look for chrome_devtools_* entries\n\n### MCP: Connection Failed\n\n```bash\n# Test the MCP server directly\nnpx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --version\n\n# Check for port conflicts\nlsof -i :9222\n\n# Kill any conflicting processes\nkill $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n```\n\n### MCP: Port Already in Use\n\n```bash\n# Find the process using port 9222\nlsof -i :9222\n\n# Kill it\nkill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n\n# Confirm the port is free\nlsof -i :9222\n# Should return nothing\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. RESOURCES\n\n### Related Documentation\n\n| Document        | Location                                                                  | Purpose              |\n| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |\n| SKILL.md        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/SKILL.md`                      | Complete workflows   |\n| CDP Patterns    | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/cdp_patterns.md`    | Domain patterns      |\n| Troubleshooting | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/troubleshooting.md` | Detailed fixes       |\n| Examples        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/examples/README.md`            | Production templates |\n| Code Mode Guide | `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md`                          | Code Mode setup      |\n\n### Configuration Paths\n\n| Client  | Configuration                 | Purpose                      |\n| ------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- |\n| **CLI** | `CHROME_PATH` env var         | Browser location             |\n| **MCP** | `.mcp.json` / `opencode.json` | Code Mode server             |\n| **MCP** | `.utcp_config.json`           | Chrome DevTools registration |\n\n### MCP Tools Reference (26 tools)\n\n| Tool                    | Purpose            | CLI Equivalent                                   |\n| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |\n| `navigate_page`         | Navigate to URL    | `bdg <url>`                                      |\n| `take_screenshot`       | Capture screenshot | `bdg screenshot`                                 |\n| `list_console_messages` | Get console logs   | `bdg console logs`                               |\n| `resize_page`           | Set viewport size  | `bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`     |\n| `click`                 | Click element      | `bdg js \"document.querySelector('...').click()\"` |\n| `type_text`             | Type into element  | `bdg js \"...\"`                                   |\n| `get_page_content`      | Get page HTML      | `bdg dom query \"html\"`                           |\n\n**Tool naming convention:** `{instance}.{instance}_{tool_name}`\n\n```typescript\n// Example invocations\nchrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })\nchrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({})\nchrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })  // parallel instance\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference Card\n\n### Installation\n\n```bash\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### Discovery\n\n```bash\nbdg --list                    # List all CDP domains\nbdg --describe Page           # Show domain methods\nbdg --search screenshot       # Find methods by keyword\n```\n\n### Session Control\n\n```bash\nbdg <url>                     # Start session\nbdg status                    # Check session state\nbdg stop                      # Stop session\n```\n\n### Common Operations\n\n```bash\nbdg screenshot <path>         # Capture screenshot\nbdg console logs              # Get console messages\nbdg network cookies           # Get all cookies\nbdg dom query \"<selector>\"    # Query DOM\nbdg js \"<expression>\"         # Execute JavaScript\nbdg har export <path>         # Export HAR file\n```\n\n### MCP Quick Start\n\n```typescript\n// When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n  const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  return screenshot;\n`)\n```\n\n### Environment Setup\n\n```bash\n# Add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n# Linux: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n```\n\n### Validation Checkpoints Summary\n\n| Checkpoint          | Meaning                              |\n| ------------------- | ------------------------------------ |\n| `phase_1_complete`  | Prerequisites validated              |\n| `phase_2_complete`  | CLI installation complete            |\n| `phase_3_complete`  | Configuration complete               |\n| `phase_4_complete`  | Verification complete                |\n| `phase_5_complete`  | System operational                   |\n\n---\n\n## Version History\n\n| Version | Date       | Changes                                                   |\n| ------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |\n| 2.1.0   | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, formal checkpoints, STOP blocks   |\n| 2.0.0   | 2025-10-01 | Two-approach architecture, MCP fallback, security section |\n| 1.0.0   | 2025-06-01 | Initial CLI guide                                         |\n\n---\n\n**Need help?** See [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) or load the `mcp-chrome-devtools` skill for detailed #!/usr/bin/env bash\n2: # Animation Testing Script\n3: # Purpose: Test animation performance and visual state with assertions\n4: # Usage: ./animation-testing.sh [URL] [SELECTOR] [TRIGGER_CLASS]\n5: # Example: ./animation-testing.sh https://example.com \".hero\" \"animate-in\"\n6: \n7: set -euo pipefail\n8: \n9: # Configuration\n10: URL=\"${1:-https://example.com}\"\n11: SELECTOR=\"${2:-.animated-element}\"\n12: TRIGGER_CLASS=\"${3:-animate}\"\n13: OUTPUT_DIR=\"animation-tests\"\n14: DATE_STAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)\n15: \n16: # Performance thresholds\n17: MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT=3\n18: MAX_RECALC_COUNT=5\n19: MAX_TASK_DURATION=200  # milliseconds\n20: \n21: # Create output directory\n22: mkdir -p \"$OUTPUT_DIR\"\n23: \n24: echo \"🎬 Testing animation performance for: $URL\"\n25: echo \"🎯 Target selector: $SELECTOR\"\n26: echo \"🔄 Trigger class: $TRIGGER_CLASS\"\n27: \n28: # Start browser session\n29: echo \"🌐 Starting browser session...\"\n30: bdg \"$URL\" 2>&1\n31: \n32: # Wait for page load\n33: echo \"⏳ Waiting for page load (2 seconds)...\"\n34: sleep 2\n35: \n36: # Capture before animation\n37: echo \"📸 Capturing state before animation...\"\n38: bdg dom screenshot \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-before-${DATE_STAMP}.png\" 2>&1\n39: \n40: # Clear performance metrics\n41: bdg cdp Performance.enable > /dev/null 2>&1\n42: \n43: # Trigger animation\n44: echo \"🎬 Triggering animation...\"\n45: bdg dom eval \"document.querySelector('$SELECTOR').classList.add('$TRIGGER_CLASS')\" 2>&1\n46: \n47: # Wait for animation to complete (adjust based on your animation duration)\n48: sleep 1\n49: \n50: # Capture after animation\n51: echo \"📸 Capturing state after animation...\"\n52: bdg dom screenshot \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-after-${DATE_STAMP}.png\" 2>&1\n53: \n54: # Get performance metrics\n55: echo \"📊 Capturing performance metrics...\"\n56: METRICS=$(bdg cdp Performance.getMetrics 2>&1)\n57: echo \"$METRICS\" > \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-metrics-${DATE_STAMP}.json\"\n58: \n59: # Capture console logs (check for errors)\n60: echo \"📝 Capturing console logs...\"\n61: bdg console --list > \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-console-${DATE_STAMP}.json\" 2>&1\n62: \n63: # Stop session\n64: echo \"🛑 Stopping browser session...\"\n65: bdg stop 2>&1\n66: \n67: # Parse metrics\n68: LAYOUT_COUNT=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"LayoutCount\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n69: RECALC_COUNT=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"RecalcStyleCount\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n70: TASK_DURATION=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"TaskDuration\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n71: LAYOUT_DURATION=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"LayoutDuration\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n72: \n73: # Check for console errors\n74: CONSOLE_ERRORS=$(jq -r '.[] | select(.level==\"error\") | .text' \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-console-${DATE_STAMP}.json\" 2>/dev/null || echo \"\")\n75: \n76: # Generate report\n77: echo \"📋 Generating performance report...\"\n78: cat > \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\" <<EOF\n79: Animation Testing Report\n80: Generated: $(date)\n81: URL: $URL\n82: Selector: $SELECTOR\n83: Trigger Class: $TRIGGER_CLASS\n84: \n85: Performance Metrics:\n86:   Layout Count: $LAYOUT_COUNT (threshold: ≤$MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT)\n87:   Style Recalc Count: $RECALC_COUNT (threshold: ≤$MAX_RECALC_COUNT)\n88:   Task Duration: ${TASK_DURATION}ms (threshold: ≤${MAX_TASK_DURATION}ms)\n89:   Layout Duration: ${LAYOUT_DURATION}ms\n90: \n91: Visual Captures:\n92:   Before: animation-before-${DATE_STAMP}.png\n93:   After: animation-after-${DATE_STAMP}.png\n94: \n95: Console Errors:\n96: $(if [ -n \"$CONSOLE_ERRORS\" ]; then echo \"$CONSOLE_ERRORS\"; else echo \"  No errors\"; fi)\n97: \n98: Performance Assessment:\n99: EOF\n100: \n101: # Run assertions\n102: FAIL=0\n103: \n104: echo \"🔍 Running performance assertions...\"\n105: \n106: if [ \"$LAYOUT_COUNT\" -gt \"$MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT\" ]; then\n107: bash\n# Animation Testing Script\n# Purpose: Test animation performance and visual state with assertions\n# Usage: ./animation-testing.sh [URL] [SELECTOR] [TRIGGER_CLASS]\n# Example: ./animation-testing.sh https://example.com \".hero\" \"animate-in\"\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\n# Configuration\nURL=\"${1:-https://example.com}\"\nSELECTOR=\"${2:-.animated-element}\"\nTRIGGER_CLASS=\"${3:-animate}\"\nOUTPUT_DIR=\"animation-tests\"\nDATE_STAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)\n\n# Performance thresholds\nMAX_LAYOUT_COUNT=3\nMAX_RECALC_COUNT=5\nMAX_TASK_DURATION=200  # milliseconds\n\n# Create output directory\nmkdir -p \"$OUTPUT_DIR\"\n\necho \"🎬 Testing animation performance for: $URL\"\necho \"🎯 Target selector: $SELECTOR\"\necho \"🔄 Trigger class: $TRIGGER_CLASS\"\n\n# Start browser session\necho \"🌐 Starting browser session...\"\nbdg \"$URL\" 2>&1\n\n# Wait for page load\necho \"⏳ Waiting for page load (2 seconds)...\"\nsleep 2\n\n# Capture before animation\necho \"📸 Capturing state before animation...\"\nbdg dom screenshot \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-before-${DATE_STAMP}.png\" 2>&1\n\n# Clear performance metrics\nbdg cdp Performance.enable > /dev/null 2>&1\n\n# Trigger animation\necho \"🎬 Triggering animation...\"\nbdg dom eval \"document.querySelector('$SELECTOR').classList.add('$TRIGGER_CLASS')\" 2>&1\n\n# Wait for animation to complete (adjust based on your animation duration)\nsleep 1\n\n# Capture after animation\necho \"📸 Capturing state after animation...\"\nbdg dom screenshot \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-after-${DATE_STAMP}.png\" 2>&1\n\n# Get performance metrics\necho \"📊 Capturing performance metrics...\"\nMETRICS=$(bdg cdp Performance.getMetrics 2>&1)\necho \"$METRICS\" > \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-metrics-${DATE_STAMP}.json\"\n\n# Capture console logs (check for errors)\necho \"📝 Capturing console logs...\"\nbdg console --list > \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-console-${DATE_STAMP}.json\" 2>&1\n\n# Stop session\necho \"🛑 Stopping browser session...\"\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Parse metrics\nLAYOUT_COUNT=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"LayoutCount\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\nRECALC_COUNT=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"RecalcStyleCount\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\nTASK_DURATION=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"TaskDuration\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\nLAYOUT_DURATION=$(echo \"$METRICS\" | jq '.result.metrics[] | select(.name==\"LayoutDuration\") | .value' 2>/dev/null || echo \"0\")\n\n# Check for console errors\nCONSOLE_ERRORS=$(jq -r '.[] | select(.level==\"error\") | .text' \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-console-${DATE_STAMP}.json\" 2>/dev/null || echo \"\")\n\n# Generate report\necho \"📋 Generating performance report...\"\ncat > \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\" <<EOF\nAnimation Testing Report\nGenerated: $(date)\nURL: $URL\nSelector: $SELECTOR\nTrigger Class: $TRIGGER_CLASS\n\nPerformance Metrics:\n  Layout Count: $LAYOUT_COUNT (threshold: ≤$MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT)\n  Style Recalc Count: $RECALC_COUNT (threshold: ≤$MAX_RECALC_COUNT)\n  Task Duration: ${TASK_DURATION}ms (threshold: ≤${MAX_TASK_DURATION}ms)\n  Layout Duration: ${LAYOUT_DURATION}ms\n\nVisual Captures:\n  Before: animation-before-${DATE_STAMP}.png\n  After: animation-after-${DATE_STAMP}.png\n\nConsole Errors:\n$(if [ -n \"$CONSOLE_ERRORS\" ]; then echo \"$CONSOLE_ERRORS\"; else echo \"  No errors\"; fi)\n\nPerformance Assessment:\nEOF\n\n# Run assertions\nFAIL=0\n\necho \"🔍 Running performance assertions...\"\n\nif [ \"$LAYOUT_COUNT\" -gt \"$MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT\" ]; then\n  echo \"  ❌ FAIL: Layout count exceeds threshold ($LAYOUT_COUNT > $MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT)\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\n  FAIL=1\nelse\n  echo \"  ✅ PASS: Layout count within threshold ($LAYOUT_COUNT ≤ $MAX_LAYOUT_COUNT)\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nfi\n\nif [ \"$RECALC_COUNT\" -gt \"$MAX_RECALC_COUNT\" ]; then\n  echo \"  ❌ FAIL: Style recalc count exceeds threshold ($RECALC_COUNT > $MAX_RECALC_COUNT)\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\n  FAIL=1\nelse\n  echo \"  ✅ PASS: Style recalc count within threshold ($RECALC_COUNT ≤ $MAX_RECALC_COUNT)\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nfi\n\nif [ \"$(echo \"$TASK_DURATION > $MAX_TASK_DURATION\" | bc -l)\" -eq 1 ]; then\n  echo \"  ❌ FAIL: Task duration exceeds threshold (${TASK_DURATION}ms > ${MAX_TASK_DURATION}ms)\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\n  FAIL=1\nelse\n  echo \"  ✅ PASS: Task duration within threshold (${TASK_DURATION}ms ≤ ${MAX_TASK_DURATION}ms)\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nfi\n\nif [ -n \"$CONSOLE_ERRORS\" ]; then\n  echo \"  ⚠️ WARN: Console errors detected during animation\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nfi\n\n# Summary\necho \"\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nif [ \"$FAIL\" -eq 0 ]; then\n  echo \"✅ Animation performance tests PASSED\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nelse\n  echo \"❌ Animation performance tests FAILED\" | tee -a \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\nfi\n\n# Display full report\necho \"\"\necho \"📋 Full Report:\"\ncat \"$OUTPUT_DIR/animation-report-${DATE_STAMP}.txt\"\n\necho \"\"\necho \"📁 Results saved to: $OUTPUT_DIR/\"\n\nexit \"$FAIL\"","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":147,"totalLines":147,"truncated":false}},"title":".opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/examples/animation-testing.sh","time":{"start":1780835222883,"end":1780835222886}},"id":"prt_ea20d05600015FXB6XwZDH0aPm","sessionID":"ses_15df359c2ffe4scutel76FimGQ","messageID":"msg_ea20cf28f001o83DPHb3wPw20V"}}
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-goal-opencode-plugin/review/observability-events.jsonl:63:{"schema_version":"1.0","event_id":"0c1c2bbe-0a5a-4396-94c6-914e737084f1","producer":"fanout-run","stream":"orchestration-status","subject":{"run_id":null,"label":"minimax-review","loop_type":null,"spec_folder":null},"event":"progress","status":"running","observed_at_iso":"2026-07-04T06:49:13.515Z","payload":{"event":"progress","label":"minimax-review","at":"2026-07-04T06:49:13.515Z","duration_ms":930056,"gauges":{"lag":2,"pending":0,"failed":0}}}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/109-skill-readme-standardization/012-mcp-chrome-devtools-readme/context/seats/iter-002/deepseek.jsonl:5:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780835206548,"sessionID":"ses_15df359c1ffegP1ICxrHMIv19i","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_03_gsj4m1VbHXX36HhzQDSA5029","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/INSTALL_GUIDE.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/INSTALL_GUIDE.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Chrome DevTools Installation Guide\n2: \n3: Complete installation and configuration guide for Chrome DevTools browser debugging, providing AI assistants with browser control through two approaches. The CLI tool (bdg) offers direct, fast, token-efficient browser debugging. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode for multi-tool orchestration and parallel browser testing. Covers screenshot capture, DOM inspection, console evaluation, network monitoring and performance profiling.\n4: \n5: > **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n6: > **Package:** `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | **Dependencies:** Node.js 18+, Chrome/Chromium\n7: \n8: **Version:** 2.1.0 | **Updated:** 2026-02-20 | **Protocol:** Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP)\n9: \n10: ---\n11: \n12: ## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n13: \n14: Copy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n15: \n16: ```\n17: I want to install the Chrome DevTools CLI (bdg) for browser debugging.\n18: \n19: Please help me:\n20: 1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n21: 2. Install browser-debugger-cli globally\n22: 3. Verify Chrome/Chromium is available\n23: 4. Test with a basic screenshot capture\n24: 5. (Optional) Configure MCP fallback if needed\n25: \n26: My platform is: [macOS / Linux / Windows with WSL]\n27: \n28: Guide me through each step with the exact commands needed.\n29: ```\n30: \n31: The AI will:\n32: - Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system\n33: - Install `browser-debugger-cli` globally\n34: - Test Chrome/Chromium availability\n35: - Run a basic screenshot test to confirm installation\n36: - Configure MCP fallback if you need multi-tool workflows\n37: \n38: **Expected setup time:** 3 to 5 minutes\n39: \n40: ### Quick Success Check (30 seconds)\n41: \n42: After installation, run these two tests immediately:\n43: \n44: 1. Open your terminal and run: `bdg --version`\n45: 2. Version output appears: CLI is working\n46: 3. In Claude Code, ask: \"Take a screenshot of example.com\"\n47: 4. Screenshot captured: full system is working\n48: \n49: Not working? Go to [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).\n50: \n51: ---\n52: \n53: ## 1. OVERVIEW\n54: \n55: Chrome DevTools gives AI assistants browser debugging capabilities through two approaches. The CLI (`bdg`) is the primary method: direct, fast and token-efficient. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode and suits multi-tool orchestration or parallel browser testing.\n56: \n57: ### Core Principle\n58: \n59: > **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint. Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.\n60: \n61: ### Source Repository\n62: \n63: | Property         | Value                                                                                       |\n64: | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n65: | **GitHub (CLI)** | [szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli](https://github.com/szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli)           |\n66: | **GitHub (MCP)** | [ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp](https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp) |\n67: | **npm (CLI)**    | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha`                                                                |\n68: | **npm (MCP)**    | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`                                                                |\n69: | **License**      | MIT / Apache-2.0                                                                            |\n70: \n71: ### Two Approaches\n72: \n73: | Approach            | Package                      | When to Use                             | Token Cost |\n74: | ------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ---------- |\n75: | **CLI (bdg)**       | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Single browser, quick tasks, debugging  | Lowest     |\n76: | **MCP (Code Mode)** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers | Higher     |\n77: \n78: Default to CLI. Use MCP only when CLI is not sufficient.\n79: \n80: ### Decision Flowchart\n81: \n82: ```\n83: Task received → Is bdg CLI available? (command -v bdg)\n84:                       │\n85:               ┌───────┴───────┐\n86:              YES              NO\n87:               │               │\n88:               ▼               ▼\n89:         Use CLI          Is Code Mode configured?\n90:         (fastest)               │\n91:                         ┌───────┴───────┐\n92:                        YES              NO\n93:                         │               │\n94:                         ▼               ▼\n95:                    Use MCP         Install CLI:\n96:                    (fallback)      npm i -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n97: ```\n98: \n99: ### Architecture Diagram\n100: \n101: ```\n102: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n103: │                    AI Client (Claude/OpenCode)               │\n104: └─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘\n105:                               │\n106:               ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐\n107:               │                               │\n108:               ▼                               ▼\n109: ┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n110: │   CLI Approach (Primary)│     │   MCP Approach (Fallback)   │\n111: │   browser-debugger-cli  │     │   Chrome DevTools MCP       │\n112: │                         │     │   via Code Mode             │\n113: │   - Direct terminal     │     │   - call_tool_chain()       │\n114: │   - Self-documenting    │     │   - Type-safe invocation    │\n115: │   - Unix composable     │     │   - Multi-tool integration  │\n116: │   - All 644 CDP methods │     │   - 26 exposed tools        │\n117: └────────────┬────────────┘     └──────────────┬──────────────┘\n118:              │                                  │\n119:              └──────────────┬───────────────────┘\n120:                             │\n121:                             ▼\n122:               ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n123:               │    Chrome DevTools Protocol │\n124:               │    (CDP via WebSocket)      │\n125:               │    Port 9222 (localhost)    │\n126:               └─────────────┬───────────────┘\n127:                             │\n128:                             ▼\n129:               ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n130:               │    Chrome/Chromium/Edge     │\n131:               └─────────────────────────────┘\n132: ```\n133: \n134: ### MCP Architecture Detail\n135: \n136: When using MCP, Chrome DevTools routes through Code Mode, not directly to your AI client:\n137: \n138: ```\n139: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n140: │  Your AI Client (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code)                │\n141: │  - CLI: bdg command (direct, standalone, no Code Mode)          │\n142: │  - MCP: 4 Code Mode tools → chrome_devtools_1.*                 │\n143: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘\n144:                            │ (MCP only)\n145:                            ▼\n146: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n147: │  Code Mode MCP (configured in opencode.json)                    │\n148: │  - Reads .utcp_config.json for Chrome DevTools provider         │\n149: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘\n150:                            ▼\n151: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n152: │  Chrome DevTools Provider (configured in .utcp_config.json)     │\n153: │  - 26 tools via chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool}      │\n154: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n155: ```\n156: \n157: Chrome DevTools' 26 MCP tools would consume roughly 78k tokens if exposed natively. Code Mode provides on-demand access with 98% context reduction.\n158: \n159: ### Performance Targets\n160: \n161: | Operation             | Target | Typical |\n162: | --------------------- | ------ | ------- |\n163: | Session startup       | <5s    | ~3s     |\n164: | Screenshot capture    | <2s    | ~1s     |\n165: | Console log retrieval | <1s    | ~500ms  |\n166: | DOM query             | <500ms | ~200ms  |\n167: | HAR export            | <3s    | ~2s     |\n168: \n169: ---\n170: \n171: ## 2. PREREQUISITES\n172: \n173: ### Required Tools\n174: \n175: - **Node.js 18 or higher**\n176:   ```bash\n177:   node --version\n178:   # Should show v18.x or higher\n179:   ```\n180: \n181: - **npm** (comes with Node.js)\n182:   ```bash\n183:   npm --version\n184:   ```\n185: \n186: - **Chrome/Chromium Browser**, one of:\n187:   - Google Chrome (recommended)\n188:   - Chromium\n189:   - Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based)\n190: \n191: ### Platform Support\n192: \n193: | Platform    | Status         | Notes                             |\n194: | ----------- | -------------- | --------------------------------- |\n195: | **macOS**   | Native support | Recommended                       |\n196: | **Linux**   | Native support | May need sandbox configuration    |\n197: | **Windows** | WSL only       | PowerShell and Git Bash not supported |\n198: \n199: ### Common Setup Issues\n200: \n201: **Chrome path not found:**\n202: ```bash\n203: # macOS\n204: export CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n205: \n206: # Linux\n207: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n208: # or\n209: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/chromium-browser\"\n210: ```\n211: \n212: **Windows users:** WSL is required. Install it first with: `wsl --install`\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 3. INSTALLATION\n217: \n218: ### Step 1: Install browser-debugger-cli\n219: \n220: ```bash\n221: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n222: ```\n223: \n224: ### Step 2: Verify the binary\n225: \n226: ```bash\n227: # Confirm installation\n228: command -v bdg || echo \"Installation failed\"\n229: \n230: # Check version\n231: bdg --version\n232: \n233: # List available CDP domains\n234: bdg --list\n235: ```\n236: \n237: ### Step 3: Test basic operation\n238: \n239: ```bash\n240: # Start a session\n241: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n242: \n243: # Take a screenshot\n244: bdg screenshot test.png 2>&1\n245: \n246: # Check console logs\n247: bdg console logs 2>&1\n248: \n249: # Stop the session\n250: bdg stop 2>&1\n251: \n252: # Confirm screenshot was created\n253: ls -la test.png\n254: ```\n255: \n256: ### Step 4: Configure Chrome path if needed\n257: \n258: ```bash\n259: # macOS\n260: export CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n261: \n262: # Linux\n263: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n264: \n265: # Add to your shell profile for persistence\n266: echo 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\n267: source ~/.zshrc\n268: ```\n269: \n270: ### Validation: `phase_1_complete`\n271: \n272: Run these checks to confirm prerequisites are met:\n273: \n274: | Check         | Command                  | Expected                     |\n275: | ------------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------- |\n276: | CLI installed | `command -v bdg`         | Path to bdg binary           |\n277: | Version       | `bdg --version`          | `browser-debugger-cli@x.x.x` |\n278: | CDP domains   | `bdg --list \\| head -5`  | `Page, DOM, Network...`      |\n279: \n280: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix the issue before continuing.\n281: \n282: ### Validation: `phase_2_complete`\n283: \n284: Confirm CLI installation is complete:\n285: \n286: | Check          | Command                             | Expected          |\n287: | -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------------- |\n288: | Session starts | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1`      | No errors         |\n289: | Screenshot     | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` | File created      |\n290: | Session stops  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                     | Clean exit        |\n291: \n292: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) before continuing.\n293: \n294: ---\n295: \n296: ## 4. CONFIGURATION\n297: \n298: MCP is optional. Configure it only if you need multi-tool workflows or when CLI is not available.\n299: \n300: ### MCP Approach: Code Mode Provider\n301: \n302: When using MCP, Chrome DevTools is a Code Mode provider, not a standalone MCP server.\n303: \n304: | Aspect             | What This Means                                                                |\n305: | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n306: | **Configuration**  | Chrome DevTools goes in `.utcp_config.json`, not `opencode.json`               |\n307: | **Access Method**  | Tools are accessed via Code Mode's `call_tool_chain()`                         |\n308: | **Prerequisite**   | Code Mode MCP must be installed first. See [Code Mode INSTALL_GUIDE.md](../mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md) section 4 |\n309: | **Context Cost**   | Your AI sees only 4 Code Mode tools (~1.6k tokens), not 26 Chrome DevTools tools |\n310: | **Naming Pattern** | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool_name}`                              |\n311: \n312: ### When to Use MCP\n313: \n314: Use MCP when you:\n315: - Already use Code Mode for other tools such as external services or Figma\n316: - Need to chain browser operations with other MCP tools\n317: - Require parallel browser testing to compare multiple sites at once\n318: - Need complex multi-step automation in TypeScript\n319: - Require type-safe tool invocation\n320: \n321: ### Step 1: Verify Code Mode\n322: \n323: Confirm Code Mode is configured in your MCP settings. Use `.mcp.json` for Claude Code or `opencode.json` for OpenCode:\n324: \n325: ```json\n326: {\n327:   \"mcpServers\": {\n328:     \"code_mode\": {\n329:       \"command\": \"node\",\n330:       \"args\": [\"/path/to/code-mode-mcp/dist/index.js\"],\n331:       \"env\": {\n332:         \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/project/.utcp_config.json\"\n333:       }\n334:     }\n335:   }\n336: }\n337: ```\n338: \n339: ### Step 2: Add Chrome DevTools to UTCP Config\n340: \n341: Add the following to `.utcp_config.json`. Use `--isolated=true` to give each instance its own independent browser:\n342: \n343: ```json\n344: {\n345:   \"manual_call_templates\": [\n346:     {\n347:       \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\",\n348:       \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n349:       \"config\": {\n350:         \"mcpServers\": {\n351:           \"chrome_devtools_1\": {\n352:             \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n353:             \"command\": \"npx\",\n354:             \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n355:             \"env\": {}\n356:           }\n357:         }\n358:       }\n359:     },\n360:     {\n361:       \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_2\",\n362:       \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n363:       \"config\": {\n364:         \"mcpServers\": {\n365:           \"chrome_devtools_2\": {\n366:             \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n367:             \"command\": \"npx\",\n368:             \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n369:             \"env\": {}\n370:           }\n371:         }\n372:       }\n373:     }\n374:   ]\n375: }\n376: ```\n377: \n378: Register multiple instances for parallel testing. Each needs `--isolated=true`.\n379: \n380: ### Step 3: Verify MCP tools\n381: \n382: ```typescript\n383: // In Code Mode context\n384: const tools = await list_tools();\n385: console.log(tools.filter(t => t.includes('chrome_devtools')));\n386: // Expected: chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page, chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot, etc.\n387: ```\n388: \n389: ### Validation: `phase_3_complete`\n390: \n391: | Check            | Method                  | Expected                |\n392: | ---------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------- |\n393: | Config exists    | `cat .utcp_config.json` | chrome_devtools entry   |\n394: | Tools registered | `list_tools()`          | chrome_devtools_* tools |\n395: | Server starts    | Navigate to a URL       | No connection errors    |\n396: \n397: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review your UTCP config and restart your AI client session.\n398: \n399: ### Security Considerations\n400: \n401: Port 9222 runs the Chrome DevTools Protocol for remote debugging. It grants full browser control.\n402: \n403: **Risks:**\n404: - Full browser control to anyone with port access\n405: - Cookie and session theft\n406: - JavaScript injection\n407: - Local file system access via browser\n408: \n409: **Mitigations:**\n410: \n411: | Mitigation             | Implementation                             |\n412: | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |\n413: | Bind to localhost only | `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1`     |\n414: | Firewall rules         | Block port 9222 from external access       |\n415: | Development only       | Never expose on production networks        |\n416: | Network isolation      | Use in CI/CD with isolated networking      |\n417: \n418: ```bash\n419: # Verify the port is localhost-only\n420: lsof -i :9222\n421: # Should show 127.0.0.1:9222, not *:9222\n422: ```\n423: \n424: **Linux sandbox security:**\n425: \n426: Chrome's sandbox provides process isolation. Avoid disabling it unless necessary.\n427: \n428: | Option                     | Security Level | When to Use               |\n429: | -------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------- |\n430: | Default sandbox            | Best           | Standard installations    |\n431: | `--disable-setuid-sandbox` | Medium         | When user namespaces work |\n432: | `--no-sandbox`             | Poor           | Last resort only          |\n433: \n434: **Preferred approach, from most to least secure:**\n435: \n436: 1. Run in a container with proper permissions:\n437:    ```bash\n438:    docker run --cap-add=SYS_ADMIN chromium\n439:    ```\n440: \n441: 2. Enable user namespaces:\n442:    ```bash\n443:    sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n444:    ```\n445: \n446: 3. Disable setuid sandbox only:\n447:    ```bash\n448:    export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n449:    ```\n450: \n451: 4. No-sandbox as a last resort:\n452:    ```bash\n453:    export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n454:    ```\n455: \n456: **CI/CD security flags:**\n457: ```bash\n458: export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-gpu --disable-dev-shm-usage --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1\"\n459: ```\n460: \n461: | Flag                                   | Purpose                        |\n462: | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n463: | `--disable-gpu`                        | Prevent GPU issues in headless |\n464: | `--disable-dev-shm-usage`              | Avoid shared memory issues     |\n465: | `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1` | Localhost binding only         |\n466: \n467: ---\n468: \n469: ## 5. VERIFICATION\n470: \n471: ### One-Command Health Check\n472: \n473: ```bash\n474: # Complete CLI verification in one command\n475: bdg --version 2>&1 && \\\n476:   bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \\\n477:   bdg screenshot /tmp/verify.png 2>&1 && \\\n478:   bdg stop 2>&1 && \\\n479:   ls -la /tmp/verify.png && \\\n480:   echo \"SUCCESS: All checks passed\" || \\\n481:   echo \"FAILED: Check error output above\"\n482: ```\n483: \n484: ### Full Verification Checklist\n485: \n486: | #   | Check          | Command                             | Expected Result                 |\n487: | --- | -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n488: | 1   | CLI installed  | `command -v bdg`                    | `/usr/local/bin/bdg` or similar |\n489: | 2   | Version        | `bdg --version`                     | `browser-debugger-cli@x.x.x`   |\n490: | 3   | CDP domains    | `bdg --list \\| wc -l`               | 53+ domains                     |\n491: | 4   | Session start  | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1`      | No errors                       |\n492: | 5   | Session status | `bdg status 2>&1`                   | `{\"state\": \"active\"}`           |\n493: | 6   | Screenshot     | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` | File created                    |\n494: | 7   | Session stop   | `bdg stop 2>&1`                     | Clean exit                      |\n495: \n496: ### Verify in Your AI Client\n497: \n498: In Claude Code or OpenCode, run:\n499: ```\n500: > Take a screenshot of https://example.com\n501: ```\n502: \n503: Expected result: bdg commands execute and a screenshot is captured and displayed.\n504: \n505: ### Validation: `phase_4_complete`\n506: \n507: All 7 checklist items above pass with no errors.\n508: \n509: ❌ **STOP if validation fails.** The system is not ready for use until all checks pass.\n510: \n511: ### Validation: `phase_5_complete`\n512: \n513: Your AI client successfully captures a screenshot on request. The system is operational.\n514: \n515: ❌ **STOP if validation fails** - Check MCP configuration, restart your AI client, and review [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).\n516: \n517: ---\n518: \n519: ## 6. USAGE\n520: \n521: ### Pattern 1: Quick Screenshot (CLI)\n522: \n523: ```bash\n524: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n525: bdg screenshot output.png 2>&1\n526: bdg stop 2>&1\n527: ```\n528: \n529: ### Pattern 2: Console Log Analysis (CLI)\n530: \n531: ```bash\n532: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n533: bdg console logs 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level == \"error\")'\n534: bdg stop 2>&1\n535: ```\n536: \n537: ### Pattern 3: Network Monitoring (CLI)\n538: \n539: ```bash\n540: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n541: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n542: sleep 5\n543: bdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\n544: bdg stop 2>&1\n545: \n546: # Analyze slow requests\n547: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n548: ```\n549: \n550: ### Pattern 4: Cookie Manipulation (CLI)\n551: \n552: ```bash\n553: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n554: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n555: \n556: # Get cookies\n557: bdg network cookies 2>&1\n558: \n559: # Set a cookie\n560: bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n561:   \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n562:   \"value\": \"secret-123\",\n563:   \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n564:   \"secure\": true\n565: }' 2>&1\n566: \n567: bdg stop 2>&1\n568: ```\n569: \n570: ### Pattern 5: Single Instance (MCP Fallback)\n571: \n572: When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\n573: \n574: ```typescript\n575: call_tool_chain(`\n576:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n577:   const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n578:   const logs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n579:   return { screenshot, logs };\n580: `)\n581: ```\n582: \n583: ### Pattern 6: Parallel Instances (MCP)\n584: \n585: Compare multiple sites at the same time:\n586: \n587: ```typescript\n588: call_tool_chain(`\n589:   // Instance 1: Production\n590:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n591:     url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n592:   });\n593: \n594:   // Instance 2: Staging (isolated browser)\n595:   await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n596:     url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n597:   });\n598: \n599:   // Capture both screenshots\n600:   const prod = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n601:   const staging = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n602: \n603:   return { production: prod, staging: staging };\n604: `)\n605: ```\n606: \n607: ### Tool Selection Guide\n608: \n609: | Scenario                 | Tool | Why                         |\n610: | ------------------------ | ---- | --------------------------- |\n611: | Quick screenshot         | CLI  | Fastest, lowest tokens      |\n612: | Console log check        | CLI  | Pipe to jq for filtering    |\n613: | Network trace            | CLI  | HAR export built in         |\n614: | Cookie manipulation      | CLI  | Full CDP access             |\n615: | Multi-tool workflow      | MCP  | Chain with other tools      |\n616: | Parallel browser testing | MCP  | Multiple isolated instances |\n617: | CDP method discovery     | CLI  | `--list`, `--describe`      |\n618: \n619: ### bdg Command Reference\n620: \n621: **Session Commands**\n622: \n623: | Command      | Description                     | Example                        |\n624: | ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n625: | `bdg <url>`  | Open URL, start browser session | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1` |\n626: | `bdg status` | Check session status            | `bdg status 2>&1`              |\n627: | `bdg stop`   | Stop browser, clean up session  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                |\n628: \n629: **Helper Commands**\n630: \n631: | Command                      | Description             | Example                             |\n632: | ---------------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------- |\n633: | `bdg screenshot <path>`      | Capture page screenshot | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` |\n634: | `bdg console logs`           | Get console messages    | `bdg console logs 2>&1 \\| jq '.'`   |\n635: | `bdg network cookies`        | Get all cookies         | `bdg network cookies 2>&1`          |\n636: | `bdg dom query \"<selector>\"` | Query DOM elements      | `bdg dom query \".my-class\" 2>&1`    |\n637: | `bdg js \"<expression>\"`      | Execute JavaScript      | `bdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1`      |\n638: | `bdg har export <path>`      | Export network as HAR   | `bdg har export trace.har 2>&1`     |\n639: \n640: **Discovery Commands**\n641: \n642: | Command                   | Description               | Example                                 |\n643: | ------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n644: | `bdg --version`           | Show CLI version          | `bdg --version`                         |\n645: | `bdg --list`              | List all CDP domains (53) | `bdg --list`                            |\n646: | `bdg --describe <domain>` | Show domain methods       | `bdg --describe Page`                   |\n647: | `bdg --describe <method>` | Show method signature     | `bdg --describe Page.captureScreenshot` |\n648: | `bdg --search <term>`     | Search CDP methods        | `bdg --search screenshot`               |\n649: \n650: **Raw CDP Commands**\n651: \n652: | Command                     | Description         | Example                                                     |\n653: | --------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |\n654: | `bdg cdp <method>`          | Execute CDP method  | `bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1`                                  |\n655: | `bdg cdp <method> '<json>'` | CDP with parameters | `bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\"name\":\"x\",\"value\":\"y\"}' 2>&1` |\n656: \n657: **Error Handling Pattern**\n658: \n659: Always use `2>&1` to capture stderr for error handling:\n660: \n661: ```bash\n662: #!/bin/bash\n663: trap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\n664: command -v bdg || { echo \"Install bdg first\"; exit 1; }\n665: bdg \"$URL\" 2>&1 || exit 1\n666: # ... operations ...\n667: # Cleanup runs automatically on exit\n668: ```\n669: \n670: ---\n671: \n672: ## 7. FEATURES\n673: \n674: | Feature            | CLI (bdg)                                   | MCP (Code Mode)                         |\n675: | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n676: | **Installation**   | `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Code Mode + UTCP config                 |\n677: | **Token Cost**     | Lowest                                      | Medium                                  |\n678: | **CDP Access**     | All 644 methods                             | MCP-exposed subset (26 tools)           |\n679: | **Self-Discovery** | `--list`, `--describe`, `--search`          | `search_tools()`                        |\n680: | **Best For**       | Quick debugging, inspection                 | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers |\n681: \n682: **Core capabilities available through both approaches:**\n683: - Screenshot capture\n684: - DOM inspection and querying\n685: - Console log access\n686: - Network monitoring and HAR export\n687: - CSS debugging\n688: - JavaScript execution\n689: - Cookie management\n690: - Full Chrome DevTools Protocol access\n691: \n692: ---\n693: \n694: ## 8. EXAMPLES\n695: \n696: ### Example 1: Full Debug Session\n697: \n698: ```bash\n699: #!/bin/bash\n700: # Complete debug session with error handling\n701: trap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\n702: \n703: # Verify CLI is available\n704: command -v bdg || { echo \"Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\"; exit 1; }\n705: \n706: # Start session\n707: bdg https://example.com 2>&1 || exit 1\n708: \n709: # Capture initial state\n710: bdg screenshot before.png 2>&1\n711: bdg console logs 2>&1 > console-before.json\n712: \n713: # Enable network monitoring\n714: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n715: sleep 3\n716: \n717: # Export network trace\n718: bdg har export session.har 2>&1\n719: bdg screenshot after.png 2>&1\n720: \n721: echo \"Debug session complete. Files: before.png, after.png, session.har, console-before.json\"\n722: ```\n723: \n724: ### Example 2: JavaScript Execution\n725: \n726: ```bash\n727: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n728: \n729: # Get page title\n730: bdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1\n731: \n732: # Count links on page\n733: bdg js \"document.querySelectorAll('a').length\" 2>&1\n734: \n735: # Get all link hrefs\n736: bdg js \"Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)\" 2>&1\n737: \n738: bdg stop 2>&1\n739: ```\n740: \n741: ### Example 3: DOM Query and Interaction\n742: \n743: ```bash\n744: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n745: \n746: # Query elements by class\n747: bdg dom query \".nav-link\" 2>&1\n748: \n749: # Click a button via JavaScript\n750: bdg js \"document.querySelector('#submit-btn').click()\" 2>&1\n751: \n752: # Check result after click\n753: bdg screenshot post-click.png 2>&1\n754: bdg console logs 2>&1\n755: \n756: bdg stop 2>&1\n757: ```\n758: \n759: ### Example 4: Network Analysis\n760: \n761: ```bash\n762: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n763: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n764: \n765: # Wait for full page load activity\n766: sleep 5\n767: bdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\n768: bdg stop 2>&1\n769: \n770: # Find slow requests (over 1 second)\n771: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n772: \n773: # Find failed requests\n774: jq '.log.entries[] | select(.response.status >= 400) | {url: .request.url, status: .response.status}' network-trace.har\n775: ```\n776: \n777: ### Example 5: Parallel Comparison (MCP)\n778: \n779: ```typescript\n780: // Compare production and staging side by side\n781: call_tool_chain(`\n782:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n783:     url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n784:   });\n785:   await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n786:     url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n787:   });\n788: \n789:   const prodScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n790:   const stagingScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n791:   const prodLogs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n792:   const stagingLogs = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_list_console_messages({});\n793: \n794:   return {\n795:     production: { screenshot: prodScreenshot, logs: prodLogs },\n796:     staging: { screenshot: stagingScreenshot, logs: stagingLogs }\n797:   };\n798: `)\n799: ```\n800: \n801: ### Example 6: Cookie Session Management\n802: \n803: ```bash\n804: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n805: bdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n806: \n807: # Export current cookies\n808: bdg network cookies 2>&1 > cookies.json\n809: \n810: # Inject an auth token\n811: bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n812:   \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n813:   \"value\": \"test-session-token\",\n814:   \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n815:   \"path\": \"/\",\n816:   \"secure\": true,\n817:   \"httpOnly\": true\n818: }' 2>&1\n819: \n820: # Reload to test authenticated state\n821: bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1\n822: bdg screenshot authenticated.png 2>&1\n823: \n824: bdg stop 2>&1\n825: ```\n826: \n827: ---\n828: \n829: ## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING\n830: \n831: ### Error/Cause/Fix Reference\n832: \n833: | Error | Cause | Fix |\n834: | ----- | ----- | --- |\n835: | `command not found: bdg` | CLI not installed or not in PATH | Run `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha`. Check PATH with `npm config get prefix` |\n836: | `Error: Could not find Chrome` | Chrome path not set or binary missing | Set `CHROME_PATH` env var. See path examples below |\n837: | `Error: No active session` | Session not started or crashed | Run `bdg stop 2>&1` then retry `bdg <url> 2>&1` |\n838: | `Failed to move to new namespace` | Linux sandbox restriction | Enable user namespaces or set `CHROME_FLAGS`. See Linux sandbox section |\n839: | `bdg fails on Windows` | Native Windows not supported | Install WSL with `wsl --install`, then install bdg inside WSL |\n840: | Chrome DevTools tools missing in Code Mode | UTCP config missing or disabled | Check `.utcp_config.json`, confirm `disabled: false`, restart your session |\n841: | MCP server fails to start | Port conflict or config error | Run `lsof -i :9222` and kill conflicting processes |\n842: | `EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::9222` | Port 9222 already taken | Run `kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)` to free the port |\n843: \n844: ### CLI: Command Not Found\n845: \n846: ```bash\n847: # Install bdg\n848: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n849: \n850: # Or fix PATH\n851: npm config get prefix\n852: export PATH=\"$(npm config get prefix)/bin:$PATH\"\n853: \n854: # Verify\n855: command -v bdg\n856: ```\n857: \n858: ### CLI: Chrome Not Found\n859: \n860: ```bash\n861: # macOS\n862: export CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n863: \n864: # Linux\n865: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n866: \n867: # Confirm Chrome exists at the path\n868: ls -la \"$CHROME_PATH\"\n869: \n870: # Add to your shell profile\n871: echo 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\n872: ```\n873: \n874: ### CLI: Session Won't Start\n875: \n876: ```bash\n877: # Force stop any existing sessions\n878: bdg stop 2>&1\n879: \n880: # Find zombie Chrome processes\n881: ps aux | grep -i chrome | grep -i debug\n882: \n883: # Kill zombie processes\n884: pkill -f \"chrome.*remote-debugging\"\n885: \n886: # Retry\n887: bdg https://example.com 2>&1\n888: ```\n889: \n890: ### CLI: Sandbox Errors (Linux)\n891: \n892: Fix in order of preference:\n893: \n894: ```bash\n895: # Option 1: Enable user namespaces (preferred)\n896: sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n897: \n898: # Option 2: Disable setuid sandbox only\n899: export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n900: \n901: # Option 3: No sandbox (least secure, last resort)\n902: export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n903: ```\n904: \n905: ### CLI: Windows Not Supported\n906: \n907: ```bash\n908: # Install WSL\n909: wsl --install\n910: \n911: # Inside WSL, install bdg\n912: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n913: ```\n914: \n915: ### MCP: Tool Not Found\n916: \n917: 1. Open `.utcp_config.json` and confirm the chrome_devtools entry exists\n918: 2. Check that `disabled` is not set to `true`\n919: 3. Restart your Claude Code or OpenCode session\n920: 4. Run `list_tools()` and look for chrome_devtools_* entries\n921: \n922: ### MCP: Connection Failed\n923: \n924: ```bash\n925: # Test the MCP server directly\n926: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --version\n927: \n928: # Check for port conflicts\n929: lsof -i :9222\n930: \n931: # Kill any conflicting processes\n932: kill $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n933: ```\n934: \n935: ### MCP: Port Already in Use\n936: \n937: ```bash\n938: # Find the process using port 9222\n939: lsof -i :9222\n940: \n941: # Kill it\n942: kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n943: \n944: # Confirm the port is free\n945: lsof -i :9222\n946: # Should return nothing\n947: ```\n948: \n949: ---\n950: \n951: ## 10. RESOURCES\n952: \n953: ### Related Documentation\n954: \n955: | Document        | Location                                                                  | Purpose              |\n956: | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |\n957: | SKILL.md        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/SKILL.md`                      | Complete workflows   |\n958: | CDP Patterns    | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/cdp_patterns.md`    | Domain patterns      |\n959: | Troubleshooting | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/troubleshooting.md` | Detailed fixes       |\n960: | Examples        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/examples/README.md`            | Production templates |\n961: | Code Mode Guide | `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md`                          | Code Mode setup      |\n962: \n963: ### Configuration Paths\n964: \n965: | Client  | Configuration                 | Purpose                      |\n966: | ------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- |\n967: | **CLI** | `CHROME_PATH` env var         | Browser location             |\n968: | **MCP** | `.mcp.json` / `opencode.json` | Code Mode server             |\n969: | **MCP** | `.utcp_config.json`           | Chrome DevTools registration |\n970: \n971: ### MCP Tools Reference (26 tools)\n972: \n973: | Tool                    | Purpose            | CLI Equivalent                                   |\n974: | ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |\n975: | `navigate_page`         | Navigate to URL    | `bdg <url>`                                      |\n976: | `take_screenshot`       | Capture screenshot | `bdg screenshot`                                 |\n977: | `list_console_messages` | Get console logs   | `bdg console logs`                               |\n978: | `resize_page`           | Set viewport size  | `bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`     |\n979: | `click`                 | Click element      | `bdg js \"document.querySelector('...').click()\"` |\n980: | `type_text`             | Type into element  | `bdg js \"...\"`                                   |\n981: | `get_page_content`      | Get page HTML      | `bdg dom query \"html\"`                           |\n982: \n983: **Tool naming convention:** `{instance}.{instance}_{tool_name}`\n984: \n985: ```typescript\n986: // Example invocations\n987: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })\n988: chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({})\n989: chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })  // parallel instance\n990: ```\n991: \n992: ---\n993: \n994: ## Quick Reference Card\n995: \n996: ### Installation\n997: \n998: ```bash\n999: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n1000: ```\n1001: \n1002: ### Discovery\n1003: \n1004: ```bash\n1005: bdg --list                    # List all CDP domains\n1006: bdg --describe Page           # Show domain methods\n1007: bdg --search screenshot       # Find methods by keyword\n1008: ```\n1009: \n1010: ### Session Control\n1011: \n1012: ```bash\n1013: bdg <url>                     # Start session\n1014: bdg status                    # Check session state\n1015: bdg stop                      # Stop session\n1016: ```\n1017: \n1018: ### Common Operations\n1019: \n1020: ```bash\n1021: bdg screenshot <path>         # Capture screenshot\n1022: bdg console logs              # Get console messages\n1023: bdg network cookies           # Get all cookies\n1024: bdg dom query \"<selector>\"    # Query DOM\n1025: bdg js \"<expression>\"         # Execute JavaScript\n1026: bdg har export <path>         # Export HAR file\n1027: ```\n1028: \n1029: ### MCP Quick Start\n1030: \n1031: ```typescript\n1032: // When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\n1033: call_tool_chain(`\n1034:   await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n1035:   const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n1036:   return screenshot;\n1037: `)\n1038: ```\n1039: \n1040: ### Environment Setup\n1041: \n1042: ```bash\n1043: # Add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc\n1044: export CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n1045: # Linux: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n1046: ```\n1047: \n1048: ### Validation Checkpoints Summary\n1049: \n1050: | Checkpoint          | Meaning                              |\n1051: | ------------------- | ------------------------------------ |\n1052: | `phase_1_complete`  | Prerequisites validated              |\n1053: | `phase_2_complete`  | CLI installation complete            |\n1054: | `phase_3_complete`  | Configuration complete               |\n1055: | `phase_4_complete`  | Verification complete                |\n1056: | `phase_5_complete`  | System operational                   |\n1057: \n1058: ---\n1059: \n1060: ## Version History\n1061: \n1062: | Version | Date       | Changes                                                   |\n1063: | ------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |\n1064: | 2.1.0   | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, formal checkpoints, STOP blocks   |\n1065: | 2.0.0   | 2025-10-01 | Two-approach architecture, MCP fallback, security section |\n1066: | 1.0.0   | 2025-06-01 | Initial CLI guide                                         |\n1067: \n1068: ---\n1069: \n1070: **Need help?** See [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) or load the `mcp-chrome-devtools` skill for detailed workflows.\n\n(End of file - total 1070 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Chrome DevTools Installation Guide\n\nComplete installation and configuration guide for Chrome DevTools browser debugging, providing AI assistants with browser control through two approaches. The CLI tool (bdg) offers direct, fast, token-efficient browser debugging. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode for multi-tool orchestration and parallel browser testing. Covers screenshot capture, DOM inspection, console evaluation, network monitoring and performance profiling.\n\n> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n> **Package:** `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | **Dependencies:** Node.js 18+, Chrome/Chromium\n\n**Version:** 2.1.0 | **Updated:** 2026-02-20 | **Protocol:** Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP)\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\nCopy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n\n```\nI want to install the Chrome DevTools CLI (bdg) for browser debugging.\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed","truncated":false,"loaded":[],"display":{"type":"file","path":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/INSTALL_GUIDE.md","text":"# Chrome DevTools Installation Guide\n\nComplete installation and configuration guide for Chrome DevTools browser debugging, providing AI assistants with browser control through two approaches. The CLI tool (bdg) offers direct, fast, token-efficient browser debugging. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode for multi-tool orchestration and parallel browser testing. Covers screenshot capture, DOM inspection, console evaluation, network monitoring and performance profiling.\n\n> **Part of OpenCode Installation.** See the [Master Installation Guide](../README.md) for complete setup.\n> **Package:** `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | **Dependencies:** Node.js 18+, Chrome/Chromium\n\n**Version:** 2.1.0 | **Updated:** 2026-02-20 | **Protocol:** Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP)\n\n---\n\n## 0. AI-FIRST INSTALL GUIDE\n\nCopy and paste this prompt to your AI assistant to get installation help:\n\n```\nI want to install the Chrome DevTools CLI (bdg) for browser debugging.\n\nPlease help me:\n1. Check if I have Node.js 18+ installed\n2. Install browser-debugger-cli globally\n3. Verify Chrome/Chromium is available\n4. Test with a basic screenshot capture\n5. (Optional) Configure MCP fallback if needed\n\nMy platform is: [macOS / Linux / Windows with WSL]\n\nGuide me through each step with the exact commands needed.\n```\n\nThe AI will:\n- Verify Node.js 18+ is available on your system\n- Install `browser-debugger-cli` globally\n- Test Chrome/Chromium availability\n- Run a basic screenshot test to confirm installation\n- Configure MCP fallback if you need multi-tool workflows\n\n**Expected setup time:** 3 to 5 minutes\n\n### Quick Success Check (30 seconds)\n\nAfter installation, run these two tests immediately:\n\n1. Open your terminal and run: `bdg --version`\n2. Version output appears: CLI is working\n3. In Claude Code, ask: \"Take a screenshot of example.com\"\n4. Screenshot captured: full system is working\n\nNot working? Go to [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).\n\n---\n\n## 1. OVERVIEW\n\nChrome DevTools gives AI assistants browser debugging capabilities through two approaches. The CLI (`bdg`) is the primary method: direct, fast and token-efficient. The MCP fallback runs through Code Mode and suits multi-tool orchestration or parallel browser testing.\n\n### Core Principle\n\n> **Install once, verify at each step.** Each phase has a validation checkpoint.
