Do not proceed until the checkpoint passes.\n\n### Source Repository\n\n| Property         | Value                                                                                       |\n| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| **GitHub (CLI)** | [szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli](https://github.com/szymdzum/browser-debugger-cli)           |\n| **GitHub (MCP)** | [ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp](https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp) |\n| **npm (CLI)**    | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha`                                                                |\n| **npm (MCP)**    | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest`                                                                |\n| **License**      | MIT / Apache-2.0                                                                            |\n\n### Two Approaches\n\n| Approach            | Package                      | When to Use                             | Token Cost |\n| ------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ---------- |\n| **CLI (bdg)**       | `browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Single browser, quick tasks, debugging  | Lowest     |\n| **MCP (Code Mode)** | `chrome-devtools-mcp@latest` | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers | Higher     |\n\nDefault to CLI. Use MCP only when CLI is not sufficient.\n\n### Decision Flowchart\n\n```\nTask received → Is bdg CLI available? (command -v bdg)\n                      │\n              ┌───────┴───────┐\n             YES              NO\n              │               │\n              ▼               ▼\n        Use CLI          Is Code Mode configured?\n        (fastest)               │\n                        ┌───────┴───────┐\n                       YES              NO\n                        │               │\n                        ▼               ▼\n                   Use MCP         Install CLI:\n                   (fallback)      npm i -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### Architecture Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    AI Client (Claude/OpenCode)               │\n└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘\n                              │\n              ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐\n              │                               │\n              ▼                               ▼\n┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n│   CLI Approach (Primary)│     │   MCP Approach (Fallback)   │\n│   browser-debugger-cli  │     │   Chrome DevTools MCP       │\n│                         │     │   via Code Mode             │\n│   - Direct terminal     │     │   - call_tool_chain()       │\n│   - Self-documenting    │     │   - Type-safe invocation    │\n│   - Unix composable     │     │   - Multi-tool integration  │\n│   - All 644 CDP methods │     │   - 26 exposed tools        │\n└────────────┬────────────┘     └──────────────┬──────────────┘\n             │                                  │\n             └──────────────┬───────────────────┘\n                            │\n                            ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n              │    Chrome DevTools Protocol │\n              │    (CDP via WebSocket)      │\n              │    Port 9222 (localhost)    │\n              └─────────────┬───────────────┘\n                            │\n                            ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────────┐\n              │    Chrome/Chromium/Edge     │\n              └─────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### MCP Architecture Detail\n\nWhen using MCP, Chrome DevTools routes through Code Mode, not directly to your AI client:\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Your AI Client (Claude Code, OpenCode, VS Code)                │\n│  - CLI: bdg command (direct, standalone, no Code Mode)          │\n│  - MCP: 4 Code Mode tools → chrome_devtools_1.*                 │\n└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │ (MCP only)\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Code Mode MCP (configured in opencode.json)                    │\n│  - Reads .utcp_config.json for Chrome DevTools provider         │\n└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Chrome DevTools Provider (configured in .utcp_config.json)     │\n│  - 26 tools via chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool}      │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\nChrome DevTools' 26 MCP tools would consume roughly 78k tokens if exposed natively. Code Mode provides on-demand access with 98% context reduction.\n\n### Performance Targets\n\n| Operation             | Target | Typical |\n| --------------------- | ------ | ------- |\n| Session startup       | <5s    | ~3s     |\n| Screenshot capture    | <2s    | ~1s     |\n| Console log retrieval | <1s    | ~500ms  |\n| DOM query             | <500ms | ~200ms  |\n| HAR export            | <3s    | ~2s     |\n\n---\n\n## 2. PREREQUISITES\n\n### Required Tools\n\n- **Node.js 18 or higher**\n  ```bash\n  node --version\n  # Should show v18.x or higher\n  ```\n\n- **npm** (comes with Node.js)\n  ```bash\n  npm --version\n  ```\n\n- **Chrome/Chromium Browser**, one of:\n  - Google Chrome (recommended)\n  - Chromium\n  - Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based)\n\n### Platform Support\n\n| Platform    | Status         | Notes                             |\n| ----------- | -------------- | --------------------------------- |\n| **macOS**   | Native support | Recommended                       |\n| **Linux**   | Native support | May need sandbox configuration    |\n| **Windows** | WSL only       | PowerShell and Git Bash not supported |\n\n### Common Setup Issues\n\n**Chrome path not found:**\n```bash\n# macOS\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n\n# Linux\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n# or\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/chromium-browser\"\n```\n\n**Windows users:** WSL is required. Install it first with: `wsl --install`\n\n---\n\n## 3. INSTALLATION\n\n### Step 1: Install browser-debugger-cli\n\n```bash\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### Step 2: Verify the binary\n\n```bash\n# Confirm installation\ncommand -v bdg || echo \"Installation failed\"\n\n# Check version\nbdg --version\n\n# List available CDP domains\nbdg --list\n```\n\n### Step 3: Test basic operation\n\n```bash\n# Start a session\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n\n# Take a screenshot\nbdg screenshot test.png 2>&1\n\n# Check console logs\nbdg console logs 2>&1\n\n# Stop the session\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Confirm screenshot was created\nls -la test.png\n```\n\n### Step 4: Configure Chrome path if needed\n\n```bash\n# macOS\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n\n# Linux\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n\n# Add to your shell profile for persistence\necho 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\nsource ~/.zshrc\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_1_complete`\n\nRun these checks to confirm prerequisites are met:\n\n| Check         | Command                  | Expected                     |\n| ------------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------- |\n| CLI installed | `command -v bdg`         | Path to bdg binary           |\n| Version       | `bdg --version`          | `browser-debugger-cli@x.x.x` |\n| CDP domains   | `bdg --list \\| head -5`  | `Page, DOM, Network...`      |\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Fix the issue before continuing.\n\n### Validation: `phase_2_complete`\n\nConfirm CLI installation is complete:\n\n| Check          | Command                             | Expected          |\n| -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------------- |\n| Session starts | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1`      | No errors         |\n| Screenshot     | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` | File created      |\n| Session stops  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                     | Clean exit        |\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) before continuing.\n\n---\n\n## 4. CONFIGURATION\n\nMCP is optional. Configure it only if you need multi-tool workflows or when CLI is not available.\n\n### MCP Approach: Code Mode Provider\n\nWhen using MCP, Chrome DevTools is a Code Mode provider, not a standalone MCP server.\n\n| Aspect             | What This Means                                                                |\n| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| **Configuration**  | Chrome DevTools goes in `.utcp_config.json`, not `opencode.json`               |\n| **Access Method**  | Tools are accessed via Code Mode's `call_tool_chain()`                         |\n| **Prerequisite**   | Code Mode MCP must be installed first. See [Code Mode INSTALL_GUIDE.md](../mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md) section 4 |\n| **Context Cost**   | Your AI sees only 4 Code Mode tools (~1.6k tokens), not 26 Chrome DevTools tools |\n| **Naming Pattern** | `chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_{tool_name}`                              |\n\n### When to Use MCP\n\nUse MCP when you:\n- Already use Code Mode for other tools such as external services or Figma\n- Need to chain browser operations with other MCP tools\n- Require parallel browser testing to compare multiple sites at once\n- Need complex multi-step automation in TypeScript\n- Require type-safe tool invocation\n\n### Step 1: Verify Code Mode\n\nConfirm Code Mode is configured in your MCP settings. Use `.mcp.json` for Claude Code or `opencode.json` for OpenCode:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"code_mode\": {\n      \"command\": \"node\",\n      \"args\": [\"/path/to/code-mode-mcp/dist/index.js\"],\n      \"env\": {\n        \"UTCP_CONFIG_FILE\": \"/path/to/project/.utcp_config.json\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add Chrome DevTools to UTCP Config\n\nAdd the following to `.utcp_config.json`. Use `--isolated=true` to give each instance its own independent browser:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"manual_call_templates\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_1\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"chrome_devtools_1\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n            \"env\": {}\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"chrome_devtools_2\",\n      \"call_template_type\": \"mcp\",\n      \"config\": {\n        \"mcpServers\": {\n          \"chrome_devtools_2\": {\n            \"transport\": \"stdio\",\n            \"command\": \"npx\",\n            \"args\": [\"chrome-devtools-mcp@latest\", \"--isolated=true\"],\n            \"env\": {}\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nRegister multiple instances for parallel testing. Each needs `--isolated=true`.\n\n### Step 3: Verify MCP tools\n\n```typescript\n// In Code Mode context\nconst tools = await list_tools();\nconsole.log(tools.filter(t => t.includes('chrome_devtools')));\n// Expected: chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page, chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot, etc.\n```\n\n### Validation: `phase_3_complete`\n\n| Check            | Method                  | Expected                |\n| ---------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------- |\n| Config exists    | `cat .utcp_config.json` | chrome_devtools entry   |\n| Tools registered | `list_tools()`          | chrome_devtools_* tools |\n| Server starts    | Navigate to a URL       | No connection errors    |\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** Review your UTCP config and restart your AI client session.\n\n### Security Considerations\n\nPort 9222 runs the Chrome DevTools Protocol for remote debugging. It grants full browser control.\n\n**Risks:**\n- Full browser control to anyone with port access\n- Cookie and session theft\n- JavaScript injection\n- Local file system access via browser\n\n**Mitigations:**\n\n| Mitigation             | Implementation                             |\n| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |\n| Bind to localhost only | `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1`     |\n| Firewall rules         | Block port 9222 from external access       |\n| Development only       | Never expose on production networks        |\n| Network isolation      | Use in CI/CD with isolated networking      |\n\n```bash\n# Verify the port is localhost-only\nlsof -i :9222\n# Should show 127.0.0.1:9222, not *:9222\n```\n\n**Linux sandbox security:**\n\nChrome's sandbox provides process isolation. Avoid disabling it unless necessary.\n\n| Option                     | Security Level | When to Use               |\n| -------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------- |\n| Default sandbox            | Best           | Standard installations    |\n| `--disable-setuid-sandbox` | Medium         | When user namespaces work |\n| `--no-sandbox`             | Poor           | Last resort only          |\n\n**Preferred approach, from most to least secure:**\n\n1. Run in a container with proper permissions:\n   ```bash\n   docker run --cap-add=SYS_ADMIN chromium\n   ```\n\n2. Enable user namespaces:\n   ```bash\n   sudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n   ```\n\n3. Disable setuid sandbox only:\n   ```bash\n   export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n   ```\n\n4. No-sandbox as a last resort:\n   ```bash\n   export CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n   ```\n\n**CI/CD security flags:**\n```bash\nexport CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-gpu --disable-dev-shm-usage --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1\"\n```\n\n| Flag                                   | Purpose                        |\n| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n| `--disable-gpu`                        | Prevent GPU issues in headless |\n| `--disable-dev-shm-usage`              | Avoid shared memory issues     |\n| `--remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1` | Localhost binding only         |\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFICATION\n\n### One-Command Health Check\n\n```bash\n# Complete CLI verification in one command\nbdg --version 2>&1 && \\\n  bdg https://example.com 2>&1 && \\\n  bdg screenshot /tmp/verify.png 2>&1 && \\\n  bdg stop 2>&1 && \\\n  ls -la /tmp/verify.png && \\\n  echo \"SUCCESS: All checks passed\" || \\\n  echo \"FAILED: Check error output above\"\n```\n\n### Full Verification Checklist\n\n| #   | Check          | Command                             | Expected Result                 |\n| --- | -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n| 1   | CLI installed  | `command -v bdg`                    | `/usr/local/bin/bdg` or similar |\n| 2   | Version        | `bdg --version`                     | `browser-debugger-cli@x.x.x`   |\n| 3   | CDP domains    | `bdg --list \\| wc -l`               | 53+ domains                     |\n| 4   | Session start  | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1`      | No errors                       |\n| 5   | Session status | `bdg status 2>&1`                   | `{\"state\": \"active\"}`           |\n| 6   | Screenshot     | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` | File created                    |\n| 7   | Session stop   | `bdg stop 2>&1`                     | Clean exit                      |\n\n### Verify in Your AI Client\n\nIn Claude Code or OpenCode, run:\n```\n> Take a screenshot of https://example.com\n```\n\nExpected result: bdg commands execute and a screenshot is captured and displayed.\n\n### Validation: `phase_4_complete`\n\nAll 7 checklist items above pass with no errors.\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails.** The system is not ready for use until all checks pass.\n\n### Validation: `phase_5_complete`\n\nYour AI client successfully captures a screenshot on request. The system is operational.\n\n❌ **STOP if validation fails** - Check MCP configuration, restart your AI client, and review [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting).\n\n---\n\n## 6. USAGE\n\n### Pattern 1: Quick Screenshot (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg screenshot output.png 2>&1\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Console Log Analysis (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg console logs 2>&1 | jq '.[] | select(.level == \"error\")'\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Network Monitoring (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\nsleep 5\nbdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Analyze slow requests\njq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Cookie Manipulation (CLI)\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n\n# Get cookies\nbdg network cookies 2>&1\n\n# Set a cookie\nbdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n  \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n  \"value\": \"secret-123\",\n  \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n  \"secure\": true\n}' 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Pattern 5: Single Instance (MCP Fallback)\n\nWhen CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n  const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  const logs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n  return { screenshot, logs };\n`)\n```\n\n### Pattern 6: Parallel Instances (MCP)\n\nCompare multiple sites at the same time:\n\n```typescript\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  // Instance 1: Production\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n  });\n\n  // Instance 2: Staging (isolated browser)\n  await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n  });\n\n  // Capture both screenshots\n  const prod = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  const staging = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n\n  return { production: prod, staging: staging };\n`)\n```\n\n### Tool Selection Guide\n\n| Scenario                 | Tool | Why                         |\n| ------------------------ | ---- | --------------------------- |\n| Quick screenshot         | CLI  | Fastest, lowest tokens      |\n| Console log check        | CLI  | Pipe to jq for filtering    |\n| Network trace            | CLI  | HAR export built in         |\n| Cookie manipulation      | CLI  | Full CDP access             |\n| Multi-tool workflow      | MCP  | Chain with other tools      |\n| Parallel browser testing | MCP  | Multiple isolated instances |\n| CDP method discovery     | CLI  | `--list`, `--describe`      |\n\n### bdg Command Reference\n\n**Session Commands**\n\n| Command      | Description                     | Example                        |\n| ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |\n| `bdg <url>`  | Open URL, start browser session | `bdg https://example.com 2>&1` |\n| `bdg status` | Check session status            | `bdg status 2>&1`              |\n| `bdg stop`   | Stop browser, clean up session  | `bdg stop 2>&1`                |\n\n**Helper Commands**\n\n| Command                      | Description             | Example                             |\n| ---------------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------- |\n| `bdg screenshot <path>`      | Capture page screenshot | `bdg screenshot /tmp/test.png 2>&1` |\n| `bdg console logs`           | Get console messages    | `bdg console logs 2>&1 \\| jq '.'`   |\n| `bdg network cookies`        | Get all cookies         | `bdg network cookies 2>&1`          |\n| `bdg dom query \"<selector>\"` | Query DOM elements      | `bdg dom query \".my-class\" 2>&1`    |\n| `bdg js \"<expression>\"`      | Execute JavaScript      | `bdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1`      |\n| `bdg har export <path>`      | Export network as HAR   | `bdg har export trace.har 2>&1`     |\n\n**Discovery Commands**\n\n| Command                   | Description               | Example                                 |\n| ------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| `bdg --version`           | Show CLI version          | `bdg --version`                         |\n| `bdg --list`              | List all CDP domains (53) | `bdg --list`                            |\n| `bdg --describe <domain>` | Show domain methods       | `bdg --describe Page`                   |\n| `bdg --describe <method>` | Show method signature     | `bdg --describe Page.captureScreenshot` |\n| `bdg --search <term>`     | Search CDP methods        | `bdg --search screenshot`               |\n\n**Raw CDP Commands**\n\n| Command                     | Description         | Example                                                     |\n| --------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `bdg cdp <method>`          | Execute CDP method  | `bdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1`                                  |\n| `bdg cdp <method> '<json>'` | CDP with parameters | `bdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\"name\":\"x\",\"value\":\"y\"}' 2>&1` |\n\n**Error Handling Pattern**\n\nAlways use `2>&1` to capture stderr for error handling:\n\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\ntrap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\ncommand -v bdg || { echo \"Install bdg first\"; exit 1; }\nbdg \"$URL\" 2>&1 || exit 1\n# ... operations ...\n# Cleanup runs automatically on exit\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. FEATURES\n\n| Feature            | CLI (bdg)                                   | MCP (Code Mode)                         |\n| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |\n| **Installation**   | `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha` | Code Mode + UTCP config                 |\n| **Token Cost**     | Lowest                                      | Medium                                  |\n| **CDP Access**     | All 644 methods                             | MCP-exposed subset (26 tools)           |\n| **Self-Discovery** | `--list`, `--describe`, `--search`          | `search_tools()`                        |\n| **Best For**       | Quick debugging, inspection                 | Multi-tool workflows, parallel browsers |\n\n**Core capabilities available through both approaches:**\n- Screenshot capture\n- DOM inspection and querying\n- Console log access\n- Network monitoring and HAR export\n- CSS debugging\n- JavaScript execution\n- Cookie management\n- Full Chrome DevTools Protocol access\n\n---\n\n## 8. EXAMPLES\n\n### Example 1: Full Debug Session\n\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\n# Complete debug session with error handling\ntrap \"bdg stop 2>&1\" EXIT INT TERM\n\n# Verify CLI is available\ncommand -v bdg || { echo \"Install: npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\"; exit 1; }\n\n# Start session\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1 || exit 1\n\n# Capture initial state\nbdg screenshot before.png 2>&1\nbdg console logs 2>&1 > console-before.json\n\n# Enable network monitoring\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\nsleep 3\n\n# Export network trace\nbdg har export session.har 2>&1\nbdg screenshot after.png 2>&1\n\necho \"Debug session complete. Files: before.png, after.png, session.har, console-before.json\"\n```\n\n### Example 2: JavaScript Execution\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n\n# Get page title\nbdg js \"document.title\" 2>&1\n\n# Count links on page\nbdg js \"document.querySelectorAll('a').length\" 2>&1\n\n# Get all link hrefs\nbdg js \"Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a => a.href)\" 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Example 3: DOM Query and Interaction\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n\n# Query elements by class\nbdg dom query \".nav-link\" 2>&1\n\n# Click a button via JavaScript\nbdg js \"document.querySelector('#submit-btn').click()\" 2>&1\n\n# Check result after click\nbdg screenshot post-click.png 2>&1\nbdg console logs 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n### Example 4: Network Analysis\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n\n# Wait for full page load activity\nsleep 5\nbdg har export network-trace.har 2>&1\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Find slow requests (over 1 second)\njq '.log.entries[] | select(.time > 1000) | {url: .request.url, time}' network-trace.har\n\n# Find failed requests\njq '.log.entries[] | select(.response.status >= 400) | {url: .request.url, status: .response.status}' network-trace.har\n```\n\n### Example 5: Parallel Comparison (MCP)\n\n```typescript\n// Compare production and staging side by side\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://production.example.com\"\n  });\n  await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({\n    url: \"https://staging.example.com\"\n  });\n\n  const prodScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  const stagingScreenshot = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_take_screenshot({});\n  const prodLogs = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_list_console_messages({});\n  const stagingLogs = await chrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_list_console_messages({});\n\n  return {\n    production: { screenshot: prodScreenshot, logs: prodLogs },\n    staging: { screenshot: stagingScreenshot, logs: stagingLogs }\n  };\n`)\n```\n\n### Example 6: Cookie Session Management\n\n```bash\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\nbdg cdp Network.enable 2>&1\n\n# Export current cookies\nbdg network cookies 2>&1 > cookies.json\n\n# Inject an auth token\nbdg cdp Network.setCookie '{\n  \"name\": \"auth_token\",\n  \"value\": \"test-session-token\",\n  \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n  \"path\": \"/\",\n  \"secure\": true,\n  \"httpOnly\": true\n}' 2>&1\n\n# Reload to test authenticated state\nbdg cdp Page.reload 2>&1\nbdg screenshot authenticated.png 2>&1\n\nbdg stop 2>&1\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING\n\n### Error/Cause/Fix Reference\n\n| Error | Cause | Fix |\n| ----- | ----- | --- |\n| `command not found: bdg` | CLI not installed or not in PATH | Run `npm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha`. Check PATH with `npm config get prefix` |\n| `Error: Could not find Chrome` | Chrome path not set or binary missing | Set `CHROME_PATH` env var. See path examples below |\n| `Error: No active session` | Session not started or crashed | Run `bdg stop 2>&1` then retry `bdg <url> 2>&1` |\n| `Failed to move to new namespace` | Linux sandbox restriction | Enable user namespaces or set `CHROME_FLAGS`. See Linux sandbox section |\n| `bdg fails on Windows` | Native Windows not supported | Install WSL with `wsl --install`, then install bdg inside WSL |\n| Chrome DevTools tools missing in Code Mode | UTCP config missing or disabled | Check `.utcp_config.json`, confirm `disabled: false`, restart your session |\n| MCP server fails to start | Port conflict or config error | Run `lsof -i :9222` and kill conflicting processes |\n| `EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::9222` | Port 9222 already taken | Run `kill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)` to free the port |\n\n### CLI: Command Not Found\n\n```bash\n# Install bdg\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n\n# Or fix PATH\nnpm config get prefix\nexport PATH=\"$(npm config get prefix)/bin:$PATH\"\n\n# Verify\ncommand -v bdg\n```\n\n### CLI: Chrome Not Found\n\n```bash\n# macOS\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n\n# Linux\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n\n# Confirm Chrome exists at the path\nls -la \"$CHROME_PATH\"\n\n# Add to your shell profile\necho 'export CHROME_PATH=\"/path/to/chrome\"' >> ~/.zshrc\n```\n\n### CLI: Session Won't Start\n\n```bash\n# Force stop any existing sessions\nbdg stop 2>&1\n\n# Find zombie Chrome processes\nps aux | grep -i chrome | grep -i debug\n\n# Kill zombie processes\npkill -f \"chrome.*remote-debugging\"\n\n# Retry\nbdg https://example.com 2>&1\n```\n\n### CLI: Sandbox Errors (Linux)\n\nFix in order of preference:\n\n```bash\n# Option 1: Enable user namespaces (preferred)\nsudo sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1\n\n# Option 2: Disable setuid sandbox only\nexport CHROME_FLAGS=\"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"\n\n# Option 3: No sandbox (least secure, last resort)\nexport CHROME_FLAGS=\"--no-sandbox\"\n```\n\n### CLI: Windows Not Supported\n\n```bash\n# Install WSL\nwsl --install\n\n# Inside WSL, install bdg\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### MCP: Tool Not Found\n\n1. Open `.utcp_config.json` and confirm the chrome_devtools entry exists\n2. Check that `disabled` is not set to `true`\n3. Restart your Claude Code or OpenCode session\n4. Run `list_tools()` and look for chrome_devtools_* entries\n\n### MCP: Connection Failed\n\n```bash\n# Test the MCP server directly\nnpx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --version\n\n# Check for port conflicts\nlsof -i :9222\n\n# Kill any conflicting processes\nkill $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n```\n\n### MCP: Port Already in Use\n\n```bash\n# Find the process using port 9222\nlsof -i :9222\n\n# Kill it\nkill -9 $(lsof -t -i :9222)\n\n# Confirm the port is free\nlsof -i :9222\n# Should return nothing\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. RESOURCES\n\n### Related Documentation\n\n| Document        | Location                                                                  | Purpose              |\n| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |\n| SKILL.md        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/SKILL.md`                      | Complete workflows   |\n| CDP Patterns    | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/cdp_patterns.md`    | Domain patterns      |\n| Troubleshooting | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/references/troubleshooting.md` | Detailed fixes       |\n| Examples        | `.opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/examples/README.md`            | Production templates |\n| Code Mode Guide | `.opencode/skills/mcp-code-mode/INSTALL_GUIDE.md`                          | Code Mode setup      |\n\n### Configuration Paths\n\n| Client  | Configuration                 | Purpose                      |\n| ------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------- |\n| **CLI** | `CHROME_PATH` env var         | Browser location             |\n| **MCP** | `.mcp.json` / `opencode.json` | Code Mode server             |\n| **MCP** | `.utcp_config.json`           | Chrome DevTools registration |\n\n### MCP Tools Reference (26 tools)\n\n| Tool                    | Purpose            | CLI Equivalent                                   |\n| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |\n| `navigate_page`         | Navigate to URL    | `bdg <url>`                                      |\n| `take_screenshot`       | Capture screenshot | `bdg screenshot`                                 |\n| `list_console_messages` | Get console logs   | `bdg console logs`                               |\n| `resize_page`           | Set viewport size  | `bdg cdp Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`     |\n| `click`                 | Click element      | `bdg js \"document.querySelector('...').click()\"` |\n| `type_text`             | Type into element  | `bdg js \"...\"`                                   |\n| `get_page_content`      | Get page HTML      | `bdg dom query \"html\"`                           |\n\n**Tool naming convention:** `{instance}.{instance}_{tool_name}`\n\n```typescript\n// Example invocations\nchrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })\nchrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({})\nchrome_devtools_2.chrome_devtools_2_navigate_page({ url: \"...\" })  // parallel instance\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference Card\n\n### Installation\n\n```bash\nnpm install -g browser-debugger-cli@alpha\n```\n\n### Discovery\n\n```bash\nbdg --list                    # List all CDP domains\nbdg --describe Page           # Show domain methods\nbdg --search screenshot       # Find methods by keyword\n```\n\n### Session Control\n\n```bash\nbdg <url>                     # Start session\nbdg status                    # Check session state\nbdg stop                      # Stop session\n```\n\n### Common Operations\n\n```bash\nbdg screenshot <path>         # Capture screenshot\nbdg console logs              # Get console messages\nbdg network cookies           # Get all cookies\nbdg dom query \"<selector>\"    # Query DOM\nbdg js \"<expression>\"         # Execute JavaScript\nbdg har export <path>         # Export HAR file\n```\n\n### MCP Quick Start\n\n```typescript\n// When CLI is not sufficient, use Code Mode:\ncall_tool_chain(`\n  await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_navigate_page({ url: \"https://example.com\" });\n  const screenshot = await chrome_devtools_1.chrome_devtools_1_take_screenshot({});\n  return screenshot;\n`)\n```\n\n### Environment Setup\n\n```bash\n# Add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc\nexport CHROME_PATH=\"/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome\"\n# Linux: export CHROME_PATH=\"/usr/bin/google-chrome\"\n```\n\n### Validation Checkpoints Summary\n\n| Checkpoint          | Meaning                              |\n| ------------------- | ------------------------------------ |\n| `phase_1_complete`  | Prerequisites validated              |\n| `phase_2_complete`  | CLI installation complete            |\n| `phase_3_complete`  | Configuration complete               |\n| `phase_4_complete`  | Verification complete                |\n| `phase_5_complete`  | System operational                   |\n\n---\n\n## Version History\n\n| Version | Date       | Changes                                                   |\n| ------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |\n| 2.1.0   | 2026-02-20 | HVR compliance rewrite, formal checkpoints, STOP blocks   |\n| 2.0.0   | 2025-10-01 | Two-approach architecture, MCP fallback, security section |\n| 1.0.0   | 2025-06-01 | Initial CLI guide                                         |\n\n---\n\n**Need help?** See [Troubleshooting](#9-troubleshooting) or load the `mcp-chrome-devtools` skill for detailed workflows.","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":1070,"totalLines":1070,"truncated":false}},"title":".opencode/skills/mcp-chrome-devtools/INSTALL_GUIDE.md","time":{"start":1780835206533,"end":1780835206545}},"id":"prt_ea20cc395001mqJy7io7oIPAJm","sessionID":"ses_15df359c1ffegP1ICxrHMIv19i","messageID":"msg_ea20ca91d0015bJASNVaHwXhyZ"}}

OPERATOR-OWNED FIELDS\\n33: \\n34: ### Target\\n35: \\n36: [Set during initialization]\\n37: \\n38: ### Target Profile\\n39: \\n40: [dynamic (any agent)]\\n41: \\n42: ### Goal\\n43: \\n44: [What improvement should be tested]\\n45: \\n46: ### Constraints\\n47: \\n48: - promotion allowed only with explicit per-target approval under dynamic mode\\n49: - candidate-only targets must never be promoted\\n50: - runtime mirrors excluded from benchmark truth\\n51: - no promotion without explicit later-phase gate\\n52: - stop when no-go or repeatability rules fail\\n53: \\n54: ### Current Hypothesis\\n55: \\n56: [Why the current baseline may be improvable]\\n57: \\n58: ### Candidate Focus\\n59: \\n60: [What the next candidate should change]\\n61: \\n62: ### Benchmark Focus\\n63: \\n64: [Which fixture behaviors are being improved or protected]\\n65: \\n66: ### Integration Focus\\n67: \\n68: [Which integration dimensions need attention: mirror sync, command coverage, permission alignment, resource references]\\n69: \\n70: ---\\n71: \\n72: ## 3. MACHINE-OWNED FIELDS\\n73: \\n74: <!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->\\n75: ### What Improved\\n76: \\n77: [Reducer updates after scoring and benchmarking]\\n78: \\n79: ### Dimensional Scores\\n80: \\n81: | Dimension | Latest | Best | Trend |\\n82: | --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n83: | Structural | - | - | - |\\n84: | Rule Coherence | - | - | - |\\n85: | Integration | - | - | - |\\n86: | Output Quality | - | - | - |\\n87: | System Fitness | - | - | - |\\n88: \\n89: [Reducer populates this table after each scored iteration]\\n90: \\n91: ### What Failed\\n92: \\n93: [Reducer updates after scoring and benchmarking]\\n94: \\n95: ### Best Known State\\n96: \\n97: [Reducer names the current best state]\\n98: \\n99: ### Next Recommendation\\n100: \\n101: [Reducer recommends continue, stop, or hold]\\n102: \\n103: ### Packaging Follow-Up\\n104: \\n105: [Reducer records downstream mirror-sync or parity debt separately]\\n106: \\n107: ### Mutation Coverage\\n108: \\n109: | Dimension | Tried | Exhausted | Remaining |\\n110: | --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n111: | Structural | - | - | - |\\n112: | Rule Coherence | - | - | - |\\n113: | Integration | - | - | - |\\n114: | Output Quality | - | - | - |\\n115: | System Fitness | - | - | - |\\n116: \\n117: [Reducer populates from mutation-coverage.json after each iteration]\\n118: \\n119: ### Convergence Eligibility\\n120: \\n121: - **Data Points**: [count] / 3 minimum required\\n122: - **Stability**: [all dimensions within +/-2 across last 3 points?]\\n123: - **Exhaustion**: [any dimensions fully exhausted?]\\n124: - **Verdict**: [eligible / not eligible / insufficient data]\\n125: \\n126: [Reducer updates after each trajectory recording]\\n127: \\n128: ### Trade-Off Detection\\n129: \\n130: | Iteration | Improving | Regressing | Action |\\n131: | --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n132: | - | - | - | - |\\n133: \\n134: [Reducer populates when trade-off-detector.cjs flags a Pareto trade-off]\\n135: \\n136: **Trade-Off Resolution Guidance:**\\n137: - If hard dimension regresses (structural, integration, systemFitness): BLOCK promotion, revert mutation\\n138: - If soft dimension regresses (ruleCoherence, outputQuality): WARN operator, allow with explicit approval\\n139: - If Pareto-dominated: REJECT candidate regardless of weighted score improvement\\n140: <!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->\\n141: \\n142: ---\\n143: \\n144: ## 4. RELATED RESOURCES\\n145: \\n146: - `improvement_charter.md`\\n147: - `../../references/shared/loop_protocol.md`\\n148: - `target_manifest.jsonc`\\n149: \\n\\n(End of file - total 149 lines)\\n</content>\",\"metadata\":{\"preview\":\"---\\ntitle: Recursive Agent Strategy Template\\ndescription: Mutable runtime strategy template for deep-improvement sessions.\\ntrigger_phrases:\\n  - \\\"recursive agent strategy\\\"\\n  - \\\"improvement strategy template\\\"\\n  - \\\"operator-owned strategy fields\\\"\\n  - \\\"machine-owned status fields\\\"\\nimportance_tier: normal\\ncontextType: planning\\nversion: 1.17.0.9\\n---\\n\\n# Recursive Agent Strategy\\n\\nMutable strategy template for a deep-improvement run. Use it to capture the operator-owned goal and hypothesis while reserving the machine-owned sections for reducer updates after scoring and benchmarking.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. OVERVIEW\",\"truncated\":false,\"loaded\":[],\"display\":{\"type\":\"file\",\"path\":\"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/assets/agent_improvement/improvement_strategy.md\",\"text\":\"---\\ntitle: Recursive Agent Strategy Template\\ndescription: Mutable runtime strategy template for deep-improvement sessions.\\ntrigger_phrases:\\n  - \\\"recursive agent strategy\\\"\\n  - \\\"improvement strategy template\\\"\\n  - \\\"operator-owned strategy fields\\\"\\n  - \\\"machine-owned status fields\\\"\\nimportance_tier: normal\\ncontextType: planning\\nversion: 1.17.0.9\\n---\\n\\n# Recursive Agent Strategy\\n\\nMutable strategy template for a deep-improvement run. Use it to capture the operator-owned goal and hypothesis while reserving the machine-owned sections for reducer updates after scoring and benchmarking.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. OVERVIEW\\n\\n### Purpose\\n\\nProvides the working strategy file for a deep-improvement run.\\n\\n### Usage\\n\\nCopy this file into the runtime area, fill the operator-owned fields before the run, and let the reducer update the machine-owned status sections after each score or benchmark cycle.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. OPERATOR-OWNED FIELDS\\n\\n### Target\\n\\n[Set during initialization]\\n\\n### Target Profile\\n\\n[dynamic (any agent)]\\n\\n### Goal\\n\\n[What improvement should be tested]\\n\\n### Constraints\\n\\n- promotion allowed only with explicit per-target approval under dynamic mode\\n- candidate-only targets must never be promoted\\n- runtime mirrors excluded from benchmark truth\\n- no promotion without explicit later-phase gate\\n- stop when no-go or repeatability rules fail\\n\\n### Current Hypothesis\\n\\n[Why the current baseline may be improvable]\\n\\n### Candidate Focus\\n\\n[What the next candidate should change]\\n\\n### Benchmark Focus\\n\\n[Which fixture behaviors are being improved or protected]\\n\\n### Integration Focus\\n\\n[Which integration dimensions need attention: mirror sync, command coverage, permission alignment, resource references]\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. MACHINE-OWNED FIELDS\\n\\n<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: START -->\\n### What Improved\\n\\n[Reducer updates after scoring and benchmarking]\\n\\n### Dimensional Scores\\n\\n| Dimension | Latest | Best | Trend |\\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n| Structural | - | - | - |\\n| Rule Coherence | - | - | - |\\n| Integration | - | - | - |\\n| Output Quality | - | - | - |\\n| System Fitness | - | - | - |\\n\\n[Reducer populates this table after each scored iteration]\\n\\n### What Failed\\n\\n[Reducer updates after scoring and benchmarking]\\n\\n### Best Known State\\n\\n[Reducer names the current best state]\\n\\n### Next Recommendation\\n\\n[Reducer recommends continue, stop, or hold]\\n\\n### Packaging Follow-Up\\n\\n[Reducer records downstream mirror-sync or parity debt separately]\\n\\n### Mutation Coverage\\n\\n| Dimension | Tried | Exhausted | Remaining |\\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n| Structural | - | - | - |\\n| Rule Coherence | - | - | - |\\n| Integration | - | - | - |\\n| Output Quality | - | - | - |\\n| System Fitness | - | - | - |\\n\\n[Reducer populates from mutation-coverage.json after each iteration]\\n\\n### Convergence Eligibility\\n\\n- **Data Points**: [count] / 3 minimum required\\n- **Stability**: [all dimensions within +/-2 across last 3 points?]\\n- **Exhaustion**: [any dimensions fully exhausted?]\\n- **Verdict**: [eligible / not eligible / insufficient data]\\n\\n[Reducer updates after each trajectory recording]\\n\\n### Trade-Off Detection\\n\\n| Iteration | Improving | Regressing | Action |\\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n| - | - | - | - |\\n\\n[Reducer populates when trade-off-detector.cjs flags a Pareto trade-off]\\n\\n**Trade-Off Resolution Guidance:**\\n- If hard dimension regresses (structural, integration, systemFitness): BLOCK promotion, revert mutation\\n- If soft dimension regresses (ruleCoherence, outputQuality): WARN operator, allow with explicit approval\\n- If Pareto-dominated: REJECT candidate regardless of weighted score improvement\\n<!-- MACHINE-OWNED: END -->\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. RELATED RESOURCES\\n\\n- `improvement_charter.md`\\n- `../../references/shared/loop_protocol.md`\\n- `target_manifest.jsonc`\\n\",\"lineStart\":1,\"lineEnd\":149,\"totalLines\":149,\"truncated\":false}},\"title\":\".opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/assets/agent_improvement/improvement_strategy.md\",\"time\":{\"start\":1783026027056,\"end\":1783026027072}},\"metadata\":{\"openai\":{\"itemId\":\"fc_0ae7238cb60217fb016a46d16aee1c8191ae489c2e0af6c4df\"}},\"id\":\"prt_f24a20a2c001zHGJjnJ1DLB6h2\",\"sessionID\":\"ses_0db604ff4ffe9jIhsu9PG5okzD\",\"messageID\":\"msg_f24a1bc7c001E9ySV24FpvpqeD\"}}"}

Add File: .opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-004-improvement-target/improvement/candidates/iteration-001-toy-agent-candidate.md\\n+---\\n+name: toy-agent-candidate\\n+mode: subagent\\n+temperature: 0.1\\n+permission:\\n+  read: allow\\n+  edit: deny\\n+  bash: deny\\n+---\\n+\\n+# Toy Reviewer Agent Candidate\\n+\\n+You review short code diffs and report only actionable issues supported by the supplied diff.\\n+\\n+## 1. Core Workflow\\n+\\n+1. Identify the changed files, touched behavior, and visible test impact from the diff.\\n+2. Check correctness first: broken control flow, data loss, edge cases, unsafe defaults, and mismatched contracts.\\n+3. Check maintainability second: confusing names, unnecessary complexity, missing validation, and unclear ownership.\\n+4. Verify every finding against a concrete line, snippet, or observable behavior in the diff.\\n+5. Prefer no finding over speculative feedback when the diff does not prove a problem.\\n+\\n+## 2. Rules\\n+\\n+### ALWAYS\\n+\\n+- ALWAYS review only the supplied short diff and cite concrete lines or snippets.\\n+- ALWAYS classify each finding as `blocker`, `bug`, `risk`, or `nit`.\\n+- ALWAYS explain why the issue matters and what minimal fix would resolve it.\\n+- ALWAYS order findings from highest severity to lowest severity.\\n+- ALWAYS say `No findings` when the diff does not show an actionable issue.\\n+\\n+### NEVER\\n+\\n+- NEVER invent surrounding code, requirements, tests, or runtime behavior that is not visible in the prompt.\\n+- NEVER rewrite unrelated code or propose broad refactors as review findings.\\n+- NEVER report vague style preferences without a concrete maintenance or behavior impact.\\n+- NEVER hide uncertainty; state what evidence is missing when a concern cannot be confirmed.\\n+\\n+### ESCALATE IF\\n+\\n+- Ask for more context when the diff omits a required caller, schema, or test fixture needed to verify a suspected bug.\\n+- Mark a finding as a risk, not a bug, when the failure mode depends on unknown production inputs.\\n+\\n+## 3. Output Verification\\n+\\n+☐ Findings are ordered by severity before any summary.\\n+☐ Each finding includes severity, location, problem, impact, and minimal fix.\\n+☐ Every finding cites evidence from the supplied diff.\\n+☐ The response states `No findings` when there is no actionable issue.\\n+☐ Residual risks or missing tests are named separately from confirmed findings.\\n+\\n+## 4. Anti-Patterns\\n+\\n+- **Never** report generic advice such as \\\"be careful\\\" or \\\"improve readability\\\" without a concrete failing behavior.\\n+- **Never** expand review scope beyond the provided diff.\\n+- **Never** treat a preference as a defect unless it can cause incorrect behavior, unclear ownership, or avoidable maintenance cost.\\n+\\n+## 5. Capability Scan\\n+\\n+| Tool | Permission | Purpose |\\n+| --- | --- | --- |\\n+| `Read` | allow | Inspect the supplied diff or referenced local context when provided. |\\n+\\n+## 6. COMBINED STOP RULE:\n644:                a) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_ALLOWED\": decision = \"STOP\"\n645:                b) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"STOP_BLOCKED\":\n646:                     decision = \"BLOCKED\"\n647:                     stop_blocked = true\n648:                     stopReason = \"blockedStop\"\n649:                     # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the\n650:                     # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`\n651:                     # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full\n652:                     # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator blocked_by_csv = graph_blockers_csv\n655:                     graph_blocker_detail_json = graph_blockers_json\n656:                     recovery_strategy = \"Resolve review coverage-graph blockers before stopping.\"\n657:                c) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == \"CONTINUE\":\n658:                     decision = \"CONTINUE\"\n659:                     stop_blocked = false\n660:                     reason = \"graph_convergence_continue\"\n661:                d) else keep the existing STUCK_RECOVERY or CONTINUE outcome\n662:         outputs:\n663:           - decision: \"STOP | graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED\"\n666:           - blocked_by_json: \"JSON array of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED\"\n667:           - blocked_by_csv: \"Comma-separated list of for convergenceGate\"\n670:           - dimension_coverage_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for dimensionCoverageGate\"\n671:           - dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json: \"JSON array of covered p0ResolutionGate\"\n674:           - active_p0_count: \"Active P0 finding count at stop-check time\"\n675:           - evidence_density_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for evidenceDensityGate\"\n676:           - avg_evidence_per_finding: \"Numeric evidence density for active findings\"\n677:           - hotspot_saturation_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for hotspotSaturationGate\"\n678:           - claim_adjudication_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for claimAdjudicationGate (false vetoes STOP)\"\n679:           - claim_adjudication_active_count: \"Count of active P0/P1 findings evaluated by claimAdjudicationGate\"\n680:           - fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for fixCompletenessReplayGate (false vetoes STOP on security-sensitive fix reruns)\"\n681:           - security_sensitive_fix_scope: \"Boolean true when the review is a security-sensitive fix rerun\"\n682:           - fix_completeness_replay_required_rows: \"Count of closed-gate replay rows required before STOP\"\n683:           - fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows: \"Count of required closed-gate replay rows that passed with evidence\"\n684:           - candidate_coverage_gate_pass: \"Boolean pass/fail for candidateCoverageGate (false vetoes STOP for unresolved reducer-owned search debt or uncovered required bug classes)\"\n685:           - candidate_coverage_search_debt_json: \"JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations used as blocked-stop evidence\"\n686:           - candidate_coverage_missing_json: \"JSON array of required bug classes missing from required bug classes missing cited fallback ledger rows\"\n690:           - graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason: \"Explicit unavailability reason when graphCoverageMode == unavailable_blocked\"\n691:           - graph_decision: \"CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED from step_graph_convergence\"\n692:           - graph_signals_json: \"JSON object returned by graph convergence\"\n693:           - graph_blockers_json: \"JSON array of graph blockers\"\n694:           - graph_blockers_csv: \"Comma-separated graph blocker summary\"\n695:           - recovery_strategy: \"One-line hint describing the next review action when decision == BLOCKED or STUCK_RECOVERY\"\n696: \n697:       step_emit_blocked_stop:\n698:         action: \"Persist blocked-stop evidence whenever the review legal-stop decision tree vetoes STOP\"\n699:         if_blocked:\n700:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"blocked_stop\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"run\":{current_iteration},\"blockedBy\":{blocked_by_json},\"gateResults\":{\"convergenceGate\":{\"pass\":{convergence_gate_pass},\"score\":{convergence_gate_score}},\"dimensionCoverageGate\":{\"pass\":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},\"covered\":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},\"missing\":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},\"p0ResolutionGate\":{\"pass\":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},\"activeP0\":{active_p0_count}},\"evidenceDensityGate\":{\"pass\":{evidence_density_gate_pass},\"avgEvidencePerFinding\":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},\"hotspotSaturationGate\":{\"pass\":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},\"claimAdjudicationGate\":{\"pass\":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},\"activeP0P1\":{claim_adjudication_active_count}},\"fixCompletenessReplayGate\":{\"pass\":{fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass},\"securitySensitive\":{security_sensitive_fix_scope},\"requiredRows\":{fix_completeness_replay_required_rows},\"passingRows\":{fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows}},\"candidateCoverageGate\":{\"pass\":{candidate_coverage_gate_pass},\"searchDebt\":{candidate_coverage_search_debt_json},\"missing\":{candidate_coverage_missing_json}},\"graphlessFallbackGate\":{\"pass\":{graphless_fallback_gate_pass},\"mode\":\"{graphless_fallback_mode}\",\"missing\":{graphless_fallback_missing_json},\"unavailabilityReason\":\"{graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason}\"}},\"graphBlockerDetail\":{graph_blockers_json},\"recoveryStrategy\":\"{recovery_strategy}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n701:           log: \"Blocked STOP persisted for review loop. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}\"\n702:         if_not_blocked:\n703:           proceed: true\n704: \n705:       step_handle_convergence:\n706:         action: \"Act on convergence decision\"\n707:         if_stop:\n708:           branch_on: \"convergence_mode == 'divergent' AND reason == 'all_dimensions_clean'\"\n709:           if_divergent_eligible:\n710:             eligibility_contract:\n711:               required_decision: \"STOP\"\n712:               eligible_reasons: [all_dimensions_clean]\n713:               excluded_reasons: [maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused]\n714:               fail_closed: \"Any missing or ambiguous reason uses if_existing_terminal_path without preparing a pivot. The nine legal-stop gates have already run and remain unchanged.\"\n715:             steps:\n716:               step_prepare_divergent_pivot:\n717:                 action: \"Generate state-grounded read-only review candidates and prepare or resume the pivot transaction\"\n718:                 command: |\n719:                   node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n720:                   import {\n721:                     buildReviewPivotPreparationInput,\n722:                     renderReviewPivotSeatPrompt,\n723:                   } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/scripts/divergent-review-pivot.ts';\n724:                   import {\n725:                     preparePivotTransaction,\n726:                   } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n727: \n728:                   const input = buildReviewPivotPreparationInput({\n729:                     artifactRoot: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n730:                     configPath: '{state_paths.config}',\n731:                     stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n732:                     strategyPath: '{state_paths.strategy}',\n733:                     registryPath: '{state_paths.findings_registry}',\n734:                     currentIteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n735:                     maxIterations: Number('{max_iterations}'),\n736:                     triggerReason: '{reason}',\n737:                   });\n738:                   const prepared = await preparePivotTransaction(input);\n739:                   const seatPrompts = prepared.status === 'pending'\n740:                     ? Object.fromEntries(prepared.missingSeats.map((seat) => [\n741:                         seat.id,\n742:                         renderReviewPivotSeatPrompt(seat, prepared.dispatchContext),\n743:                       ]))\n744:                     : {};\n745:                   process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify({\n746:                     ...prepared,\n747:                     seatPrompts,\n748:                     boundaryRejections: input.boundaryRejections,\n749:                   })}\\n`);\n750:                   EOF\n751:                 working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n752:                 outputs:\n753:                   - pivot_prepare_json: \"The complete parsed JSON result\"\n754:                   - pivot_prepare_status: \"status from the prepare result\"\n755:                   - pivot_id: \"pivotId from the prepare result\"\n756:                   - pivot_paths_json: \"paths from the prepare result as JSON\"\n757:                   - pivot_resumed: \"resumed from the prepare result\"\n758:                   - pivot_missing_seat_ids: \"missingSeats ids from a pending result\"\n759:                   - pivot_seat_001_json: \"The seat-001 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n760:                   - pivot_seat_002_json: \"The seat-002 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n761:                   - pivot_seat_003_json: \"The seat-003 mandate object from missingSeats\"\n762:                   - pivot_seat_001_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-001']\"\n763:                   - pivot_seat_002_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-002']\"\n764:                   - pivot_seat_003_prompt: \"seatPrompts['seat-003']\"\n765:                   - pivot_dispatch_context_json: \"dispatchContext from a pending result as JSON\"\n766:                 short_circuit: \"When status is completed or failed, skip all dispatch and record steps and bind this result as pivot_result.\"\n767: \n768:               step_dispatch_pivot_seat_001:\n769:                 action: \"Natively dispatch the analytical read-only review direction-selection seat\"\n770:                 skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-001'\"\n771:                 capture_timing: true\n772:                 if_native:\n773:                   dispatch:\n774:                     agent: deep-review\n775:                     model: opus\n776:                     context_source: \"pivot_seat_001_prompt\"\n777:                   wait_for_completion: true\n778:                 failure_contract: \"A native dispatch failure binds status=rejected, a {name,message} error, and the measured timestamps/duration, then proceeds to step_record_pivot_seat_001.\"\n779:                 outputs:\n780:                   - pivot_seat_001_dispatch_status: \"fulfilled or rejected\"\n781:                   - pivot_seat_001_output_json: \"Raw native return serialized as JSON when fulfilled\"\n782:                   - pivot_seat_001_error_json: \"{name,message} when rejected\"\n783:                   - pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso: \"Dispatch start timestamp\"\n784:                   - pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso: \"Dispatch completion timestamp\"\n785:                   - pivot_seat_001_duration_ms: \"Dispatch duration in milliseconds\"\n786: \n787:               step_record_pivot_seat_001:\n788:                 action: \"Durably record the seat-001 native dispatch outcome\"\n789:                 skip_when: \"pivot_prepare_status != 'pending' or pivot_missing_seat_ids does not contain 'seat-001'\"\n790:                 command: |\n791:                   node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n792:                   import { recordPivotSeatResult } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/divergent-pivot.ts';\n793:                   const status = '{pivot_seat_001_dispatch_status}';\n794:                   const outcome = status === 'fulfilled'\n795:                     ? { status, output: {pivot_seat_001_output_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_001_duration_ms}') }\n796:                     : { status: 'rejected', error: {pivot_seat_001_error_json}, startedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_started_at_iso}', completedAtIso: '{pivot_seat_001_completed_at_iso}', durationMs: Number('{pivot_seat_001_duration_ms}') };\n797:                   process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(recordPivotSeatResult({ paths: {pivot_paths_json}, pivotId: '{pivot_id}', seat: {pivot_seat_001_json}, outcome }))}\\n`);\n798:                   EOF\n799:                 working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n800: \n801:               step_dispatch_pivot_seat_002:\n802:                 action: \"Natively dispatch the critical read-only review  working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n876:                 outputs:\n877:                   - pivot_finalize_json: \"The complete parsed final result\"\n878: \n879:               step_resolve_divergent_pivot_result:\n880:                 action: \"Use prepare's terminal result on resume/short-circuit, otherwise use finalize's result\"\n881:                 branch_on: \"pivot_prepare_status\"\n882:                 if_pending: { bind: { pivot_result: \"{pivot_finalize_json}\" } }\n883:                 if_completed_or_failed: { bind: { pivot_result: \"{pivot_prepare_json}\" } }\n884: \n885:               step_apply_divergent_pivot_result:\n886:                 action: \"Adopt a successful read-only review direction or fail closed to the existing synthesis path\"\n887:                 branch_on: \"pivot_result.status\"\n888:                 if_completed:\n889:                   set:\n890:                     next_dimension: \"{pivot_result.restoredFocus}\"\n891:                     pivot_lineage: \"Pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} selected {pivot_result.selectedCandidate.id} from source iteration {current_iteration}.\"\n892:                     saturated_directions: \"{pivot_result.completedEvent.saturatedDirections}\"\n893:                   command: \"node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}\"\n894:                   log: \"Divergent review pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} restored focus to {pivot_result.restoredFocus}; continuing iteration {current_iteration}.\"\n895:                   proceed_to: step_normalize_pause_events\n896:                 if_failed:\n897:                   log: \"Divergent review pivot {pivot_result.pivotId} failed closed ({pivot_result.failureReason}); proceeding to synthesis.\"\n898:                   skip_to: phase_synthesis\n899:             on_error:\n900:               log: \"Divergent review pivot integration error; failing closed to the existing synthesis path.\"\n901:               skip_to: phase_synthesis\n902:           if_existing_terminal_path:\n903:             log: \"Loop stopping. Reason: {reason}\"\n904:             skip_to: phase_synthesis\n905:         if_blocked:\n906:           log: \"Legal-stop gates blocked STOP. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Continuing review.\"\n907:         if_stuck_recovery:\n908:           log: \"Stuck detected ({stuck_count} consecutive no-progress). Entering recovery.\"\n909:           recovery_strategies:\n910:             - \"Change granularity: file-level → function-level → line-level review\"\n911:             - \"Traceability protocol replay: re-run unresolved core or overlay checks\"\n912:             - \"Escalate severity review: re-examine P2 findings for potential P1 upgrades\"\n913:           pivot_target: \"Pivot to the least-covered dimension (the review dimension with the fewest iteration passes and lowest coverage ratio).\"\n914:           set:\n915:             next_dimension: \"RECOVERY: {recovery_strategy}. Least-covered dimension: {least_explored}\"\n916:             pending_stop_event: \"stuck_recovery\"\n917:             pending_stop_reason: \"stuckRecovery\"\n918:             pending_stop_detail: \"Recovery path activated after repeated no-progress iterations\"\n919:           reset: { stuck_count: 0 }\n920:         if_continue:\n921:           log: \"Iteration {current_iteration}: Continuing. Dimension: {next_dimension}\"\n922: \n923:       step_normalize_pause_events:\n924:         action: \"Rewrite raw pause and recovery events to the frozen STOP_REASONS enum at emission time: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused\"\n925:         if_pending_pause_event:\n926:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"userPaused\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"stopReason\":\"userPaused\",\"reason\":\"{pending_stop_detail}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n927:           message: \"Review paused. Delete review/.deep-review-pause to resume.\"\n928:           reset:\n929:             pending_stop_event: null\n930:             pending_stop_reason: null\n931:             pending_stop_detail: null\n932:           halt: true\n933:         if_pending_stuck_recovery:\n934:           append_jsonl: '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"stuckRecovery\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"stopReason\":\"stuckRecovery\",\"fromIteration\":{current_iteration},\"strategy\":\"{recovery_strategy}\",\"targetDimension\":\"{least_explored}\",\"outcome\":\"pending\",\"reason\":\"{pending_stop_detail}\",\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\",\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"generation\":{generation}}'\n935:           reset:\n936:             pending_stop_event: null\n937:             pending_stop_reason: null\n938:             pending_stop_detail: null\n939:         if_no_pending_event:\n940:           proceed: true\n941: \n942:       step_generate_state_summary:\n943:         action: \"Generate compact state summary for dispatch context (~200 tokens)\"\n944:         generate: |\n945:           STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):\n946:           Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}\n947:           Dimension: {next_dimension}\n948:           Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}\n949:           Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})\n950:           Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}\n951:           {resource_map_prompt_line}\n952:           Coverage Age: {coverage_age}\n953:           Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}\n954:           Stuck count: {stuck_count}\n955:           Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\n956: \n957:       step_marker_scan:\n958:         action: \"H-2 loop-prevention guard: scan previous iteration's rendered prompt for nested review marker\"\n959:         algorithm: |\n960:           if current_iteration > 1:\n961:             PREV_PROMPT_PATH = \"{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{previous_iteration}.md\"\n962:             if file_exists(PREV_PROMPT_PATH):\n963:               PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE = head -1 of PREV_PROMPT_PATH\n964:               if PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE matches ^(DEEP-REVIEW|DEEP-RESEARCH|CODE-REVIEW)$:\n965:                 log \"ERROR: nested review-of-review loop detected (marker found in previous iteration prompt). Aborting.\"\n966:                 halt with exit code 2\n967:         note: |\n968:           False-positive prevention: only matches when the first line is exactly one of the\n969:           three canonical markers. Markers appearing in comments or body text are ignored.\n970:           True-positive triggers only when a previous iteration's rendered prompt literally\n971:           starts with a deep-loop marker, indicating a nested review-of-review dispatch.\n972: \n973:       step_dispatch_iteration:\n974:         action: \"Dispatch @deep-review agent for one review iteration\"\n975:         resolve_executor:\n976:           source: \"config.executor\"\n977:           loader: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig\"\n978:         pre_dispatch:\n979:           generate_summary: |\n980:             STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):\n981:             Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}\n982:             Dimension: {next_dimension}\n983:             Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}\n984:             Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})\n985:             Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}\n986:             {resource_map_prompt_line}\n987:             Coverage Age: {coverage_age}\n988:             Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}\n989:             Stuck count: {stuck_count}\n990:             Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\n991:           render_prompt_pack:\n992:             template_path: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl\"\n993:             renderer: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts#renderPromptPack\"\n994:             output_path: \"{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md\"\n995:             variables:\n996:               state_summary: \"{state_summary}\"\n997:               current_iteration: \"{current_iteration}\"\n998:               max_iterations: \"{max_iterations}\"\n999:               next_dimension: \"{next_dimension}\"\n1000:               review_target: \"{review_target}\"\n1001:               review_scope_files: \"{review_scope_files}\"\n1002:               p0_count: \"{p0_count}\"\n1003:               p1_count: \"{p1_count}\"\n1004:               p2_count: \"{p2_count}\"\n1005:               pivot_lineage: \"{pivot_lineage}\"\n1006:               saturated_directions: \"{saturated_directions}\"\n1007:               state_paths_config: \"{state_paths.config}\"\n1008:               state_paths_state_log: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1009:               state_paths_findings_registry: \"{state_paths.findings_registry}\"\n1010:               state_paths_strategy: \"{state_paths.strategy}\"\n1011:               state_paths_iteration_pattern: \"{state_paths.iteration_pattern}\"\n1012:               state_paths_delta_pattern: \"{state_paths.delta_pattern}\"\n1013:         pre_dispatch_audit:\n1014:           skip_when: \"config.executor.kind == 'native'\"\n1015:           call: \".opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts#writeFirstRecordExecutor\"\n1016:           args:\n1017:             stateLogPath: \"{state_paths.state_log}\"\n1018:             executor: \"{config.executor}\"\n1019:             iteration: \"{current_iteration}\"\n1020:         branch_on: \"config.executor.kind\"\n1021:         if_native:\n1022:           dispatch:\n1023:             agent: deep-review\n1024:             model: opus\n1025:             context_source: \"rendered_prompt_pack\"\n1026:           wait_for_completion: true\n1027:         if_cli_copilot:\n1028:           command: |\n1029:             node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n1030:             import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n1031:             import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';\n1032: \n1033:             import { runAuditedExecutorCommand } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts';\n1034:             import { buildCopilotPromptArg } from './.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts';\n1035: \n1036:             const promptPath = '{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md';\n1037:             const prompt = readFileSync(promptPath, 'utf8');\n1038: \n1039:             // Workflow-resolved spec folder is the only legal write authority.\n1040:             // The helper (`validateSpecFolder` inside `buildCopilotPromptArg`)\n1041:             // is the single source of truth for rejecting empty / unresolved\n1042:             // template / whitespace-only / string-coerced empty values. The\n1043:             // YAML hands the raw `{spec_folder}` directly; on rejection the\n1044:             // helper safe-fails to `type:\"missing\", writeIntent:true` (Gate-3\n1045:             // enforcement, --allow-all-tools stripped).\n1046:             const specFolderRaw = '{spec_folder}';\n1047:             const targetAuthority = { type: 'approved', specFolder: specFolderRaw };\n1048: \n1049:             const baseArgs = [\n1050:               '-p',\n1051:               prompt,\n1052:               '--model',\n1053:               '{config.executor.model}',\n1054:               '--allow-all-tools',\n1055:               '--no-ask-user',\n1056:             ];\n1057: \n1058:             const built = buildCopilotPromptArg({\n1059:               promptPath,\n1060:               prompt,\n1061:               targetAuthority,\n1062:               baseArgv: baseArgs,\n1063:             });\n1064: \n1065:             // Large-prompt @PROMPT_PATH wrapper: helper emits a `promptFileBody`\n1066:             // containing `<TARGET AUTHORITY preamble>\\n\\n---\\n\\n<original>`.\n1067:             // Write it to disk so the file Copilot reads via @path opens with\n1068:             // the authority block. Without this, recovered/bootstrap folder\n1069:             // mentions inside the file could still anchor the model.\n1070:             if (built.promptFileBody !== undefined) {\n1071:               writeFileSync(promptPath, built.promptFileBody, 'utf8');\n1072:             }\n1073: \n1074:             // Copilot is routed through the audited executor wrapper so it produces\n1075:             // INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts alongside claude-code/opencode\n1076:             // There is no cli-copilot kind in the executor schema, so it\n1077:             // dispatches as kind:'native' (full env passthrough, no recursion-guard\n1078:             // layer configured for copilot); the receipt still records the real\n1079:             // 'copilot' command + built.argv as facts.\n1080:             const dispatchFacts = {\n1081:               command: 'deep/review',\n1082:               mode: 'review',\n1083:               specFolder: '{spec_folder}',\n1084:               artifactDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}',\n1085:               iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n1086:               executorKind: '{config.executor.kind}',\n1087:               branchId: '{config.executor.kind}',\n1088:               sessionId: '{session_id}',\n1089:               generation: Number('{generation}'),\n1090:             };\n1091:             const dispatchId = 'review-i{current_iteration}-g{generation}';\n1092:             process.exit(\n1093:               runAuditedExecutorCommand({\n1094:                 command: 'copilot',\n1095:                 args: built.argv,\n1096:                 cwd: '{repo_root}',\n1097:                 timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n1098:                 stateLogPath: '{state_paths.state_log}',\n1099:                 executor: {\n1100:                   kind: 'native',\n1101:                   model: '{config.executor.model}',\n1102:                   configDir: null,\n1103:                   reasoningEffort: null,\n1104:                   serviceTier: null,\n1105:                   sandboxMode: null,\n1106:                   timeoutSeconds: Number('{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}'),\n1107:                   governor: null,\n1108:                 },\n1109:                 iteration: Number('{current_iteration}'),\n1110:                 receiptDir: '{state_paths.packet_dir}/dispatch-receipts',\n1111:                 dispatchId,\n1112:               }),\n1113:             );\n1114:             EOF\n1115:           working_directory: \"{repo_root}\"\n1116:           timeout_seconds: \"{config.executor.timeoutSeconds}\"\n1117:           wait_for_completion: true\n1118:           notes:\n1119:             - \"Routed through runAuditedExecutorCommand so copilot produces INTENT + COMPLETION dispatch receipts; built.argv (@PROMPT_PATH or positional) is forwarded verbatim as the receipt facts\"\n1120:             - \"copilot has no stdin support; prompt is positional\"\n1121:             - \"Size check uses 16KB threshold; above that uses @PROMPT_PATH wrapper prompt\"\n1122:             - \"Large-prompt path relies on copilot honoring @path references within its prompt string\"\n1123:             - \"buildCopilotPromptArg attaches a TARGET AUTHORITY preamble bound to the workflow-approved spec folder; recovered context cannot override it (authority guard source: .opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml)\"\n1124:             - \"If spec_folder is missing/malformed/unresolved on a prompt file at promptPath to include the TARGET AUTHORITY preamble (prefix + divider + original body); the argv becomes a bare @promptPath reference\"\n1126:         if_cli_claude_code:\n1127:           command: |\n1128:             node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'\n1129:             import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n1130:             import { createHash } from const executor = {\n1137:               kind: 'cli-claude-code',\n1138:               model: '{config.executor.model}',\n1139:               configDir: configDir || null,\n1140: v1.14.39 reads stdin at startup and hangs at 0% CPU after +60s snapshot prune without it (the `cli-external/cli-opencode` mode's SKILL.md (`.opencode/skills/cli-external/cli-opencode/SKILL.md`) §4
