Build `activeFindings`, `findingClasses`, and `affectedSurfacesSeed` from reducer-owned state plus iteration JSONL `findingDetails`; if a field is missing, mark it `UNKNOWN` instead of inferring it from prose\\n\\n          3. **Active Finding Registry**\\n             - Unified registry for active P0/P1/P2 findings\\n             - For each: ID, severity, title, dimension, file:line, evidence, impact, fix recommendation, disposition, findingClass, scopeProof, affectedSurfaceHints\\n\\n          4. **Remediation Workstreams**\\n             - Ordered action groups: P0 first, then P1, with P2 advisories separated\\n\\n          5. **Spec Seed**\\n             - Concrete seed bullets for follow-on spec updates\\n\\n          6. **Plan Seed**\\n             - Concrete starter tasks for `/speckit:plan`\\n\\n           7. **Traceability Status**\\n              - Summarize required core protocols first, then applicable overlay protocols\\n              - Include status, evidence, and any unresolved drift per protocol\\n              - Include `AC_COVERAGE` status for lifecycle-active spec-folder targets: exempt, disabled, pass, or advisory-shortfall, with covered/total and floor when available\\n\\n          If `resource_map_present` is true, insert **## Resource Map Coverage Gate** after `Traceability Status` with exactly three bullets:\\n             - `Entries touched`: count of resource-map paths that appear in applied reports\\n             - `Entries not touched`: paths in resource-map not in implementation, labeled `expected-by-scope` or `gap`\\n             - `Implementation paths absent from resource-map`: `target_files` in applied reports not on the map\\n\\n          8. **Deferred Items**\\n             - Advisory-only or backlog-worthy follow-ups that do not block the current verdict\\n\\n          9. **Search Ledger**\\n             - Always include this section before the appendix.\\n             - Render reducer-owned `searchCoverage`, `candidateCoverage`, `searchDebt`, `ruledOutCandidates`, and `cleanSearchProof`.\\n             - If all arrays are empty or absent, include: `*No search-depth state captured (legacy v1 record)*`.\\n             - When `searchDebt` is non-empty, state `hasSearchDebt: true` and carry the dashboard verdict as CONDITIONAL unless active P0 findings make it FAIL.\\n\\n          10. **Audit Appendix**\\n             - Convergence summary, coverage summary, ruled-out claims, sources reviewed\\n             - Cross-reference appendix MUST be split into `Core Protocols` and `Overlay Protocols`\\n\\n          Preserve machine-owned markers so the reducer-owned registry, dashboard, and strategy anchors can be refreshed on re-run without mutating prior synthesis. (Note: `completed-continue` lineage branch is deferred — see `deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches`.)\\n        output: \\\"{state_paths.review_output}\\\"\\n\\n      step_convergence_report:\\n        action: \\\"Append convergence report to JSONL\\\"\\n        command: |\\n          node --input-type=module <<'EOF'\\n          import { appendFileSync, existsSync, readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs';\\n\\n          const paths = {\\n            stateLog: String.raw`{state_paths.state_log}`,\\n            registry: String.raw`{state_paths.findings_registry}`,\\n            review: String.raw`{state_paths.review_output}`,\\n            dashboard: String.raw`{state_paths.dashboard}`,\\n          };\\n\\n          function hasFile(filePath) {\\n            try {\\n              return existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isFile();\\n            } catch {\\n              return false;\\n            }\\n          }\\n\\n          function readJson(filePath) {\\n            try {\\n              return JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));\\n            } catch {\\n              return null;\\n            }\\n          }\\n\\n          function readJsonl(filePath) {\\n            if (!hasFile(filePath)) return [];\\n            return readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8')\\n              .split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)\\n              .map((line) => line.trim())\\n              .filter(Boolean)\\n              .flatMap((line) => {\\n                try {\\n                  return [JSON.parse(line)];\\n                } catch {\\n                  return [];\\n                }\\n              });\\n          }\\n\\n          function countIterationFindings(record) {\\n            if (!record || record.type !== 'iteration') return 0;\\n            const numeric = Number(record.findingsCount);\\n            if (Number.isFinite(numeric) && numeric > 0) return Math.floor(numeric);\\n            if (Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) return record.findingDetails.length;\\n            return 0;\\n          }\\n\\n          function normalizeFindingKey(value) {\\n            return String(value || '').trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\\\\s+/g, ' ');\\n          }\\n\\n          function findingKeys(candidate) {\\n            if (typeof candidate === 'string') {\\n              const key = normalizeFindingKey(candidate);\\n              return key ? [key] : [];\\n            }\\n            if (!candidate || typeof candidate !== 'object' || Array.isArray(candidate)) return [];\\n            return [...new Set([\\n              candidate.id,\\n              candidate.findingId,\\n              candidate.title,\\n              candidate.summary,\\n              candidate.text,\\n              candidate.finding,\\n              candidate.description,\\n            ].map(normalizeFindingKey).filter(Boolean))];\\n          }\\n\\n          function collectIterationFindingGroups(record) {\\n            if (!record || record.type !== 'iteration' || !Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) return [];\\n            return record.findingDetails\\n              .map(findingKeys)\\n              .filter((keys) => keys.length > 0);\\n          }\\n\\n          function collectRegistryFindingKeys(registry) {\\n            return [registry?.openFindings, registry?.resolvedFindings, registry?.repeatedFindings]\\n              .filter(Array.isArray)\\n              .flatMap((findings) => findings)\\n              .flatMap(findingKeys);\\n          }\\n\\n          function appendEvent(event) {\\n            appendFileSync(paths.stateLog, `${JSON.stringify(event)}\\\\n`, 'utf8');\\n          }\\n\\n          const registry = readJson(paths.registry);\\n          const openCount = Array.isArray(registry?.openFindings) ? registry.openFindings.length : null;\\n          const resolvedCount = Array.isArray(registry?.resolvedFindings) ? registry.resolvedFindings.length : 0;\\n          const repeatedCount = Array.isArray(registry?.repeatedFindings) ? registry.repeatedFindings.length : 0;\\n          const registryFindingCount = openCount === null ? null : openCount + resolvedCount + repeatedCount;\\n          const stateRecords = readJsonl(paths.stateLog);\\n          const iterationFindingCount = stateRecords.reduce((sum, record) => sum + countIterationFindings(record), 0);\\n          const identifiableFindingGroups = stateRecords.flatMap(collectIterationFindingGroups);\\n          const registryFindingKeys = new Set(collectRegistryFindingKeys(registry));\\n          const missingStructuredFindingCount = identifiableFindingGroups.filter((keys) => !keys.some((key) => registryFindingKeys.has(key))).length;\\n          const identifiableFindingCount = identifiableFindingGroups.length;\\n          const missingArtifacts = [\\n            ['registry', paths.registry],\\n            ['review', paths.review],\\n            ['dashboard', paths.dashboard],\\n          ].filter(([, filePath]) => !hasFile(filePath)).map(([name, filePath]) => ({ name, path: filePath }));\\n          const invariantFailures = [];\\n          if (missingArtifacts.length > 0) invariantFailures.push('missing_synthesis_artifacts');\\n          if (registryFindingCount === null) invariantFailures.push('registry_missing_openFindings');\\n          if (iterationFindingCount > 0 && (registryFindingCount ?? 0) === 0) invariantFailures.push('state_findings_not_reflected_in_registry');\\n          if (identifiableFindingCount > 0 && registryFindingCount !== null && registryFindingCount < identifiableFindingCount) invariantFailures.push('structured_state_findings_partially_reflected_in_registry');\\n          if (missingStructuredFindingCount > 0) invariantFailures.push('structured_state_findings_missing_from_registry');\\n\\n          if (invariantFailures.length > 0) {\\n            appendEvent({\\n              type: 'event',\\n              event: 'synthesis_incomplete',\\n              mode: 'review',\\n              severity: 'warning',\\n              totalIterations: Number('{iteration_count}'),\\n              activeP0: Number('{active_p0}'),\\n              activeP1: Number('{active_p1}'),\\n              activeP2: Number('{active_p2}'),\\n              dimensionCoverage: Number('{dimension_coverage}'),\\n              verdict: '{verdict}',\\n              releaseReadinessState: '{release_readiness_state}',\\n              stopReason: '{reason}',\\n              reason: 'synthesis_artifact_invariant_failed',\\n              invariantFailures,\\n              missingArtifacts,\\n              registryFindingCount,\\n              iterationFindingCount,\\n              identifiableFindingCount,\\n              missingStructuredFindingCount,\\n              timestamp: '{ISO_8601_NOW}',\\n            });\\n          } else {\\n            appendEvent({\\n              type: 'event',\\n              event: 'synthesis_complete',\\n              mode: 'review',\\n              totalIterations: Number('{iteration_count}'),\\n              activeP0: Number('{active_p0}'),\\n              activeP1: Number('{active_p1}'),\\n              activeP2: Number('{active_p2}'),\\n              dimensionCoverage: Number('{dimension_coverage}'),\\n              verdict: '{verdict}',\\n              releaseReadinessState: '{release_readiness_state}',\\n              stopReason: '{reason}',\\n              timestamp: '{ISO_8601_NOW}',\\n            });\\n          }\\n          EOF\\n        note: \\\"Emit as numeric ratio (e.g., 0.75), not quoted string. Compute: dimensions_covered / total_dimensions.\\\"\\n\\n      step_update_config_status:\\n        action: \\\"Mark config as complete\\\"\\n        edit: \\\"{state_paths.config}\\\"\\n        set_field: { status: \\\"complete\\\" }\\n\\n      step_stage_artifact_dir:\\n        action: \\\"Stage all skill-owned artifacts under artifact_dir for the operator's next git commit\\\"\\n        command: \\\"git add {state_paths.packet_dir}\\\"\\n        note: \\\"Skill-owned contract fix (028): without this stage step, operators who do `git add review-report.md` lose the iteration trail (iterations/, deltas/, state.jsonl, strategy, findings-registry, etc.). Staging the whole artifact_dir keeps the audit trail intact. Operators retain commit authority and can `git restore --staged {path}` to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal — log warning and continue.\\\"\\n        on_fail: \\\"Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture) which is a valid case.\\\"\\n\\n  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\\n  # PHASE 4: SAVE\\n  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\\n  phase_save:\\n    purpose: Preserve review context to memory system\\n    steps:\\n\\n      step_compose_save_payload:\\n        action: \\\"Compose the structured JSON payload consumed by generate-context.js\\\"\\n        output: \\\"/tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json\\\"\\n        requirements:\\n          - \\\"Write this file before invoking generate-context.js; the loader fails closed if the explicit JSON file is absent.\\\"\\n          - \\\"Include `specFolder`, a concise `sessionSummary`, curated `observations`, `recent_context`, `toolCalls`, and `exchanges` from the completed review loop.\\\"\\n          - \\\"Use a session-scoped temp filename; do not use the retired shared `/tmp/save-context-data.json` path.\\\"\\n        example_payload: |\\n          {\\n            \\\"specFolder\\\": \\\"{spec_folder}\\\",\\n            \\\"sessionSummary\\\": \\\"Deep review completed; see review/review-report.md for verdict, findings, and workstreams.\\\",\\n            \\\"observations\\\": [\\\"Summarize the active findings, verdict, and remediation guidance.\\\"],\\n            \\\"recent_context\\\": [\\\"Point to the generated review artifacts and next action.\\\"],\\n            \\\"exchanges\\\": [],\\n            \\\"toolCalls\\\": []\\n          }\\n\\n      step_generate_context:\\n        action: \\\"Route continuity content into the correct canonical spec document via generate-context.js\\\"\\n        command: \\\"node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json {spec_folder}\\\"\\n        critical_rule: \\\"NEVER manually author files under retired memory/ paths (the runtime rejects them); use generate-context.js so the save routes into decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md\\\"\\n\\n      step_index_memory:\\n        action: \\\"Index the routed canonical spec document with memory_save when immediate MCP visibility is required\\\"\\n        mcp_tool: memory_save\\n        invocation: 'memory_save({ filePath: \\\"[routed-canonical-spec-doc-path]\\\" })'\\n        note: \\\"Use the actual canonical spec-doc path returned by generate-context.js (decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, or handover.md); memory_index_scan remains the bulk fallback\\\"\\n\\n      step_verify_save:\\n        action: \\\"Verify canonical save routed content into the correct spec document\\\"\\n        check: \\\"Does {spec_folder}/implementation-summary.md, {spec_folder}/decision-record.md, or {spec_folder}/handover.md carry the new continuity update?\\\"\\n        on_fail: \\\"Log warning but do not block completion\\\"\\n\\n      step_release_lock:\\n        action: \\\"Release the single-writer lock after save, skip-save, or cancellation cleanup has resolved\\\"\\n        command: \\\"node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}\\\"\\n        append_to_jsonl: '{\\\"type\\\":\\\"event\\\",\\\"event\\\":\\\"lock_released\\\",\\\"path\\\":\\\"{state_paths.lock_file}\\\",\\\"releasePhase\\\":\\\"save\\\",\\\"timestamp\\\":\\\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\\\"}'\\n\\n  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\\n  # COMPLETION\\n  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\\n  completion:\\n    message: |\\n      Deep review complete.\\n      Iterations: {iteration_count} | Stop reason: {reason}\\n      Findings: P0={active_p0} P1={active_p1} P2={active_p2}\\n      Verdict: {verdict} | hasAdvisories={has_advisories}\\n\\n      Artifacts:\\n        - review/review-report.md (compiled from {iteration_count} iterations)\\n        - {iteration_count} iteration files in review/iterations/\\n        - State files in review/: deep-review-config.json, deep-review-state.jsonl, deep-review-findings-registry.json, deep-review-strategy.md, deep-review-dashboard.md\\n        - Memory context saved\\n\\n      STATUS=OK PATH={spec_folder}\\n    next_steps:\\n      - \\\"/speckit:plan [remediation] (if FAIL or CONDITIONAL verdict)\\\"\\n      - \\\"/create:changelog (if PASS, including PASS with hasAdvisories=true)\\\"\\n      - \\\"/deep:review [target] (for another review)\\\"\\n      - \\\"/memory:save {spec_folder} (refresh canonical continuity before the next session)\\\"\\n\\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# ERROR RECOVERY\\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nerror_recovery:\\n  agent_dispatch_timeout: \\\"Retry once with reduced scope (fewer files per iteration). If second timeout, mark as 'timeout' and continue.\\\"\\n  state_file_missing: \\\"If JSONL missing during loop, reconstruct from iteration files. If strategy missing, create minimal version.\\\"\\n  consecutive_failures: \\\"After 3 consecutive errors, halt loop and enter synthesis with partial findings.\\\"\\n  memory_save_failure: \\\"Preserve the review packet in review/ as backup. Log warning.\\\"\\n\\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# RULES\\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nrules:\\n  ALWAYS:\\n    - follow_workflow_phases_in_order\\n    - dispatch_deep_review_agent_per_iteration\\n    - check_convergence_before_each_iteration\\n    - write_all_state_to_files\\n    - save_memory_context_after_synthesis\\n    - respect_max_iterations_cap\\n    - treat_review_target_as_read_only\\n    - deduplicate_findings_before_synthesis\\n    - run_adversarial_selfcheck_on_p0_p1\\n  NEVER:\\n    - skip_convergence_check\\n    - dispatch_non_leaf_agents\\n    - hold_iteration_findings_in_memory_only\\n    - modify_config_after_initialization\\n    - overwrite_jsonl_state_log\\n    - proceed_without_state_file_verification\\n    - modify_files_under_review\\n    - report_p0_without_file_line_evidence\\n\\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n# REFERENCE-ONLY APPENDIX\\n# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nreference_only_appendix:\\n  checkpoint_commit:\\n    note: \\\"Checkpoint commits are intentionally excluded from workflow.steps.\\\"\\n    commands:\\n      - \\\"git add {state_paths.iteration_pattern} {state_paths.state_log} {state_paths.findings_registry} {state_paths.strategy} {state_paths.review_output}\\\"\\n      - 'git commit -m \\\"chore(deep-review): iteration {NNN} complete\\\"'\\n  severity_weights:\\n    note: \\\"Severity weights for newFindingsRatio calculation\\\"\\n    weights:\\n      P0: 10.0\\n      P1: 5.0\\n      P2: 1.0\\n    p0_override: \\\"If ANY new P0 discovered → newFindingsRatio = max(calculated, 0.50). A single new P0 blocks convergence.\\\"\\n    clean_pass: \\\"If total_findings == 0 (clean pass) → newFindingsRatio = 0.0\\\"\\n  review_dimensions:\\n    note: \\\"All 4 default review dimensions with priority ordering\\\"\\n    dimensions:\\n      - { priority: 1, name: \\\"correctness\\\", checks: \\\"Logic, state flow, edge cases, error handling\\\" }\\n      - { priority: 2, name: \\\"security\\\", checks: \\\"Auth, input/output safety, data exposure, permissions\\\" }\\n      - { priority: 3, name: \\\"traceability\\\", checks: \\\"Spec/checklist alignment, cross-reference integrity, evidence coverage\\\" }\\n      - { priority: 4, name: \\\"maintainability\\\", checks: \\\"Patterns, documentation quality, safe follow-on change clarity\\\" }\\n  cross_reference_protocols:\\n    note: \\\"Cross-reference verification protocols split into core and overlay classes\\\"\\n    core_protocols:\\n      - { check: \\\"Spec vs Code\\\", source: \\\"spec.md claims\\\", target: \\\"implementation\\\" }\\n      - { check: \\\"Checklist vs Evidence\\\", source: \\\"[x] items\\\", target: \\\"cited evidence\\\" }\\n    overlay_protocols:\\n      - { check: \\\"SKILL.md vs Agent\\\", source: \\\"Skill contracts\\\", target: \\\"agent files\\\" }\\n      - { check: \\\"Agent Cross-Runtime\\\", source: \\\".claude\\\", target: \\\".opencode/.agents\\\" }\\n      - { check: \\\"Feature Catalog vs Code\\\", source: \\\"Catalog claims\\\", target: \\\"implementation\\\" }\\n      - { check: \\\"Playbook vs Support\\\", source: \\\"Scenario preconditions\\\", target: \\\"actual support\\\" }\",\"numLines\":644,\"startLine\":960,\"totalLines\":1604}}}"}

.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/z_archive/016-sk-deep-rename/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:10:- No new standalone finding. The hunter pass **amends P0-004**: the self-rename over-replacement is broader than Phase 002. Phase 003 also says `deep-review to deep-review` and `deep-research to deep-research` in `specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/056-sk-deep-rename/003-opencode-internals/spec.md:7` and `specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/056-sk-deep-rename/003-opencode-internals/graph-metadata.json:20`; Phase 004 repeats the same pattern in `specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/056-sk-deep-rename/004-runtime-mirrors/spec.md:8`, `specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/056-sk-deep-rename/004-runtime-mirrors/spec.md:179`, and `specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/056-sk-deep-rename/004-runtime-mirrors/graph-metadata.json:20`. Recommended remediation: restore source-side `sk-deep-review` / `sk-deep-research` wording in child-phase rename narratives and metadata, then grep Packet 070 for identity rename strings.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/z_archive/016-sk-deep-rename/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:44:1. **P0-004 spot-check**: `specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/056-sk-deep-rename/002-skill-folder-rename/spec.md:74` still says to rename `.opencode/skills/deep-review/` to `.opencode/skills/deep-review/`; `tasks.md:67` and `implementation-summary.md:73` repeat the same identity-rename pattern. The cited evidence is correct.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/z_archive/006-deep-skill-evolution/008-deep-stack-playbook-validation/006-release-readiness-synthesis/release-readiness-matrix.md:78:- **Child 009 (copilot→opencode swap):** copilot is org-policy-blocked, so the 18 CP stress scenarios were re-run via `cli-opencode`/`deepseek-v4-pro` (direct DeepSeek API). opencode natively expands `/deep:*` slash commands. The 5 PARTIALs are deepseek-phrasing-vs-literal-grep misses (e.g. "Deep review loop complete" vs grep "complete", uppercase "REFUSE" vs "refuse", slugified topic label, pretty-JSON breaking dotted-path greps) — the underlying skill behaviors (setup binding, leaf-refusal, critic challenge, legal-stop gate bundle) are all present in the transcripts. Orchestrator-verified each against produced `/tmp/cp-*` artifacts + git tripwire. Also caught + fixed a swap defect: agent-body messages starting with `---` made opencode print help (parse failure); a newline-prepend fix was applied to CP-056/057.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/scratch/angle-schedule-modes.json:85:      "directive": "Concrete reference impls: benchmark item design, statistical significance / confidence intervals / paired tests, contamination detection, LLM-judge calibration + de-biasing, cost-normalized scoring, elo/bradley-terry ranking. Real repos + code. Actionable designs for model-benchmark (tie to packet-056 benchmark-validity lessons)." },
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/scratch/angle-schedule-modes.json:87:      "directive": "Benchmark VALIDITY threats: train-test contamination, judge bias/self-preference, high variance / too-few-items, gameable rubrics, gold-set errors. Find detection + guardrails (echo the packet-056 two-reviewer audit findings). Recommend how model-benchmark stays valid and self-audits." },
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/scratch/angle-schedule-modes.json:96:      "directive": "Concrete reference impls: skill scoring rubrics, gold-set design + validation (detecting empty/overfit gold), executor-matrix benchmarking, controlling model-vs-skill confounds, per-scenario baselines. Real repos + code. Actionable designs for skill-benchmark (tie to packet-056)." },
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/scratch/synthesis-digest.md:12:- **ArjunPanickssery/self_recognition** <https://github.com/ArjunPanickssery/self_recognition> [high] — Blind generator identity and model-family provenance from detectors and scorers; logical role prompts alone do not prevent self-preference.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/scratch/synthesis-digest.md:268:- **A larger cross-family panel is reliably safer because averaging cancels individual judge bias.** vs PoLL supports heterogeneous panels empirically, but RoPoLL proves ordinary mean aggregation can have unbounded bias under any positive biased contamination and therefore panel size alone is not a robustness guarantee. _(ev: https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.18796 versus https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30931)_
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/scratch/synthesis-digest.md:297:- **More votes eventually wash out judge bias.** vs The July 2026 Bayesian-ranking study reports naive aggregation plateauing at the wrong top-k under systematically biased judges; additional correlated votes reinforce the bias. _(ev: https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.02104)_

standard packet==packetSkillName==deep-<mode>; do not rename (folder non-load-bearing)","evidence":"ai-council/SKILL.md:2; skill-graph-db.ts:601"}],"refutations":[{"target":"CORRECTION #2: hub SKILL.md:64,97 + README:24 still use bare packet names","held":false,"note":"Stale — rg finds only new names (deep-context/ deep-research/ deep-review/ ai-council/ deep-improvement/) at SKILL.md:64,97 and README:24; rename swept in commit c84b7477d1. Keep a regression guard (C11), but the named instance is closed."},{"target":"One-identity keystone: nested packets must drop graph-metadata.json","held":true,"note":"discoverGraphMetadataFiles:601 DFS-collects every file named graph-metadata.json at any depth; nested SKILL.md never read, so folder name is non-load-bearing and a nested metadata file would create a 2nd skill."},{"target":"Tension-5 claim that Python<->TS lexical divergence is the core risk","held":false,"note":"TS (aliases.ts) does no lexical scoring — only alias->mode projection; the sole shared artifact is the projection, which the registry now owns. Weight divergence across runtimes is a future boundary, not a current one."}],"standardize":["sk-doc/references/skill_creation.md §9.5 Parent Skills with Nested Mode Packets: anatomy + ALWAYS/NEVER + registry contract (advisorRouting/routingClass/advisorDefaultMode/packetSkillName)","/create:parent-skill (type on /create:skill) + parent_skill_md_template.md + mode_registry_template.json; scaffolds 1 hub graph-metadata + N packets with none; fail-closed I1-I7","/doctor:parent-skill read-only route in doctor/_routes.yaml with checks C1-C12 incl. C10 derivation-parity and C12 grandfather-INFO","routing/discovery benchmark via /deep:skill-benchmark on deep-loop-workflows itself: D1 per-routingClass gold (adds context+improvement), D2 exactly-one-skill discovery proof, D3 over-routing floor, pre-flight = doctor invariants","routing-parity-registry-coverage.vitest.ts iterating every registry mode (closes T4) shared as the benchmark gold set"],"risks":["Forced ai-council rename is high-blast (agent id, command skill: refs, artifactRoot, 9 changelogs) for zero advisor benefit — standard must forbid it","C10 parity check must diff BOTH Python and aliases.ts exports; live parity test shells Python only, so TS drift can false-green","Parent skill in a family outside ALLOWED_FAMILIES is invisible until skill-graph-db.ts:133 is edited — /create must hard-stop (ESCALATE), not emit silently","Coverage fixture guards pattern-group existence, not weight equality — cross-runtime weight drift stays uncovered if a future TS scorer lands"]}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/117-parent-nested-skill-pattern/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:223:      "resolution": "add routing-parity-registry-coverage fixture iterating every mode = doctor invariants = benchmark gold set",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/117-parent-nested-skill-pattern/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:259:    "routing-parity-registry-coverage.vitest.ts iterating every registry mode (closes T4) shared as the benchmark gold set"
.opencode/specs/system-skill-advisor/012-skill-advisor-tuning/008-semantic-shadow-prove-or-freeze/plan.md:51:The seed path mirrors the existing lane-weight sweep: load the full advisor projection, build a fixture projection of every described skill, seed each skill description through the embedding provider (cache-backed for determinism), and spy `loadSkillEmbeddings` to return the seeded vectors. Scoring against a fixture projection avoids the sqlite embedding-staleness gate and any live daemon DB open. All described skills are seeded (not just the corpus gold set) so the cosine lane can pull toward ANY skill — a wrong flip (an abstain false-fire) is as observable as a right one. The provider is pinned by `providerModelId` and asserted equal; a mismatch is a loud failure under the flag. Each corpus row's query embedding is fetched once and the same embedding is used for both arms, so the only difference between arms is whether the semantic lane contributes. The runtime degradation detector drives the lane's existing stale-projection path and reads `getSemanticShadowRuntimeHealth().disabledReason`, proving a silent degradation is surfaced as telemetry.
.opencode/specs/system-skill-advisor/012-skill-advisor-tuning/008-semantic-shadow-prove-or-freeze/implementation-summary.md:94:| Seed ALL described skills, not just the corpus gold set | So the cosine lane can pull toward any skill and a wrong flip (abstain false-fire) is as observable as a right one |
.opencode/specs/system-skill-advisor/007-skill-advisor-embedder-stack/002-jina-swap-and-reindex/implementation-summary.md:80:   - Two reviewers, two perspectives, same finding → high signal
.opencode/specs/system-skill-advisor/007-skill-advisor-embedder-stack/001-pluggable-architecture/review/review-report.md:80:**Confirmation:** iter 8 (documentation-alignment) caught the SAME issue from a different angle ("docs claim env-var swap; impl uses vec_metadata"). The same defect surface, two reviewers, two perspectives — high signal.

.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/research/iterations-modes/iteration-020.md:2436:A gold set anchors the GRADER, not the proposer: a small output set with human-agreed target scores, re-scored whenever the grader model changes. Disagreement beyond ~2 of 25 (or 8 of 100) means recalibrate before trusting new grades. Keep gold outputs and target scores version-locked outside any tree a loop can write.

Disagreement beyond ~2 of 25 (or 8 of 100) means recalibrate before trusting new grades. Keep gold outputs and target scores version-locked outside any tree a loop can write.

Disagreement beyond ~2 of 25 (or 8 of 100) means recalibrate before trusting new grades. Keep gold outputs and target scores version-locked outside any tree a loop can write.

.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/research/iterations-modes/iteration-029.md:3500:## 3. GOLD-SET RULES
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/065-deep-loop-innovation/002-deep-loop-effectiveness-and-fanout/research/iterations-modes/iteration-029.md:3502:A gold set anchors the GRADER, not the proposer: a small output set with human-agreed target scores, re-scored whenever the grader model changes. Disagreement beyond ~2 of 25 (or 8 of 100) means recalibrate before trusting new grades. Keep gold outputs and target scores version-locked outside any tree a loop can write.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/066-command-surface-benchmark/000-command-benchmark-contract/verdict-and-ownership.md:16:This contract keeps benchmark validity, deterministic conformance, behavioral adherence, and generic document validation as separate claims with separate owners and native vocabularies.

.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/001-gpt-behavioral-hardening-research/research/lineages/sonnet-critical/deep-research-strategy.md:45:- **KQ-CRIT-4** (iter 4, iter 8): The ai-council "naming drift" is a code-traced, deterministic emitter/validator self-contradiction inside one YAML file, not a registry-vs-YAML drift; it is a precondition for KQ1/KQ6 benchmark validity.
.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/z_archive/023-sk-deep-research-evolution/005-sk-deep-research-review-mode/research/deep-research-strategy.md:200:- Direct nested invocation between the two reviewers is the wrong reuse model because both agents are LEAF-only; the orchestrator should mediate optional seed/calibration packet exchange instead

.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/002-gpt-reliability-research/research/briefs/R5-ranking-crosscheck.md:49:| F-025 | A6 | 033 benchmark methodology | CONFOUND FOUND IN OUR OWN BENCHMARK: the vague-ask scenario prompts embed fixture PATHS whose tokens (deep-loops, council-target, improvement-target) leak intent evidence to the scorer — natural-language routing is weaker than 033 measured; future scenario authoring should keep vague prompts path-free | Note for 033 package maintenance: path-free vague variants (optimize, S) | Benchmark validity improvement | S | 006 |
